EDCS-00380253.summary: This inscription is a personal name, likely identifying the owner or dedicatee of a monument.

EDCS-00380254.translation: Of the Laekani family, of Viator.
EDCS-00380254.summary: This inscription is a personal name, likely identifying the owner or dedicatee of a monument.

EDCS-00380255.translation: Of the Laekani family, of Viator.
EDCS-00380255.summary: This inscription is a personal name, likely identifying the owner or dedicatee of a monument.

EDCS-00380257.summary: This inscription is a personal name, likely identifying the owner or dedicatee of a monument, with a possible query about the cognomen.

EDCS-00380825.text_edition: \n idius Quirina Novatus praefectus electorum expeditorum ex Raetia bello Dacico praefectus cohortis III Brittonum equitatae donis militaribus hasta pura vexillo corona murali ab imperatore donatus voto suscepto in expeditione Germanica\n
EDCS-00380825.translation: Novatus, prefect of the elect body of expeditores from Raetia, during the Dacian War, prefect of the 3rd Cohort of Britons (mounted), decorated with military gifts (a spear, a vexillum, and a mural crown) by the emperor, having undertaken a vow during the German expedition.
EDCS-00380825.summary: This inscription records the military career and honors of Novatus, a prefect in the Roman army, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-00380826.text_edition: \n Coeunto he meam et conlibertos aut ego te ipsum pedicabo Calventius Super fecit\n
EDCS-00380826.translation: Come to me, and my freedmen, or I will defile you myself. Calventius Super made this.
EDCS-00380826.summary: This inscription is an aggressive personal statement and a dedication of a work by Calventius Super, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-03000758.translation: To Tiberius Claudius, son of Tiberius.
EDCS-03000758.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Tiberius Claudius, son of Tiberius, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-03000762.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius [Antoninus] Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestatis sic pater patriae consul proconsul vias et pontes veteres restituit curam agente Pontio Pontiano legato Augusti pro praetore a Brigetione milia passuum XXXIIII\n
EDCS-03000762.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the fatherland, consul, proconsul, restored the old roads and bridges, with Pontius Pontianus, imperial legate with the authority of a proconsul, in charge, 34 miles from Brigetio.
EDCS-03000762.summary: This inscription records Emperor Marcus Aurelius's restoration of roads and bridges, overseen by his legate Pontius Pontianus, dated to his reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-03000763.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Opellius Severo sic Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus consul II proconsul tribunicia potestate pontifex maximo sic et Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diatumenianus sic nobilissimus Caesar vias et pontes vetustate conlabsos sic restituerunt curam agente Aelio Trecciano sic legato Auggustorum pro praetore ab sic Brigetione milia passuum XXXIIII\n
EDCS-03000763.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, consul II, proconsul, holding tribunician power, chief priest, and Marcus Opellius Antoninus Diatumenianus, most noble Caesar, restored the roads and bridges which had collapsed from age, with Aelius Treccianus, legate of the emperors, in charge, 34 miles from Brigetio.
EDCS-03000763.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperors Macrinus and his son Diatumenianus, overseen by their legate Aelius Treccianus, dated to 217-218 CE.

EDCS-03000764.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus vias vetustate conlapsas cum pontibus restituit a Brigetione milia passuum XXXIIII\n
EDCS-03000764.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus restored the roads which had collapsed from age, along with the bridges, 34 miles from Brigetio.
EDCS-03000764.summary: This inscription records Emperor Philip the Arab's restoration of roads and bridges, dated to his reign (244-249 CE).

EDCS-03300753.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a personal name, possibly Marcus Tineius.

EDCS-03300757.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureli Soteris nutritoris et amici rarae fidei Cassius Crescentianus a militiis\n
EDCS-03300757.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Aurelius Soter, a nurse and friend of rare loyalty, Cassius Crescentianus, from the military service.
EDCS-03300757.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates a monument to Aurelius Soter, a loyal friend and nurse, by Cassius Crescentianus, who served in the military.

EDCS-03300758.text_edition: \n Cordula Quinti Aconi Veri principis legionis XV Apollinaris\n
EDCS-03300758.translation: Cordula, daughter of Quintus Aconius Verus, principal of the 15th Apollinaris legion.
EDCS-03300758.summary: This inscription appears to record the name and affiliation of Cordula, daughter of Quintus Aconius Verus, a high-ranking officer in the 15th Apollinaris legion.

EDCS-03300759.translation: Publius Cocceius Pollio, centurion of the 15th legion.
EDCS-03300759.summary: This inscription records the name and rank of Publius Cocceius Pollio, a centurion in the 15th legion.

EDCS-03700674.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Nepoti vixit annos X\n
EDCS-03700674.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Nepos lived 10 years.
EDCS-03700674.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription recording the death of a child named Nepos at the age of 10.

EDCS-03700679.text_edition: \n Aelius Marinus decurio coloniae Poetovionensis equo publico praefectus cohortis II Hispanorum et Publius Aelius Marcianus decurio coloniae eiusdem equo publico praefectus cohortis I Germanorum parentibus pientissimis\n
EDCS-03700679.translation: Aelius Marinus, decurion of the colony of Poetovio, by public horse (equo publico), prefect of the 2nd Cohort of Spaniards, and Publius Aelius Marcianus, decurion of the same colony, by public horse (equo publico), prefect of the 1st Cohort of Germans, to [their] most dutiful parents.
EDCS-03700679.summary: This inscription honors the parents of two military officers, Aelius Marinus and Publius Aelius Marcianus, who held significant positions in auxiliary cohorts.

EDCS-03700680.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Geusius Domesticus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-03700680.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Geusius Domesticus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-03700680.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Gaius Geusius Domesticus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-03700681.text_edition: \n Soranus Iamelici filius miles cohortis Augustae Ituraiorum sic Ituraius sic nnorum L stipendiorum XIIX hic situs est |(centurio) Germanus |(centurio) titulum memoriae posuit\n
EDCS-03700681.translation: Soranus, son of Iamelicus, a soldier of the Augustan Cohort of Ituraeans, thus Ituraean, aged 50 years, with 18 years of service, lies here. Germanus, a centurion, placed this memorial title.
EDCS-03700681.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Soranus, an Ituraean soldier, and states that it was erected by Germanus, a centurion.

EDCS-03700682.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudius Lucillus alae III Thracum sibi et Aeliae Faustae coniugi pientissimae \n
EDCS-03700682.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Claudius Lucillus of the 3rd Thracian Ala, for himself and for Aelia Festa, his most dutiful wife.
EDCS-03700682.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Claudius Lucillus of the 3rd Thracian Ala for himself and his wife, Aelia Festa.

EDCS-04600170.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the name Vindicius and his age of 90 years.

EDCS-04900841.text_edition: \n Vala Verodis filia annorum XV hic sita est pater posuit\n
EDCS-04900841.translation: Vala, daughter of Verodis, aged 15, lies here; her father placed this.
EDCS-04900841.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of a young girl, Vala, placed by her father.

EDCS-04900842.text_edition: \n Bitutriso F Cotisai filius Boius annorum XII Annia filia hic intus est situs Cotisai annorum XXV p(ater) posuit\n
EDCS-04900842.translation: Bitutriso, son of Cotisai, a Boian, aged 12, and Annia, daughter of Cotisai, aged 25, lie within; their father placed this.
EDCS-04900842.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of two individuals, Bitutriso and Annia, placed by their father.

EDCS-04900843.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly a name or a dedication, the meaning of which is unclear.

EDCS-04900844.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum Iulia Censorina votum solvit libens laeta merito\n
EDCS-04900844.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Iulia Censorina willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-04900844.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Mercury by Iulia Censorina, likely fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900845.text_edition: \n Silvanis sacrum Aurelia cilla votum libens m(erito) sulvit sic\n
EDCS-04900845.translation: Sacred to the Silvani. Aurelia Cilla willingly, deservedly, and rightfully fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-04900845.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the Silvani (nature spirits) by Aurelia Cilla, likely fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900848.text_edition: \n Marcus Herennius Marci filius Voltinia Phil<i>ppis miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXXIX stipendiorum XVII hic situs est\n
EDCS-04900848.translation: Marcus Herennius Philippis, son of Marcus, of the Voltinian tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 39, with 17 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-04900848.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Herennius Philippis, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, detailing his age and service.

EDCS-04900849.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Pompeius Ingenuus miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Claudi Augustani nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XV hic situs heres Pompeius Amandus fratri pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-04900849.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Gaius Pompeius Ingenuus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, in the century of Claudius Augustanus, aged 35, with 15 years of service, lies here; his heir Pompeius Amandus placed this for the most devoted brother.
EDCS-04900849.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Pompeius Ingenuus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, placed by his heir, Pompeius Amandus.

EDCS-04900850.text_edition: \n Silavano et Minervae Gaius votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-04900850.translation: To Silvanus and Minerva. Gaius willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-04900850.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus and Minerva by Gaius, likely fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900852.summary: A dedication to the household god Silvanus Domestiscus.

EDCS-04900855.summary: A dedication to the household god Silvanus Domestiscus.

EDCS-04900860.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico solvit votum Atalonius miles legionis XIIII\n
EDCS-04900860.translation: Atalonius, a soldier of the Legio XIIII, fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-04900860.summary: A soldier of Legio XIIII dedicates this to Silvanus Domesticus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900861.summary: This is a sacred dedication to Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-04900864.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Aurelius Aulusanus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-04900864.translation: Aurelius Aulusanus joyfully and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-04900864.summary: Aurelius Aulusanus dedicates this to Silvanus, joyfully fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900865.summary: This inscription is dedicated to Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-04900866.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri sacrum Valeria Valeri votum libens laeta solvit\n
EDCS-04900866.translation: Valeria Valeri joyfully and happily fulfilled her vow to Silvanus Silvestris.
EDCS-04900866.summary: Valeria Valeri dedicates this to Silvanus Silvestris, joyfully fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900867.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Valeri-- Maxim-- votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-04900867.translation: Valerius Maximius joyfully and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-04900867.summary: Valerius Maximius dedicates this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900868.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae sacrum Aureli-- Flav votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-04900868.translation: Aurelius Flavius fulfilled his vow to Diana Augusta.
EDCS-04900868.summary: Aurelius Flavius dedicates this to Diana Augusta, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-04900873.text_edition: \n Domino fieri ut pactus iussit visu Lucium Messium m beneficiarium consularis legionis\n
EDCS-04900873.translation: Lucius Messius, beneficiarius of the consular legion, had it made as agreed and ordered by his vision.
EDCS-04900873.summary: A beneficiarius of a consular legion records an order for a monument to be made, based on a vision.

EDCS-04900876.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Titus Disius S.
EDCS-04900876.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manes by Titus Disius.

EDCS-04900879.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Martino Saturini filio annorum XXX et Valentinae annorum V et Ceriliae coniugi\n
EDCS-04900879.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: for Martino, son of Saturinus, aged thirty years, and for Valentina, aged five years, and for Cerilia, his wife.
EDCS-04900879.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to the Dis Manes for Martino, his daughter Valentina, and his wife Cerilia.

EDCS-04900880.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Victori Valentis filio ALFIMAAC legionis X L et sibi vivi Victorinus et Florentinus Candida Bella Iulia filii et heredes \n
EDCS-04900880.translation: To the Divine Shades. Victorinus and Florentinus and Candida Bella Iulia, his children and heirs, erected this for Victorius, son of Valentis, a soldier of the 10th Legion, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-04900880.summary: A funerary epitaph for Victorius, a soldier of the 10th Legion, erected by his children and heirs.

EDCS-04900881.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Firmani Cassronis armorum custodis cohortis I sagittariorum stipendiorum XIII domo Lai Dasillus vexillarius heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-04900881.translation: To the Divine Shades. Dasillus, a vexillarius and heir, saw to the construction of this for Firmanus, armor custodian of the I Cohort of Archers, after 13 years of service.
EDCS-04900881.summary: A funerary epitaph for Firmanus, an armor custodian in the I Cohort of Archers, commissioned by his heir.

EDCS-04900882.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Nero Sabini miles cohortis I Aeliae sagittariorum annnorum XXX stipendiorum XVI hic situs est Lucanus Natonis heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-04900882.translation: To the Divine Shades. Nero Sabini, a soldier of the I Aelian Cohort of Archers, aged 30, with 16 years of service, lies here; Lucanus Natonis, his heir, saw to the construction.
EDCS-04900882.summary: A funerary epitaph for Nero Sabini, a soldier in the I Aelian Cohort of Archers, erected by his heir.

EDCS-04900883.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Surio Provincialis filius miles cohortis I Aeliae sagittariorum armorum custos annorum XXXVII stipendiorum XVII Ulpius Philippus optio heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-04900883.translation: To the Divine Shades. Surio Provincialis, son of Surio, a soldier and armor custodian of the I Aelian Cohort of Archers, aged 37, with 17 years of service; Ulpius Philippus, a optio and heir, saw to the construction.
EDCS-04900883.summary: A funerary epitaph for Surio Provincialis, a soldier and armor custodian in the I Aelian Cohort of Archers, erected by his heir.

EDCS-04900884.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Liccai Licini equitis cohortis I Aeliae sagittariorum stipendiorum XV annorum XXX Nigrinus heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-04900884.translation: To the Divine Shades. Nigrinus, heir, saw to the construction for Liccai Licini, a cavalryman of the I Aelian Cohort of Archers, aged 30, with 15 years of service.
EDCS-04900884.summary: A funerary epitaph for Liccai Licini, a cavalryman in the I Aelian Cohort of Archers, erected by his heir.

EDCS-04900885.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpio Avito veterano cohortis I Aeliae sagittariorum annorum LXXV et Victorinae coniugi annorum XL et Avitae filiae annorum X et Emerito filio annorum VIIII et Superiae filiae annorum VI parentibus pientissimis Victoria filia et cognatis heres ex testamento\n
EDCS-04900885.translation: To the Divine Shades. Victoria, daughter and heir by testament, erected this for her most devoted parents Ulpius Avitus, a veteran of the I Aelian Cohort of Archers, aged 75, and Victorina, aged 40, and for her sister Avita, aged 10, and her brother Emeritus, aged 9, and her sister Superia, aged 6.
EDCS-04900885.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpius Avitus, a veteran of the I Aelian Cohort of Archers, and his family, erected by his daughter and heir.

EDCS-04900886.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimio Karino tesser(ario) annorum XXXVIII stipendiorum XXII et Septimio Crescenti militi cohortis sagittariorum Antoninianae annorum XXX stipendiorum X fratribus Septimiae Crescentinae annorum L matri Septimio Geniali patri Septimiae Quetae et Septimiae Quaetillae et Septimiae Praesentinae sororibus et Septimiae Victorinae coniugi Septimius Karus veteranus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-04900886.translation: To the Divine Shades. Septimius Karus, a veteran, saw to the construction for his brothers Septimius Karinus, a tesserarius, aged 38, with 22 years of service, and Septimius Crescens, a soldier of the Antoninian Cohort of Archers, aged 30, with 10 years of service, and for their mother Septimia Crescentina, aged 50, and their father Septimius Genialis, and their sisters Septimia Queta, Septimia Quaetilla, and Septimia Praesentina, and his wife Septimia Victorina.
EDCS-04900886.summary: A funerary epitaph for multiple family members, including two brothers who served in cohorts of archers, erected by a veteran.

EDCS-04900890.text_edition: \n Dis Inferis Manibus Marcus Ulpius Nigrinus Marci Ulpi Uxsperi sic veterani filius vixit annos X hic situs est pater faciendum curavit et Picarus Dumnadiciae libertus Secundus\n
EDCS-04900890.translation: To the Infernal Shades. Marcus Ulpius Nigrinus, son of the veteran Marcus Ulpius Uxsper, lived 10 years; he lies here; his father saw to the construction, and also Secundus, freedman of Picarus and Dumnadicia.
EDCS-04900890.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Nigrinus, a child, erected by his father and also mentioning a freedman.

EDCS-04900891.translation: Severus, having made a vow, gladly fulfilled it as deserved.

EDCS-04900892.text_edition: \n Aeliano annorum II Aelius Servatinus eques et Ulpia Maxima parentes filiis pientissimis s p\n
EDCS-04900892.translation: Aeliano, aged 2, Aelius Servatinus, a cavalryman, and Ulpia Maxima, his parents, for their most devoted son, with health and good wishes.
EDCS-04900892.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aeliano, a child, erected by his parents.

EDCS-04900894.translation: She who is to come to the lands, may the gods protect her.
EDCS-04900894.summary: This inscription is a short votive dedication, possibly a prayer or omen, with an unknown recipient and date.

EDCS-05000671.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae numini sancto et praestantissimo Lucius Vanderius votum solvit libens merito XIIII Kalendas Novembres Severo et Quintiano p p SII\n
EDCS-05000671.translation: Lucius Vanderius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the unconquered Sun god Mithras, holy and most excellent, 14 days before the Kalends of November, under Severus and Quintianus, consuls.
EDCS-05000671.summary: This is a dedication to the god Sol Invictus Mithras, made by Lucius Vanderius, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE based on the consular dating.

EDCS-05000674.text_edition: \n Eponae Augustae sacrum Claudi Maximi legati Augusti pro praetore superiumentari et muliones votum solverunt laeti libentes merito\n
EDCS-05000674.translation: To Epona Augusta, sacred. Claudius Maximinus, legate of the emperor with proconsular power of the upper regions, and the muliones (muleteers or drivers) fulfilled their vow joyfully, gladly, and deservedly.
EDCS-05000674.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Epona Augusta by Claudius Maximinus, legate, and the muliones, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05000676.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ae usin us C linus libertus et heres patrono et sibi vivus et Longiniae Viaticae uxori faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-05000676.translation: To the Divine Manes. Clinius, freedman and heir, had this made for his patron and himself while alive, and for Longinia Viaticus, his wife.
EDCS-05000676.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Clinius, a freedman, for himself, his patron, and his wife, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-05200557.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or memorial to a group of brothers, with an unknown context and date.

EDCS-05200558.text_edition: \n Felix Itala Dis Manibus Augustaniae Cassiae Marciae coniugi incomparabili quae vixit annos XXXIIII menses XI dies XIII quaequae sic dum explesset fati sui laborem meliora sibi sperans vitam functa est et Marco Antonio Augustanio Phileto filio nnimo qui vixit nnos III menses VIII dies X cui dii nefandi parvulo contra votum genitorum vita privaverunt Marcus Antonius Basilides frumentarius legionis X geminae coniugi et filio pientissimis Simplici Urani vobis terram levem\n
EDCS-05200558.translation: Felix Itala to the Divine Manes. To Augustania Cassia Marcia, incomparable wife, who lived 34 years, 11 months, 13 days, and who, having completed the labor of her destined life, departed, hoping for better things for herself. And to Marcus Antonius Augustanius Philetus, son, who lived 3 years, 8 months, 10 days, whom impious gods, against the wish of his parents, deprived of life when he was a little child. Marcus Antonius Basilides, grain official of the Legio X Gemina, for his most dutiful wife and son. To you, Simplicius Uranius, may the earth be light.
EDCS-05200558.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription by Marcus Antonius Basilides for his wife Augustania Cassia Marcia and son Marcus Antonius Augustanius Philetus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, given the legion mentioned.

EDCS-05200559.text_edition: \n Cava Curmisagi filia annorum L et Troucillius Flori filius annorum XVI hic siti sunt Florus Segili filius vivus sibi et coniugi et filio\n
EDCS-05200559.translation: Cava Curmisagis, daughter, aged 50, and Troucillius Florus, son, aged 16, lie here. Florus, son of Segilius, while alive, for himself, his wife, and his son.
EDCS-05200559.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Cava Curmisagis and Troucillius Florus, and commissioned by Florus Segili for his family, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-06100475.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus isti sunt Viator et Flavia Arca soror et IBIM Ursa \n
EDCS-06100475.translation: To the Divine Manes. These are Viator and Flavia Arca, sister, and Ursa.
EDCS-06100475.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Viator, Flavia Arca, and Ursa, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-06100490.summary: This is a very short inscription, likely a dedication by Gaius Aelius Comes, with an unknown context and date.

EDCS-06100493.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus a Secund--- quae vixit nnos LX \n
EDCS-06100493.translation: To the Divine Manes. [Dedication to] Secundus, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-06100493.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely for a person named Secundus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-06100494.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Secundinus nnum XI Iulia Octavia soror fratri pientissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-06100494.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Julius Secundinus, aged eleven. Iulia Octavia, his sister, arranged for this monument to be made for her most devoted brother.
EDCS-06100494.summary: A tombstone commemorating Gaius Julius Secundinus, erected by his sister Iulia Octavia.

EDCS-07000804.translation: Crescens, daughter of Verianus Finita, daughter of Attus.
EDCS-07000804.summary: A tombstone marking the death of Crescens, daughter of Verianus and Finita, Attus's daughter.

EDCS-07000805.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpiae Ammae hic sitae annorum XV virgo a et \n
EDCS-07000805.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ulpia Amma, buried here, aged fifteen, a virgin.
EDCS-07000805.summary: A tombstone commemorating Ulpia Amma, a fifteen-year-old virgin.

EDCS-07000806.text_edition: \n Collegio cultorum Ditis Patris et Proserpinae Ulpius Andria ex voto dono dedit\n
EDCS-07000806.translation: Ulpius Andria, by a vow, gave this as a gift to the college of the worshippers of Dis Pater and Proserpina.
EDCS-07000806.summary: A dedication to the gods Dis Pater and Proserpina by Ulpius Andria, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-07000814.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Saturninus Sextili et Anna Arri parentes vivi sibi fecerunt et Tabanoni filio annorum XVIIII\n
EDCS-07000814.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Saturninus and his parents Sextilius and Anna Arria, being alive, made this for themselves and for their son Tabanon, aged nineteen.
EDCS-07000814.summary: A tombstone for Saturninus, his parents Sextilius and Anna Arria, and their son Tabanon.

EDCS-07000815.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Festo veterano cohortis II Batavorum et Ulpiae Iaiiae parentibus et Cocceio Festivo filio Aelia Festa faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-07000815.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Festa arranged for this monument to be made for Aelius Festus, a veteran of the Second Cohort of Batavians, and for her parents Ulpia Iaia, and for her son Cocceius Festivus.
EDCS-07000815.summary: A tombstone erected by Aelia Festa for her parents, her son, and a veteran of the Second Cohort of Batavians.

EDCS-07000816.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Florentinae annorum VI Septimius December armorum qustos sic cohortis I Aeliae sagittariae et Aurelia Victorina parentes filiae karissimae et sibi faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-07000816.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Florentina, aged six. Septimius December, an armament keeper of the First Cohort of Aelian Archers, and Aurelia Victorina, her parents, arranged for this monument to be made for their dearest daughter and for themselves.
EDCS-07000816.summary: A tombstone for Aurelia Florentina, aged six, erected by her parents Septimius December and Aurelia Victorina.

EDCS-07000820.text_edition: \n Marimogio sacrum Pistus et Philete pro salute Phileti fili votum solverunt\n
EDCS-07000820.translation: Sacred to Marimogius. Pistus and Philete fulfilled their vow for the safety of Philete, their son.
EDCS-07000820.summary: A votive dedication to Marimogius by Pistus and Philete for their son's well-being.

EDCS-07000821.text_edition: \n Fonti et Nymphis pro salute Gaetulici et Gaetuliciani et Venerandae et Laetae dominorum meorum Hilarus vikarius sic votum solvit libens merito X Kalendas Maias Apro et Maximo consulibus\n
EDCS-07000821.translation: To the Fountain and the Nymphs. Hilarus, the substitute, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the safety of his masters Gaetulicus, Gaetulicianus, Veneranda, and Laeta. On the 22nd of April, when Apro and Maximus were consuls.
EDCS-07000821.summary: A votive dedication to the Fountain and Nymphs by Hilarus for the safety of his masters, dated by the consulship of Apro and Maximus.

EDCS-07000822.text_edition: \n Lucio Caesio Cai filio Tuendo annorum L et Petroniae Quinti filiae Crispinae coniugi annorum VL Caius Caesius Cai filius Victor annorum fratri piissimo Caius Caesius Cai filius Vitulus annorum Lucius Caesius Luci filius Optatus annorum et Caesia Cai filia Graeca annorum nepotes avoncu<l=I>o sic \n
EDCS-07000822.translation: Lucius Caelsius Victor, son of Caius, aged [---], to his most devoted brother Lucius Caelsius Optatus, son of Lucius, aged [---], and to his uncle Lucius Caelsius Tuendo, son of Caius, aged fifty, and to his aunt Petronia Crispina, daughter of Quintus, aged forty-five, and to his niece Caesia Graeca, daughter of Caius, aged [---].
EDCS-07000822.summary: A family tombstone commemorating multiple relatives, including Lucius Caelsius Tuendo, Petronia Crispina, Lucius Caelsius Optatus, Caius Caelsius Vitulus, and Caesia Graeca, with Lucius Caelsius Victor as the primary dedicant.

EDCS-07000824.translation: A horse for Ursicinus, the magistrate.
EDCS-07000824.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a horse, likely to a deity or for a public purpose, dedicated by an unknown person to Ursicinus, who held a magistracy, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600364.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Antonio colonia CARAS Valenti veterano legionis XIII geminae C agraria missione HOT A in ALIPIA Antonio Fortunato IIIANTI hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-07600364.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Lucius Antonius Valens, veteran of the XIII Geminae legion, from the colony of Caras, who received land from the agrarian commission, and who served in Alipia. This monument will not pass to his heir. Antonius Fortunatus dedicated it.
EDCS-07600364.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Antonius Valens, a veteran of the XIII Geminae legion, dated to the Roman Imperial period, likely the 1st or 2nd century CE, with a specific clause about the monument's inheritance.

EDCS-07600365.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Vitalis QV filius eorum B\n
EDCS-07600365.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Aelius Vitalis, son of QV. And their children.
EDCS-07600365.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Publius Aelius Vitalis and his children, dated to the Roman Imperial period, likely the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07600366.text_edition: \n Nutricibus Augustis sacrum Vitalis et Vintumila coniugi pro Maximilla filia votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-07600366.translation: Sacred to the August Nurses. Vitalis and Vintumila, his wife, fulfilled their vow for their daughter Maximilla with willing hearts and deservedly.
EDCS-07600366.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the August Nurses (divine nurses) by Vitalis and Vintumila for their daughter Maximilla, from the Roman Imperial period, likely the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07600367.text_edition: \n Nutricibus Augustis deis Aur<e=F>lius Servandus et Ulpia Secundina pro Secu<n=M>dino et Servato filiis votum votum solverunt libentes merito \n
EDCS-07600367.translation: Sacred to the August Nurses. Aurelius Servandus and Ulpia Secundina fulfilled their vow for their sons Secundinus and Servatus with willing hearts and deservedly.
EDCS-07600367.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the August Nurses by Aurelius Servandus and Ulpia Secundina for their sons, from the Roman Imperial period, likely the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07600368.translation: Sacred to the August Nurses. Publius MiI...
EDCS-07600368.summary: This is a fragmentary votive inscription dedicated to the August Nurses by Publius MiI..., from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600369.text_edition: \n Nutricibus Augustis Aelia Vera pro salute iar Antoniae filiae ni fili votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600369.translation: Sacred to the August Nurses. Aelia Vera fulfilled her vow for the health of her daughter Iar Antoniae and her son, with a willing heart and deservedly.
EDCS-07600369.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the August Nurses by Aelia Vera for the health of her daughter and son, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600370.text_edition: \n Nutricibus Augustis sacrum Titus Cassius Verinus decurio coloniae Poetovionensium praefectus fabrum praefectus pro IIviris et Donnia Maximilla coniux pro salute Titi Cassi Severi fili\n
EDCS-07600370.translation: Sacred to the August Nurses. Titus Cassius Verinus, decurion of the colony of Poetovionensium, prefect of artisans, prefect, for the IIviri, and his wife Donnia Maximilla, for the health of their son Titus Cassius Severus.
EDCS-07600370.summary: This is a complex votive inscription dedicated to the August Nurses by Titus Cassius Verinus, a local official in Poetovionensium, and his wife Donnia Maximilla, for their son's health, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600371.text_edition: \n Nutricibus Au<g=C>(ustis) sacrum Lucius Fuscinius Exsuperatus Augustalis coloniae Poetovionensium et Aelia Honorata pro salute Fuscini Honorati fili votum solverunt \n
EDCS-07600371.translation: Sacred to the August Nurses. Lucius Fuscinius Exsuperatus, an Augustalis of the colony of Poetovionensium, and Aelia Honorata fulfilled their vow for the health of their son Fuscinus Honoratus.
EDCS-07600371.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the August Nurses by Lucius Fuscinius Exsuperatus, a prominent local figure in Poetovionensium, and his wife Aelia Honorata, for their son's health, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600372.text_edition: \n Memoriae Iuliae Cupitae obsequentissimae et rarissimae propter pietatem coniugi quae vixit nnos XX menses VIIII dies XIII Apuleius Lupus miles legionis XIIII geminae et Iulia Fecula filia superstis sic et Tiberia Domitiana mater et sibi vivi faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-07600372.translation: In memory of Iulia Cupita, most obedient and very rare, for her piety. She lived 20 years, 9 months, 13 days. Apuleius Lupus, a soldier of the XIIII Geminae legion, and her daughter Iulia Fecula, who survived her, and also Tiberia Domitiana, mother, and for themselves, arranged to have this built while alive.
EDCS-07600372.summary: This is a funerary monument for Iulia Cupita, recording her age and virtues, commissioned by her surviving daughter, daughter-in-law (implied by Tiberia Domitiana's relation to Apuleius Lupus), and husband, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600373.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Apollinaris Quinti Sabini Verani tertiae partis conductoris portorii Illyrici servus tabularius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600373.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Dispeller, Apollinaris, a clerk from the third part of the tax farm of Illyricum, owned by Quintus Sabinius Veranius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600373.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Apollinaris, an employee involved in tax collection, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07600374.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Spectatius Maro votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07600374.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Spectatius Maro willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600374.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Spectatius Maro, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07600375.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Vaberio Titi filio Celsino annorum LX et Semproniae Celerinae coniugi annorum XL et Vaberio Celerino annorum XX et Vaberio Celsino annorum XVI filiis Vaberii Martinus et Atto liberti et heredes patrono optimo faciendum curaverunt hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-07600375.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Titus Vaberius Celsinus, son of Titus, aged 60, and his wife Sempronia Celerina, aged 40, and their sons Vaberius Celerinus, aged 20, and Vaberius Celsinus, aged 16. Martinus and Atto, freedmen and heirs of Vaberius, had this monument made for their most excellent patron; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-07600375.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by freedmen and heirs for their patron and his family, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07600376.text_edition: \n Silvanus sic Silvana sic Ser Como votum merito solvit libens\n
EDCS-07600376.translation: To Silvanus, Silvana, Ser Como willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-07600376.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus by Ser Como, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07600377.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus by Quintus Laberius Crescens, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-07600378.text_edition: \n Valerius Proculus annorum VII hic situs est curaverunt titulum Valerius Potitus et Aluntio parentes\n
EDCS-07600378.translation: Valerius Proculus, aged 7, lies here. His parents, Valerius Potitus and Aluntia, had this tombstone made.
EDCS-07600378.summary: This is a tombstone for a young boy named Valerius Proculus, erected by his parents, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08300492.text_edition: \n Nymphis Iasis Flavius Hermadion circitor vectigalis Illyrici et Ulpia Piste eius cum Avito et Suriaco filiis phialam argenteam pondo II donum dederunt\n
EDCS-08300492.translation: To the Nymphs of the spring Iasis, Flavius Hermadion, a watchman for the Illyrian customs duties, and his wife Ulpia Piste, along with their sons Avitus and Suriacus, gave a silver phiale weighing 2 pounds as a gift.
EDCS-08300492.summary: This is a dedication of a silver phiale to the Iasis Nymphs by Flavius Hermadion, a customs official, and his family, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08400438.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo numini Augusti Lucius Caecilius Viator Augustalis municipii Brigetionis collegio libertinorum ob honorem centenariam donum dedit Felix Lucius Adiutor Viatorianus Germanus Eutichas sic Viator Iulius Celer Muttius Victorinus Octavius Firmus Catinius Trofimus Iulius Lentinus Iulius Primio Aurelius Patronius\n
EDCS-08400438.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and the divine will of Augustus, Lucius Caecilius Viator, an Augustalis of the municipium of Brigetio, gave this gift to the college of freedmen in honor of reaching the age of 100 years. Felix Lucius Adiutor, Viatorianus Germanus, Eutichas, Viator, Iulius Celer, Muttius Victorinus, Octavius Firmus, Catinius Trofimus, Iulius Lentinus, Iulius Primus, Aurelius Patronius.
EDCS-08400438.summary: This inscription records a gift made by Lucius Caecilius Viator to the college of freedmen in Brigetio to celebrate a centenarian honor, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08500516.text_edition: \n Manius Flavius Mani libertus Favor nnorum XXX hic situs est Manius Flavius Mani filius Velina Marcellus liberto fecit ob merita\n
EDCS-08500516.translation: Manius Flavius Favor, freedman of Manius, aged 30, lies here. Manius Flavius Marcellus, son of Manius, of the Velina tribe, made this for his freedman because of his merits.
EDCS-08500516.summary: This is a tombstone for Manius Flavius Favor, a freedman, erected by his patron Manius Flavius Marcellus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08600579.text_edition: \n urius Luci filius miles legionis XV Apolllinaris annorum \n
EDCS-08600579.translation: Iurius, son of Lucius, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged...
EDCS-08600579.summary: This is a fragment of a tombstone for Iurius, a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08600580.text_edition: \n ParaM Pudes (denarios) LXX scutella cuparius Cersiaca saga (denarios) XV (denarios) XV Nonis Ianuaris Monomarus\n
EDCS-08600580.translation: ParaM Pudes (denarii) 70, Cersiaca with her cup (denarii) 15, 15. On the Nones of January, Monomarus.
EDCS-08600580.summary: This inscription appears to be a record of financial transactions or accounts, possibly related to the sale of goods by individuals named ParaM Pudes and Cersiaca, dated to January.

EDCS-08600581.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Heliopolitano Quintus Pomponius Sosipater\n
EDCS-08600581.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus of Heliopolis, Quintus Pomponius Sosipater.
EDCS-08600581.summary: This is a dedication to the god Jupiter Optimus Maximus of Heliopolis by Quintus Pomponius Sosipater, likely on a religious monument.

EDCS-08600582.translation: Of the centuria of Restutus Ingenuus.
EDCS-08600582.summary: This inscription identifies a unit or property belonging to Restutus Ingenuus, possibly marking a grave or a land boundary.

EDCS-08600583.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caviae Ingenuae et Aureliae Alexandriae et Aurelio Procliano et Aureliae Barbillae Marcus Aurelius Alexander veteranus legionis XIIII coniugi et filis carissimis\n
EDCS-08600583.translation: To the spirits of the departed Cavia Ingenua, Aurelia Alexandria, and Aurelius Proclianus, and Aurelia Barbilla; Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a veteran of the 14th legion, to his dearest wife and children.
EDCS-08600583.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a veteran of the 14th legion, to his wife and children.

EDCS-08600596.text_edition: \n una cum Aurelia Marcellina eius et Aurelio Emerito frumentario legionis X geminae dicaverunt sacerdotibus Vibio Crescente et Herennio Nigriniano Sabino II et Venusto consulibus\n
EDCS-08600596.translation: Together with his wife Aurelia Marcellina and Aurelius Emeritus, a provisioner of the 10th Gemina legion, they dedicated [this] during the consulship of Vibius Crescens, Herennius Nigrinianus Sabinus (holding the office) twice, and Venustus.
EDCS-08600596.summary: This inscription records a joint dedication made by Aurelia Marcellina and Aurelius Emeritus, a soldier serving in the 10th Gemina legion, during a specific consular period.

EDCS-08600597.translation: To the Mother Goddesses, Quitus Comosus paid his vow.
EDCS-08600597.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the Matres or Mother Goddesses by Quitus Comosus.

EDCS-08600598.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Caesernius Marcellinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08600598.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Caesernius Marcellinus, a deputy of the governor, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-08600598.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Caesernius Marcellinus, a military official, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, expressing gratitude.

EDCS-08600600.summary: This brief inscription likely records a payment or a dedication of sixteen units (possibly denarii or years) to Calpurnia Crispa.

EDCS-08600602.text_edition: \n Iovi Optumo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci A[ureli] Commodi Antonini Augusti Pii Felicis Claudius Claudianus praefectus cohortis II Alpinorum templum a fundamentis constituit sub cura Prastinae Messalini legati Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-08600602.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, for the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Antoninus Augustus Pius Felix, Claudius Claudianus, prefect of the 2nd Cohort of Alpiners, established this temple from its foundations, under the supervision of Prastina Messalinus, imperial legate pro praetore.
EDCS-08600602.summary: This inscription records the construction of a temple from its foundations, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the emperor's well-being, by Claudius Claudianus, prefect of a military cohort, under the supervision of a governor.

EDCS-08900382.text_edition: \n Aecornae Augustae Publius Cassius Secundus praefectus alae Britannicae milliariae civium Romanorum bis torquatae donis donatus bis bello Dacico ab Imperatore Caesare Nerva Traiano Augusto Germanico Dacico coronis vexillis hastis\n
EDCS-08900382.translation: To Aecorna Augusta, Publius Cassius Secundus, prefect of the British ala milliaria of Roman citizens, twice decorated with torcs, twice honored in the Dacian war by the Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus with crowns, standards, and spears.
EDCS-08900382.summary: This is a dedication by Publius Cassius Secundus, a cavalry prefect, to Aecorna Augusta, listing his military honors received during the Dacian Wars from Emperor Trajan.

EDCS-08900537.translation: Vintio, use this (gift) happily in God.
EDCS-08900537.summary: This is a short funerary epitaph, likely Christian, recording the name Vintio and a blessing.

EDCS-08901090.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Pullius Aemilianus miles legionis XIII geminae ex voto libens laetus\n
EDCS-08901090.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Pullius Aemilianus, a soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, fulfills his vow willingly and gladly.
EDCS-08901090.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Pullius Aemilianus, a soldier of Legio XIII Gemina, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08901091.text_edition: \n Cereri Augustae Minervae et Genio huiusce loci pro salute Imperatoris Severi Augusti Flavius Mucatra tesserarius legionis XIIII geminae in honorem collegii quaestor posuit Imperatore Severo II Clodio Albino Caesare II consulibus\n
EDCS-08901091.translation: For Ceres Augusta, Minerva, and the Genius of this place, for the safety of the Emperor Severus Augustus, Flavius Mucatra, a tesserarius of Legio XIIII Gemina, placed this in honor of the quaestor's association, during the consulship of Emperor Severus II and Clodius Albinus Caesar II.
EDCS-08901091.summary: This is a dedication to several deities for the emperor's safety, set up by Flavius Mucatra of Legio XIIII Gemina, dating to 197 CE.

EDCS-08901092.text_edition: \n Esdrius Stai filius domo Brixia Fabia tubicen cohortis I Montanorum nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XIIII hic situs est Verdavi duo cognati posuerunt\n
EDCS-08901092.translation: Esdrius, son of Staius, from Brixia, a trumpeter of the Cohors I Montanorum, aged 35, with 14 years of service, lies here. Verdavus and his two relatives placed this.
EDCS-08901092.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Esdrius, a trumpeter in Cohors I Montanorum, who died at age 35 after 14 years of service, with his relatives erecting the monument, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08901093.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Iulius Celerinus votum rettulit libe<n=I>s \n
EDCS-08901093.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Julius Celerinus willingly returned his vow.
EDCS-08901093.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus by Julius Celerinus, who is fulfilling a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08901104.text_edition: \n Sancto Silvano sacrum Ursinius votum libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-08901104.translation: Sacred to Holy Silvanus. Ursinius willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-08901104.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus by Ursinius, who is fulfilling a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100890.text_edition: \n Libero et Liberae sacrum Caius Catullinius Firmus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit\n
EDCS-09100890.translation: Sacred to Liber and Libera. Caius Catullinius Firmus, a beneficiary of a consular legate, paid his vow.
EDCS-09100890.summary: This is a dedication to Liber and Libera by Caius Catullinius Firmus, a soldier with special duties, who is fulfilling a vow, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09100891.summary: This is a short inscription identifying a military unit, specifically the century belonging to Flavius Primi.

EDCS-09100892.text_edition: \n Centuria Lupi corniculiarii primi CAI Cai Lami\n
EDCS-09100892.translation: The century of Lupus, the chief horn-blower, of the first cohort of Caius Lamius.
EDCS-09100892.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, possibly indicating the century of Lupus, a horn-blower, and potentially part of a cohort named after Caius Lamius.

EDCS-09100893.summary: This is a short inscription identifying a military unit, specifically the century belonging to Iulia.

EDCS-09100894.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis VIIII consul III pater patriae restituit milia passuum XVII\n
EDCS-09100894.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, restored seventeen miles of road.
EDCS-09100894.summary: This inscription records the restoration of seventeen miles of road by Emperor Alexander Severus, likely during his reign (222-235 AD).

EDCS-09100896.translation: May you prosper, Florentius Lupianus, beneficiary.
EDCS-09100896.summary: This is a personal inscription wishing prosperity to Florentius Lupianus, who held the position of beneficiary, possibly from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09100897.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Publius Aelius Verus |(centurio)? legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis Romulianus artifex fecit\n
EDCS-09100897.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Dolichenus, Publius Aelius Verus, possibly a centurion of the I Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Romuliana legion, the craftsman, made (this).
EDCS-09100897.summary: This dedicatory inscription honors Jupiter Dolichenus, made by Publius Aelius Verus, identified as a possible centurion and craftsman, dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09200292.text_edition: \n Lucius Pedanius Luci filius Dialo vivus fecit sibi et Helviae Titi filiae Cirpae uxori (et) Lucio Pedanio Luci filio Optato filio\n
EDCS-09200292.translation: Lucius Pedanius Dialus, son of Lucius, made (this) while alive for himself and for his wife Helvia Cirpa, daughter of Titus, and for his son Lucius Pedanius Optatus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-09200292.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned during the lifetime of Lucius Pedanius Dialus for himself, his wife Helvia Cirpa, and his son Lucius Pedanius Optatus, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09200294.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Fructuosus Auggugustorum nnostrorum dispensator pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit\n
EDCS-09200294.translation: Fructuosus, a procurator of our August Emperors, fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of his family to Jupiter Best and Greatest the Protector.
EDCS-09200294.summary: This votive inscription records Fructuosus, a procurator, fulfilling a vow for his well-being and that of his family to Jupiter, dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09200400.text_edition: \n terina Nertomari filius posuit coniugi suae carissimae\n
EDCS-09200400.translation: Terina, son of Nertomarus, set up (this) for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-09200400.summary: This inscription records Terina, son of Nertomarus, setting up a monument for his wife, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09200402.text_edition: \n Brigia Sacri filia annorum VC hic sita est Blatumarus \n
EDCS-09200402.translation: Brigia, daughter of Sacrus, aged 85, is buried here. Blatumarus...
EDCS-09200402.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Brigia, daughter of Sacrus, who died at age 85, with a partial mention of Blatumarus, likely from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09200405.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a funerary inscription, possibly recording ages or names, with a lacuna, dating from the 1st-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09200422.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai Claudia Maxsimus sic Savaria miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXXIV stipendiorum XVI hic situs est vivi fecerunt Iulia Comati filia coniugi bene merenti et Iulius Maximinus patri pro pietate\n
EDCS-09200422.translation: Caius Iulius Maximus, son of Caius and Claudia, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 34 with 16 years of service, is buried here. They set (this up) while alive: Iulia Comati, daughter of Comati, to her deserving husband, and Iulius Maximinus to his father for his dutifulness.
EDCS-09200422.summary: This complex funerary inscription records the burial of soldier Caius Iulius Maximus and includes separate dedications by his wife and son, dating to the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09200423.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Titi filius Claudia Isidorus Astup annorum miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Metili Iusti stipendiorum V hic situs est heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09200423.translation: Titus Flavius Isidorus, son of Titus, of Astura, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, in the century of Metilius Justus, with 5 years of service, is buried here. The heir took care to have (this) made.
EDCS-09200423.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Flavius Isidorus, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion with 5 years of service, and notes that his heir arranged for the monument, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-09200426.text_edition: \n Lucius Comagius filius Voltinia Severinus Avennione miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis annorum XXXIX stipendiorum hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09200426.translation: Lucius Comagius Severinus, son of Voltinia, a soldier of the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion, aged 39, with 39 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by his will, and his heir had it constructed.
EDCS-09200426.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Lucius Comagius Severinus, a soldier, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200434.translation: To the Genius of Caius Calpurnius.
EDCS-09200434.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of Caius Calpurnius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200435.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae Sextia -- SERSEC votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09200435.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera, Sextia SERSEC fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09200435.summary: This is a votive inscription to the gods Liber Pater and Libera, dedicated by Sextia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200437.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Clodius Spectatus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09200437.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Clodius Spectatus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09200437.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Clodius Spectatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200439.text_edition: \n pro salute oni Caesaris et Baetua posuerunt\n
EDCS-09200439.translation: For the health of the Emperor Caesar and Baetua, they set this up.
EDCS-09200439.summary: This is a dedication set up for the health of the Emperor Caesar and Baetua, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200440.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Caius Avius Voltilius itumrus \n
EDCS-09200440.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Caius Avius Voltilius [---].
EDCS-09200440.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Caius Avius Voltilius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200442.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Mercatorius Maecili --- b(ene)f(iciarius) procuratoris Augusti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09200442.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Mercatorius Maecili [---], beneficiary of the procurator of Augustus, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09200442.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Mercatorius Maecili, a beneficiary of a procurator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09200446.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico et Silvanae Seius Ingenuus beneficiarius consularis pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito Imperatore Antonino III et Geta Caesare II consulibus\n
EDCS-09200446.translation: To Silvanus Domestus and Silvana, Seius Ingenuus, a consular beneficiarius, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly for himself and his family, during the consulship of Emperor Antoninus III and Caesar Geta II.
EDCS-09200446.summary: This is a votive inscription to Silvanus Domestus and Silvana by Seius Ingenuus, a consular beneficiarius, dated to 198 CE.

EDCS-09300473.text_edition: \n Marcus Petronius Quintus vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Aquilinae coniugi et Petronio Crispiniano filio et Petroniae Veneriae sorori et Cordio Mercatori et Cordiae Rufinillae nepti\n
EDCS-09300473.translation: Marcus Petronius Quintus made this for himself while alive, and for his wife Iulia Aquilina, and for his son Petronius Crispinianus, and for his sister Petronia Veneria, and for Cordius Mercator and his granddaughter Cordia Rufiniilla.
EDCS-09300473.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Marcus Petronius Quintus for himself and his family members, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300474.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae Titus Flavius Titi filius Papiria Oesci Domitius Valerianus |(centurio) legionarius factus at sic suffragium legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis Severianae votum solvit libens merito Pompeiano et Paeligniano consulibus\n
EDCS-09300474.translation: To Fortuna Augusta, Titus Flavius Domitius Valerianus, son of Titus Flavius, of the Papiria tribe, a centurion, who was made a legionary but served as a centurion in the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix Severiana legion, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus.
EDCS-09300474.summary: This is a votive inscription to Fortuna Augusta by Titus Flavius Domitius Valerianus, a centurion of the 14th Legion, dated to 217 CE.

EDCS-09301174.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii et Veri Caesaris cohors I Aelia sagittaria cui praeest Caius Iulius Longinus tribunus Quintillo et Prisco consulibus\n
EDCS-09301174.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of Emperors Antoninus Augustus Pius and Verus Caesar, the First Aelian Cohort of Archers, commanded by its tribune Gaius Julius Longinus, under the consulship of Quintillus and Priscus.
EDCS-09301174.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by the First Aelian Cohort of Archers, likely from the period of Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus' joint rule (mid-2nd century AD).

EDCS-09301176.text_edition: \n Viva fecit Marcana Fausta |(mulieris) liberta sibi et Nobilini libertae suae annorum XXV et Quinto Miccionio Quinti liberto Felici annorum XXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09301176.translation: Marcana Fausta, freedwoman of a woman, made this (monument) while alive for herself and for her freedwoman Nobilina, aged 25, and for Quintus Miccionius Felix, freedman of Quintus, aged 35. He is buried here.
EDCS-09301176.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Marcana Fausta for herself and two freedpersons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09301177.text_edition: \n Egnatiae Quinti libertae Successae nnorum XXVIII hic sita est\n
EDCS-09301177.translation: For Egnatia Successa, freedwoman of Quintus, aged 28. She is buried here.
EDCS-09301177.summary: This is a tombstone for Egnatia Successa, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09301179.text_edition: \n Titus Valerius <T=F>a<r=B>(ia) Primus Brixia miles legionis I adiutricis stipendiorum XIX nnorum XL heres faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-09301179.translation: Titus Valerius Primus of Tarrha, a soldier in the First Adjutant Legion, with 19 years of service, aged 40, arranged for this to be made as his heir.
EDCS-09301179.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Valerius Primus, a soldier with 19 years of service, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09301181.translation: To Silvanus, he paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09301181.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Silvanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09301183.summary: This is a short dedication to the god Silvanus Viator, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09301185.text_edition: \n Marimogio Heutychus sic Lamyri votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09301185.translation: Heutychus, the servant of Marimogius, paid his vow to Lamyra willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09301185.summary: This is a votive inscription from Heutychus to Lamyra, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09301186.text_edition: \n Lucius Annaeus Luci <filius> Claudia Surus Celeia miles legionis XIII geminae nnorum XXXX stipendiorum XVIII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-09301186.translation: Lucius Annaeus Surus, son of Lucius, of Claudia, from Celeia, a soldier in the Thirteenth Twin Legion, aged 40, with 18 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-09301186.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Annaeus Surus, a soldier who served 18 years, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09400593.text_edition: \n Cliternia Prisca ancillae lllibertisque se viva posuit hic sitae sunt\n
EDCS-09400593.translation: Cliternia Prisca placed this while alive for her slave girls and freedwomen. They are buried here.
EDCS-09400593.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Cliternia Prisca for her slaves and freedwomen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401149.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dunomarus Brogimari votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09401149.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dunomarus, son of Brogimarus, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09401149.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter by Dunomarus, likely from the Roman Imperial period, possibly with Celtic influences.

EDCS-09401150.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salutem sic Augusti Septimius Quartinis sic filius Iuven voto fecit dedicavit votum libens solvit laetus merito\n
EDCS-09401150.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of the Emperor, Septimius Quartinis, son of Iuven, made this offering and gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09401150.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Septimius Quartinis for the emperor's safety, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-09401151.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Augustorum Aurelius Victorinus donavit cum filiis Marco Aurelio Antonino III et Get[a] II consulibus\n
EDCS-09401151.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of the Emperors, Aurelius Victorinus donated this with his sons, under the consulship of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus III and Geta II.
EDCS-09401151.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Aurelius Victorinus and his sons, mentioning specific consuls and thus dating it to their joint consulship.

EDCS-09401154.summary: This is a tombstone for Ila, daughter of Sasul, recording her age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401159.text_edition: \n Domino et Dominae posuit Fannius Firminus Cassianus\n
EDCS-09401159.translation: Fannius Firminus Cassianus set this up for the Lord and Lady.
EDCS-09401159.summary: This inscription records Fannius Firminus Cassianus setting up a monument for 'the Lord and Lady', possibly referring to imperial couple or deities, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401160.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum fecit Valentinius Saturninus votum libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09401160.translation: Valentinius Saturninus gladly and deservedly made this sacred offering to Silvanus Domesticius, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-09401160.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Domesticius by Valentinius Saturninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401161.text_edition: \n Genio Iovis Optimi Maximi Heliopolitani pro salute Augusti in honorem Octavi Valeriani patroni Octavii Maximus et Leucianus fratres iussu dei posuerunt Silanis consulibus\n
EDCS-09401161.translation: For the safety of the Emperor, in honor of Octavius Valerianus, our patron, Octavii Maximus and Leucianus, brothers, by order of the god, erected this to the Genius of Jupiter Best and Greatest of Heliopolis, under the consulship of Silanis.
EDCS-09401161.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of Jupiter of Heliopolis and in honor of patron Octavius Valerianus, erected by Octavii Maximus and Leucianus, mentioning an emperor's safety and a consulship.

EDCS-09401162.text_edition: \n Cinturoca Dagedoni filius annorum XV hic situs est Matuco Cavi\n
EDCS-09401162.translation: Cinturoca, son of Dagedon, aged 15, lies here; Matucus Cavi [buried him].
EDCS-09401162.summary: This is a tombstone for Cinturoca, son of Dagedon, aged 15, with indication of burial by Matucus Cavi, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401163.text_edition: \n pro salute Augusti Publius Aelius Pacatianus beneficiarius legati legionis XIIII geminae pro se et suis omnibus votum solvit Pompeiano et Paeligniano consulibus\n
EDCS-09401163.translation: For the safety of the Emperor, Publius Aelius Pacatianus, a beneficiary of the legate of the 14th Legion Gemina, fulfilled his vow for himself and all his family, under the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus.
EDCS-09401163.summary: This is a votive inscription by Publius Aelius Pacatianus, a soldier in the 14th Legion Gemina, for the emperor's safety and his family, dated by the mentioned consuls.

EDCS-09401164.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Firminus summus curator equitum singularium cum Septimia Maximilla coniuge votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09401164.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aelius Firminus, supreme curator of the mounted guards, with his wife Septimia Maximilla, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09401164.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Aelius Firminus, a high-ranking cavalry officer, and his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09401525.text_edition: \n Legio XIIII gemina Martia victrix Legio XIIII M M Legio XIIII Legio X IIII\n
EDCS-09401525.translation: The 14th Legion Gemina Martia Victrix; The 14th Legion MM; The 14th Legion; The 14th Legion.
EDCS-09401525.summary: This inscription is a series of abbreviated references to the 14th Legion Gemina, possibly marking a legionary base or a monument erected by the legion.

EDCS-09401534.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae neppos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XVI consul III pater patriae a Savaria milia passuum\n
EDCS-09401534.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 16th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, [built this road] from Savaria for X miles.
EDCS-09401534.summary: This inscription records a road built or repaired under Emperor Hadrian, dating to around AD 132.

EDCS-09401539.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerio Materno veterano legionis II adiutricis Valerius Lupercianus filius pientissimus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09401539.translation: To the departed spirits. Valerius Lupercianus, a devoted son, arranged for the construction of this monument for Valerius Maternus, a veteran of Legio II Adjutrix.
EDCS-09401539.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Valerius Maternus, a veteran, set up by his son Valerius Lupercianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09401541.text_edition: \n Pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti Marcus Ulpius Quintianus decurio coloniae Aquincensium IIviralis quinquennalis designatus et Titus Flavius Aprilis decurio coloniae suprascriptae equo publico praepositi annonae votum solverunt libentes merito Imperatore Antonino IIII et Balbino II consulibus\n
EDCS-09401541.translation: For the health of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus. Marcus Ulpius Quintianus, a decurion of the colony of Aquincum, a quinquennial duovir designate, and Titus Flavius Aprilis, a decurion of the aforesaid colony, appointed to oversee the grain supply, fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly, with Antoninus and Balbinus as consuls.
EDCS-09401541.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by two officials responsible for the grain supply in Aquincum, likely dating to the joint consulship of Antoninus (likely Caracalla) and Balbinus, possibly in the early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-09500132.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus In Cai filius Festus sibi et Flavi- Expo \n
EDCS-09500132.translation: To the departed spirits. Titus Festus, son of Caius, for himself and...
EDCS-09500132.summary: This is the beginning of a funerary inscription for Titus Festus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500133.text_edition: \n nnnorum XL et Ursulo filio annorum\n
EDCS-09500133.summary: This text is a continuation of a funerary inscription, mentioning ages of individuals, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500137.text_edition: \n MaximianL Marcus Flavius D vivus fecit Marco G obito nnorum LXX PONTI posu---\n
EDCS-09500137.translation: Maximian[us?]. Marcus Flavius erected this while alive for Marcus G[---], deceased, aged 70 years. PONTI...
EDCS-09500137.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Marcus Flavius for a deceased Marcus G., likely dating to the late Roman Empire given the name Maximian.

EDCS-09500138.text_edition: \n M ano annorum mater optima Valenti annorum XXV mater optima \n
EDCS-09500138.translation: deceased, aged [---] years, mother optima Valenti, aged 25 years, mother optima...
EDCS-09500138.summary: This text is part of a funerary inscription, mentioning the ages of deceased individuals and a mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500142.text_edition: \n Sagri filius turma Murci alae Pannoniorum nnorum XXV stipendiorum IIII hic situs est Gavius heres posuit\n
EDCS-09500142.translation: of Sagrius, son of [---], of the troop Murcius of the Pannonian ala, aged 25 years, with 4 years of service, is buried here. Gavius the heir placed this.
EDCS-09500142.summary: This inscription records the burial of a soldier from the Pannonian ala, identified as being 25 years old with 4 years of service, and placed by his heir Gavius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500143.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Finitius Lucanus tesserarius legionis X geminae Cosini consularis votum libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09500143.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Finitius Lucanus, tesserarius of Legio X Gemina, dutifully fulfilled his vow willingly and gladly.
EDCS-09500143.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to the god Silvanus by Finitius Lucanus, a tesserarius (messenger or guard) of Legio X Gemina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500152.text_edition: \n Caesernia Sexti filia Pusilla annorum LX hic sita est coniunx coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-09500152.translation: Caesernia Pusilla, daughter of Sextus, aged 60 years, is buried here. Her spouse placed this for her spouse.
EDCS-09500152.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caesernia Pusilla, aged 60, set up by her spouse, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09500153.text_edition: \n Crescens Iulionis filius miles cohortis I Augustae Ituraeorum nnorum XXXXIII stipendiorum XXIII hic situs est titulum memoriae posuit titulo Victor heres\n
EDCS-09500153.translation: Crescens, son of Iulionis, a soldier of the First Cohort Augusta of Ituraeans, aged 43 years and having served 23 years, is laid here; Victor, his heir, erected this memorial.
EDCS-09500153.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier named Crescens, who served in the Ituraean cohort, erected by his heir, Victor.

EDCS-09500154.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Asper eques alae Hispanorum I Aurianae Iucundo Talalni filio principi civitatis Azaliorum patri vivo et \n
EDCS-09500154.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Asper, an equestrian of the First Spanish Wing Auriana, erected this for his father Iucundus, son of Talalnus, prince of the Azalian people, while he was still alive.
EDCS-09500154.summary: This is an inscription dedicating a monument to Iucundus, a civic leader of the Azalii, erected by his son Asper, an equestrian.

EDCS-09500155.text_edition: \n Prosostus Couconis filius eques annorum XXX Couco Ble<g=C>issae filius nnorum LXX titulum memoriae posuit sibi et filio \n
EDCS-09500155.translation: Prosostus, son of Couco, a soldier, aged 30 years; Coucus, son of Ble<g=C>issa, aged 70 years. He erected this memorial for himself and his son.
EDCS-09500155.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Prosostus and his father Coucus, erected by Prosostus for both of them.

EDCS-09500156.text_edition: \n Nerotomarus Carmionis filius vivus sibi et \n
EDCS-09500156.translation: Nerotomarus, son of Carmionis, while alive for himself and...
EDCS-09500156.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording a dedication made by Nerotomarus for himself and possibly others.

EDCS-09500157.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Acutae Luci filiae annorum XX titulum posuit Valens Licali filius coniugi et sibi vivus Titus Aelius Gallunus bruti bene merenti faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09500157.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Valens, son of Licalis, erected this memorial for his wife Acuta, daughter of Lucius, aged 20 years, and for himself while alive; Titus Aelius Gallunus had this made for the deserving.
EDCS-09500157.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Acuta, erected by her husband Valens and also commissioned by Titus Aelius Gallunus.

EDCS-09500158.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aventinio Nigrino veterano cohortis I Ulpiae nnoniorum vixit nnos LXXII Ulpiae Liciniae uxori eius vixit nnos LX Septimius Victorinus armatura cohortis supra scriptae et Aventinius Nigrinianus librarius legionis I adiutricis filii parentibus karissimis faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09500158.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aventinius Nigrinus, a veteran of the First Cohort Ulpia of soldiers, lived 72 years; Ulpia Licinia, his wife, lived 60 years. Septimius Victorinus, a junior officer of the aforementioned cohort, and Aventinius Nigrinianus, a clerk of the First Adjutant Legion, had this made for their dearest parents.
EDCS-09500158.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aventinius Nigrinus and his wife Ulpia Licinia, commissioned by their sons.

EDCS-09500159.text_edition: \n Sancto Libero Patri et Liberae Augustae sacrum pro salute Imperatoris et Threpti dispensatoris Surus vikarius sic pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09500159.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater and Libera Augusta. For the safety of the Emperor and Threptus, the administrator, Surus, the deputy, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly, for himself and his family.
EDCS-09500159.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater and Libera Augusta, by Surus for the well-being of the Emperor and an administrator.

EDCS-09500160.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aemilio Aureliano quondam militi legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis ex adiutore officici cornicularii legati stipendiorum XIII qui vixit annos XXXI Marcus Aemilius Fortunatus Augustalis municipii Brigetionensium et Aurelia Candida parentes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09500160.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Aemilius Aurelianus, formerly a soldier of the First Adjutant Legion, pious and faithful, from the office of the legate's cornicularius, who served 13 years and lived 31 years. Marcus Aemilius Fortunatus, an Augustalis of the municipium of Brigetio, and Aurelia Candida, his parents, had this made.
EDCS-09500160.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aemilius Aurelianus, a soldier who served in the First Adjutant Legion, erected by his parents.

EDCS-09700244.text_edition: \n et Genio vici Chanazibo et Genio civitatis Caeseriensium sic Germaniciensium Fabius LonginusI \n
EDCS-09700244.translation: ...and to the Genius of the neighborhood of Chanazibo and to the Genius of the Caesarian people, thus of the Germanician people. Fabius Longinus...
EDCS-09700244.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicating a monument to the Genii of a neighborhood and two peoples, possibly commissioned by Fabius Longinus.

EDCS-09700263.text_edition: \n Terentius Aurelius Felix Sexar(ius Valentinus Marcius Florus Aurelius? CriN dedicatum Albino II et Maximo consulibus VIIII Kalendas\n
EDCS-09700263.translation: ...dedicated by Terentius Aurelius Felix, Sex(arius) Valentinus, Marcius Florus, Aurelius? CriN, during the consulship of Albinus II and Maximus, on the ninth day before the Kalends of...
EDCS-09700263.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording a dedication made by several individuals during the consulship of Albinus II and Maximus.

EDCS-09701112.text_edition: \n vixit annos XIIII Aelia Mulsa coniu<g=C>i et filiis carissimis faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-09701112.translation: Aelia Mulsa commissioned this to be made for her dearest husband and sons, having lived 14 years.
EDCS-09701112.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the age of the deceased and commissioned by the surviving spouse.

EDCS-09701137.text_edition: \n Lucius Iulius Optatus medicus hic intus situs est fututor Lucius Iulius Faustus de suo feci\n
EDCS-09701137.translation: Lucius Iulius Optatus, a doctor, lies within here; Lucius Iulius Faustus made this at his own expense.
EDCS-09701137.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a doctor, Lucius Iulius Optatus, paid for by Lucius Iulius Faustus.

EDCS-09701152.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Cato Lucconis annorum C Stuba Ransai annorum Busio Catonis annorum Ingenua Talani annorum XX titulum poni iusserunt \n
EDCS-09701152.translation: Tiberius Claudius Cato, aged X, Stuba Ransai, aged X, Busio Catonis, aged X, and Ingenua Talani, aged 20, ordered this monument to be set up.
EDCS-09701152.summary: This funerary epitaph records the names and ages of four individuals and states that they ordered the monument.

EDCS-09701166.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, with names and possibly patronymic or familial relationships.

EDCS-09701672.text_edition: \n Caius Titius Antonius Peculiaris conductor vectigalis octavi nnoniarum II nno XII\n
EDCS-09701672.translation: Gaius Titius Antonius Peculiaris, contractor of the tax collection for the Octaviani, for 8 years and 12 years.
EDCS-09701672.summary: This inscription records the name of a tax contractor and the duration of his contracts.

EDCS-09900001.text_edition: \n Diis sic Auguralibus item Silvano et Apollini et Mercurio item Beleno Augusto \n
EDCS-09900001.translation: To the Augural Gods, and also to Silvanus, Apollo, Mercury, and also to Belenus Augustus.
EDCS-09900001.summary: This is a religious dedication to multiple deities, including Roman and possibly Celtic gods.

EDCS-09900002.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Decimus Clodius Saturninus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900002.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Decimus Clodius Saturninus, a beneficiary of the consular legate, willingly fulfilled his vow deservedly.
EDCS-09900002.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Decimus Clodius Saturninus, a beneficiary of a consular legate.

EDCS-09900003.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Firmus pro se et suis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09900003.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Julius Firmus fulfilled his vow for himself and his family, willingly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-09900003.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Julius Firmus for himself and his family.

EDCS-09900004.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Manilius Florus miles legionis X geminae beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900004.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Manilius Florus, a soldier of the tenth legion Gemina and a beneficiary of the consular legate, willingly fulfilled his vow deservedly.
EDCS-09900004.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Manilius Florus, a soldier and beneficiary of a consular legate.
