EDCS-09900005.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno sacrum Marcus Ulpius Finitianus et Gaius Valerius Marcianus bbenefficiarii consularis legionis X geminae votum solverunt libentes merito Imperatore Antonino III et [Geta Caesare] II consulibus\n
EDCS-09900005.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. Marcus Ulpius Finitianus and Gaius Valerius Marcianus, beneficiaries of the consular legate of the tenth legion Gemina, willingly fulfilled their vows deservedly, while Antoninus was consul for the third time and Geta Caesar for the second time.
EDCS-09900005.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by two beneficiaries of a consular legate, dated by the consuls to the reign of Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-09900007.text_edition: \n Lunae Luciferae Lucius Satrius Auspicatus AI\n
EDCS-09900007.translation: To Luna Lucifera, Lucius Satrius Auspicatus [dedicated].
EDCS-09900007.summary: A dedication to Luna Lucifera by Lucius Satrius Auspicatus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09900008.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae sacrum Lucius Valerius Valerianus decurio coloniae Flaviae Sisciensium IIvir iure dicundo flamen divi Claudi IIvir quinquennalis sacerdos provinciae Pannoniae superioris ex voto\n
EDCS-09900008.translation: Sacred to Nemesis Augusta: Lucius Valerius Valerianus, decurion of the colony of Siscia Flavia, IIvir iure dicundo, priest of the deified Claudius, IIvir quinquennalis, priest of the province of Upper Pannonia, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09900008.summary: A dedication to Nemesis Augusta by Lucius Valerius Valerianus, a prominent magistrate and priest in the colony of Siscia Flavia, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900013.text_edition: \n Lucio Blassio Antigono annorum LX liberto et Terentiae Proculae coniugi a[nnorum ------\n
EDCS-09900013.translation: To Lucius Blassius Antigonus, aged 60, freedman, and to Terentia Procula, wife, aged [---].
EDCS-09900013.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Blassius Antigonus and his wife Terentia Procula, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900015.text_edition: \n Iulia Priscilla viva fecit sibi et Quinto Claudio Martiniano coniugi annorum XXX et Claudiis Decriano annorum et Martino annorum filiis\n
EDCS-09900015.translation: Iulia Priscilla made [this] while alive for herself, and for her husband Quintus Claudius Martinianus, aged 30, and for her sons Claudius Decrianus, aged [---], and Martinus, aged [---].
EDCS-09900015.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Iulia Priscilla for herself and her family, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900016.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Sempronius Cai filius Claudia Marcellinus annorum vivus fecit sibi et Sempronio Marcelliano filio annorum XXV et Sempronio Marcello filio annorum XXI et Sempronio Passeri fratri annorum XXX et Sempronio Florentino fratri annorum Claudiae Spectatae coniugi nnum\n
EDCS-09900016.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Sempronius, son of Caius, Claudia Marcellinus, aged [---], made [this] while alive for himself, and for his son Sempronius Marcellianus, aged 25, and for his son Sempronius Marcellus, aged 21, and for his brother Sempronius Passerus, aged 30, and for his brother Sempronius Florentinus, aged [---], and for his wife Claudia Spectata, aged [---].
EDCS-09900016.summary: A funerary inscription by Caius Sempronius Claudia Marcellinus for himself and his deceased male relatives and wife, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900018.text_edition: \n Nomina candidatorum Quinto Luxilio Iustino praefecto Firmo aedili Caesio ValeV\n
EDCS-09900018.translation: Names of candidates: Quintus Luxilius Iustinus, prefect, Firminus, aedile, Caelsius Vale[---].
EDCS-09900018.summary: A list of names, likely candidates for public office, including a prefect and an aedile, possibly from a public building inscription in the late Roman period.

EDCS-09900019.text_edition: \n Nammius Quintus custor sic cymiteri sic annorum LXXXVI et Aurelia Irene coniux annorum LX<X=L>XI \n
EDCS-09900019.translation: Nammius Quintus, guardian of the cemetery, aged 86, and Aurelia Irene, wife, aged 61.
EDCS-09900019.summary: A funerary inscription for Nammius Quintus, a cemetery guardian, and his wife Aurelia Irene, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900021.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio coloniae Claudiae Savariensium Titus Flavius Vitalis et Bruttius Valens beneficiarii consularis votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-09900021.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the Genius of the colony of Savaria Claudia, Titus Flavius Vitalis and Bruttius Valens, legates of the consular governor, fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900021.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and the Genius of Savaria by two officials, Titus Flavius Vitalis and Bruttius Valens, who were legates of a consular governor, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900022.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Salvius Primus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900022.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Salvius Primus, legate of the consular governor, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900022.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Lucius Salvius Primus, a legate of a consular governor, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900028.text_edition: \n VTRIV coniugi IN Aelio ito annorum L et Marciano Aeli I nnorum \n
EDCS-09900028.translation: To [---] VTRIV, wife, to Aelius [---] ito, aged 50, and to Marcianus, son of Aelius [---], aged [---].
EDCS-09900028.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning a wife and two men, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900029.text_edition: \n Aurelio Iulio Falado civi annorum L Aurelius Bassus cives sic \n
EDCS-09900029.translation: To Aurelius Iulius Faladus, a citizen aged 50. Aurelius Bassus, citizen, [made this].
EDCS-09900029.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Aurelius Iulius Faladus, dedicated by Aurelius Bassus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900030.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Luci filius Claudia Viator vivus fecit sibi et Corneliae \n
EDCS-09900030.translation: Lucius Cornelius, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, made this for himself and Cornelia, alive.
EDCS-09900030.summary: A funerary dedication made by Lucius Cornelius for himself and Cornelia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900031.text_edition: \n I Firmo iuveni innocenti defuncto annorum XX IMadius filio RAONTO EX PIVIND D \n
EDCS-09900031.translation: To Firminus, a young man, innocent, deceased at age 20. Madius for his son.
EDCS-09900031.summary: A funerary inscription for Firminus, a young man aged 20, set up by his father Madius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900032.text_edition: \n Marianus vivus fecit Zenoni avunculo civi Suro ex regione Cirro vico RE NGAL annorum LX IROI OPI RE\n
EDCS-09900032.translation: Marianus made this, alive, for his uncle Zeno, a citizen from the district of Cirrus, village of Rengal, aged 60.
EDCS-09900032.summary: A funerary inscription made by Marianus for his uncle Zeno, a citizen aged 60, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900033.text_edition: \n LA A P menses X dies XXVI et Iannum I et Ulpi-- Paulin et Valeri-- Lucill\n
EDCS-09900033.translation: L. A. P., 10 months and 26 days, and Iannus I, and Ulpius Paulinus, and Valerius Lucillus.
EDCS-09900033.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly recording birth or death dates and names, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900035.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Vicirius Messor annorum Claudi-- Val annorum XL Vicirilliae annorum XV Viciri-- AL \n
EDCS-09900035.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Vicirius Messor, aged [---]. Claudius Val, aged 40. Vicirillia, aged 15. Vicir[---].
EDCS-09900035.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Vicirius Messor and members of his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900036.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary memorial, mentioning Gaius Virtius and Claudia Constans, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900038.text_edition: \n nae annorum XV sorori hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-09900038.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription indicating the burial of a sister aged 15, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900040.text_edition: \n VNDAN conpar Publiaia libertus SARTEA [Primus ---] [ CAPITILN---]\n
EDCS-09900040.translation: Vundan bought. Publia's freedman, Sartea Primus...
EDCS-09900040.summary: A fragmented inscription possibly recording a purchase or a will, mentioning Vundan and Publia's freedman Sartea Primus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900042.text_edition: \n Santo Eliomari filio annorum L et Sunnurae Suadelli filiae coniugi et Saturnino filio annorum L\n
EDCS-09900042.translation: To Santo, son of Eliomarus, aged 50, and to Sunnura, daughter of Suadellus, his wife, and to Saturninus, his son, aged 50.
EDCS-09900042.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Santo, his wife Sunnura, and his son Saturninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900043.text_edition: \n Diannae sic Augustae sacrum Flavia Quintilia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900043.translation: To Diana Augusta, Flavia Quintilia willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-09900043.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Diana Augusta made by Flavia Quintilia.

EDCS-09900047.text_edition: \n Herculi IVA decurio coloniae Claudiae Savariensium aedilis ex voto votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09900047.translation: To Hercules, IV A, decurion of the colony of Savariensium, aedile, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900047.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Hercules made by a decurion and aedile of the colony of Savariensium.

EDCS-09900048.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Cr votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900048.translation: To Hercules Augustus, Cr willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900048.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Hercules Augustus made by someone identified as Cr.

EDCS-09900049.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Laurentius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900049.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Flavius Laurentius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900049.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Titus Flavius Laurentius.

EDCS-09900050.text_edition: \n Mercurio Augusto sacrum Gaius Iulius Maximus decurio coloniae Claudiae Savariae aedilicius et praefectus collegiorum fabrorum centonariorum votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09900050.translation: To Mercury Augustus, Gaius Julius Maximus, decurion of the colony of Claudia Savaria, aedilician official, and prefect of the guilds of builders and weavers, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900050.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mercury Augustus made by Gaius Julius Maximus, a prominent official in the colony of Savaria.

EDCS-09900052.text_edition: \n Damanaeus Buri filius vivus sibi et Sunamini matri et fratribus Disdosini et Blegissae et Claudiae ciae annorum XXX hic siti posuit\n
EDCS-09900052.translation: Damanaeus, son of Buri, while alive, placed this for himself, his mother Sunamin, and his brothers Disdosinus and Blegissa, and Claudia, aged 30.
EDCS-09900052.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the placement of a monument by Damanaeus for his family members.

EDCS-09900053.text_edition: \n Quinto Gavio Probo --- ann(orum) L hic est situs et Plotiae Festae coniugi vivae coniunx et filii posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900053.translation: His wife and sons placed this for Quintus Gavius Probus, aged 50, and for his wife, Plotia Festa, while she was alive.
EDCS-09900053.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the placement of a monument by a wife and sons for their deceased husband and father, and for the wife while she was alive.

EDCS-09900054.text_edition: \n Quintus Cai filius Dis Manibus Lucius F Calendinus us se posuit et Octaviae Florentinae\n
EDCS-09900054.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Lucius F Calendinus placed this for himself and for Octavia Florentina.
EDCS-09900054.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Manes, recording the placement of a monument by Lucius F Calendinus for himself and Octavia Florentina.

EDCS-09900055.text_edition: \n coniugi et Octaviae Iustinae conlibertae eius et FBTO IO et \n
EDCS-09900055.translation: To his wife and Octavia Justina, his freedwoman, and FBTO IO.
EDCS-09900055.summary: This fragment likely belongs to a funerary inscription, mentioning a wife and a freedwoman, Octavia Justina.

EDCS-09900056.text_edition: \n Lucius Naevius Terti filius Rufus Mediolano veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris deductus coloniam Claudiam Savariensium annorum LXXV hic situs est et Naeviae Lucidae coniugi suae titulum fieri iussit Lucius Naevius Silvanus libertus faciendum curavit locus monumenti quoquo versus pedes XX\n
EDCS-09900056.translation: Lucius Naevius Silvanus, a freedman, took care to have this monument made for Lucius Naevius Terti son Rufus, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion from Mediolanum, settled in the colony of Claudia Savariensium, aged 75, who is buried here, and for his wife, Naevia Lucida. The plot for the monument is 20 feet in every direction.
EDCS-09900056.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, placed by his freedman, also recording the plot size and a deceased wife.

EDCS-09900057.text_edition: \n Sextus Uttiedius Cai filius Claudia Celer veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum LXX hic situs est titulum fieri iussit sibi et Uttiediae Fuscae libertae et Valentinae provinciae libertae Repentinae Verecundae libertae ae Iinae tura VIS et IOV nepotis\n
EDCS-09900057.translation: Sextus Uttiedius Celer, son of Caius, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged seventy years, lies here; he ordered this tombstone to be made for himself, and for his freedwomen Uttiedia Fusca, Valentina, and Repentina Verecunda, and for the freedwoman of Iina, and for his grandsons and for IOV.
EDCS-09900057.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Sextus Uttiedius Celer, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, for himself and his freedwomen and grandsons, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09900058.text_edition: \n Uttiedia Sexti liberta Carnuntina nnorum XXV hic sita est Sextus Uttiedius Sexti libertus Narcissus pater vivus fecit sibi et suis\n
EDCS-09900058.translation: Uttiedia Carnuntina, freedwoman of Sextus, aged twenty-five years, lies here. Her father, Sextus Narcissus, freedman of Sextus, made this while alive for himself and his family.
EDCS-09900058.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Uttiedia Carnuntina, freedwoman of Sextus, and was erected by her father, Sextus Narcissus, a freedman.

EDCS-09900060.text_edition: \n culae annorum no annorum V Claudiae Magnae Urso et GEN \n
EDCS-09900060.translation: of ... aged five years for Claudia Magna. Urso and GEN
EDCS-09900060.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the death of a child, Claudia Magna, aged five, and mentions Urso.

EDCS-09900062.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Primio annorum vivus fecit sibi et Claudiae Firminae coniugi an[norum ------\n
EDCS-09900062.translation: Tiberius Claudius Primio, aged... alive, made this for himself and for his wife Claudia Firmina, aged...
EDCS-09900062.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Tiberius Claudius Primio, who erected it for himself and his wife Claudia Firmina.

EDCS-09900063.text_edition: \n Marcus Sempronius Marci filius Velina Severus nnorum LX hic situs est et Blassiae Luci filiae Severae coniugi Marcus Sempronius Vibius et Sempronia Marci filia Quarta filii parentibus piissimis arbitratu Cai Semproni Praesentis\n
EDCS-09900063.translation: Marcus Sempronius Severus, son of Marcus, of the Velina tribe, aged sixty years, lies here, and for his wife Blassia Severa, daughter of Lucius. Marcus Sempronius Vibius and Sempronia Quarta, daughter of Marcus, children, for their most devoted parents, under the direction of Gaius Sempronius Praesens.
EDCS-09900063.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Marcus Sempronius Severus and his wife Blassia Severa, and was erected by their children, Marcus Sempronius Vibius and Sempronia Quarta.

EDCS-09900064.text_edition: \n Atta Bataionis filius nnorum LV negotiator \n
EDCS-09900064.translation: Atta, son of Bataio, aged fifty-five years, a merchant.
EDCS-09900064.summary: This inscription records the death of Atta, son of Bataio, a merchant aged fifty-five.

EDCS-09900065.text_edition: \n Gaius Valerius Gai filius Dento annorum LXXV hic situs est et Vetticiae Sexti filiae Avvae coniugi titulum fieri iussit\n
EDCS-09900065.translation: Gaius Valerius Dento, son of Gaius, aged seventy-five years, lies here, and for his wife Avva, daughter of Sextus. He ordered this tombstone to be made.
EDCS-09900065.summary: This tombstone was erected by Gaius Valerius Dento for himself and his wife Avva.

EDCS-09900066.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ceterisque diis Quintus Terentius Potentinus miles legionis X geminae piae fidelis Severianae beneficiarius consularis Albino et Maximo consulibus\n
EDCS-09900066.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and all the other gods, Quintus Terentius Potentinus, a soldier of the X Gemina legion, pious, faithful, Severian, a beneficiarius consularis, under the consuls Albinus and Maximus.
EDCS-09900066.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter and other gods by Quintus Terentius Potentinus, a soldier of the X Gemina legion, during the consulship of Albinus and Maximus.

EDCS-09900067.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Publius Callius Scipio votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900067.translation: To Father Liber, Publius Callius Scipio fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900067.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater by Publius Callius Scipio.

EDCS-09900068.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum Lucius Arellius In votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900068.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Lucius Arellius fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900068.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Mercury by Lucius Arellius.

EDCS-09900069.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae sacrum Primitivus ex voto\n
EDCS-09900069.translation: Sacred to Nemesis Augusta, from Primitivus in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-09900069.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Nemesis Augusta by Primitivus.

EDCS-09900070.translation: Sacred to Nemesis, from Iulia Musa.
EDCS-09900070.summary: This is a dedication to Nemesis by Iulia Musa.

EDCS-09900072.text_edition: \n Lucius Calavius Luci filius Sergia Nicopolis Vitalis veteranus legionis I annorum LV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09900072.translation: Lucius Calavius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, from Nicopolis, a veteran of the First Legion, aged 55, lies here.
EDCS-09900072.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lucius Calavius, a 55-year-old veteran of the First Legion.

EDCS-09900073.text_edition: \n Comagia Cai filia Ingenua nnorum XXX hic sita est Titus Sariolenus Luci filius Pollia veteranus coniugi suae vivus fecit\n
EDCS-09900073.translation: Comagia Ingenua, daughter of Caius, aged 30, lies here. Titus Sariolenus, son of Lucius, of the Pollia tribe, a veteran, made this for his wife while alive.
EDCS-09900073.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Comagia Ingenua and was erected by her husband, Titus Sariolenus, a veteran.

EDCS-09900074.text_edition: \n Cotonius Cai filius Pollia Campanus veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris magister ludorum annorum LXXVII hic situs est Cotonia Prima liberta coniugi et sibi viva fecit Fortunatus Tor Servatus IKI curaverunt erunt quae meliora IAMSEROS vixi servat me securus quoque nunc contegor hic tumulo\n
EDCS-09900074.translation: Cotonius Campanus, son of Caius, of the Pollia tribe, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris Legion, an organizer of games, aged 77, lies here. Cotonia Prima, his freedwoman, made this for her husband and herself while alive. Fortunatus and Tor Servatus looked after it.
EDCS-09900074.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Cotonius Campanus, a veteran and organizer of games, and was made by his freedwoman, Cotonia Prima, with care provided by Fortunatus and Tor Servatus.

EDCS-09900075.text_edition: \n Petronia Ianuaria sibi et Tito Petronio Verecundo filio militi legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XX Tito Petronio Nivali filio annorum V Petroniae Mussae filiae annorum XX Petroniae Quartillae filiae annorum Tito Petronio Salutari filio annorum XXX Petroniae annorum XL Tito Petronio annorum VII \n
EDCS-09900075.translation: Petronia Ianuaria for herself and for her son Titus Petronius Verecundus, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, aged 20; for her son Titus Petronius Nivalis, aged 5; for her daughter Petronia Mussa, aged 20; for her daughter Petronia Quartilla, aged [?]; for her son Titus Petronius Salutaris, aged 30; for her son Titus Petronius, aged 7.
EDCS-09900075.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Petronia Ianuaria, dedicating a tomb for herself and several children, including a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion.

EDCS-09900076.text_edition: \n Salvius Aebutius Sexti filius Pollia veteranus legionis XV annorum L hic situs est Lucius Lucilius heres fecit\n
EDCS-09900076.translation: Salvius Aebutius, son of Sextus, of the Pollia tribe, a veteran of the XV Legion, aged 50, lies here. Lucius Lucilius, the heir, made this.
EDCS-09900076.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Salvius Aebutius, a 50-year-old veteran of the XV Legion, and was erected by his heir, Lucius Lucilius.

EDCS-09900077.text_edition: \n Caius Sextilius Senecio decurio Scarbantiae nnorum XXV Sextilia Prima annorum XXX Sextilia Vera annorum XVI Kania Titi liberta Urbana nnorum LX hic siti sunt Sextus Sextilius Sexti filius Lemonia Nepos filiis et uxori et sibi vivus fecit\n
EDCS-09900077.translation: Gaius Sextilius Senecio, a decurion of Scarbantia, aged 25; Sextilia Prima, aged 30; Sextilia Vera, aged 16; the freedwoman Urbana, daughter of Titus, aged 60, lie here. Sextus Sextilius, son of Sextus, of the Lemonia tribe, made this for his sons, wife, and himself while alive.
EDCS-09900077.summary: This epitaph records the burial of several individuals, including a decurion and his family, and was made by Sextus Sextilius for himself and his relatives.

EDCS-09900078.text_edition: \n Apollinaris filia annorum LX hic sita est Noricus A Olicanti filius uxsori sic\n
EDCS-09900078.translation: Apollinaris, daughter of [---], aged 60, lies here. Noricus A Olicanti, son of [---], for his wife thus.
EDCS-09900078.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Apollinaris and was erected by her husband, Noricus.

EDCS-09900079.text_edition: \n Larcio Aucto Larcio Saturnino libertis vivis\n
EDCS-09900079.translation: For Larcio Auctus and Larcio Saturninus, his freedmen, while alive.
EDCS-09900079.summary: This is a dedication by an unknown individual for his freedmen, Larcio Auctus and Larcio Saturninus, made during their lifetime.

EDCS-09900081.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius F\n
EDCS-09900081.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter, likely on a small altar or offering, made by Caius Iulius F.

EDCS-09900086.text_edition: \n Marcia Marci Expectati filia Marcellina annorum XXV hic sita est coniunx VEI faciendam curavit\n
EDCS-09900086.translation: Marcellina, daughter of Marcus Marcius Expectatus, aged 25, is laid here. Her husband VEI took care of the construction.
EDCS-09900086.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Marcellina, wife of VEI, who erected the monument.

EDCS-09900087.text_edition: \n Castrucius Tiberi libertus annorum XL hic situs est filii parenti fecerunt curam agente Urso Tiberi liberto Tiberius eques titulum posuit\n
EDCS-09900087.translation: Castrucius, freedman of Tiberius, aged 40, is laid here. His sons made this for their father, with Urso, freedman of Tiberius, overseeing the care. Tiberius the equestrian placed the inscription.
EDCS-09900087.summary: This is a tombstone for Castrucius, a freedman, set up by his sons, with an equestrian relative likely responsible for the erection.

EDCS-09900089.text_edition: \n Cereri Marcus Valerius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900089.translation: Marcus Valerius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Ceres.
EDCS-09900089.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Valerius to the goddess Ceres.

EDCS-09900091.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Iulius Senilis negotiator votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900091.translation: Iulius Senilis, a businessman, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-09900091.summary: This is a votive inscription by Iulius Senilis, a merchant, to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-09900092.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. LI Mace -- SECIMVS.
EDCS-09900092.summary: This inscription, partially lost, is a dedication to the god Silvanus, possibly made by an individual with the initial LI.

EDCS-09900094.text_edition: \n Bonae me<m=N>oriae Valeri Gaiani militis ducenarii annorum LX Aurelia Caianilla patri piissimo \n
EDCS-09900094.translation: To good memory. Aurelia Caianilla for her most dutiful father, Valerius Gaianus, soldier of a ducenarian regiment, aged 60.
EDCS-09900094.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Aurelia Caianilla for her father, Valerius Gaianus, a soldier of a ducenarian cohort.

EDCS-09900095.text_edition: \n Balti et Arvieiabus Flavius Mati pro se ac suis\n
EDCS-09900095.translation: Flavius Mati for himself and his family, to Baltus and Arvieiabus.
EDCS-09900095.summary: This is a dedication or offering made by Flavius Mati for his family, possibly to household deities or ancestors named Baltus and Arvieiabus.

EDCS-09900096.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ala I Ulpia contariorum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum cui praeest Quintus Terentius Ingenuus praefectus\n
EDCS-09900096.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. The ala I Ulpia contariorum miliaria, of Roman citizens, commanded by Quintus Terentius Ingenuus, prefect.
EDCS-09900096.summary: This inscription records the ala I Ulpia contariorum miliaria, a Roman cavalry unit, and names its commander, Quintus Terentius Ingenuus.

EDCS-09900097.text_edition: \n ala I Ulpia contariorum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum cui praeest Claudius Vindex praefectus equitum votum libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-09900097.translation: The ala I Ulpia contariorum miliaria, of Roman citizens, commanded by Claudius Vindex, prefect of cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900097.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by the ala I Ulpia contariorum miliaria, commanded by Claudius Vindex.

EDCS-09900098.text_edition: \n Pro salute Impperatorum Caessarum Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini Auggustorum Septimi Getae Caesaris et Iuliae Augustae matris Caesarum ala I Ulpia contariorum civium Romanorum |(miliaria) cui praeest CIlius Caianus praefectus p curam agens Aurelius Gracilis |(centurio) legionis X geminae\n
EDCS-09900098.translation: For the safety of the Emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti, of Caesar Septimius Geta and of Julia Domna, mother of the Caesars, the I Ulpia ala of crossbowmen, Roman citizens, a unit of a thousand men, commanded by Gaius Vilius Caianus, prefect, with Aurelius Gracilis, centurion of the Legio X Gemina, in charge.
EDCS-09900098.summary: This inscription records a military dedication made by the ala I Ulpia contariorum for the imperial family's safety, likely during the reign of Septimius Severus and his sons.

EDCS-09900100.text_edition: \n OFOLNInis annorum L hic situs est Petronia Festa contubernali suo karissimo pro pietate connatis VIIII\n
EDCS-09900100.translation: Here lies Petronia Festa, aged 50, for her dearest companion, on account of her conjugal devotion, [she lived] nine years.
EDCS-09900100.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Petronia Festa, dedicated by her beloved companion, highlighting her devotion and the duration of their relationship.

EDCS-09900101.text_edition: \n Publius Naevius Publi libertus Apollonius sibi et Vibiae Firmae coniugi et Naevio ConstantIIIII\n
EDCS-09900101.translation: Publius Naevius Apollonius, son of Publius, for himself and his wife Vibia Firma, and Naevius Constans.
EDCS-09900101.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone or a dedication, set up by Publius Naevius Apollonius for himself, his wife, and Naevius Constans.

EDCS-09900102.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto in honorem COI Quinti IIO TT\n
EDCS-09900102.translation: To Hercules Augustus, in honor of Quintus Coius, he willingly carried out his vow.
EDCS-09900102.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules Augustus by Quintus Coius, likely to fulfill a vow.

EDCS-09900104.text_edition: \n In memoriam Faustinae iunioris Caius Iulius Flavianus pater \n
EDCS-09900104.translation: In memory of Faustina the Younger, by Gaius Julius Flavianus, her father.
EDCS-09900104.summary: This inscription is a memorial to Faustina the Younger, erected by her father, Gaius Julius Flavianus.

EDCS-09900105.text_edition: \n Caio Aprio Frontino et Valeria Secunda viva fecit sibi et Caius Valerius Messor et Caius Valerius Iustus et Valeria Cupita et Valeria Iusta\n
EDCS-09900105.translation: Caio Aprio Frontino and Valeria Secunda made [this] while alive for themselves, and for Gaius Valerius Messor, Gaius Valerius Iustus, Valeria Cupita, and Valeria Iusta.
EDCS-09900105.summary: This is a funerary inscription indicating that Caio Aprio Frontino and Valeria Secunda created this monument during their lifetime for themselves and four other individuals.

EDCS-09900106.text_edition: \n annorum LXXX N annorum XXV Dignus nnorum XX Iulius Iustinus I annorum X Caius Iulius Ursinus pater et Annia Digna mater vivi posuerunt Iuliae Veturiae nepoti et Caio Iulio Digno nepoti et Iulio Ursulo nepoti Cania Ursula coniugi et filiis et nepotibus viva fecit\n
EDCS-09900106.translation: Eighty years old, N, twenty-five years old, Dignus, twenty years old, Julius Iustinus, ten years old. Gaius Julius Ursinus, the father, and Annia Digna, the mother, placed [this] while alive for their granddaughter Julia Veturia, and for their grandson Gaius Julius Dignus, and for their grandson Julius Ursulus. Cania Ursula made [this] while alive for her husband and sons and grandsons.
EDCS-09900106.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription recording the lives of multiple family members, with parents dedicating a monument for their grandchildren and a wife doing so for her entire family.

EDCS-09900107.text_edition: \n Caesernius Cai filius Senecio annorum vivus fecit sibi et Marciae Quinti filiae ae coniugi annorum et Caesernio Caeo filio annorum XX hic situs est \n
EDCS-09900107.translation: Caesernius Senecio, son of Caius, aged [..], made [this] while alive for himself and for Marcia, daughter of Quintus, his wife, aged [..], and for Caesernius Caius, his son, aged twenty, here he lies.
EDCS-09900107.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Caesernius Senecio for himself, his wife Marcia, and their son Caesernius Caius, also recording the son's death.

EDCS-09900108.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Faustus nnorum XXXXV Iulia Fausta nnorum XL hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-09900108.translation: Tiberius Iulius Faustus, aged forty-five, and Iulia Fausta, aged forty, here they lie.
EDCS-09900108.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Tiberius Iulius Faustus and Iulia Fausta, along with their ages.

EDCS-09900109.text_edition: \n Titus Opponius Marcianus annorum XV hic situs est\n
EDCS-09900109.translation: Titus Opponius Marcianus, aged fifteen, here he lies.
EDCS-09900109.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death and age of Titus Opponius Marcianus.

EDCS-09900110.text_edition: \n M Maximiani LIII am devotus numini maiestatique eorum posuit consulibus Constantino Ianuari\n
EDCS-09900110.translation: Dedicated to the divine power and majesty of Maximian, for fifty-three years, he placed (this), under the consulship of Constantinus Ianuarius.
EDCS-09900110.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Maximian, dated to the consulship of Constantinus Ianuarius, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-09900115.summary: This is a very brief epitaph or monument fragment simply stating the name and status of Crispinianus as a veteran, likely Roman.

EDCS-09900116.text_edition: \n vivus? fecit? sibi? et? --- Ulpi? ANC\n
EDCS-09900116.translation: He made (it) alive? for himself? and? Ulpius?
EDCS-09900116.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that seems to record someone making a monument for themselves and possibly another person named Ulpius.

EDCS-09900118.text_edition: \n Invicto Deo Mithrae Gaius Caetronius Peculiaris votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09900118.translation: To the Invincible God Mithras, Gaius Caetronius Peculiaris gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900118.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Gaius Caetronius Peculiaris, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09900119.text_edition: \n AIsICci annorum LV Tappia Ammionis coniux coniugi karissimo posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-09900119.translation: Tappia Ammionis, his wife, placed (this) for her dearest husband, (aged) fifty-five years; he is buried here.
EDCS-09900119.summary: This epitaph records Tappia Ammionis setting up a monument for her husband, specifying his age and burial.

EDCS-09900120.text_edition: \n EE quam optarem sit terra levis properavit aetas hoc voluit fatus meus sic hic sita est Iulia Dubitata annorum XX parentes filiae posuerunt quibus ea facere deberet sic ita fecit sic Gaius Iulius Romulus et Valeria Matero memoriam sibi et posteris\n
EDCS-09900120.translation: How much I wished the earth might be light upon you! Swift was the age; this was my fate's will. Thus here lies Iulia Dubitata, twenty years old. Her parents, to whom she ought to do (this), thus did it; thus Gaius Iulius Romulus and Valeria Matero (made) a memorial for themselves and for posterity.
EDCS-09900120.summary: This is a touching epitaph for Iulia Dubitata, who died at twenty, with her parents fulfilling their obligation to create a memorial.

EDCS-09900121.text_edition: \n Cascellia Exorata annorum XV hic sita est Titus K Florentinus annorum vivus fecit\n
EDCS-09900121.translation: Cascellia Exorata, fifteen years old, is buried here. Titus K. Florentinus made (it) alive.
EDCS-09900121.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Cascellia Exorata, aged fifteen, with the monument erected by Titus K. Florentinus.

EDCS-09900122.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, possibly part of a larger epitaph or dedication, mentioning the name Valerius.

EDCS-09900123.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Antonius Cogitatus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900123.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Antonius Cogitatus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900123.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Antonius Cogitatus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09900125.text_edition: \n Libero Patr[i] [[]] [[]] [[]] [[]] Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Mercator VIV votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900125.translation: To Liber Pater and Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Caius Iulius Mercator VIV gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900125.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater and Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Iulius Mercator VIV, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-09900127.text_edition: \n Aurelius Apollinaris duplarius legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis pro salute sua et conmanipulorum votum solvit libens merito Pio et Proculo consulibus\n
EDCS-09900127.translation: Aurelius Apollinaris, a soldier of rank duplarius in the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own safety and that of his tent-mates, during the consulship of Pius and Proculus.
EDCS-09900127.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier of the Second Adiutrix legion dedicated for the health of himself and his comrades, dated by consuls to the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900128.text_edition: \n IIILL Senecio votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900128.translation: Senecio willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900128.summary: A short votive inscription by an individual named Senecio, likely from the Roman army, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09900129.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely commemorating or addressing an individual named Tiberius Claudius.

EDCS-09900131.text_edition: \n sino Caius Iulius inianus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis a parentes filio posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900131.translation: Gaius Iulius Ianianus of the Second Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion and his parents dedicated this to their son.
EDCS-09900131.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Gaius Iulius Ianianus of the Second Adiutrix legion and his parents for their son.

EDCS-09900132.text_edition: \n Neptuno Augusto Tiberius Claudius Matinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900132.translation: Tiberius Claudius Matinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Neptune Augustus.
EDCS-09900132.summary: A votive inscription by Tiberius Claudius Matinus dedicated to Neptune Augustus.

EDCS-09900134.text_edition: \n Flavia Titi filia Flora annorum XXVI hic sita est Titus Flavius Maximus sorori optimae suae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900134.translation: Flavia Flora, daughter of Titus, aged 26, rests here. Titus Flavius Maximus arranged for this to be made for his excellent sister.
EDCS-09900134.summary: This tombstone records the death of Flavia Flora at age 26 and was commissioned by her brother, Titus Flavius Maximus.

EDCS-09900138.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Marcus Ulpius Gaddas sexvir coloniae Aquincensium et Ulpia Victorina pro salute sua et suorum VLP TGEN\n
EDCS-09900138.translation: Marcus Ulpius Gaddas, a member of the college of six officials of the colony of Aquincum, and Ulpia Victorina dedicated this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina for their own safety and that of their family.
EDCS-09900138.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and Juno by Marcus Ulpius Gaddas, a civic official in Aquincum, and Ulpia Victorina for their family's well-being.

EDCS-09900142.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Flavius Titi filius P \n
EDCS-09900142.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Flavius, son of Titus P.
EDCS-09900142.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Flavius, son of Titus.

EDCS-09900145.text_edition: \n Aurelius Rufinus miles cohortis |(miliariae) Himisenorum sic annorum XX consobrino et Aurelia Iezena aviae annorum LXXX Aurelius Proculinus pequarius sic legionis \n
EDCS-09900145.translation: Aurelius Proculinus, a soldier of the Himisenorum milliary cohort, aged 20, and Aurelia Iezena, his great-aunt, aged 80, also Aurelius Rufinus, a soldier aged 20, dedicated this.
EDCS-09900145.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the deaths of a young soldier, Aurelius Rufinus, and his elderly great-aunt, Aurelia Iezena, with a dedication possibly by another relative named Aurelius Proculinus.

EDCS-09900146.translation: Sextus Acurius Dexter dedicated this to Hercules.
EDCS-09900146.summary: A dedication to Hercules by Sextus Acurius Dexter.

EDCS-09900147.text_edition: \n Dexter annorum sibi et coniugi Iuliae Priscae annorum\n
EDCS-09900147.translation: Dexter, aged [for himself] and his wife Julia Prisca, aged [for her].
EDCS-09900147.summary: This inscription is a funerary text recording the ages of Dexter and his wife Julia Prisca, likely for a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-09900148.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Redita fecit sibi viva et coniugi pio annorum LX et Claudio Censorino et Claudiae Censorinae filiis vivis et Claudio Servando vivo nepoti legionis I<I> Parthicae et Claudio Claudiano annorum XVI Claudi- Servand? \n
EDCS-09900148.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Redita made this for herself while alive and for her pious husband, aged 60, and for her living children Claudius Censorinus and Claudia Censorina, and for her living grandson Claudius Servandus, a soldier of the IInd Parthica Legion, and for Claudius Claudianus, aged 16. Claudius Servandus?
EDCS-09900148.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelia Redita for herself, her husband, children, and grandson, indicating the family's respect for the deceased and the grandson's military service, likely dating to the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900150.translation: He placed [this] to Aesculapius and Hygieia.
EDCS-09900150.summary: This is a brief dedication to the gods Aesculapius and Hygieia, likely part of a larger religious monument.

EDCS-09900151.text_edition: \n Apollini et Hygiae Quintus Ulpius Felix Augustalis municipii Brigetionis porticum a portis II ad fontem Salutis a solo inpendiis suis fecit et ad epulas privilegio collegium centonariorum haberi iussit praefecto Iulio Sabino quinquennale Praesente et Extricato consulibus Nonis Novembribus\n
EDCS-09900151.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aelius Marcianus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, under the consulship of Lupus and Maximus.
EDCS-09900151.summary: This is a votive inscription by a veteran named Aelius Marcianus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dated by the consulship of Lupus and Maximus, likely from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900153.text_edition: \n Deae Balti Cocceia Marcia iussu deae\n
EDCS-09900153.translation: Cocceia Marcia by order of the goddess Balti.
EDCS-09900153.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Balti, dedicated by Cocceia Marcia under divine command.

EDCS-09900154.text_edition: \n Dis deabus omnibus Marcus Antonius Marcianus |(centurio) legionis votum solvit libens merito Barba<r=N>o et Regulo consulibus \n
EDCS-09900154.translation: To all the gods and goddesses, Marcus Antonius Marcianus, a centurion of the legion, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly, under the consulship of Barbarus and Regulus.
EDCS-09900154.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Antonius Marcianus, a centurion, fulfilling a vow to all gods and goddesses, dated by the consulship of Barbarus and Regulus, likely in the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900156.text_edition: \n Genium sic centuriae Felix Hilario et Silvinus |(centuriones) de suo posuit sic\n
EDCS-09900156.translation: To the Genius of the century, Felix Hilario and Silvinus, centurions, placed [this] from their own funds.
EDCS-09900156.summary: This is a dedication to the Genius of a century, made by two centurions, Felix Hilario and Silvinus, likely from the mid-1st to mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900157.text_edition: \n Genio legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis scholae ||(centurionum) ob honorem ddominorum nnostrorum Caius Silvinius Caius Lucius ex optione legionis II Augustae donum dedit\n
EDCS-09900157.translation: To the Genius of the IInd Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Legion, for the association of centurions, Caius Silvinius Caius Lucius, chosen by the legionaries, gave this gift in honor of our lords. (Also inscribed) from the legionary II Augusta.
EDCS-09900157.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Genius of the IInd Adjutrix Pia Fidelis Legion's schola centurionum by Caius Silvinius Caius Lucius, who served in the IInd Augusta Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-09900158.text_edition: \n Herculi Salutari et Fortunae Domesticae Attius Mac\n
EDCS-09900158.translation: To Hercules Salutaris and Fortuna Domestica, Attius Mac[...]
EDCS-09900158.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules Salutaris and Fortuna Domestica, begun by Attius Mac[...], likely a religious offering or dedication.

EDCS-09900161.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Marcianus veteranus votum libens merito Lupo et Maximo consulibus\n
EDCS-09900161.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Aelius Marcianus, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, under the consulship of Lupus and Maximus.
EDCS-09900161.summary: A votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a veteran, dated to the consulship of Lupus and Maximus.

EDCS-09900162.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Vindex miles legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis s f libens merito\n
EDCS-09900162.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aelius Vindex, soldier of the First Adjutrix Legion, pious and faithful, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900162.summary: A soldier of the I Adjutrix Legion dedicates this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900163.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aurelius Maximus VIIE \n
EDCS-09900163.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Aurelius Maximus, a veteran of seven campaigns.
EDCS-09900163.summary: Marcus Aurelius Maximus, a veteran, dedicates this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, possibly in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900164.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Valentinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900164.translation: Aurelius Valentinus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09900164.summary: Aurelius Valentinus dedicates this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900165.text_edition: \n Soli deo Mithrae Aurelius Vitalis dat votum merito\n
EDCS-09900165.translation: Aurelius Vitalis dedicates his vow deservedly to the Sun God Mithras.
EDCS-09900165.summary: Aurelius Vitalis dedicates this to Sol Invictus Mithras, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900166.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Cassius Marcellinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900166.translation: Cassius Marcellinus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09900166.summary: Cassius Marcellinus dedicates this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900167.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio scholae signiferorum Homonius Quintianus quod signifer vovit |(centurio) solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900167.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the Genius of the standard-bearers' collegium, Homonius Quintianus, who was a standard-bearer, paid a vow as a centurion, gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900167.summary: Homonius Quintianus, a standard-bearer and centurion, dedicates this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the standard-bearers' collegium, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900168.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Octavius Valens |(centurio) legionis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900168.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Octavius Valens, a centurion of the legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900168.summary: Gaius Octavius Valens, a centurion of a legion, dedicates this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900169.text_edition: \n Iovi sacrum fecit Ulpius Maxsimus sic pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900169.translation: Ulpius Maxsimus made a sacred offering to Jupiter; thus he gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
EDCS-09900169.summary: Ulpius Maxsimus makes a votive offering to Jupiter for himself and his family, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900170.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae et Genio exploratorum cohortis VII [Breucorum ------\n
EDCS-09900170.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to Juno Regina, and to the Genius of the scouts of the Seventh Cohort [of the Breuci -----
EDCS-09900170.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, and the Genius of the scouts of the Seventh Cohort of the Breuci, with a missing portion, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900171.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Viator votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900171.translation: Marcus Ulpius Viator gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-09900171.summary: Marcus Ulpius Viator dedicates this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900173.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sanctum Flavius Adiutor tesserarius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900173.translation: Flavius Adiutor, a tesserarius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-09900173.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Flavius Adiutor to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely a personal votive offering.

EDCS-09900177.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Domitius Titus decurio Seleucia Zeugmae pro salute sua et suorum omnium votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09900177.translation: Domitius Titus, a decurion from Seleucia Zeugmae, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus for the safety of himself and all his family.
EDCS-09900177.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Domitius Titus, a decurion, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus for the well-being of his family.

EDCS-09900178.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Caius Gallonius Crispianus |(centurio) legionis I adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900178.translation: Gaius Gallonius Crispianus, a centurion of the I Adiutrix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.
EDCS-09900178.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Gaius Gallonius Crispianus, a centurion of the I Adiutrix legion, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.

EDCS-09900179.text_edition: \n arus miles legionis I adiutricis R votum posuit\n
EDCS-09900179.translation: Arus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion, willingly set up a vow.
EDCS-09900179.summary: This inscription records a vow set up by Arus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion.

EDCS-09900180.text_edition: \n Matre sic Magnae Augustae nnius Candidus veteranus legionis I adiutricis et Aurelia Marcellina pro salute sua et <f=E>iliorum ex voto restituerunt votum solverunt libentes merito IA \n
EDCS-09900180.translation: From the Great Mother Augusta, the veteran Nnnius Candidus of the I Adiutrix legion and Aurelia Marcellina, for their health and that of their children, restored and fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900180.summary: This inscription records the restoration and fulfillment of a vow by Nnnius Candidus, a veteran, and Aurelia Marcellina to the Great Mother Augusta for their family's health.

EDCS-09900181.text_edition: \n Cautopati Iulius Karodius natione Gallus cum Iulio Iuliano filio votum ssolverunt\n
EDCS-09900181.translation: Julius Karodius, a Gaul by nationality, with his son Julius Iulianus, willingly fulfilled their vow to Cautopates.
EDCS-09900181.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Julius Karodius and his son Julius Iulianus to Cautopates.

EDCS-09900182.text_edition: \n Cauti Marcus Ulpius Castrensis veteranus legionis I adiutricis fecit et posuit\n
EDCS-09900182.translation: Marcus Ulpius Castrensis, a veteran of the I Adiutrix legion, made and set this up for Cautes.
EDCS-09900182.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Marcus Ulpius Castrensis, a veteran of the I Adiutrix legion, to Cautes.

EDCS-09900183.text_edition: \n Cautopati Marcus Ulpius Castrensis veteranus fecit et posuit Invicto Deo\n
EDCS-09900183.translation: Marcus Ulpius Castrensis, a veteran, made and set this up for Cautopates, the Unconquered God.
EDCS-09900183.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Marcus Ulpius Castrensis, a veteran, to Cautopates, the Unconquered God.

EDCS-09900184.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Gaius Valerius Marinianus cornicularius legati legionis I adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900184.translation: Gaius Valerius Marinianus, a cornicularius of the legate of the I Adiutrix legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Unconquered Sun Mithras.
EDCS-09900184.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Gaius Valerius Marinianus, a cornicularius, to the Unconquered Sun Mithras.

EDCS-09900185.translation: Nonius Bassinus fulfilled his vow deservedly.
EDCS-09900185.summary: This inscription records the deserved fulfillment of a vow by Nonius Bassinus.

EDCS-09900186.text_edition: \n Nundinae Augustae Titus Flavius Clemens veteranus legionis I adiutricis piae felicis ex iussu\n
EDCS-09900186.translation: To the Augustan market days. Titus Flavius Clemens, a veteran of the First Adiutrix Legion, the Pious and Fortunate, [erected this] by order.
EDCS-09900186.summary: This inscription records the establishment of the "Nundinae Augustae" by a veteran, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900187.text_edition: \n Deo Silvano Silvestri sacrum collegius sic opificerum sic votum solvit libens laetus merito culinam collegi s Rufus Augustalis patronus nus magister Aurelius For Aelius Gregorius Aelius Bassus Iulius Amandus Septimius Secundinus Aurelius Secundinus Rutilius Secundinus Aelius Aelianus Septimius Firmus Aelius Agilis Canius Sabinus Aurelius Felix immunis Septimius Secundus Flavius Salvianus quaestor Aurelius Primitivus Aelius Vitalis Car Tilia Flavius Felix arcarius Superatus decurio \n
EDCS-09900187.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvestris. The college of workers paid their vow willingly, happily, and deservedly. As patron, Rufus Augustalis. As magistrate, Aurelius For[---]. Aelius Gregorius, Aelius Bassus, Iulius Amandus, Septimius Secundinus, Aurelius Secundinus, Rutilius Secundinus, Aelius Aelianus, Septimius Firmus, Aelius Agilis, Canius Sabinus, Aurelius Felix (immunis), Septimius Secundus, Flavius Salvianus (quaestor), Aurelius Primitivus, Aelius Vitalis, Car[---] Tilia, Flavius Felix (arcarius), Superatus (decurio).
EDCS-09900187.summary: This lengthy inscription records a college of workers fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Silvestris, listing numerous members and officials, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900188.text_edition: \n Silvano Lucius Annius Secundinus miles votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900188.translation: To Silvanus. Lucius Annius Secundinus, a soldier, paid his vow willingly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-09900188.summary: This inscription records a soldier named Lucius Annius Secundinus dedicating a votive offering to the god Silvanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900189.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Flavius Victorinus custos armorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900189.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris. Flavius Victorinus, keeper of the arms, paid his vow willingly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-09900189.summary: This inscription records Flavius Victorinus, who was responsible for arms, dedicating a votive offering to Silvanus Silvestris, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900190.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Septimius Lucius miles legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae\n
EDCS-09900190.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticius. Septimius Lucius, a soldier of the First Adiutrix Legion, the Pious and Faithful Severiana.
EDCS-09900190.summary: This inscription records a soldier named Septimius Lucius dedicating something to Silvanus Domesticius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900191.text_edition: \n Deo Silvano Domestico Tacitus votum libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-09900191.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticius. Tacitus paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900191.summary: This inscription records a person named Tacitus dedicating a votive offering to Silvanus Domesticius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900192.text_edition: \n In honorem domus divinae Silvano Domestico Secundi[n--- ------\n
EDCS-09900192.translation: In honor of the divine house. To Silvanus Domesticius. Secundi[n--- ...
EDCS-09900192.summary: This inscription records a dedication in honor of the imperial family to Silvanus Domesticius, with the dedicator's name partially preserved, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900193.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicating something to Silvanus Domesticius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900194.text_edition: \n MDE Augusto sacrum -IIRT SEC LMIS et Proculus CL SEATI votum solverunt laeti libentes m(erito)\n
EDCS-09900194.translation: Sacred to Augustus. -IIRT SEC LMIS and Proculus CL SEATI paid their vows happily and willingly.
EDCS-09900194.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Augustus by two individuals, Proculus and another whose name is mostly lost, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900197.text_edition: \n Aurelius Secundianus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900197.translation: Aurelius Secundianus paid his vow willingly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-09900197.summary: This inscription is a votive offering by Aurelius Secundianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900198.text_edition: \n Septimius Vibianus duplarius legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09900198.translation: Septimius Vibianus, a duplarius in the I Adiutrix legion, loyal and dutifully devoted to Antoninus, fulfilled his vow willingly, joyfully, and deservedly.
EDCS-09900198.summary: This inscription records a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Antonine period.

EDCS-09900199.translation: Valens, the chief curator, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900199.summary: This inscription records Valens, a chief curator, fulfilling a vow, possibly related to public office or a religious duty.

EDCS-09900200.text_edition: \n GIIO Aur(elius) Victorinus eques legionis I adutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900200.translation: GIIO Aurelius Victorinus, an equestrian in the I Adiutrix legion, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900200.summary: This inscription records an equestrian officer named Aurelius Victorinus of the I Adiutrix legion fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-09900206.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Mercatori quondam immuni caeriarensi sic legionis I adiutricis qui vixit annos XXXIIII stipendiorum XVIII Septimia Firmilla conugi karissimo et Publius Aelius Mercatianus et Mercatilla patri faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09900206.translation: Septimia Firmilla to her most beloved husband, Publius Aelius Mercator, formerly an immune soldier from Caere and a sicarius of the I Adiutrix legion, who lived 34 years and served 18 periods of service; Publius Aelius Mercatianus and Mercatilla arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900206.summary: This funerary inscription honors Publius Aelius Mercator, a former soldier and sicarius of the I Adiutrix legion, by his wife and children.

EDCS-09900207.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus pientissimae parentibus suis Amiciae Digniolae Lucius Masuinnius Amicus filiae karissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900207.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for his most devoted parents, Amicia Digniola, and for his dearest daughter, Lucius Masuinnius Amicus arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900207.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Masuinnius Amicus for his parents and his daughter.

EDCS-09900208.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ammonio Valentiniano duplario militi legionis I adiutricis qui vixit nnos XXXVII stupendiorum sic XX Valeria Valerina coniugi karissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900208.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Ammonius Valentinianus, a duplarius soldier in the I Adiutrix legion, who lived 37 years and served 20 periods of service; Valeria Valerina arranged for this to be made for her most beloved husband.
EDCS-09900208.summary: This funerary inscription honors Ammonius Valentinianus, a duplarius soldier of the I Adiutrix legion, by his wife.

EDCS-09900209.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus memoriae Titi Atoni Fortunati Augustalis municipii Brigetionis qui vixit annos XXXXV Iulia Filete coniugi pientissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900209.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for the memory of Titus Antonius Fortunatus, Augustalis of the municipality of Brigetio, who lived 45 years; Iulia Filete arranged for this to be made for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-09900209.summary: This funerary inscription honors Titus Antonius Fortunatus, an Augustalis from Brigetio, by his wife.

EDCS-09900210.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Antiochiae quae vixit annum Marcus Aurelius Antiochianus decurio municipii Brigetionis et Iunia Lucilla parentes filiae dulcissimae faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09900210.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Aurelia Antiochia, who lived one year; Marcus Aurelius Antiochianus, a decurio of the municipality of Brigetio, and Iunia Lucilla, parents, arranged for this to be made for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-09900210.summary: This funerary inscription honors Aurelia Antiochia, an infant, by her parents who were members of the municipality of Brigetio.

EDCS-09900211.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Antoninus cives sic Surus ex regione Dolicu sic qui vixit nnos XXX Aurelius Vibinus posuit\n
EDCS-09900211.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Aurelius Antoninus, a citizen from Syria from the region of Doliche, who lived 30 years; Aurelius Vibinus placed this.
EDCS-09900211.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Aurelius Antoninus, a Syrian citizen from the Doliche region, by Aurelius Vibinus.

EDCS-09900212.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Memoriae Valeri Corifi sic Valerius Valerius frater sic Aurelio Basso Abbosatis filio qui vixit nnos XXVII Aurelius Iulianus proximus eius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900212.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for the memory of Valerius Corifus; Valerius Valerius, his brother, arranged for this to be made for Aurelius Bassus, son of Abbosatis, who lived 27 years; Aurelius Iulianus, his kinsman, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900212.summary: This funerary inscription honors Aurelius Bassus by his brother and kinsman.

EDCS-09900213.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Cogitato qui vixit annis XXV Secundinus miles legionis I adiutricis et <C=G>ogitata parentes filio pientissimo faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-09900213.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Cogitatus, who lived 25 years. Secundinus, a soldier of the First Adjutant Legion, and Cogitata, his parents, had this made for their most devoted son.
EDCS-09900213.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Cogitatus, dedicated by his parents, one of whom was a soldier in the First Adjutant Legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900214.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marci Aurelli sic Crhysi sic pueri innocentissimi adque sic iucundissimi qui vixit nnos plus minus XVIII\n
EDCS-09900214.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Aurelius Crhysus, a boy, most innocent and most delightful, who lived 18 years, more or less.
EDCS-09900214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a boy named Marcus Aurelius Crhysus, recording his age and praising his innocence and pleasant nature, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900215.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Polideuco decurioni municipii Bregetionis! ex regione Dulca vico Calan qui vixit annos XXXXV Marcus Aurelius ORIT \n
EDCS-09900215.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Aurelius Polideucus, decurion of the municipium of Bregetio, from the district of Dulca, village of Calan, who lived 45 years. Marcus Aurelius ORIT [made this].
EDCS-09900215.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Polideucus, a decurion from Bregetio, recording his age and place of origin, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900216.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Vero Forensi qui vixit annis XXXIII Aurelia Verina fratri karissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900216.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Marcus Aurelius Verus Forensis, who lived 33 years. Aurelia Verina had this made for her dearest brother.
EDCS-09900216.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Verus Forensis, who lived 33 years, dedicated by his sister Aurelia Verina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900217.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucia Caecilia Bathanae vixit annis LXXX Lucius Caecilius Viator et Lucius Caecilius Saturninus fili sarcofagum matri pientissimae fecerunt\n
EDCS-09900217.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucia Caecilia Bathana, who lived 80 years. Lucius Caecilius Viator and Lucius Caecilius Saturninus, her sons, made the sarcophagus for their most devoted mother.
EDCS-09900217.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucia Caecilia Bathana, who lived 80 years, commissioned by her sons for a sarcophagus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900218.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus memoriae Titia Claudia Aemerita sic quae vixit annos V menses III dies VIIII Titus Claudius Severianus pater filiae pientissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900218.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To the memory of Titia Claudia Aemerita, who lived 5 years, 3 months, and 9 days. Titus Claudius Severianus, her father, had this made for his most devoted daughter.
EDCS-09900218.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titia Claudia Aemerita, recording her age at death (5 years, 3 months, 9 days), dedicated by her father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900219.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudium Verecundum annorum III et Aurelium Celsinianum qui et Placidium annorum VIII Aurelius Celsinianus |(centurio) legionis I adiutricis et Ulpia Atticilla parentes infelicissimi Arvi reliquias originis suae sitos commendaverunt\n
EDCS-09900219.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Claudius Verecundus, aged 3 years, and Aurelius Celsinianus, who was also called Placidius, aged 8 years. Aurelius Celsinianus, a centurion of the First Adjutant Legion, and Ulpia Atticilla, their most unfortunate parents, commended the remains of their origin here.
EDCS-09900219.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two young boys, Claudius Verecundus and Aurelius Celsinianus (Placidius), dedicated by their parents, one of whom was a centurion in the First Adjutant Legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900220.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Cledulius Voltinia Vitalis Nemauso miles legionis I adiutricis annorum XLIX ex testamento Iulius Valens heres amico pientissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900220.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Titus Cledulius, of the Voltinian tribe, from Nemausus, a soldier of the First Adjutant Legion, aged 49 years. Julius Valens, the heir, had this made for his most devoted friend, according to the will.
EDCS-09900220.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Cledulius, a soldier of the First Adjutant Legion aged 49, commissioned by his heir Julius Valens according to his will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900221.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavio Abbati nnorum XI et Afranio Faustino Flavius Antiochanus\n
EDCS-09900221.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Flavius Abba, aged 11 years, and Afranius Faustinus. Flavius Anthiochanus [dedicated this].
EDCS-09900221.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two boys, Flavius Abba (aged 11) and Afranius Faustinus, dedicated by Flavius Anthiochanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900222.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Iulianus quondam |(centurio) legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis nnorum LXVIII Titus Flavius Iulianus et Flavia Iulia filii patri carissimo adque pientissimo optamus sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09900222.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Titus Flavius Julianus, formerly a centurion of the First Adjutant Legion, pious and loyal, aged 68 years. Titus Flavius Julianus and Flavia Julia, his sons, wish you, dearest and most devoted father, a light earth (may the earth lie lightly on you).
EDCS-09900222.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Julianus, a former centurion of the First Adjutant Legion, who lived 68 years, dedicated by his children with a wish for a light earth, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900223.text_edition: \n Flavio Iul adiutricis et Baentinae N annorum XLV Flavintino CLI et Iuvenientissim? \n
EDCS-09900223.translation: To Flavius, of the adiutricis and Baentina cohort, aged 45 years. Flavintinus and Iuvenientissim? [erected this].
EDCS-09900223.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Flavius, a soldier, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE, placed by his relatives.

EDCS-09900224.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Titiano qui et Eusebius nnum VIII Titus Flavius Maturus beneficiarius legati et Aviania Castilla parentes filio rarissimo\n
EDCS-09900224.translation: To Titus Flavius Titianus, also known as Eusebius, aged 8 years. Titus Flavius Maturus, a beneficiary of the legate, and Aviania Castilla, his parents, [dedicated this] to their most precious son.
EDCS-09900224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Titus Flavius Titianus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE, dedicated by his parents.

EDCS-09900225.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Candidiano qui vixit nnos XVIII menses VII omnibus studiis liberalibus erudito Iulius Crescens filio karissimimo et Caecilia et Marcellina faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09900225.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Gaius Julius Candidianus, who lived 18 years and 7 months, educated in all liberal studies. Julius Crescens, his most cherished son, and Caecilia and Marcellina, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900225.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Candidianus, a young man educated in liberal arts, likely from the 2nd century CE, commissioned by his family.

EDCS-09900226.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Iuli Respecti Savaria equitis legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis stipendiorum XX hic situs est Caius Victorinius Messor heres\n
EDCS-09900226.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Gaius Julius Respectus, an equestrian soldier of the I Adiutrix Pia Fidelis legion, with 20 years of service, he is buried here. Gaius Victorinius Messor, his heir, [arranged this].
EDCS-09900226.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Julius Respectus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, commissioned by his heir.

EDCS-09900227.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Ursulae Marci Dignii Ursulus Iustus et Valentinus matri pientissimae ex testamento fieri praecepit sic per Iulium Avitum libertum\n
EDCS-09900227.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Julia Ursula. Ursulus Justus and Valentinus ordered this to be made for their most devoted mother, from her will, through her freedman Julius Avitus.
EDCS-09900227.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Ursula, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, commissioned by her sons according to her will.

EDCS-09900228.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et memoriae Luci Art Apollinaris Art Pallas pater et Aninius Servandus et Surus Felicis dulcissimis\n
EDCS-09900228.translation: To the Divine Shades and the memory of Lucius Art Apollinaris. Art Pallas, his father, and Aninius Servandus and Surus Felicis, [dedicated this] to their most beloved.
EDCS-09900228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Art Apollinaris, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, dedicated by his father and friends.

EDCS-09900229.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Lucianus duumvir quinquennalis Brigetionis qui vixit annos LIII Dumpia Camilla coniux carissima faciendum quravit sic\n
EDCS-09900229.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Marcus Lucianus, duumvir quinquennalis of Brigetio, who lived 53 years. Dumpia Camilla, his dearest wife, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900229.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Lucianus, a civic official of Brigetio, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, commissioned by his wife.

EDCS-09900231.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Parthenopae alumno sic nnorum XV Flavius Secundus praefectus\n
EDCS-09900231.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Parthenopa, his pupil, aged 15 years. Flavius Secundus, the prefect, [dedicated this].
EDCS-09900231.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Parthenopa, a student, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, dedicated by his teacher who was a prefect.

EDCS-09900232.text_edition: \n Petroniae Severinae innocentissimae puellae quae vixit nnos XIII Caius Petronius Nundinus Augustalis municipii Brigetionensium pater filiae dulcissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900232.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Petronia Severina, a most innocent girl, who lived 13 years. Gaius Petronius Nundinus Augustalis of the municipality of Brigetionenses, father, arranged for this to be made for his most beloved daughter.
EDCS-09900232.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Petronia Severina, a young girl, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, commissioned by her father.

EDCS-09900233.text_edition: \n Matronae innocentissimae Aeliae Valerianae coniugi Primitivus actor octavarum coniugi carissimae\n
EDCS-09900233.translation: To the most innocent Matrona. To Aelia Valeriana, his wife. Primitivus, the tax collector, to his most beloved wife.
EDCS-09900233.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelia Valeriana, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, commissioned by her husband.

EDCS-09900234.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Samro veterano et Claudi- In coniugi \n
EDCS-09900234.translation: To the spirits of Lucius Samrus, a veteran, and Claudia, his wife.
EDCS-09900234.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a veteran and his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900235.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Senecionio Luciano veterano legionis I adiutricis annorum L Septimius Valerianus candidatus legionis supra scriptae heres et adfinis bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-09900235.translation: To the spirits of Publius Senecionius Lucianus, a veteran of the I Adjutrix legion, aged 50. Septimius Valerianus, a candidate of the aforementioned legion, his heir and relation, had this made for him as he deserved well.
EDCS-09900235.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a veteran of the I Adjutrix legion and was erected by his heir and relation, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900236.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et memoriae Luci Sepltimi Quinti Cup<i=L>tiani militis legionis I adiutricis et Petroniae<q=O>(ue Vaiaitae parentibus et Petroniaeque Annae aviae Septimius Petronius custos armorum parentibus dignissimis faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-09900236.translation: To the spirits and memory of Lucius Septimius Quintus Cupitianus, a soldier of the I Adjutrix legion, and Petronia Quinta Vaiaita, his parents, and Petronia Anna, his grandmother. Septimius Petronius, keeper of arms, had this made for his most deserving parents.
EDCS-09900236.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates a soldier of the I Adjutrix legion and his parents and grandmother, with a custos armorum as the dedicator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900237.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimius Messor veteranus Ae<l=I>(iae) Magnillae coniugi annorum L Sepptimiis Messorino et Primo filiis bene merentibus \n
EDCS-09900237.translation: To the spirits of Septimius Messor, a veteran, and Aelia Magnilla, his wife, aged 50. Septimius Messorinus and Primus, his sons, had this made for their well-deserving parents.
EDCS-09900237.summary: This funerary inscription honors a veteran and his wife, erected by their sons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900238.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimiae Victorinae quondam quae vixit nnos --- Sept(imius) Victor |(centurio) legionis I adiutricis coniugi karissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900238.translation: To the spirits of Septimia Victorina, who lived --- years. Septimius Victor, centurion of the I Adjutrix legion, had this made for his dearest wife.
EDCS-09900238.summary: This funerary inscription is for Septimia Victorina, erected by her husband, a centurion in the I Adjutrix legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900239.text_edition: \n Marco Ulpio quondam Romano militi praetoriano et <p=R>rimoscrinio praeffectorum qui vixit annos XXXV Marcus Ulpius Celerinus salariarius legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis interprex sic Dacorum vivus sibi et filio suo supra scripto carissimo faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-09900239.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Romanus, a praetorian soldier and primuscrinius of the prefects, who lived 35 years. Marcus Ulpius Celerinus, a salary-earner of the I Adjutrix legion, interpreter of the Dacians, pious and faithful, had this made for himself and his most beloved son, mentioned above, during his lifetime.
EDCS-09900239.summary: This funerary inscription records Marcus Ulpius Romanus, a praetorian soldier and interpreter, and was commissioned by his son, Marcus Ulpius Celerinus, also associated with the I Adjutrix legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900240.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus CAI Canio annorum X hic situs AITA RPE Marcus Ulpius SPESI heredes bene merenti et Iuliae bene merenti V\n
EDCS-09900240.translation: To the spirits of Caius Canius, aged 10. Marcus Ulpius [---] and Iulia [---], his heirs, had this made for the well-deserving Caius, and also for the well-deserving Iulia.
EDCS-09900240.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Caius Canius, a child, and Iulia, erected by their heirs, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900242.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeriae Lucillae quondam Quae vixit nnos XL Aurelius Martialis nauclerus portus Pontis Aeni coniugi carissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900242.translation: To the spirits of Valeria Lucilla, who lived 40 years. Aurelius Martialis, shipmaster of the port of Pons Aenus, had this made for his dearest wife.
EDCS-09900242.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Valeria Lucilla, erected by her husband, a shipmaster at Pons Aenus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900243.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Valerio Luci filio Palatina Ru<f=E>o veterano legionis I decurio Brigetionensium quaestori et magistro nnorum LXX et Anmae coniugi eiius Lucii Valerii Lu et anus heredesIT faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-09900243.translation: To the spirits of Lucius Valerius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Palatine tribe, veteran of the I Adjutrix legion, decurion of the Brigetionenses, quaestor, and master for 70 years, and to Annia, his wife. Lucius Valerius and [his] heirs had this made.
EDCS-09900243.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Valerius Rufus, a veteran of the I Adjutrix legion with civic offices, and his wife Annia, commissioned by his son and heirs, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900244.text_edition: \n e Titius Quintus et Valeria Servata parentes filiae faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-09900244.translation: Titius Quintus and Valeria Servata, parents, had this made for their daughter.
EDCS-09900244.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording parents commissioning a monument for their deceased daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.
