EDCS-09900245.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeriae Valentinae quae vixit nnos XXVII Ulpia Sabina mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900245.translation: To the divine spirits. Ulpia Sabina, the mother, arranged for this to be made for Valeria Valentina, who lived 27 years.
EDCS-09900245.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Valentina, erected by her mother Ulpia Sabina.

EDCS-09900246.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Valerio Serotino decurioni municipii Brigetionis nnorum IX Ulpia Sabina coniux faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900246.translation: To the divine spirits. Ulpia Sabina, the wife, arranged for this to be made for Gaius Valerius Serotinus, a decurion of the municipality of Brigetio, aged 9 years.
EDCS-09900246.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Valerius Serotinus, a child decurion, erected by his wife Ulpia Sabina.

EDCS-09900247.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Venusiae Proculinae qui sic vixit annos L quod Valerius Veratius veteranus ex beneficiario consularis sibi et coniugi vivus prosuit qui obitus est annorum LX Aurelia Emerita heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900247.translation: To the divine spirits. Valerius Veratius, a veteran and former beneficiary of a consular official, who lived 50 years, arranged for this to be made for himself and his wife during his lifetime, and for Aurelia Emerita, his heir, who died at the age of 60.
EDCS-09900247.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Veratius and his wife, with the expense arranged by his heir Aurelia Emerita.

EDCS-09900252.translation: The legate of the emperor measured the weight.
EDCS-09900252.summary: A very brief inscription recording a measurement by an imperial legate.

EDCS-09900253.text_edition: \n pro salute Auggustorum Marcus Ulpius Marcus veteranus IO \n
EDCS-09900253.translation: For the safety of the emperors, Marcus Ulpius Marcus, a veteran.
EDCS-09900253.summary: An inscription dedicated for the safety of the emperors by a veteran named Marcus Ulpius Marcus.

EDCS-09900254.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et perpetuae securitati Queti Petri qui egit actum Antoni Agrippini primi pili qui vixit annos XXII menses X dies XII horas VI Quetius Arrianus pater filio pientissimo faciendum curavit et Antonio Gelasio canabario nepoti eius\n
EDCS-09900254.translation: To the divine spirits and for eternal security. Quetius Arrianus arranged for this to be made for his most devoted son, Queti Petrus, who lived 22 years, 10 months, 12 days, and 6 hours, and for his grandson Antonio Gelasio, a Canaarian.
EDCS-09900254.summary: A funerary epitaph for Queti Petrus and his grandson Antonio Gelasio, erected by Quetius Arrianus, the father and grandfather respectively.

EDCS-09900256.text_edition: \n Forensium loca pedes XV Dionisya sic vidua\n
EDCS-09900256.translation: 15 feet of space for the Forenses, by Dionsya, his widow.
EDCS-09900256.summary: A brief record concerning land allocation or space designated for the 'Forenses', arranged by Dionysis, a widow.

EDCS-09900258.summary: A dedication for Revocata, daughter of Quintilla.

EDCS-09900259.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Impperatorum Caessarum Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti et Marci Aureli Antonini et Publi Septimi Getae Caesaris Severi Augusti totiusque domus divinae aram <v=A>e<t=I>ustate conlapsam Aemilius Pacatianus praefectus cohortis I Thracum a solo restituit Nonio Muciano et Annio Fabiano consulibus Victoria Auggustorum \n
EDCS-09900259.translation: Aemilius Pacatianus, prefect of the I Cohort of Thracians, restored an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the safety of the emperors Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar Severus Augustus, and of the entire divine family, which had collapsed, from its foundations; in the consulship of Nonius Mucianus and Annius Fabianus. Victory of the Augusti.
EDCS-09900259.summary: An official record of the restoration of a Jupiter altar by Aemilius Pacatianus, prefect of a Thracian cohort, during the consulship of Nonius Mucianus and Annius Fabianus.

EDCS-09900267.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio huiusce loci pro salute dddominorum nnnostrorum Marcus Aelius Honoratus |(centurio) legionis I adiutricis regionarius sub cura Fulvi Maximi consularis votum solvit libens merito Idibus Octobribus Faustino et Rufino consulibus\n
EDCS-09900267.translation: Marcus Aelius Honoratus, a centurion of the I Adiutrix legion and a regional official, fulfilled his vow freely and deservedly to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of this place for the safety of our lords, the Emperors, on the Ides of October, in the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus. Victory of the Augusti.
EDCS-09900267.summary: A votive inscription by Marcus Aelius Honoratus, a centurion and regional official, dedicated to Jupiter and the Genius of the place during the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus.

EDCS-09900268.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae sacrum Marcus Ulpius Silvester veteranus legionis I adiutricis ex optione spe\n
EDCS-09900268.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina, from Marcus Ulpius Silvester, a veteran of the First Legion Adjutrix, appointed by the optio.
EDCS-09900268.summary: This is a religious dedication to Jupiter and Juno by a veteran of the legion I Adjutrix, likely dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE when the legion was active.

EDCS-09900271.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Victor votum libens laetus\n
EDCS-09900271.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Victor, a vow willingly and gladly made.
EDCS-09900271.summary: A personal religious dedication to Jupiter Victor, likely from an individual fulfilling a vow, with no specific date inferable.

EDCS-09900274.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae et Genio loci Marcus Cornelius Secundianus miles legionis I adiutricis Antoninianae clavicularius votum solvit libens laetus merito Sabino II et Anullino consulibus\n
EDCS-09900274.translation: To Nemesis Augusta and the Genius of the place, Marcus Cornelius Secundianus, a soldier of the First Legion Adjutrix Antoniniana, a clavicularius, fulfilled his vow willingly, gladly, and deservedly, in the consulship of Sabinus II and Anullinus.
EDCS-09900274.summary: This inscription records a soldier of the legion I Adjutrix Antoniniana fulfilling a vow to Nemesis and the Genius loci, dated to 206 CE.

EDCS-09900275.text_edition: \n legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis Marcianus servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900275.translation: Marcianus, a slave of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900275.summary: This is a religious dedication by a slave of the legion XIV Gemina Martia Victrix, likely dating to the period when the legion was active in the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-09900276.text_edition: \n Iuliae Aquiliae Sever[ae] sanctissimae Augustae matri castrorum senatus ac patriae ordo municipii Brigetionis\n
EDCS-09900276.translation: To Julia Aquilia Severa, most holy Augusta, mother of the camp, the senate, and the fatherland, from the ordo of the municipality of Brigetio.
EDCS-09900276.summary: This is an honorific inscription dedicated by the municipal council of Brigetio to Julia Aquilia Severa, wife of Emperor Severus Alexander, likely dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900277.text_edition: \n Albiunus tini filius decurio alae annorum occisus in Dacia etnus Piaeionis filius annorum XX hic siti sunt Toria Licconis filia mater sibi et filiis suis viva posuit\n
EDCS-09900277.translation: Albinus, son of Tinus, a decurion of the ala, aged [missing], and Aethnus, son of Piaeio, aged 20, were killed and buried here. Toria, daughter of Licco, mother, placed this for herself and her sons while alive.
EDCS-09900277.summary: This is a funerary monument commemorating two men, Albinus and Aethnus, who died in Dacia, set up by Albinus' mother, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900278.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Marinio quondam militi legionis primae adiutricis PVIARO vixit annos Basso Aeli-- Maxim ANIIVna mater pientissima faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900278.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Aelius Marinius, formerly a soldier of the First Legion Adjutrix, who lived [missing] years. His most dutiful mother, Basso Aeli-- Maxim ANIIVna, had this made.
EDCS-09900278.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aelius Marinius, a former soldier of the legion I Adjutrix, commissioned by his mother, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900280.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimio Diomedi immuni capsario legionis I adiutricis et Flaviae Asclepiodotae coniugi \n
EDCS-09900280.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Septimius Diomedes, an immunes capsarius of the First Legion Adjutrix, and for his wife Flavia Asclepiodota.
EDCS-09900280.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Septimius Diomedes, an immunes capsarius of the legion I Adjutrix, and his wife, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900281.text_edition: \n Septimius Secundianus nnorum <L=E> et Septimiae Munnae filiae Septimii Hilario Qauartus et Primitivus liberti ex suo faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-09900281.translation: Septimius Secundianus, aged 50, and his daughter Septimia Munna. Quartus and Primitivus, freedmen of Septimius Hilario, had this made at their own expense.
EDCS-09900281.summary: This is a funerary monument for Septimius Secundianus and his daughter Septimia Munna, commissioned by freedmen, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900285.summary: This is a religious dedication to Liber Pater by Aurelius Iulianus, with no specific date inferable.

EDCS-09900287.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus memoriae Aureliae Valeriae quae vixit nnos XX Aurelius Valerianus veteranus ex decurione alae decurio municipii Mogontianae et Aelia Iusta parentes filiae dulcissimae fecerunt adiuvante Domitiano optione spei fratre\n
EDCS-09900287.translation: To the Divine Spirits, in memory of Aurelia Valeria, who lived 20 years. Aurelius Valerianus, a veteran and former decurion of the cavalry wing, and decurion of the municipality of Mogontiana, and Aelia Iusta, her parents, made this for their dearest daughter, with the help of Domitianus, optio and heir, her brother.
EDCS-09900287.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Valeria, set up by her parents, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900288.text_edition: \n Nymphis sacrum Lucius Aurelius Gallus legatus Augusti\n
EDCS-09900288.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs, by Lucius Aurelius Gallus, legate of the Emperor.
EDCS-09900288.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs by a high-ranking imperial official, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900290.text_edition: \n Caelius Saconis filius annorum XXX hic situs est milis sic alae |(miliariae) Flaviae Domitianae civium Romanae sic Britannicae pro testamento pater Saco vivus posuit\n
EDCS-09900290.translation: Caelius, son of Saco, aged 30, is buried here; he was a soldier of the Flavian Domitian cavalry wing, of Roman and British citizenship. His father, Saco, set up this tombstone for him during his lifetime, as he had wished.
EDCS-09900290.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caelius, a soldier, set up by his father, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900291.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Basso ex regione Seugma vico Odia curante Aurelio Marinus sic filius sic faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900291.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Aurelius Bassus, from the district of Seugma, village of Odia. Aurelius Marinus, his son, saw to it that this was done.
EDCS-09900291.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Bassus, arranged by his son, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900292.text_edition: \n Trasamus Dasentis fili- annorum LX hic sit- est patri Simirasius posuit titulum VS\n
EDCS-09900292.translation: Trasamus, son of Dasentis, aged 60, is buried here. His father, Simirasius, set up this tombstone, fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-09900292.summary: A funerary epitaph for Trasamus, set up by his father, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900293.text_edition: \n Varzia Liccai filia annorum LV hic sita est posuerunt titulum filii et filias sic\n
EDCS-09900293.translation: Varzia, daughter of Liccai, aged 55, is buried here. Her sons and daughters set up this tombstone.
EDCS-09900293.summary: A funerary epitaph for Varzia, set up by her children, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900294.text_edition: \n ICVABATMSS IN sita est posuerunt filii I \n
EDCS-09900294.translation: [...] is buried here. Her sons set up [the tombstone].
EDCS-09900294.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph, recording that sons set up a tombstone for a woman, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900295.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Admonia Caraconis nnorum XXVI coniugi eius Abascantus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09900295.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Admonia, wife of Caracon, aged 26. Abascantus set this up for his deserving wife.
EDCS-09900295.summary: A funerary epitaph for Admonia, set up by her husband, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900296.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Dasses defunctus annorum LXXXI Aelia Marina coniu<gi=SS> titulum posuit heres posuit \n
EDCS-09900296.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Publius Aelius Dasses, deceased, aged 81. Aelia Marina, his wife, set up the tombstone; the heir set it up.
EDCS-09900296.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publius Aelius Dasses, set up by his wife, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900297.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Iustino librario legati legionis I adiutricis stipendiorum V qui vixit nnos XXV decedit sic expeditione Dacisca sic heredes Aelii Claudiani posuerunt\n
EDCS-09900297.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Aelius Iustinus, scribe of the legate of the First Adjutant Legion, who served 5 years, lived 25 years, and died on a Dacian campaign. The heirs of Aelius Claudianus set this up.
EDCS-09900297.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelius Iustinus, a legionary scribe who died on campaign, set up by his heirs, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900298.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Victorino duplario legionis I adiutricis Antoninianae stipendiorum XXI vixit annos et Pacatae coniugi pientissimae et Aeliis Pacato et Domitiano et Victorianae et Victoriae filiis pientissimis Aurelius Genialis duplarius legionis suprascriptae s(upra) s(cripae) secundus heres ex testamento faciundum curavit\n
EDCS-09900298.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Genialis, a second duplicarius of the aforementioned legion, as second heir by the will, arranged for this to be made for Publius Aelius Victorinus, a duplicarius of the I Adiutrix Antoniniana legion, with 21 years of service, who lived [xx] years, and for his most devoted wife Pacata, and for his most devoted children Pacatus, Domitianus, Victoriana, and Victoria.
EDCS-09900298.summary: This funerary inscription honors a duplicarius of the I Adiutrix legion and his family, arranged by his heir, dating likely to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900299.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Avitianus milis sic legionis I adiutricis militavit nnis XI vixit annis XXXI defunctus in Parthia et Flavia Gemella mater aeius sic Marcus Aurelius Genialis secundus haeres sic faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900299.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Aurelius Avitianus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion, served 11 years and lived 31 years, dying in Parthia. His mother Flavia Gemella and Marcus Aurelius Genialis, as second heir, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900299.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Aurelius Avitianus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion who died in Parthia, with his mother and heir as dedicants, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900300.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Firmanus miles cohortis I Ulpiae Pannuniorum sic et Vic<t=I>orinus nepos eius Aurelia Surilli sic soror Firmani fuciendum sic curavit \n
EDCS-09900300.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelius Firmanus, a soldier of the I Ulpia Pannoniorum cohort, and his nephew Victorinus, and his sister Aurelia Surilli arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09900300.summary: This funerary inscription honors Aurelius Firmanus, a soldier of the I Ulpia Pannoniorum cohort, with his nephew and sister as dedicants, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900301.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Lucius Titius Aurelius Marci filius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900301.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno. Lucius Titius Aurelius, son of Marcius, willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-09900301.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a promise to Jupiter and Juno by Lucius Titius Aurelius, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900302.translation: To the Nymphs. Ulpius Pompeianus, father of his country.
EDCS-09900302.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Ulpius Pompeianus, an official, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900303.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tacitus libens de suo merito titulum votum\n
EDCS-09900303.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Tacitus willingly offered this dedication and vow from his own resources as deserved.
EDCS-09900303.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a monument and a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Tacitus, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900304.text_edition: \n Silvano Camurius Pusinnio votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900304.translation: To Silvanus. Camurius Pusinnio willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-09900304.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a promise to Silvanus by Camurius Pusinnio, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900305.text_edition: \n anc filius annorum XX hic situs est P SO\n
EDCS-09900305.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of a son at the age of 20, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900306.text_edition: \n Aturo TEITIEI filius annorum LX Ao Aturoni filius XX Ao et Altu sibi et \n
EDCS-09900306.translation: Aturo, son of Teiteius, aged 60. Ao Aturo, son [of ?], aged 20. And Altu for himself.
EDCS-09900306.summary: This inscription appears to be a funerary monument recording the ages and relationships of several individuals, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900307.text_edition: \n IunI LXX titulum posuit Sabicius de suo\n
EDCS-09900307.translation: Sabicius placed this monument from his own resources for Juno, aged 70.
EDCS-09900307.summary: This is a funerary or votive inscription mentioning Juno and Sabicius, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900308.text_edition: \n Madena Lasci filia annorum LX hic sita est filius posuit titulum memoriae\n
EDCS-09900308.translation: Madena, daughter of Lascius, aged 60, lies here. Her son erected this tombstone in memory.
EDCS-09900308.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Madena, erected by her son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900310.text_edition: \n Nigrina Caleti filia annorum VI At CM \n
EDCS-09900310.translation: Nigrina, daughter of Caletus, aged 6 years.
EDCS-09900310.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Nigrina, a young girl, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900318.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae Serratius Regulianus optio et Furius Lucilianus et Aurelius Cogitatus et ceteri conlatores votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-09900318.translation: To the August Gods, Serratius Regulianus, optio, and Furius Lucilianus, and Aurelius Cogitatus, and the other contributors, fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900318.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortunae Augustae by several individuals, including an optio, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900319.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Serapidi pro salute et victoria Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti \n
EDCS-09900319.translation: To the Unconquered God Serapis, for the safety and victory of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-09900319.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Deus Invictus Serapis for the well-being and victory of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely dating to his reign (161-180 AD).

EDCS-09900320.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900321.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Valentina annorum XLI Valentinus filius\n
EDCS-09900321.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelia Valentina, aged 41. Her son Valentinus [erected this].
EDCS-09900321.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Valentina, erected by her son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900322.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Claudius Fortis veteranus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900322.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Claudius Fortis, a veteran, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900322.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a veteran named Claudius Fortis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900323.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dulicheno sic pro salute Imperatoris Severi Alexandri Augusti Romanus \n
EDCS-09900323.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Dulichenus. Thus, for the safety of the Emperor Severus Alexander Augustus. Romanus [dedicated this].
EDCS-09900323.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dulichenus by Romanus for the safety of Emperor Severus Alexander, likely dating to his reign (222-235 AD).

EDCS-09900324.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et perpetuae securitati Aeliae Vitalinae coniugi inconparabili quae vixit annos XXXIII menses V dies XVIIII Suriacus Secuindinus decurio municipii Mogetianae quattuorvirus sic maritus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900324.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed and eternal security. To Aelia Vitalina, his incomparable wife, who lived 33 years, 5 months, and 19 days. Suriacus Secuindinus, decurion and magistrate of the municipality of Mogetiana, his husband, had this made. She lived 33 years, 5 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-09900324.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Vitalina, erected by her husband Suriacus Secuindinus, a decurion and magistrate, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900325.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cocceiae Quartae vixit nnos XXX in matrim(onio) nnos III menses IIII coniugi piae rarissimae et Cocceio Moderato beneficiario consularis vixit nnos XLIII stipendiorum X Gratae vixit nnos parentibus eius et Honorato militi legionis XIIII geminae stipendiorum X fratriatri Iulius Martialis legionis II adiutricis pro memoria fecit\n
EDCS-09900325.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Cocceia Quarta, his most pious and rare wife, who lived 30 years in marriage for 3 years and 4 months. And to Cocceius Moderatus, beneficiary of a consular governor, who lived 43 years with 10 years of service. To his parents, and to Honoratus, a soldier of the 14th Gemina Legion with 10 years of service, his brother. Julius Martialis of the 2nd Adiutrix Legion erected this for memory.
EDCS-09900325.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription honoring Cocceia Quarta, Cocceius Moderatus, the parents of the dedicant, and Honoratus, a soldier, erected by Julius Martialis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900326.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flaviae Quintae annorum XLV et Marco Kaninio Rustico vivo Kaninii Iustus et Urbana parentibus\n
EDCS-09900326.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Flavia Quinta, aged 45, and Marcus Kaninius Rusticus. While still alive, Kaninius Iustus and Urbana, parents, erected this.
EDCS-09900326.summary: A funerary epitaph for Flavia Quinta and Marcus Kaninius Rusticus, erected by their parents, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900327.text_edition: \n lniae Primae vixsit sic annos XXXX coniugi pientissimae E Titus Aelius rmata veteranus et sibi vivus fecit et Tito Aelio Dextro et Tito Aelio ti vivo et Zillutae nutrici fecit\n
EDCS-09900327.translation: To Inia Prima, who lived 40 years, a most devoted wife. E. Titus Aelius firmata, a veteran, made this for her and for himself while alive, and for Titus Aelius Dextrus, and for Titus Aelius, and for Zilluta, the nurse.
EDCS-09900327.summary: A funerary monument erected by a veteran, Titus Aelius firmata, for his wife Inia Prima and other family members and a nurse, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900328.text_edition: \n Aicca Cansali filia Asalia annorum XL Racio uxoris suae titulum posuit\n
EDCS-09900328.translation: Aicca, daughter of Cansalus, from Asalia, aged 40. Racio erected this monument for his wife.
EDCS-09900328.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aicca, erected by her husband Racio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900330.text_edition: \n Silvanae sacrum Silvanae RO- Licinius votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-09900330.translation: Sacred to Silvana. Licinius fulfilled his vow willingly, joyfully, and deservedly.
EDCS-09900330.summary: A dedication to the goddess Silvana, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Licinius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900331.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Licinius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900331.translation: To Silvanus Domestius. Licinius fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900331.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus Domestius, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Licinius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900332.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900332.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. He fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09900332.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus, recording the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900335.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Prisco militi? cohortis I Ulpiae nnoniorum et Aeliae Procellae parentibus pientissimis et Aureliae Priscillae sorori Marcus Aurelius Proclianus cornicularius tribuni militum legionis I adiutricis Antoninianae filius ex frugalitate et stipendio suorum fecit\n
EDCS-09900335.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Marcus Aurelius Priscus, a soldier of the first cohort of Ulpians of the non-jurors, and for Aelia Procellas, his most devoted parents, and for Aurelia Priscilla, his sister. Marcus Aurelius Proclianus, a cornicularius of a military tribune of the first Antonine legion I Adiutrix, erected this from his own frugality and pay.
EDCS-09900335.summary: A funerary monument erected by Marcus Aurelius Proclianus for his parents and sister, including a soldier of the Roman army, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09900336.text_edition: \n hic situs est Titus Iunius Martinus et Iunius Saturninus patri \n
EDCS-09900336.translation: Titus Iunius Martinus is buried here, and Iunius Saturninus for his father.
EDCS-09900336.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Titus Iunius Martinus and a dedication for his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900337.summary: A dedication or funerary inscription for Licinius Biarchus and Iulia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900338.text_edition: \n Solva Iucundi principis Azaliorum filia nnorum IIX pater posuit\n
EDCS-09900338.translation: Solva, daughter of Iucundus the chief of the Azali, aged 8 years. Her father erected this monument.
EDCS-09900338.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Solva, erected by her father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900339.text_edition: \n aeternae securitati ---tesserarii legionis I adiutricis stipendiorum aetatis XLVI librarius consularis et Valerius Pr anus fili patri faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-09900339.translation: To eternal security. The tesseraarii of the First Adjutant Legion, aged 46, a clerk of a consular official, and Valerius Pranus, erected this for his father. They took care to have it made.
EDCS-09900339.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument for eternal security by tesseraarii of the First Adjutant Legion and a consular clerk, dedicated to their father, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900341.translation: A soldier of the Second Adjutant Legion, Pious, Faithful, Antoninian, served 14 years. He lived [for]...
EDCS-09900341.summary: This is a tombstone fragment for a soldier of the Second Adjutant Legion, mentioning his service length and age at death, likely from the Antonine period.

EDCS-09900344.translation: AFA made this. Dases, son of Busturon.
EDCS-09900344.summary: This inscription records that Dases, son of Busturon, erected the monument, likely a tombstone or a dedication, during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900346.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Vindex sacerdos votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900346.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Aelius Vindex, priest, willingly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-09900346.summary: This inscription records a priest named Aelius Vindex fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900347.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aeterno Marcus Valerius Felix |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis\n
EDCS-09900347.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus. Marcus Valerius Felix, centurion of the Second Adjutant Legion, Pious, Faithful.
EDCS-09900347.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus by Marcus Valerius Felix, a centurion of the Second Adjutant Legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900348.text_edition: \n Libero Patri et Liberae Publius Aelius C votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900348.translation: To Liber Pater and Libera. Publius Aelius C. willingly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-09900348.summary: This inscription records Publius Aelius C. fulfilling a vow to the deities Liber Pater and Libera, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900349.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus e socr<u>i et iminae coniugi dulcissimae et SeveSAIONNO \n
EDCS-09900349.translation: To the Divine Spirits. [Erected by] from his mother-in-law and his dearest wife, and SeveSAIONNO...
EDCS-09900349.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Spirits and family members, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900360.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestro sic Augusto Iulius Secundinus veteranus templum spatio minori sic ampliavit de suo libens merito Aemiliano et Aquilino consulibus\n
EDCS-09900360.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris, the Divine Augustus. Julius Secundinus, a veteran, willingly enlarged the temple from his own funds, as was deserved. In the consulship of Aemilianus and Aquilinus.
EDCS-09900360.summary: This inscription records a veteran, Julius Secundinus, enlarging a temple dedicated to Silvanus Silvestris, funded by himself, during the consulship of Aemilianus and Aquilinus.

EDCS-09900361.text_edition: \n Iulius Secundinus cornicularius praefecti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900361.translation: Julius Secundinus, a cornicularius of the prefect, willingly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-09900361.summary: This inscription records Julius Secundinus, a cornicularius (an officer's assistant) serving a prefect, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900363.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Valerius Clemens miles cohortis XIIX voluntariorum centuria Ulpi Frontonis stipendiorum XXIIII hic situs est Antonius Macr heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09900363.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Valerius Clemens, a soldier of the 18th cohort of volunteers, in the century of Ulpius Fronto, served 24 years. Antonius Macrheres took care to have this made.
EDCS-09900363.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Clemens, a soldier with 24 years of service in a volunteer cohort, erected by Antonius Macrheres, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09900453.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Aurelius Tertius titulum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900453.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and Juno, Aurelius Tertius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09900453.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter and Juno by Aurelius Tertius, likely erected after a vow was fulfilled.

EDCS-09900455.text_edition: \n Libero Pateri sic et Liberae Mamilia Firmina votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09900455.translation: To Liber Pater and likewise to Libera, Mamilia Firmina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-09900455.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deities Liber Pater and Libera by Mamilia Firmina.

EDCS-09900456.text_edition: \n hic situs est et a Cai filia Ru<f=E>illa coniunx viva titulum <m=NH>(emoriae) posuit Maximus et Celerina <p=F>(arentibus) N posuerunt \n
EDCS-09900456.translation: He is buried here and his living wife Rufilla set up this memorial tablet to him; Maximus and Celerina set it up for their parents.
EDCS-09900456.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by a wife for her deceased husband and by children for their parents, with the names of the deceased and the dedicators provided.

EDCS-10000364.text_edition: \n vivus fecit sibi et Amatuni Buccionis filiae uxori suae @(obitae) Valenti @(obito) libertis libertabus locus monumenti quoquo versus pedes XX\n
EDCS-10000364.translation: He made this while alive for himself and for his wife Amatunia, daughter of Buccio, who had died, and for his freedmen and freedwomen, a burial plot twenty feet in every direction.
EDCS-10000364.summary: This is an epitaph recording the provision of a burial plot by a man for himself, his deceased wife, and their freedpeople.

EDCS-10000365.translation: To Fronton, son of Quintus (?), aged 50, and to his mother.
EDCS-10000365.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the names and ages of a man and his mother, likely erected by a surviving relative.

EDCS-10000369.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered God Mithras, and to Silvanus Augustus.
EDCS-10000369.summary: This is a dedication to the deities Mithras and Silvanus Augustus, with the name of the dedicator partially lost.

EDCS-10000372.text_edition: \n Pudenti Brixia militi cohortis II praetoriae centuria Grani militavit annos VII vixit nnos XXV testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-10000372.translation: Pudens served as a soldier in the II Praetorian cohort, in the century of Granius; he served for 7 years and lived for 25 years; he ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-10000372.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pudens, a soldier in the Praetorian Guard, recording his service and age and stating that the monument was ordered by his will.

EDCS-10000373.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Crispiniano militi legionis XIII geminae Sentius Alexander optio posuit\n
EDCS-10000373.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Marcus Aurelius Crispinianus, a soldier of the XIII Gemina legion. Sentius Alexander, optio, set this up.
EDCS-10000373.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier of the XIII Gemina legion, erected by his comrade Sentius Alexander.

EDCS-10000374.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Antonia Maximilla. Laberius Priscus set this up for his worthy and most deserving daughter-in-law.
EDCS-10000374.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Antonia Maximilla, erected by her father-in-law, Laberius Priscus.

EDCS-10000375.translation: To Accepta Caesernia, daughter of Sextus, Secunda; to Caesernia, daughter of Sextus, Vitalis; to Caeparia, daughter of Gnaeus, Tertia, wives who had died.
EDCS-10000375.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing three women, likely family members, with their filiations and patronymics.

EDCS-10000376.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Bononius Ahilleus to his daughter-in-law.
EDCS-10000376.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Bononius Ahilleus for his daughter-in-law, Tiberia.

EDCS-10000377.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Antoninus [son of ...vius]...
EDCS-10000377.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Antoninus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000380.translation: For my dearest husband, she made this while alive.
EDCS-10000380.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that a wife erected a monument for her husband during her lifetime.

EDCS-10000386.translation: Secundinus [made this] for his parents, alive. The monument's location extends 30 feet in each direction.
EDCS-10000386.summary: A funerary monument erected by Secundinus for his parents, with specifications for the monument's dimensions and plot size.

EDCS-10000388.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Marcus Claudius Avitus |(centurio) legionis X geminae piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000388.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Marcus Claudius Avitus, centurion of the Tenth Legion Gemina Pia Fidelis, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000388.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Claudius Avitus, a centurion of the Tenth Legion, expressing gratitude for fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10000389.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Octavius Cai filius Oufentina Victor EM O A praet P\n
EDCS-10000389.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Octavius, son of Caius, from the tribe Oufentina, Victor, former praetor...
EDCS-10000389.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Octavius Victor, a man of equestrian or senatorial rank, possibly dating to the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-10000390.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Marcus Secundinius Vitalis decurio alae I Thracum pro se et Claudia Priscilla coniuge et Secundinia Vitalina filia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000390.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Dispeller. Marcus Secundinius Vitalis, decurion of the First Ala of Thracians, for himself, Claudia Priscilla his wife, and Secundinia Vitalina his daughter, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000390.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Secundinius Vitalis, a decurion in an auxiliary cavalry unit, for himself and his family.

EDCS-10000391.text_edition: \n Victoriae Augustae sacrum Caius Cassius Silvester veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae felicis proproe \n
EDCS-10000391.translation: Sacred to Victory Augusta. Gaius Cassius Silvester, a veteran of the Fourth Flavia Felix Legion, for himself...
EDCS-10000391.summary: A dedication to Victoria Augusta by Gaius Cassius Silvester, a veteran of the Fourth Flavia Felix Legion.

EDCS-10000392.text_edition: \n August-- sacrum Caius Novellius Vitalis Augustalis coloniae Poetovionensis cum Valeria Satonia coniuge votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000392.translation: Sacred to the Augustan [divinity/divine power?]. Gaius Novellius Vitalis, an Augustalis of the colony of Poetovio, with Valeria Satonia his wife, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000392.summary: A votive inscription to a deified emperor by Gaius Novellius Vitalis, a prominent civic official in the colony of Poetovio, for himself and his wife.

EDCS-10000395.text_edition: \n centuria Titi Crescentis Cai Iuli Flavi C\n
EDCS-10000395.translation: Century of Titus Crescens, Gaius Iulius Flavius [...].
EDCS-10000395.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely marking a boundary or property associated with the centuria of Titus Crescens.

EDCS-10000397.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Flavius Va coniugi \n
EDCS-10000397.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Publius Flavius Va, his wife.
EDCS-10000397.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to a wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000398.text_edition: \n coniugi rarissimae optimae i posuit Valerius tor eiius sic\n
EDCS-10000398.translation: Valerius erected (this) for his exceptionally rare and excellent wife.
EDCS-10000398.summary: A funerary dedication by Valerius for his wife, recording her virtues, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000403.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication mentioning a wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000405.text_edition: \n Invicto Deo omnipotenti Valerius Marcellianus ex voto libens laetus posuit\n
EDCS-10000405.translation: Valerius Marcellianus erected (this) to the Invictus, Almighty God, willingly and happily, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10000405.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Sol Invictus by Valerius Marcellianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001080.summary: The name of Emperor Alexander Severus, possibly part of a larger inscription or a standalone dedication, dating to his reign (222-235 CE).

EDCS-10001081.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Bona erected (this) in memory of her most devoted husband, Gaius Bolonius Maximus.
EDCS-10001081.summary: A funerary inscription by Aurelia Bona commemorating her devoted husband, Gaius Bolonius Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001082.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quarta obita annorum L et Tertia filia obita annorum V\n
EDCS-10001082.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Quarta, who died at the age of 50, and Tertia, her daughter, who died at the age of 5.
EDCS-10001082.summary: A funerary inscription honoring two deceased individuals, Quarta and her daughter Tertia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001083.summary: A dedication to the Greek gods of healing, Asclepius and Hygieia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001108.translation: Pardus, may you live in God.
EDCS-10001108.summary: A posthumous wish for eternal life, possibly with early Christian connotations, for Pardus, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-10001109.translation: [---] for Rai[---], his son, who lived for 17 years, 3 months, 6 days. His father Quintianus.
EDCS-10001109.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription by Quintianus for his son who died at a young age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001110.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely commemorating Apullus and his son, with an incomplete name at the end.

EDCS-10001111.translation: and to his wife [3], and to Avita, daughter of [3]
EDCS-10001111.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, recording the dedication to a wife and a daughter named Avita.

EDCS-10001115.translation: Evandus Marcellianum lived 65 years, as long as he lived.
EDCS-10001115.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Evandus Marcellianum at the age of 65.

EDCS-10001117.text_edition: \n Cornelia Luci filia Secunda annorum XXXX hic sita est Optatus filius posuit ma<t=I>(ri) \n
EDCS-10001117.translation: Cornelia Secunda, daughter of Lucius, 40 years old, is buried here. Her son Optatus dedicated this to his mother.
EDCS-10001117.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelia Secunda, erected by her son Optatus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001118.translation: Sacred to Fortuna Augusta, from Restutus.
EDCS-10001118.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Fortuna Augusta, likely erected by Restutus.

EDCS-10001120.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Eudio P libertus Tiberius Barbius Primus bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-10001120.translation: Tiberius Barbius Primus, freedman of Tiberius Claudius Eudius, made this for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10001120.summary: This funerary inscription records Tiberius Barbius Primus, a freedman, erecting a monument for a 'well-deserving' individual.

EDCS-10001124.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a name, possibly Quintus Gavius Secundus.

EDCS-10001126.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription, possibly indicating ownership or belonging to someone named Rufianus.

EDCS-10001129.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum C Ursula votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001129.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. C. Ursula gladly fulfilled her vow according to his merit.
EDCS-10001129.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Silvanus Domesticus, dedicated by C. Ursula.

EDCS-10001130.text_edition: \n Silvanis Augustis sacrum QVIPIARV FINI Victor libens merito\n
EDCS-10001130.translation: Sacred to the August Silvani. Victor gladly and deservedly [fulfilled his vow].
EDCS-10001130.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Silvani Augusti, dedicated by Victor.

EDCS-10001133.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Flavius Victorinus miles legionis XIII geminae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-10001133.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Flavius Victorinus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001133.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus by Flavius Victorinus, a soldier of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-10001135.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Publi filio Aeliano scribae decurioni IIIIviro municipii Iasorum annorum XLV Aellius Laelianus patri piissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10001135.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aellius Laelianus had this made for his most devoted father, Publius Aelius Aelianus, son of Publius, scribe, decurion, and magistrate of the municipality of Iasus, aged 45.
EDCS-10001135.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Aelianus, a local official in Iasus, set up by his son.

EDCS-10001136.text_edition: \n Sextus Valerius Sexti filius Quirina Secundus Mediolano veteranus legionis XIII geminae Valerius Victorinus filius eius heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10001136.translation: Sextus Valerius Secundus, son of Sextus, of the tribe Quirina, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina from Milan, and his son Valerius Victorinus, his heir, had this made.
EDCS-10001136.summary: This funerary inscription records Sextus Valerius Secundus, a veteran of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, and his son Valerius Victorinus.

EDCS-10001137.text_edition: \n Deanae Augustae sacrum Domitius Martialis |(centurio) legionis XIII geminae pro salute sua votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001137.translation: Sacred to the August Goddess Deana. Domitius Martialis, a centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own health.
EDCS-10001137.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Deana Augusta by Domitius Martialis, a centurion of the Thirteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-10001141.text_edition: \n Deae? Iunoni Matri Deum Magnae sacrum Albucia Titi filia Celerina Albucius Titi filius Rufus\n
EDCS-10001141.translation: Sacred to the Goddess Juno, Great Mother of the Gods. Albucia Celerina, daughter of Titus, and Albucius Rufus, son of Titus.
EDCS-10001141.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno, the Great Mother of the Gods, by siblings Albucia Celerina and Albucius Rufus.

EDCS-10001142.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Primitivi Augusti nostri liberti circitoris Illyrici suorumque omnium Mercurialis libertus ex voto\n
EDCS-10001142.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Mercurialis, freedman of Primitivus, freedman of our Emperor, a circitor of Illyricum, and all his dependents, made this offering according to his vow.
EDCS-10001142.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Mercurialis, a freedman and official, for himself and his dependents.

EDCS-10001144.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Hadrianus \n
EDCS-10001144.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Publius Aelius Hadrianus.
EDCS-10001144.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius Hadrianus.

EDCS-10001146.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae A et Aureli-- V\n
EDCS-10001146.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered God Mithras. Aurelius... and V...
EDCS-10001146.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mithras, with incomplete dedicant names.

EDCS-10001148.text_edition: \n Cauti pro salute Flavi Hermadionis et Aviti Syriaci et filiorum Felix libertus\n
EDCS-10001148.translation: For the health of Flavius Hermadion, Avitus Syricus, and their sons. Felix, his freedman, [made this].
EDCS-10001148.summary: This is a dedication for the health of Flavius Hermadion and Avitus Syricus and their children, set up by a freedman named Felix.

EDCS-10001150.summary: This is a very brief inscription recording a gift given by Valerius M.

EDCS-10001153.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Marcus Gongius Aquileiensis pro salute sua suorumque omnium votum solvit libens merito dono dedit VIIII Kalendas Ianuarias posuit pater pientissimus Florentius\n
EDCS-10001153.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Marcus Gongius of Aquileia, for the welfare of himself and all his family, willingly and deservedly paid his vow and gave this gift. He placed it on the ninth day before the Kalends of January. His devoted father Florentius placed it.
EDCS-10001153.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Mithras, dedicated by Marcus Gongius for his family's well-being and placed by his father Florentius, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001155.text_edition: \n M Luci Co Maecia Cilonis |(centurionis) legionis XIII Geminae\n
EDCS-10001155.translation: To M. Lucius, from Maecia, daughter of Cilo (centurion) of the 13th Gemina Legion.
EDCS-10001155.summary: This is an epitaph for M. Lucius, honoring Maecia and referencing her father who was a centurion in the 13th Gemina Legion, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001156.text_edition: \n Grania Luci filia Procula domo Aquileia viva fecit sibi et nio \n
EDCS-10001156.translation: Grania Procula, daughter of Lucius, from Aquileia, made this while alive for herself and (her family/husband/children).
EDCS-10001156.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Grania Procula of Aquileia making a dedication for herself and her family, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001168.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Culminali Augusto et Genio dominorum sacrum Iuli Iuliani Ulpiae Maximianae vilicus Scithicus sic servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10001168.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Culminalis Augustus and to the Genius of the Lords. Scithicus, the steward, and Sic servus, the slave, willingly and deservedly paid their vow.
EDCS-10001168.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to Jupiter and the Genius of the Lords, made by a steward named Scithicus and a slave named Sic servus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001172.text_edition: \n Minervam Augustam Lucius Claudius Tiberi filius Quirina Moderatus decurio coloniae Claudiae Savariae decurio municipii Aeli Sallae sacerdos arae Augusti equo p(ublico)? in quinque decuriis posuit\n
EDCS-10001172.translation: Lucius Claudius Moderatus, son of Tiberius, of the Quirina tribe, decurion of the colony of Savaria, decurion of the municipium of Aelia Salda, priest of the altar of Augustus, dedicated this statue of Minerva Augusta, as a public servant, in the five decuries.
EDCS-10001172.summary: This inscription records Lucius Claudius Moderatus, a prominent local official and priest, dedicating a statue of Minerva Augusta in Savaria, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001175.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs by Caesius, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001176.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs by Aurelius, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001177.text_edition: \n Lucius Larius Veler primus pilus legionis XIIII geminae \n
EDCS-10001177.translation: Lucius Larius Veler, first spear (primus pilus) of the 14th Gemina Legion.
EDCS-10001177.summary: This inscription identifies Lucius Larius Veler as a primus pilus (senior centurion) of the 14th Gemina Legion, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10001179.text_edition: \n e Marcus Aurelius nobilissimus Caesar? a Poetovione milia passuum \n
EDCS-10001179.translation: From Poetovio, Marcus Aurelius, most noble Caesar, [---] miles.
EDCS-10001179.summary: This inscription mentions Marcus Aurelius, possibly Caesar, and a distance in miles from Poetovio, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100575.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Aurelius Marci filius votum solvit\n
EDCS-10100575.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Aurelius, son of Marcius, paid his vow.
EDCS-10100575.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus Augustus by Aurelius, son of Marcius, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10102105.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae Domitia Decimi liberta Pusilla votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10102105.translation: To Fortune Augusta, Pusilla, freedwoman of Decimus, willingly and deservedly paid her vow.
EDCS-10102105.summary: A vow paid to the goddess Fortuna Augusta by a freedwoman, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102108.text_edition: \n POIIPI probatus Bradua et Varo posuit Orfito et Maximo votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10102108.translation: Probatus Bradua and Varo set this up to Orfitus and Maximus; willingly and deservedly paid their vow.
EDCS-10102108.summary: A dedication to the individuals Orfitus and Maximus, possibly as patrons or recipients of a vow, set up by Probatus Bradua and Varo, likely in the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102109.text_edition: \n Crescens praefectus collegii fabrorum donum dederunt Apro et Maximo consulibus\n
EDCS-10102109.translation: Crescens and others, officials of the guild of craftsmen, gave this gift to Apro and Maximus, consuls.
EDCS-10102109.summary: A dedication to Apro and Maximus, who were consuls, made by Crescens and other officials of the craftsmen's guild, likely in the late 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10102111.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning the name Maximus, possibly a dedication or memorial.

EDCS-10102112.text_edition: \n ML Septimi- Sever? Marc- Aureli- M\n
EDCS-10102112.translation: [From the estate of] M. Septimius Severus Aurelius Marcus...
EDCS-10102112.summary: A partial inscription mentioning the name M. Septimius Severus Aurelius Marcus, likely related to a tomb or property.

EDCS-10102113.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Valerio Luci filio Verecundo domo Siscia veterano legionis XV Apollinaris annorum LXV quo minus in funus expensum erat quod collegius sic veteranorum contulit ad titulum hic situs est\n
EDCS-10102113.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Valerius Verecundus, son of Lucius, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 65. The expenses for his funeral, which the veterans' guild contributed, are listed here, and he is buried here.
EDCS-10102113.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Valerius Verecundus, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, funded in part by his veterans' guild, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10701826.text_edition: \n Genium pro salute Imperatoris [Caesaris Marci Aureli] [Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti] Lucius Octavius Marci filius Faustinianus decurio coloniae Claudiae Augustae Savariae et coloniae Septimiae Aureliae Antoninianae Karnunti equo publico sacerdotalis provinciae Pannoniae superioris tribunus militum legionis XIII geminae Antoninianae tribunus cohortis II Mattiacorum |(miliariae) equitatae praefectus alae I Septimiae Surorum |(miliariae) collegio fabrum Karnuntensium donum dedit Dedicatum Imperatore An[tonino Augusto] II et Sacerdote consulibus X Kalendas Septembres agente praefecto Tito Aelio Constante maggistris collegii Aelio Herculano et Ulpio Marcellino\n
EDCS-10701826.translation: To the Genius of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, Lucius Octavius Faustinianus, son of Marcus, a decurion of the colony of Claudia Augusta Savaria and of the colony of Septimia Aurelia Antoniniana Carnuntum, a knight in public service, a priest of the province of Upper Pannonia, tribune of soldiers of the 13th Gemina Antoniniana legion, tribune of the 2nd cohort of Mattiacorum (miliaria) equitata, prefect of the 1st ala Septimia Surorum (miliaria), gave this gift to the guild of craftsmen of Carnuntum. Dedicated during the consulship of Emperor Antoninus Augustus II and Sacerdos, on the tenth day before the Kalends of September, under the prefect Titus Aelius Constans, with the guild masters Aelius Herculanus and Ulpius Marcellinus.
EDCS-10701826.summary: A complex dedication to the Genius of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, by a prominent local official and military officer named Lucius Octavius Faustinianus, to the guild of craftsmen in Carnuntum, dated to the consulship of Antoninus and Sacerdos (likely in the mid-3rd century CE).

EDCS-11200002.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Aelius Tutor stator alae I Cannanefat(ium) civium R(omanorum) votum solvit libens laetus merito Flacco et Gallo consulibus votum libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-11200002.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Aelius Tutor, a standard-bearer of the 1st ala Cannanefatium civium Romanorum, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly paid his vow, during the consulship of Flaccus and Gallus; willingly and joyfully and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-11200002.summary: A votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aelius Tutor, a standard-bearer of the 1st ala Cannanefatium, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11200004.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae sacrum Titus Flavius Surillio stator praefecti alae I nnatium votum solvit libens laetus merito duobus Aspris consulibus\n
EDCS-11200004.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina. Titus Flavius Surillius, standard-bearer of the 1st ala of the [N]natium, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly paid his vow, during the consulship of the two Asprī.
EDCS-11200004.summary: A votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Juno Regina by Titus Flavius Surillius, a standard-bearer of a cavalry unit, during the consulship of the two Asprī (likely in the late 2nd century CE).

EDCS-11200005.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ala I Cannanefatium Lucius Aurelius Dionysius \n
EDCS-11200005.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the 1st ala Cannanefatium, Lucius Aurelius Dionysius...
EDCS-11200005.summary: A partial inscription dedicating something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, mentioning the 1st ala Cannanefatium and an individual named Lucius Aurelius Dionysius.

EDCS-11200008.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Antonius Iulianus stator praefecti alae I Cannanefatium Gordianae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-11200008.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Antonius Iulianus, orderly of the prefect of the 1st Wing of Canninefates Gordiana, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200008.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by an auxiliary cavalryman, likely from the 3rd century AD, given the reference to the Gordian dynasty.

EDCS-11200012.text_edition: \n Deo Magno Aeterno sacrum Maximinus stator praefecti alae I Cannanefatium votum solvit libens laetus merito pius in suis\n
EDCS-11200012.translation: Sacred to the Great Eternal God. Maximinus, orderly of the prefect of the 1st Wing of Canninefates, willingly, joyfully, deservedly, and piously towards his own, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200012.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to an unidentified "Great Eternal God" by an auxiliary cavalryman, probably in the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200015.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolichenno sic Augusto aedem cum signo et ornamentis et porticu pro salute Maroni Agathangeli sacerdotis Iovis Dolicheni Gerulatensis Maronius Secundus libertus pecunia sua fecit\n
EDCS-11200015.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to Jupiter Dolichenus Augustus. Maronius Secundus, freedman, built at his own expense a temple with an image, furnishings, and a portico for the health of Maro Agathangelus, priest of Jupiter Dolichenus Gerulatensis.
EDCS-11200015.summary: This is a dedication of a temple and its appurtenances to Jupiter Dolichenus, funded by a freedman for the health of a priest, likely in the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200016.translation: from the estate of Maria, by imperial order.
EDCS-11200016.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly recording property or an order related to property managed by Maria, dating is uncertain.

EDCS-11200028.translation: and Marcus [...]tinus arranged for it to be made.
EDCS-11200028.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription recording that Marcus [...]tinus arranged for something to be constructed or done, likely of the 3rd century AD or later.

EDCS-11200032.text_edition: \n legionis II Italicae piae f(idelis)annorum XXII ASME E veteranorum hered--- RIO posuit\n
EDCS-11200032.translation: of the 2nd Italian Legion, pious, faithful, aged 22 years [...] RIO erected (this).
EDCS-11200032.summary: This inscription records details about a soldier from the Legio II Italica, including his age and veteran status, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200033.translation: equestrian soldier, with 10 years of service.
EDCS-11200033.summary: This is a very brief inscription noting an equestrian soldier with 10 years of service, the purpose and full date are unknown.

EDCS-11200036.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Valerius Chrysogonus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11200036.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Lucius Valerius Chrysogonus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11200036.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Lucius Valerius Chrysogonus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-11200038.text_edition: \n IIIIviro iure dicundo praefecto fabrum aediliciis potestatibus II[II ------\n
EDCS-11200038.translation: member of the college of four men for dispensing justice, prefect of craftsmen, with aedilic powers, for four [...] years.
EDCS-11200038.summary: This fragmentary inscription lists various civic and potentially military offices held by an individual, likely dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-11300950.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Attonius Carpophorus, by order.
EDCS-11300950.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, stating that Gaius Attonius Carpophorus fulfilled a vow by order, likely from the Roman Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-11300952.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Repeller. Surianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-11300952.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, invoked with epithets indicating his protective role, likely a private dedication made by Surianus.

EDCS-11300953.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Dolichenean. Valerius Aelianus, standard-bearer of the 13th Legion Gemina, [dedicated this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11300953.summary: This is a military dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus, an eastern deity popular with soldiers, made by Valerius Aelianus, a standard-bearer in the 13th Legion.

EDCS-11300954.translation: Sacred to the August Lares. Lucius Dindius Priscus, the [priest], and Lucius Dindius Priscianus gave [this].
EDCS-11300954.summary: This is a dedication to the Lares Augusti, the household gods of the imperial family, given by Lucius Dindius Priscus, possibly a priest, and his son Priscianus.

EDCS-11300955.translation: To the August Lares. Laletus and his son Doryphorus, slaves of Gaius Decius Avitus, gave this offering.
EDCS-11300955.summary: This is a dedication to the Lares Augusti made by two slaves, Laletus and Doryphorus, who served Gaius Decius Avitus.

EDCS-11300956.translation: To the divine Augustus Mercury. Marcus Aurelius Serenus, a [member of the VIvirate], willingly placed [this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11300956.summary: This is a dedication to Mercury, addressed as Augustus, made by Marcus Aurelius Serenus, a member of a college of six officials, who fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-11300957.translation: Sacred to Augusta Nemesis. Primitivianus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11300957.summary: This is a dedication to Nemesis, addressed as Augusta, made by Primitivianus who willingly fulfilled a vow, likely for good fortune or justice.

EDCS-11300958.translation: To Oraea from a command. [She] willingly placed [this] with a willing heart. To the Great Mother of the Gods.
EDCS-11300958.summary: This dedication is to the Great Mother of the Gods (Cybele), also referred to as Oraea, placed by an unnamed individual, possibly on divine instruction.

EDCS-11300959.translation: Sacred to the August and Holy Victory. Sextus Vibunnius Avitus [dedicated this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11300959.summary: This is a dedication to Victory, addressed as Augusta and Sancta, made by Sextus Vibunnius Avitus in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-11300960.translation: To Victory. Titus P. T. willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11300960.summary: This is a dedication to Victory made by Titus P. T., who willingly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-11300961.translation: To Victory. Lucius Barbius willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11300961.summary: This is a dedication to Victory made by Lucius Barbius, who willingly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-11300963.translation: To Gaius Volusius Maximus Firmus, Optatus Proculus, and Constans, servants, and also Publicius Porcius Munitus, Clodius Dexter, Tullius Secundus, Priscus, whoever they are, all adversaries.
EDCS-11300963.summary: This inscription appears to be a curse or denunciation against unspecified adversaries, possibly related to a legal dispute or public office, from the Roman period.

EDCS-11300968.text_edition: \n Titus Caesernius Assupae libertus Diphilus Aquileiae sexvir sic vivus sibi fecit Dignus libertus vivus\n
EDCS-11300968.translation: Titus Caesernius Assupa's freedman Diphilus had this made for himself while alive in Aquileia; he was a sexvir. Dignus, a freedman, also had this made while alive.
EDCS-11300968.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by two freedmen, Diphilus and Dignus, for themselves during their lifetime, indicating their social status and place of residence in Aquileia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11300969.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Eutyches, the freedman, wrote this for Brinnia Maxima, wife of Lucius Metellus Clementis, IIvir and priest, and for Helvia Severina, mother-in-law.
EDCS-11300969.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Brinnia Maxima and Helvia Severina by Eutyches, a freedman, mentioning the deceased's husband who held the important civic and religious office of IIvir, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11300970.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Gallius Exsoratus, for his most unfortunate daughter Exsorata, aged seven.
EDCS-11300970.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating a young girl named Exsorata, who died at the age of seven, dedicated by her father, Gallius Exsoratus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11300971.summary: This is a simple funerary dedication to a freedman named Vitalis, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11300972.translation: For Primigenius, freedman, for Fidelius, freedman, for Expedītus, freedman.
EDCS-11300972.summary: This is a funerary inscription listing three freedmen: Primigenius, Fidelius, and Expedītus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-11300981.translation: Masculus, son of Ressi/marus, aged 60; also for his wife Bellona, daughter of Secundus, aged 50; also [unreadable text] and [unreadable text].
EDCS-11300981.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Masculus, his wife Bellona, and mentions additional deceased individuals, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11300982.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Mammius Maximus, husband, aged 40, and for Lucius Mammius Severus, son, aged 35, [unreadable text], [unreadable text]. This monument will not follow its heir.
EDCS-11300982.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Mammius Maximus and his son Lucius Mammius Severus, indicating they died at ages 40 and 35 respectively, and includes a clause stating the monument is not hereditary, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11300983.text_edition: \n Publius Paetinius filius Clementianus occissus sic in stabulo scelere nnorum XVIII ? et Paterniae Sexti filiae Gallae Paetinius Peregrinus fratri et matri vivus fecit\n
EDCS-11300983.translation: Publius Paetinius Clementianus, son of Publius, was killed in a stable by a crime at the age of 18. Paetinius Peregrinus made this for his brother and mother, Paternia Galla, daughter of Sextus, while alive.
EDCS-11300983.summary: This funerary inscription records the violent death of Publius Paetinius Clementianus at age 18 and was erected by his brother Paetinius Peregrinus for both him and their mother, Paternia Galla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11300989.text_edition: \n Maritimo et Ulpio Secundo legionis IIIIX geminae annorum X\n
EDCS-11300989.translation: For Maritimus and Ulpius Secundus of the Fourth Legion Gemina, aged 10.
EDCS-11300989.summary: This inscription commemorates two individuals, Maritimus and Ulpius Secundus, likely soldiers or sons of soldiers from the Fourth Legion Gemina, who died at the age of 10, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11300991.text_edition: \n Romani filius annorum LX testamento fieri iussit et Suro filio vivo\n
EDCS-11300991.translation: Romanus, son of Romanus, sixty years old, ordered this to be made by his will, and for his son Suro, while alive.
EDCS-11300991.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Romanus, who was sixty years old and arranged for his own monument, to also be for his son Suro.

EDCS-11300992.text_edition: \n Aesculapio Augusto sacrum Marcus Messius Messor Auggustorum nnostrorum ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-11300992.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius Augustus. Marcus Messius Messor set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11300992.summary: This is a votive dedication to Aesculapius Augustus by Marcus Messius Messor.

EDCS-11300995.text_edition: \n Caius Ulpius Aurelius Gaianus Isidi Victrici et Serapi pro salute sua suorumque omnium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11300995.translation: Gaius Ulpius Aurelius Gaianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled a vow to Isis the Victorious and Serapis for his own health and that of all his family.
EDCS-11300995.summary: This is a votive dedication to Isis and Serapis by Gaius Ulpius Aurelius Gaianus for the health of himself and his family.

EDCS-11300996.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Isis, likely by someone named Publius Valerius.

EDCS-11300998.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Bassianus.
EDCS-11300998.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Bassianus.

EDCS-11301004.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo CApitolino Iuli-- Eutych \n
EDCS-11301004.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest Capitolinus. Iuli-- Eutych--
EDCS-11301004.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest Capitolinus by someone named Eutychus.

EDCS-11301007.text_edition: \n Libero et Liberae sacrum Lucius Valerius Verus decurio coloniae Poetovionensis praefectus fabrum quaestor aedilis pontifex IIvir iure dicundo\n
EDCS-11301007.translation: Sacred to Liber and Libera. Lucius Valerius Verus, decurion of the colony of Poetovio, prefect of engineers, quaestor, aedile, pontiff, and twice chief magistrate with judicial authority.
EDCS-11301007.summary: This is a dedication to Liber and Libera by Lucius Valerius Verus, a prominent magistrate in the colony of Poetovio.

EDCS-11301010.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Augusto sacrum E Augusti servus h tab votum solvit\n
EDCS-11301010.translation: Sacred to Sol Invictus Augustus. E Augusti's slave dedicated this fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-11301010.summary: This is a votive dedication to Sol Invictus Augustus by a slave of "Augustus".

EDCS-11301011.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Sextus Vibius Hermes Augustalis coloniae Ulpiae Traianae Poetovienensis signum argenteum cum base sua donum dedit cum suis patre prosedente Lucio Vernasio Heraclida\n
EDCS-11301011.translation: Sextus Vibius Hermes, Augustalis of the colony of Ulpia Traiana Poetovio, gave a silver statue with its base as a gift to Sol Invictus Mithras, with his father Lucius Vernasius Heraclitus assisting.
EDCS-11301011.summary: This is a dedication of a silver statue of Sol Invictus Mithras by Sextus Vibius Hermes, assisted by his father.

EDCS-11301016.text_edition: \n leggionum V Macedonicae et XIII geminae Gallienarum Flavius Aper vir egregius praepositus\n
EDCS-11301016.translation: Flavius Aper, an outstanding man, prefect.
EDCS-11301016.summary: This inscription honors Flavius Aper, an outstanding man and prefect, possibly associated with the legions V Macedonica and XIII Gemina.

EDCS-11301024.text_edition: \n Nutricibus Augustis sacrum Aurelius Siro pro salute Aureli Primiani votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301024.translation: Sacred to the August Nurses. Aurelius Siro gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the health of Aurelius Primianus.
EDCS-11301024.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the August Nurses by Aurelius Siro for the health of Aurelius Primianus.

EDCS-11301025.text_edition: \n Nutricibus Augustis Successius Maximianus et Malia Verina pro salute Successiae Verae filiae votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-11301025.translation: Successius Maximianus and Malia Verina gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to the August Nurses for the health of their daughter, Successia Vera.
EDCS-11301025.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the August Nurses by Successius Maximianus and Malia Verina for the health of their daughter, Successia Vera.

EDCS-11301026.text_edition: \n Nutricibus Augustis Valerius Secunndianus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-11301026.translation: Valerius Secundianus gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the August Nurses.
EDCS-11301026.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the August Nurses by Valerius Secundianus.

EDCS-11301027.text_edition: \n Silvano ? conveniunt voto suscepto sacrarium sacraverunt\n
EDCS-11301027.translation: To Silvanus. They consecrated a shrine and performed rites, having undertaken a vow.
EDCS-11301027.summary: An inscription recording the consecration of a shrine to Silvanus after undertaking a vow.

EDCS-11301029.summary: An inscription recording the name of Gaius Julius Epaphroditus.

EDCS-11301030.text_edition: \n us beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301030.translation: A beneficiarius of a consular fulfilled his vow gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-11301030.summary: A votive inscription by a beneficiarius of a consular.

EDCS-11301031.text_edition: \n cornicularius tribuni memoriae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301031.translation: A cornicularius of a tribune fulfilled his vow gladly and deservedly in memory.
EDCS-11301031.summary: A votive inscription by a cornicularius of a tribune, possibly commemorating something.

EDCS-11301033.text_edition: \n te et C Cogitato Aurelio Leontio Arriano et Papo consulibus\n
EDCS-11301033.translation: in the consulship of C. Cogitato Aurelius Leontius Arrianus and Papus.
EDCS-11301033.summary: An inscription fragment recording the consulship of C. Cogitato Aurelius Leontius Arrianus and Papus.

EDCS-11301035.text_edition: \n E Helena votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301035.translation: Helena fulfilled her vow gladly and deservedly.

EDCS-11301036.text_edition: \n cum Maxima et nilla filiis pro salute sua et suorum votum solv-- liben-- merito\n
EDCS-11301036.translation: with Maxima and [---]illa, for the health of themselves and their family, fulfilled [his/her/their] vow gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-11301036.summary: A votive inscription by an individual, possibly male given the verb, along with Maxima and another person, for the health of their family.

EDCS-11301037.text_edition: \n Ulpius Proculus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301037.translation: Ulpius Proculus willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-11301037.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Ulpius Proculus fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity, around the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301049.translation: In the consulship of NO and Severus.
EDCS-11301049.summary: This is a dating formula indicating the consuls of a particular year, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301051.text_edition: \n Lucius Fannius Luci filius Claudia Quadratus optio legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis missione nummaria missus PCM S annorum IX libertIX Urban? IustIRSI IA Primitivus libertus faciendum curav---\n
EDCS-11301051.translation: Lucius Fannius Quadratus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, a centurion's deputy (optio) in the I Adjutrix Pia Fidelis legion, discharged with a monetary payment, died at the age of 69 years. Primitivus, his freedman, had this made.
EDCS-11301051.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Fannius Quadratus, a retired optio of the I Adjutrix legion, set up by his freedman, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301052.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Gargilius Luci filius Quirina Felix Tacapis veteranus legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis missus missione nummaria nnorum L Lucius S L \n
EDCS-11301052.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Gargilius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, a veteran of the I Adjutrix Pia Fidelis legion, discharged with a monetary payment, lived 50 years. Lucius S [set this up].
EDCS-11301052.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Gargilius Felix, a retired veteran of the I Adjutrix legion, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301053.text_edition: \n Caius Memmius Cai filius Primus Fabia miles legionis XIII geminae \n
EDCS-11301053.translation: Gaius Memmius Primus, son of Gaius, of the Fabia tribe, a soldier in the XIII Gemina legion.
EDCS-11301053.summary: This is a military inscription identifying Gaius Memmius Primus as a soldier in the XIII Gemina legion, possibly for identification on equipment or a tomb, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301054.text_edition: \n coniugi karissimo militi legionis XIIII geminae qui vixit nnos LXV et Munatio Valentino filio karissimo qui vixit nnos XVIII\n
EDCS-11301054.translation: To my dearest wife who lived 65 years, and to my dearest son Munatius Valentinus who lived 18 years.
EDCS-11301054.summary: This is a tombstone inscription commemorating a wife and son, likely set up by the surviving husband/father, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301055.text_edition: \n Aulus Postumius Spuri filius Seneca domo Parma veteranus missicius legionis XI annorum XXCV hic situs est Postumia Iucunda et Primigenius posierunt sic liberti\n
EDCS-11301055.translation: Aulus Postumius Seneca, son of Spurius, from Parma, a supernumerary veteran of the XI legion, 85 years old, is buried here. Postumia Iucunda and Primigenius, his freedmen, placed this.
EDCS-11301055.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Aulus Postumius Seneca, a retired veteran of the XI legion, set up by his freedmen, dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301061.summary: This is a dedication to Aurelius Barbus, the meaning of 'V AEI' is unclear but likely part of a formulaic expression of devotion or dedication, possibly from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301064.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai libertus Nikander annorum I hic situs est\n
