EDCS-11301064.translation: Nikander, freedman of Gaius Julius, aged 1 year, is buried here.
EDCS-11301064.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for an infant freedman named Nikander, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301065.text_edition: \n servo annorum XX bene merito poni iussit Lupianus actuarius ex praecepto domini fecit\n
EDCS-11301065.translation: To a deserving slave, 20 years old, it was ordered to be placed. Lupianus, a clerk, made this by order of his master.
EDCS-11301065.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a slave, set up by an administrative official named Lupianus under the direction of his master, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301066.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a man named Priscus, dated to the 1st century CE based on the numeral style.

EDCS-11301067.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et perpetuae saecuritati sic ne grave sit quicumque legis cognoscere casus Quintiniae est in II tegitur fato rapta malo et iniquo numineNSIRVCI annorum LX semissis requiem ex more vetusto perpetuamdec ex provincia RITIIASV coniugi incomparabili IIINICREDV II \n
EDCS-11301067.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed and eternal security, lest it be burdensome to whosoever reads to know the fate of Quintinia, who is buried here, snatched away by a bad and unjust destiny, aged 60 and a half, she rests in everlasting peace according to ancient custom, to her incomparable wife from the province of RITIIASV, IIINICREDV II.
EDCS-11301067.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Quintinia by her wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, given the style of the epitaph and the mention of the province.

EDCS-11301068.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Ravius Restutus sibi et parentibus suis et coniugi suae Vibiae Marcel<l=I>inae et filiis Caio Ravio Restuto et Munatio et filiis vivis \n
EDCS-11301068.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Ravius Restutus for himself and for his parents and for his wife, Vibbia Marcel(l)ina, and for his sons Gaius Ravius Restutus and Munatius and his living sons.
EDCS-11301068.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Ravius Restutus, establishing a family tomb for himself, his parents, wife, and sons, probably from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-11301069.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription or dedication mentioning Titus Rufius E, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11301071.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Statiniae Phoebes Caius Caecina Florus coniugi piissimae fecit\n
EDCS-11301071.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Caecina Florus made this for his most dutiful wife, Statinia Phoebes.
EDCS-11301071.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Caecina Florus for his wife, Statinia Phoebes, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-11301073.text_edition: \n rTo annorum VII Tae annorum XXV o et Verecund arco et Ulpi-- Secundinae et miae Rufinae anae fratri hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-11301073.translation: rTo aged 7, Tae aged 25, and Verecundus, and Ulpi-- Secundina, and mia Rufina, brother, this monument shall not follow an heir.
EDCS-11301073.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the ages of at least two individuals, possibly siblings, and stipulating that the monument is not to be inherited, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-11301074.text_edition: \n Manibus sacrum Marcus Titius Zosimus Titiae Syrae coniugi karissimae et bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-11301074.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Titius Zosimus placed this for his dearest and well-deserving wife, Titia Syra.
EDCS-11301074.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Marcus Titius Zosimus for his wife Titia Syra, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-11301075.text_edition: \n VG us Cai filius ilus ? posuit\n
EDCS-11301075.translation: VG us, son of Caius, ? placed this.
EDCS-11301075.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication or funerary inscription mentioning a son of Caius, dating possibly to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11301097.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a name and parentage for a monument or official record, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-11301099.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Viatori et Liciniae C V\n
EDCS-11301099.translation: For Publius Aelius Viator and Licinia C V.
EDCS-11301099.summary: This is a funerary or commemorative inscription for Publius Aelius Viator and Licinia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-11301101.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius <G=C>eminus Savi filius annorum Volusia \n
EDCS-11301101.translation: Marcus Ulpius Geminus, son of Savius, aged [40?], of Volusia.
EDCS-11301101.summary: An epitaph recording the name, parentage, age, and tribe of Marcus Ulpius Geminus.

EDCS-11301102.text_edition: \n Dominae et deae Dianae monitu votum solvit Eburia Ursula RE\n
EDCS-11301102.translation: At the divine prompting of our Lady and Goddess Diana, Eburia Ursula willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11301102.summary: A votive offering to Diana by Eburia Ursula, prompted by divine inspiration.

EDCS-11301104.text_edition: \n Silvanis Augustis sacrum Cornelia Restuta pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301104.translation: Sacred to the August Silvani, Cornelia Restuta willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for herself and her family.
EDCS-11301104.summary: A dedication to the Silvani Augusti by Cornelia Restuta for herself and her household.

EDCS-11301105.text_edition: \n Silvanis Augustis sacrum Pompeia Florentina pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301105.translation: Sacred to the August Silvani, Pompeia Florentina willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for herself and her family.
EDCS-11301105.summary: A dedication to the Silvani Augusti by Pompeia Florentina for herself and her household.

EDCS-11301108.text_edition: \n M M Pontius posuit sibi et coniugi et Marco C A\n
EDCS-11301108.translation: M. Mummius Pontius erected this for himself, his wife, and Marcus C. A.
EDCS-11301108.summary: A funerary inscription recording the erection of a monument by M. Mummius Pontius for his family.

EDCS-11301112.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Caius Aponius Longinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301112.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Caius Aponius Longinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11301112.summary: A votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Aponius Longinus.

EDCS-11301113.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto ob virtutem et salutem IO \n
EDCS-11301113.translation: To Mars Augustus, for bravery and health.
EDCS-11301113.summary: A dedication to Mars Augustus, likely for military success or well-being.

EDCS-11301114.text_edition: \n MOMVS Silvano sacrum AVPICA libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-11301114.translation: Momus to Silvanus. Aupica willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-11301114.summary: A votive offering to Silvanus by Aupica, possibly relating to the deity Momus.

EDCS-11301115.summary: A dedication to the deities Vidasus and Thana.

EDCS-11301116.text_edition: \n Vidaso et Thanae sacrum Euhelpistus Hispani servus I votum solvit\n
EDCS-11301116.translation: Sacred to Vidasus and Thana; Euhelpistus, a servant from Hispania, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11301116.summary: A dedication to the gods Vidasus and Thana by a freedman, likely in the Roman Empire.

EDCS-11301117.summary: A fragmented inscription recording the name and profession of a craftsman, likely from the Roman Empire.

EDCS-11301119.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valentiniae Festae @(obitae) annorum L posterisque eius Serena soror et Ingenua et Serenus filii\n
EDCS-11301119.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Valentinia Festa, who died at the age of 50, and her descendants. Serena, her sister, and Ingenua and Serenus, her children.
EDCS-11301119.summary: A tomb inscription for Valentinia Festa and her family, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301121.text_edition: \n Isidi Augustae sacrum Vol Maxima ex visu votum solvit libens laeta merito\n
EDCS-11301121.translation: Sacred to Isis Augusta; Vol... Maxima fulfilled her vow willingly, joyfully, and deservedly, as revealed in a vision.
EDCS-11301121.summary: A votive inscription to the goddess Isis Augusta by a woman named Vol... Maxima, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301126.text_edition: \n Titus Tullius Titi filius Pupinia Tergeste Tertius annorum LV hic situs est\n
EDCS-11301126.translation: Titus Tullius, son of Titus, of the Pupinia tribe, from Tergeste, lies here at the age of 55.
EDCS-11301126.summary: A tomb inscription for Titus Tullius, recording his name, age, and origin, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301130.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae sacrum Ser Felix et Valerius Proculus milites legionis I adiutricis votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-11301130.translation: Sacred to Nemesis Augusta; Ser... Felix and Valerius Proculus, soldiers of the I Adiutrix legion, fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-11301130.summary: A votive inscription to Nemesis Augusta by two soldiers of the I Adiutrix legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301131.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto res publica Iasorum\n
EDCS-11301131.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, from the public administration of the Iasi.
EDCS-11301131.summary: An official dedication to Emperor Gordian III by the community of Iasi, likely in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-11301132.text_edition: \n Sabiniae Tranquillinae Augustae res publica Iasorum\n
EDCS-11301132.translation: To Sabinia Tranquillina Augusta, from the public administration of the Iasi.
EDCS-11301132.summary: An official dedication to Empress Sabina Tranquillina by the community of Iasi, likely during the reign of Gordian III.

EDCS-11301134.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ursioni et Sirmiae Potamilia Proba fratri et sorori pientissimis et Oginiae matri\n
EDCS-11301134.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Ursio and Siria, most dutiful brother and sister, and for Oginia, their mother.
EDCS-11301134.summary: A tomb inscription honoring Ursio, Siria, and their mother Oginia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301137.text_edition: \n Imperatoris Caesaris Cai Iuli Veri Maximini Pii Felicis Augusti pontificis maximi tribunicia potestate consulis patris patriae proconsulis a Siscia milia passuum et Cai Iuli Veri Maximi nobilissimi Caesaris a Siscia XXX\n
EDCS-11301137.translation: From Siscia, [XX] miles, and from Siscia, 30 miles, for the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul, and for Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-11301137.summary: An inscription likely marking distances from Siscia, dedicated to Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Maximinus, dating to 236-238 AD.

EDCS-11301623.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae Lucius MaC Celer veteranus legionis I ex beneficiario tribuni\n
EDCS-11301623.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno, and Minerva, Lucius MaC Celer, a veteran of the First Legion, from the staff of a tribune.
EDCS-11301623.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva by a veteran of the Legio I, who served as an assistant to a tribune.

EDCS-11301631.text_edition: \n Tu qui hic oras Iustiane in mente habe nnm matrem tuam \n
EDCS-11301631.translation: You who pray here, Iustianus, keep in mind your mother.
EDCS-11301631.summary: This is a personal admonition inscribed for the benefit of Iustianus, likely near a place of prayer or worship.

EDCS-11301641.text_edition: \n Deae Corypheae sive Caelesti Augustae Marcus Aurelius Alexander beneficiarius consularis legionis X geminae votum solvit libens merito Lupo et Maximo consulibus\n
EDCS-11301641.translation: To the goddess Coryphaea, also known as Caelestis Augusta, Marcus Aurelius Alexander, an assistant to the consular governor of the Tenth Gemina Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Lupinus and Maximus.
EDCS-11301641.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Caelestis Augusta by an official of the Legio X Gemina, dated to the consulship of Lupinus and Maximus.

EDCS-11301642.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Valerius Avitus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301642.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Valerius Avitus, an assistant to the consular governor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11301642.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest by an assistant to a consular governor, Gaius Valerius Avitus.

EDCS-11301643.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Lucius Septimius Avitus miles legionis X geminae beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-11301643.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the Genius of the place, Lucius Septimius Avitus, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina Legion, an assistant to the consular governor, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11301643.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter and the local Genius was made by Lucius Septimius Avitus, a soldier and assistant to the consular governor of the Legio X Gemina.

EDCS-11301644.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci pro salute sua et suis sic Marcus Aurelius Zenas beneficiarius consularis legionis XI<V> geminae Martiae victricis votum ob v q solvit libens merito Severo et Quintiano consulibus\n
EDCS-11301644.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and the Genius of the place, for his own safety and that of his family, Marcus Aurelius Zenas, an assistant to the consular governor of the Legio XI *Geminan Martia Victrix*, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in the consulship of Severus and Quintianus.
EDCS-11301644.summary: This votive inscription for the health of himself and his family was dedicated to Jupiter and the local Genius by Marcus Aurelius Zenas, an official of the Legio XI Gemina Martia Victrix, dated to the consulship of Severus and Quintianus.

EDCS-11301646.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori et Genio loci ZoticIus Basilei Auggustorum nnostrorum vilici vikarius sic servus eiusdem votum solvit libens merito M ANN L P P KA \n
EDCS-11301646.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Expeller, and the Genius of the place, Zotius Basileus, steward and agent for our Emperors, and his slave, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow. He lived 50 years.
EDCS-11301646.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter and the local Genius by Zotius Basileus, a steward and agent for the emperors, and his slave, dating from an imperial period.

EDCS-11301659.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo IN Rufinus E SVR servus E- p p votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301659.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, IN Rufinus, a slave, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-11301659.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Best and Greatest by a slave named Rufinus.

EDCS-11301661.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Metilius Iustianus votum solvit pro salute sua votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-11301661.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, Metilius Iustianus willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own health.
EDCS-11301661.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mithras by Metilius Iustianus, dedicated for his own health.

EDCS-11301662.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae L O N Marcianus votum reddidit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-11301662.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, Marcianus willingly, joyfully, and deservedly rendered his vow.
EDCS-11301662.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Unconquered god Mithras by Marcianus.

EDCS-11301666.text_edition: \n Vettoniano et Silvano? co(n)s(ulibus) Caius Mullonius Verus veteranus legionis X geminae fecit sibi suisque vivus ad nomen suum subiecit Mulloni et Secundiani fili sui @(obiti) equitis praetoriani stipendiorum VIII annorum XXV\n
EDCS-11301666.translation: During the consulship of Vettonianus and Silvanus, Gaius Mullonius Verus, a veteran of the Legio X Gemina, made this for himself and his family, having placed his name while alive beneath: Mullonius and Secundianus, his son, who died as a praetorian cavalryman after 8 years of service and 25 years of age.
EDCS-11301666.summary: This is a funerary inscription by a veteran of the Legio X Gemina, Gaius Mullonius Verus, for himself and his family, mentioning his son.

EDCS-11301669.text_edition: \n Adnamo Canonis filius annorum LXX hic situs est Sennemetomara Germani coniunx annorum EX I filia ? posierunt sic\n
EDCS-11301669.translation: Adnamus, son of Canon, aged 70, is buried here. Sennemetomara, his wife, aged 20, and a daughter, placed this.
EDCS-11301669.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Adnamus and notes that his wife and daughter erected the monument.

EDCS-11301670.text_edition: \n LEI Apti filiae viva coniugi posuit per heredes Aviti\n
EDCS-11301670.translation: Lei placed this for his living wife, Apti, through the heirs of Avitus.
EDCS-11301670.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Apti, erected by her husband Lei and his heirs.

EDCS-11301671.text_edition: \n Luci Baibi sic Semproni Escingai sic coniugis suai sic\n
EDCS-11301671.translation: Lucius Baibus, Sempronius Escingai, his wife.
EDCS-11301671.summary: This very brief inscription likely marks the burial place of Lucius Baibus and his wife Sempronia Escingai.

EDCS-11301672.text_edition: \n Tiberius? Claudius mat annorum L hic situs est sibi et Claudiae Tiberi filiae Conertae uxori testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-11301672.translation: Tiberius Claudius, aged 50, is buried here; he ordered this to be made by his will for himself and for Claudia Conerta, his wife, daughter of Tiberius.
EDCS-11301672.summary: This funerary inscription states that Tiberius Claudius was buried here and had it constructed by will for himself and his wife.

EDCS-11301674.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Marcius Respectus vivus fecit sibi et Marcia Satullaliae coniugi pientissimae annorum L\n
EDCS-11301674.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Marcius Respectus, while alive, made this for himself and for his most devoted wife, Marcia Satullalia, aged 50.
EDCS-11301674.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Titus Marcius Respectus, made while alive for himself and his devoted wife Marcia Satullalia.

EDCS-11301676.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus vivus fecit Secundus Victoris et Locitae coniuge sic suae an annorum XXX\n
EDCS-11301676.translation: To the Divine Manes. Secundus, son of Victor, made this while alive for himself and his wife Locita, aged 30.
EDCS-11301676.summary: This funerary inscription records Secundus, son of Victor, dedicating a monument while alive for himself and his wife.

EDCS-11301677.text_edition: \n Sirus Brogimari filius vivus fecit sibi et Matugnatae Admatonis nnnorum XXXX\n
EDCS-11301677.translation: Sirus, son of Brogimarus, made this while alive for himself and Matugnata, daughter of Admaton, aged 40.
EDCS-11301677.summary: This is a funerary epitaph by Sirus, son of Brogimarus, dedicated while alive to himself and Matugnata, daughter of Admaton.

EDCS-11301678.text_edition: \n Togivepus Optati filius et Melissa Cai vivi fecerunt sibi et Sperato filio annorum XVIII\n
EDCS-11301678.translation: Togivepus, son of Optatus, and Melissa, daughter of Caius, made this while alive for themselves and for their son, Speratus, aged 18.
EDCS-11301678.summary: This funerary inscription was made by Togivepus, son of Optatus, and Melissa, daughter of Caius, for themselves and their son Speratus.

EDCS-11301680.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, likely the beginning of a funerary epitaph, possibly for someone named Ovisca.

EDCS-11301697.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Carminius Maturus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301697.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Gaius Carminius Maturus, benefiting from the rank of consular legate, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-11301697.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a soldier, Gaius Carminius Maturus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301698.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Peregrinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301698.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Tiberius Claudius Peregrinus, benefiting from the rank of consular legate, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-11301698.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a soldier, Tiberius Claudius Peregrinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301699.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Clodius Priscus beneficiarius legati consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-11301699.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Lucius Clodius Priscus, benefiting from the rank of the consular legate, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-11301699.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a soldier, Lucius Clodius Priscus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301700.text_edition: \n Tertullo et Clemente consulibus Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Augusti Marcus Victorius Victorinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit\n
EDCS-11301700.translation: During the consulship of Tertullus and Clemens, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the health of the Emperor, Marcus Victorius Victorinus, benefiting from the rank of consular legate, paid his vow.
EDCS-11301700.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the Emperor's health, made by Marcus Victorius Victorinus, a soldier, during the consulship of Tertullus and Clemens.

EDCS-11301701.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Candidius Ursus beneficiarius consularis legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens merito [[]] [[]] Idibus Octo<b=S>ribus \n
EDCS-11301701.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, Candidius Ursus, benefiting from the rank of consular legate of the Legio XIIII Gemina, willingly and deservedly paid his vow on the Ides of October.
EDCS-11301701.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place by Candidius Ursus, a soldier of Legio XIIII Gemina, dated to the Ides of October.

EDCS-11301702.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Lucius Varius Surianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito Sabino et Venusto consulibus die III Kalendas Octobres\n
EDCS-11301702.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, Lucius Varius Surianus, benefiting from the rank of consular legate, willingly and deservedly paid his vow on the third day before the Kalends of October, during the consulship of Sabinus and Venustus.
EDCS-11301702.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place by Lucius Varius Surianus, a soldier, dated to the third day before the Kalends of October during the consulship of Sabinus and Venustus.

EDCS-11301704.translation: To Nature, Vius Exoratus, from a vow.
EDCS-11301704.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Nature by Vius Exoratus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301709.text_edition: \n pro salute Tertullianus Augusti nostri vilicus cum C<hr=RH>estene coniuge sua cum suis omnibus votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-11301709.translation: For the health of our Augustus, Tertullianus, the estate manager, with his wife C(h)restene and all his family, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-11301709.summary: This is a votive inscription for the health of the Emperor by Tertullianus, an estate manager, with his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301713.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Valerianus vivus fecit Apuleiae Marcellae uxori obitae annorum LXV et filiis suis vivis\n
EDCS-11301713.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Julius Valerianus made this tombstone while alive for his wife Apuleia Marcella, who died at the age of 65, and for his living sons.
EDCS-11301713.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Apuleia Marcella, set up by her husband Julius Valerianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301714.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Cai filiae cundae \n
EDCS-11301714.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, Iulia, daughter of Caius, beloved.
EDCS-11301714.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia, daughter of Caius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11301715.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio M Belliciano patri et Sabinae matri et Bellicinae \n
EDCS-11301715.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Lucius M. Bellicianus, my father, and Sabina, my mother, and Bellicina.
EDCS-11301715.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the deceased parents of the dedicator, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11500003.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of the deceased, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11800664.text_edition: \n Marcus Aemilius Quinti filius Aniensis Ursio Valeria Prima Quintus Acilius Publi filius Sergia Fuscus gener et Aemilia \n
EDCS-11800664.translation: Marcus Aemilius Ursio, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe; Valeria Prima; Quintus Acilius Fuscus, son of Publius, of the Sergia tribe, son-in-law; and Aemilia.
EDCS-11800664.summary: This is a family tomb inscription, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period, listing several individuals associated with the tomb.

EDCS-12000844.text_edition: \n Valerianus immunis tubularius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-12000844.translation: Valerianus, an immunis (soldier exempt from duties), a trumpeter, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-12000844.summary: This is a dedication by Valerianus, an immunis tubicen (trumpeter), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-12100053.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12100053.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filius divi Commodi frater divi Antonini Pii nepos divi Hadriani pronepos divi Traiani Parthici abnepos divi Nervae adnepos Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIIII imperator XIII consul III pater patriae proconsul Imperator Caesar Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filius divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepos divi Antonini Pii pronepos divi Hadriani abnepos divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepos Marcus Aurellius Antoninus Pius Augustus tribunicia potestate VIIII consul II proconsul nomina militum qui militaverunt in cohortibus praetoriis decem I II III <IV> V VI VII VIII VIIII X piis vindicibus qui pie et fortiter militia functi sunt ius <c>onubi tribuimus dumtaxat singulis et primis uxoribus ut etiamsi peregrini iuris feminas in matrimonio suo iunxerint proinde liberos tollant ac si ex duobus civibus Romanis natos ante diem VIII Kalendas Martias Marco Nummio Albino et Lucio Fulvio Aemiliano consulibus cohors VII praetoria pia vindex Caio Iulio Cai filio Papiria Martino Poetovione descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici filius divi <C=I>ommodi frater divi Antonini Pii nepos divi Hadriani pronepos divi Traiani Parthici abnepos divi Nervae adnepos Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIIII imperator XIII consul III pater patriae proconsul Imperator Caesar Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filius divi Marci Antonini Pii Germanici Sarmatici nepos divi Antonini Pii pronepos divi Hadriani abnepos divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepos Marcus Aurellius Antoninus Pius Augustus tribunicia potestate VIIII consul II proconsul nomina militum qui militaverunt in cohortibus praetoriis decem I II III IIII V VI VII VIII VIIII X piis vindicibus qui pie et fortiteor militia functi sunt ius <c>onubii tribuimus dumtaxat singulis et primis uxoribus ut etiamsi peregrini iuris feminas in matrimonio suo NIO iunxerint proinde liberos tollant a<c=I> si ex duobus <c=I>ivibus Romanis natos ------ ante diem VII]I Kalendas Martias Marco Nummio Albino et Lucio Fulvio Aemiliano consulibus cohortis VII praetoriae piae vindicis Caio Iulio Cai filio Papiria Martino Poetovione descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Publi Aeli Victoris Marci Ianuari Iuventi Marci Quinti Luci Cai Iuli Optatini Cai Iuli Fortunati Primi Iuniani \n
EDCS-12100053.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, son of the deified Antoninus Pius, brother of the deified Commodus, nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 14th time, Imperator for the 13th time, Consul for the 3rd time, father of the fatherland, proconsul. Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, nephew of the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-nephew of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-nephew of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-nephew of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, with tribunician power for the 9th time, Consul for the 2nd time, proconsul. We grant the right of marriage to the soldiers who served in the ten praetorian cohorts, the pious defenders, who faithfully and bravely completed their service, to themselves and their first wives, so that even if they have married women of non-Roman status, they shall have children as if born of two Roman citizens. [Recorded] on the 8th day before the Kalends of March, in the consulship of Marcus Nummius Albinus and Lucius Fulvius Aemilianus. The 7th praetorian cohort, the pious defender, for Gaius Julius Martinus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, from Poetovio. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the wall behind the Temple of the deified Augustus, near the Minerva.
EDCS-12100053.summary: This is an imperial rescript, likely issued by Septimius Severus and Caracalla, granting soldiers of the praetorian guard the right to marry, issued in 205 AD.

EDCS-12300209.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300209.translation: Nero Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Claudius, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the deified Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 7th time, Imperator for the 7th time, Consul for the 4th time, granted citizenship and the right of marriage with the wives they then had (or, if they were unmarried, with those they would later marry, only one each) to the infantry and cavalry serving in the 7 cohorts (named: I of the Asturians and Callaecians, I of the Spaniards, I of the Alpines, I of the Lusitanians, II of the Alpines, II of the Spaniards, and V of the Lucensians and Callaecians), who are in Illyricum under Lucius Salvidienus Rufus, and who had completed twenty or more years of service. Their names are listed below. [Recorded] on the 6th day before the Nones of July, in the consulship of Gnaeus Pedanius Salinator and Lucius Velleius Paterculus. For the 2nd Cohort of Spaniards, commanded by Gaius Cassius Aper, for the cavalryman Iantumarus, son of Andeduni, of the Varci tribe. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed on the Capitoline Hill, on the left side of the aedes of the chariots, on the exterior.
EDCS-12300209.summary: This is an imperial grant of citizenship and connubium (right of legal marriage) by Emperor Nero to auxiliary soldiers who had completed 20 years of service, issued in 65 AD.

EDCS-12300224.translation: Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 5th time, Imperator for the 13th time, father of the fatherland, Consul for the 5th time, designated Consul for the 6th time, censor. Granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the cavalry and infantry serving in six alae and twelve cohorts (named: I Flavia Gemina, I Cannenefatium, II Flavia Gemina, Picentiana, Scubulorum, Claudia Nova, I Thracum, I Asturum, I Aquitanorum veterana, I Aquitanorum Biturigum, II Augusta Cyrenaica, III Gallorum, III and IIII Aquitanorum, IIII Vindelicorum, V Hispanorum, V Dalmatarum, VII Raetorum), who are in Germania under Gnaeus Pinarius Cornelius Clemens, and who had completed twenty or more years of service. Their names are listed below. [Recorded] on the 12th day before the Kalends of June, in the consulship of Quintus Petillius Cerialis Caesius Rufus (II) and Titus Clodius Eprius Marcellus (II). For the Ala Scubulorum, commanded by Tiberius Claudius Atticus, son of Spurius, for the ranker Veturio, son of Teutomis, from Pannonia. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitoline Hill, entering on the left, on the wall between two arches.
EDCS-12300224.summary: This is an imperial grant of citizenship and connubium by Emperor Vespasian to auxiliary soldiers who had completed 20 years of service, issued in 74 AD.

EDCS-12300230.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300230.translation: Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasianus Augustus, son of the deified Vespasian, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 9th time, Imperator for the 15th time, father of the fatherland, censor, Consul for the 8th time. Granted citizenship and the right of marriage to those who served as cavalry and infantry in four alae and thirteen cohorts (named: I Aravacorum, I of Roman Citizens, II Aravacorum Frontoniana, I Alpinorum, I Montanorum, I Noricorum, I Lepidiana, I Augusta Ituraeorum, II Lucensium, I Alpinorum, I Britannica, II Asturum et Callaecorum, II Hispanorum, III Thracum, V Breucorum, VIII Raetorum), who are in Pannonia under Titus Atilius Rufus, having completed twenty or more years of service and been honorably discharged; likewise to those serving in two alae (I of Roman Citizens and II Aravacorum) and the 8th Cohort of Raetorum, who are under the same commander and have completed twenty or more years of service. Their names are listed below. [Recorded] on the Ides of June, in the consulship of Lucius Lamia Plautius Aelianus, [Gaius Marius] Marcellus Octavius Publius Cluvius Rufus. For the 1st Cohort of Montanorum, commanded by [Sextus] Nerianus Clemens, son of Sextus, of the Clustumina tribe, from the infantry, for Soio, son of Muscellus, from Bessos. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitoline Hill, behind the Temple of Fides Publica, on the wall.
EDCS-12300230.summary: This is an imperial grant of citizenship and connubium by Emperor Titus to auxiliary soldiers serving in Pannonia, issued in 80 AD.

EDCS-12300234.translation: Emperor Caesar Domitianus Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Vespasian, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, Imperator for the 7th time, father of the fatherland, Consul for the 10th time. Granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the cavalry and infantry serving in five alae and thirteen cohorts (named: I Civium Romanorum, I and II Aravacorum, Frontoniana, Siliana, I Noricorum, I Britannica, I Montanorum, I Lusitanorum, I and I and II Alpinorum, II Hispanorum, III Thracum, V Gallorum, V Callaecorum Lucensium, VI Thracum, and VIII Raetorum), who are in Pannonia under Lucius Funisulanus Vettonianus, and who had completed twenty or more years of service. Their names are listed below. [Recorded] on the 3rd day before the Nones of September, in the consulship of Gaius Tullius Capito Pomponianus Plotus Firmus and Gaius Cornelius Gallicanus. For the 1st Cohort of Montanorum, commanded by Nipius Aquila, for the foot soldier Dasios, son of Dasentis, from Dalmatia. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitoline Hill, behind the old treasury.
EDCS-12300234.summary: This is an imperial grant of citizenship and connubium by Emperor Domitian to auxiliary soldiers serving in Pannonia, issued in 84 AD.

EDCS-12300235.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300235.translation: Emperor Caesar Domitianus Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Vespasian, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, Imperator for the 9th time, Consul for the 11th time, with censorian power, father of the fatherland. Granted citizenship and the right of marriage to the cavalry and infantry serving in six alae and fifteen cohorts (named: I Civium Romanorum, I and II Aravacorum, Frontoniana, Praetoria, Siliana, I and I Montanorum, I Noricorum, I Britannica milliaria, I Brittonum milliaria, I and I Alpinorum, I Lusitanorum, II Asturum et Callaecorum Lucensium, II Hispanorum, V Gallorum, VI Thracum, VII Breucorum, and VIII Raetorum), who are in Pannonia under Lucius Funisulanus Vettonianus, having completed twenty or more years of service and been honorably discharged. Their names are listed below. [Recorded] on the Nones of September, in the consulship of Decimus Aburius Bassus and Quintus Julius Balbus. For the 1st Cohort of Lusitanorum, commanded by Gaius Cisso Honoratus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, for the foot soldier Fronton, son of Scenius, from Iaso. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the Capitoline Hill, on the back of the base of the column, which is next to Jupiter Africus.
EDCS-12300235.summary: This is an imperial grant of citizenship and connubium by Emperor Domitian to auxiliary soldiers serving in Pannonia, issued in 85 AD.

EDCS-12300250.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300250.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII pater patriae consul III equitibus et peditibus qui militant in alis tribus et cohortibus viginti et una quae appellantur praetoria et I Claudia nova et II Pannoniorum et I Flavia Bessorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et I Flavia Hispanorum |(miliaria) et I Antiochensium et I Lusitanorum et I Montanorum civium Romanorum et I Cisipadensium et I Cretum et I Vindelicorum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum et I Thracum Syriaca et I Cilicum et II Hispanorum et II Gallorum Macedonica et II Brittonum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum pia fidelis et II Flavia Commagenorum et III Brittonum et IIII Raetorum et V Gallorum et V Hispanorum et VI Thracum et VII Breucorum civium Romanorum et sunt in Moesia superiore sub Caio Cilnio Proculo item dimissis honesta missione qui quina et vicena plurave stipendia meruerunt quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut siqui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VIII Idus Maias Tito Pomponio Mamiliano Lucio Herennio Saturnino consulibus cohortis I Antiochensium cui praeest Marcus Calpurnius Sabinus pediti Sapiae Sarmosi filio Anazarbo descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator <C=T>aesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus <p=F>ontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII pater patriae consul III equitibus et peditibus qui militant in alis tribus et cohortibus viginti et una quae appellantur <p=I>raetoria et I Claudia nova et II Pannoniorum et I F<l=I>avia Bessorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et I F<l=I>av<i=L>a Hispanorum |(miliaria) et I Antiochensium et I Lusitanorum et I Mo<n=V>tanorum civium Romanorum et I Cisipadensium et I Cretum et I Vindelicorum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum et I Thracum Siriaca sic et I Cilicum et II Hispanorum et II Gallorum Macedonica et II Brittonum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum pia fidelis et II Flavia Commagenorum et III Brittonum et IIII Ra<e=F>torum et V Gallorum et V Hispanorum et VI Thracum et VII Breucorum <c=F>(ivium) Romanorum et sunt in Moesia superiore sub Caio Cilnio Proculo item dimissis honesta m<i=T>ss<i=T>one qui quina et v<i=T>cena plurave st<i=T>pend<i=T>a meruerunt quorum nom<i=T>na subscr<i=T>pta sunt ips<i=T>s l<i=T>beris posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis da<t=I>a aut siqui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat s<i=T>nguli singulas ante diem VIII Idus Maias Tito Pomponio Mamiliano Herennio Saturnino consulibus cohortis I Antiochensium cui praeest Marcus Calpurnius Sabinus pediti Sapiae Sarmosi filio Anazarbo descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Quinti Pompei Homeri Auli Ampi Epaphroditi Tiberi Claudi Vitalis Cai Iuli Aprilis Cai Vettieni Modesti Luci Pulli Verecundi Luci Pulli Sperati \n
EDCS-12300250.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the fourth time, father of the country, consul for the third time, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen serving in three alae and twenty-one cohorts named Praetoria, I Claudia Nova, II Pannoniorum, I Flavia Bessorum, I Thracum (of Roman citizens), I Flavia Hispanorum (miliaria), I Antiochensium, I Lusitanorum, I Montanorum (of Roman citizens), I Cisipadensium, I Cretum, I Vindelicorum (miliaria) (of Roman citizens), I Thracum Syriaca, I Cilicum, II Hispanorum, II Gallorum Macedonica, II Brittonum (miliaria) (of Roman citizens), loyal and faithful, II Flavia Commagenorum, III Brittonum, IIII Raetorum, V Gallorum, V Hispanorum, VI Thracum, and VII Breucorum (of Roman citizens), who are in Upper Moesia under Gaius Cilnius Proculus, and also to those discharged with honorable release who have completed twenty or more years of service, whose names are listed below, he granted citizenship and the right to marry to them, their children, and their descendants, with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman. This was decreed on the eighth day before the Ides of May, with Titus Pomponius Mamilianus and Lucius Herennius Saturninus as consuls. For the cohort I Antiochensium, commanded by Marcus Calpurnius Sabinus, to the infantryman Sapiae, son of Saramos, from Anazarbus. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed at Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near the Minervam.
EDCS-12300250.summary: This is an imperial grant of Roman citizenship and marriage rights by Emperor Trajan to auxiliary soldiers who had completed 20 years of service, dated to 105 AD. It lists many of the auxiliary units serving in Upper Moesia and names the commanding officer of one cohort and a recipient of the grant.

EDCS-12300261.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300261.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the fourteenth time, emperor for the sixth time, consul for the fifth time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in two alae and ten cohorts named I (of Roman citizens), I Augusta Ituraeorum, I Augusta Ituraeorum sagittariorum, I Britannica (miliaria) (of Roman citizens), I Hispanorum Pia Fidelis, I Thracum (of Roman citizens), I Ituraeorum, I Flavia Ulpia Hispanorum (miliaria) (of Roman citizens), II Gallorum Macedonica, III Campestris (of Roman citizens), IIII Cypria (of Roman citizens), VIII Raetorum (of Roman citizens), and the singular auxiliary infantrymen from Britain, who are in Dacia under Decimus Terentius Scaurianus, having completed twenty or more years of service and discharged with honorable release, whose names are listed below, he granted citizenship and the right to marry to them, their children, and their descendants, with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman. This was decreed on the thirteenth day before the Kalends of March, with Servius Scipio Salvidienus Orfitus and Marcus Peducaeus Priscus as consuls. For the ala I Augusta Ituraeorum, commanded by Gaius Vettius Priscus, to the auxiliary soldier Thaemo, son of Horatius, from Ituraea, and his son Nal, and his son Marcus, and his son Antonius. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed at Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near the Minervam.
EDCS-12300261.summary: This is an imperial grant of Roman citizenship and marriage rights by Emperor Trajan to auxiliary soldiers who had completed 20 years of service, dated to 111 AD. It lists many of the auxiliary units serving in Dacia and names the commander of one ala and a recipient of the grant.

EDCS-12300265.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300265.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Trajan Optimus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the eighteenth time, emperor for the seventh time, consul for the sixth time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in two alae and six cohorts named I Flavia Gaetulorum et Frontoniana, I Alpinorum, I Montanorum, I Alpinorum, I Lusitanorum, and II Augusta Nervia Pacensis (miliaria) Brittonum, and III Lusitanorum, and also the ala I Flavia Augusta Britannica (miliaria) (of Roman citizens), sent on campaign, having completed twenty or more years of service and discharged with honorable release, whose names are listed below, he granted citizenship and the right to marry to them, their children, and their descendants, with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman. This was decreed on the Kalends of September, with Lucius Lollius Avitus and Lucius Messius Rusticus as consuls. For the ala Frontoniana, commanded by Lucius Calpurnius Honoratus, to the auxiliary soldier Nertomarus, son of Irducissa, a Boian, and his wife Custae, daughter of Magnus, from Aquincum, and his son Victor, and his son Propinquus, and his daughter Bellae. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed at Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near the Minervam.
EDCS-12300265.summary: This is an imperial grant of Roman citizenship and marriage rights by Emperor Trajan to auxiliary soldiers who had completed 20 years of service, dated to 114 AD. It lists auxiliary units serving in Lower Pannonia and names the commander of one ala and a recipient of the grant.

EDCS-12300273.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300273.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI consul III proconsul equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis decem et tribus et cohortibus triginta et septem quae appellantur I nnum Sabiniana et I nniorum Tampiana et I Hispanorum Asturum et I Tungrorum et II Asturum et Gallorum Picentiana et Gallorum et Thracum classiana civium Romanorum et Gallorum Petriana |(miliaria) civium Romanorum et Gallorum Sebosiana et Vettonum Hispanorum civium Romanorum et Agrippiana Miniata et Augusta Gallorum et Augusta Vocontiorum civium Romanorum et I Nervia Germanorum |(miliaria) et I Celtiberorum et I Thracum et I Afrorum civium Romanorum et I Lingonum et I fida Vardullorum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum et I Frisiavonum et I Vangionum |(miliaria) et I Hamiorum sagittaria et I Delmatarum et I Aquitanorum et I Ulpia Traiana Cugernorum civium Romanorum et I Morinorum et I Menapiorum et I Sunucorum et I Baetasiorum et I Batavorum et I Tungrorum et I Hispanorum et II Gallorum et II Vasconum civium Romanorum et II Thracum et II Lingonum et II Asturum et II Delmatarum et II Nerviorum et III Nerviorum et III Bracarorum et III Lingonum et IIII Gallorum et IIII Breucorum et IIII Delmatarum et V Raetorum et V Gallorum et VI Nerviorum et VII Thracum quae sunt in Britannia sub Aulo Platorio Nepote quinque et viginti stipendis emeritis dimissis honesta missione per Pompeium Falconem quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis singulas ante diem XVI Kalendas Augustas Tiberio Iulio Capitone Lucio Vitrasio Flaminino consulibus alae I nnum Tampianae cui praeest Fabius Sabinus ex sesquiplicario Gemello Breuci filio nnio descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VI consul III procnsul equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis decem et tribus et cohortibus triginta et septem quae appellantur I nnm Sabiniana et I Pannoniorum Tampiana et I Hispanorum Asturum et I Tungrorum et II Asturum et Gallorum Picentiana et Gallorum et Thracum classiana civium Romanorum Sebosiana et Vettonum Hispanorum civium Romanorum et Agrippiana Miniata et Augusta Gallorum et Augusta Vocontiorum civium Romanorum et I Frisiavonum et I Vangionum milliaria et I Celtib<e=I>rorum et I Thracum et I Afrorum civium Romanorum et I Nervia Germanorum miliaria et I Hamionum sic sagittaria et I Delmatarum et I Aquitanorum et I Ulpia Traiana Cugernorum civium Romanorum et I Morinorum et I Menapiorum et I Sunucorum et I Betasiorum et I Batavorum et I Tungrorum et I Hispanorum et II Gallorum et II Basconum sic civium Romanorum et II Thracum et II Lingonum et II Asturum et II Delmatarum et II Nerviorum et III Bracarorum et III Lingonum et IV Gallorum et IV Lingonum et IV Breucorum et IV Delmatarum et V Raetorum et V Gallorum et VI Nerviorum et VII Thracum quae sunt in Brittannia sub Aulo Platorio nepote quinque et viginti stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione per Pompeium Falconem quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis singulas ante diem XVI Kalendas Augustas Tiberio Iulio Capitone Lucio Vitrasio Flaminino consulibus alae I Pannoniorum Tampianae cui praeest Fabius Sabinus ex sesquipliciario sic Gemello Breuci filio nnio Tiberi Claudi Menandri Auli Fulvi Iusti Tiberi Iuli Urbani Luci Pulli Daphni Luci Noni Victoris Quinti Lolli Festi Luci Pulli Anthi \n
EDCS-12300273.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 6th time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, granted citizenship and the right to marry to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in thirteen alae and thirty-seven cohorts, namely [list of cohorts and alae] and to their children and descendants, and to those who were unmarried at the time citizenship was granted, or to those whom they would subsequently marry, but only one wife each. This was done on the 16th day before the Kalends of August, when Tiberius Julius Capito and Lucius Vitrasius Flamininus were consuls. For the First Wing of the Pannonians Tampiana, commanded by Fabius Sabinus, from the rank of sesquiplicarius, Gemellus, son of Breucus, [and others]. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed in Rome on the wall behind the Temple of the deified Augustus, near the Temple of Minerva.
EDCS-12300273.summary: This is an imperial rescript issued by Emperor Hadrian granting Roman citizenship and the right of legal marriage to auxiliary soldiers and their descendants who served in various units in Britannia.

EDCS-12300300.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300300.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XI imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V et cohortibus VII et sunt in Pannonia superiore sub Pontio Laeliano XXV stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis cohortis I Ulpiae nnum cui praeest Aulus Baebius Regillus Suessa ex pedite Attae Nivionis filio Azalo Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XI imperator II consul IIII pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V et cohortibus VII quae appellantur I Ulpia contariorum |(milliaria) et I Thracum victrix et I Hispanorum Arvacorum et I Cannenefatium civium Romanorum et III Augusta Thracum sagittaria et I Ulpia nnoniorum et I Aelia sa<g=C>ittaria et XVIII voluntariorum civium Romanorum et IIII voluntariorum civium Romanorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et V Callaecorum Lucensium et II Alpinorum et sunt in nnonia superiore sub Pontio Laeliano quinque et viginti stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem VII Idus Octobres Caio Fabio A<g=C>rippino Marco Antonio Zenone consulibus cohortis I Ulpiae Pannoniorum cui praeest Aulus Baebius Re<g=C>illus Suessa ex pedite Attae Nivionis filio Azalo Descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aenea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Luci Pulli Daphni Marci Servili Getae Luci Pulli Chresimi Marci Sentili Iasi Tiberi Iuli Felicis Cai Iuli Silvani Publi Ocili Prisci \n
EDCS-12300300.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the eleventh time, emperor for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in five alae and seven cohorts, serving in Upper Pannonia under Pontius Laelianus, having completed twenty-five years of service and discharged with honorable release, he granted Roman citizenship to those among them who did not already have it, and the right to marry with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman. For cohort I Ulpia Pannoniorum, commanded by Aulus Baebius Regillus, to the infantryman Atta, son of Nivo, from Suessa. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed at Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near the Minervam.
EDCS-12300300.summary: This is an imperial grant of Roman citizenship and marriage rights by Emperor Antoninus Pius to auxiliary soldiers who had completed 25 years of service, dated to 148 AD. It mentions units serving in Upper Pannonia and names the commander of one cohort and a recipient.

EDCS-12300301.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300301.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the twelfth time, emperor for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in four alae and seven cohorts named I Thracum Victrix, I Cannenefatium (of Roman citizens), I Hispanorum Arvacorum, III Augusta Thracum, I Aelia (miliaria) sagittaria, I Ulpia Pannoniorum, I Thracum (of Roman citizens), II Alpinorum, IV voluntariorum (of Roman citizens), V Callaecorum Lucensium, and XIIX voluntariorum (of Roman citizens), serving in Upper Pannonia under Pontius Laelianus, having completed twenty-five years of service and discharged with honorable release, whose names are listed below, he granted Roman citizenship to those among them who did not already have it, and the right to marry with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman. This was decreed on the third day before the Nones of July, with Quintus Passienus Licinus and Gaius Iulius Avitus as consuls. For cohort V Callaecorum Lucensium, commanded by Titus Flavius Modestus, to the auxiliary soldier Dasmen, son of Festus, from Azalo. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed at Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near the Minervam.
EDCS-12300301.summary: This is an imperial grant of Roman citizenship and marriage rights by Emperor Antoninus Pius to auxiliary soldiers who had completed 25 years of service, dated to 149 AD. It lists auxiliary units serving in Upper Pannonia and names the commander of one cohort and a recipient.

EDCS-12300303.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300303.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIII imperator II consul IV pater patriae equitibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae appellantur I Hispanorum Aravacorum et III Augusta Thracum sagittaria quae sunt in nnonia superiore sub Claudio Maximo item I Flavia nnica |(miliaria) civium Romanorum et I Thracum veterana sagittaria et I Augusta Itureorum sagittaria quae sunt in Pannonia inferiore sub Cominio Secundo quinis et vicenis pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione per Porcium Vetustinum procuratorem cum essent in expeditione Mauretaniae Caesarensis quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas is data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis Kalendis Augustis Marco Cassio Apollinare Marco Petronio Mamertino consulibus alae I Hispanorum Aravacorum ex gregale Victori Liccai sic filio Azalo descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIII imperator II consul IV pater patriae equitibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae sunt in nnonia utrasque sic XXV pluribusve stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione per Porcium Vetustinum quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas is data aut cum is quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis Kalendis Augustis Apollinare et Mamertino consulibus alae I Aravacorum ex gregale Victori Liccai sic filio Azalo Marci Servili Getae Luci Pulli Chresimi Marci Sentili Iasi Tiberi Iuli Felicis Cai Iuli Silvani Luci Iulli Velocis ili Prisci\n
EDCS-12300303.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the thirteenth time, emperor for the second time, consul for the fourth time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen who served in five alae, namely I Hispanorum Aravacorum and III Augusta Thracum sagittaria, which are in Upper Pannonia under Claudius Maximus, and also I Flavia Britannica (miliaria) (of Roman citizens), I Thracum veteranorum sagittaria, and I Augusta Itureorum sagittaria, which are in Lower Pannonia under Cominius Secundus, having completed twenty or more years of service and discharged with honorable release through Porcius Vetustinus, the procurator, while they were on campaign in Mauretania Caesariensis, whose names are listed below, he granted Roman citizenship to those among them who did not already have it, and the right to marry with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman. This was decreed on the Kalends of August, with Marcus Cassius Apollinaris and Marcus Petronius Mamertinus as consuls. For ala I Hispanorum Aravacorum, from the auxiliary soldier Victor, son of Liccaeus, from Azalo. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed at Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near the Minervam.
EDCS-12300303.summary: This is an imperial grant of Roman citizenship and marriage rights by Emperor Antoninus Pius to auxiliary soldiers who had completed 20 or more years of service, dated to 150 AD. It specifies units serving in Pannonia Superior and Inferior and notes their participation in a campaign in Mauretania Caesariensis.

EDCS-12300368.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-12300368.translation: Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Nerva, Nerva Trajan Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, chief priest, holding tribunician power for the fourteenth time, emperor for the sixth time, consul for the fifth time, father of the country, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen serving in four alae and ten cohorts named I Flavia Augusta Britannica (miliaria) (of Roman citizens), Frontoniana, Siliana (of Roman citizens), Praetoria (of Roman citizens), I Lusitanorum, I Alpinorum, I Thracum Pia Fidelis, I Alpinorum, I Noricorum, I Montanorum, II Alpinorum, II Asturum, Callaecorum, VI Lusitanorum Pia Fidelis, V Gallorum, and a vexillation of cavalry from Syria, who are in Lower Pannonia under Titus Iulius Maximus Manlianus, having completed twenty or more years of service and also discharged with honorable release, whose names are listed below, he granted citizenship and the right to marry to them, their children, and their descendants, with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman. This was decreed on the sixth day before the Nones of July, with Gaius Erucianus Silo and Lucius Catilius Severus as consuls. For the ala Frontoniana, commanded by Lucius Calpurnius Honoratus, to the discharged decurion Gaius Petillius Vindicius, son of Gaius, a Batavian. Recorded and verified from a bronze tablet fixed at Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near the Minervam.
EDCS-12300368.summary: This is an imperial grant of Roman citizenship and marriage rights by Emperor Trajan to auxiliary soldiers who had completed 20 or more years of service, dated to 111 AD. It lists many auxiliary units serving in Lower Pannonia and names the commander of one ala and a recipient.

EDCS-12700083.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Mogetius servus saltuarius Flaviorum Victorini et Victoris votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-12700083.translation: To Silvanus, the woodland guardian Mogetius, a slave of the Flavii Victorinus and Victor, has freely fulfilled his vow in accordance with his merit.
EDCS-12700083.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Silvanus by a slave named Mogetius, likely from the late Roman Empire, thanking the deity for a favor received.

EDCS-12700084.text_edition: \n Titus Iunius Decimi filius Aniensis Montanus tribunus militum VI praefectus equitum VI praefectus fabrum II pro legato II\n
EDCS-12700084.translation: Titus Iunius Montanus, son of Decimus, of the Aniensis tribe, military tribune six times, cavalry prefect six times, engineer prefect twice, and legate's deputy twice.
EDCS-12700084.summary: This is an epitaph recording the distinguished military career of Titus Iunius Montanus, likely from the 2nd century AD, listing his numerous commands and promotions.

EDCS-13302513.text_edition: \n Valerius Publi filius Claudia Alpinus Glanate miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXX stipendiorum X hic situs est Sextus Albius heres posuit agente curam Aulo Coelio secundo herede testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-13302513.translation: Valerius Alpinus, son of Publius, of the Glanate tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 30, with 10 years of service, lies here; Sextus Albius, the heir, erected this, having ordered it to be done by will, with Aulus Coelius Secundus as executor.
EDCS-13302513.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Valerius Alpinus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, with details of his life and burial arrangements, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13302521.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae pro salute officialium Apri praepositi leggionum V Macedonicae et XIII geminae Gallienarum\n
EDCS-13302521.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, for the well-being of Apri, the prefects of the 5th Macedonian and 13th Gemina Gallienae legions.
EDCS-13302521.summary: A dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras made by military officials, likely around the mid-3rd century CE, to ensure the well-being of the legionary prefects.

EDCS-13302523.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae pro salute domini nostri Gallieni Pii Felicis Invicti Augusti Flavius Aper vir egregius libens merito\n
EDCS-13302523.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, for the well-being of our lord Gallienus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus. Flavius Aper, an outstanding man, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-13302523.summary: A religious dedication to Sol Invictus Mithras offered by Flavius Aper for the welfare of the emperor Gallienus, likely from the reign of Gallienus (253-268 CE).

EDCS-13500274.text_edition: \n Quintus Lucanus Quinti filius Pollia Fronto miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXX stipendiorum XII hic situs est arbitratu Cai Herenni Cai fili Cornelia heredis\n
EDCS-13500274.translation: Quintus Lucanus Fronto, son of Quintus, of the Pollia tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 30, with 12 years of service, lies here; by the decision of Caius Herennius, son of Caius, of the Cornelia tribe, the heir.
EDCS-13500274.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Lucanus Fronto, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, detailing his age, service, and burial arrangements, probably from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-13600187.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Soli Ulpius Vitalis panchrestarius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-13600187.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God, Ulpius Vitalis, a healer, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-13600187.summary: A votive dedication to Sol Invictus made by Ulpius Vitalis, a 'panchrestarius' (a type of physician or healer), likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13600216.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Mansuetus veteranus ex praetorio ex speculatori sic vivus sibi et filiis posuit\n
EDCS-13600216.translation: Marcus Valerius Mansuetus, a veteran from the Praetorian Guard, formerly a spy, erected this for himself and his sons while alive.
EDCS-13600216.summary: A funerary monument set up by Marcus Valerius Mansuetus, a veteran of the Praetorian Guard and a speculator, for himself and his sons, possibly dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13600217.text_edition: \n Silvanis saqrum sic posuit Troucisa votum ex iussu libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-13600217.translation: Sacred to the Silvan deities. Troucisa set this up willingly, happily, and deservedly, by order.
EDCS-13600217.summary: A religious dedication to the Silvan deities (spirits of the forest and wild places) by Troucisa, who likely set it up following a divine command, possibly in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13600364.text_edition: \n abiniae fidelieli Marcio coniugi sanctae obitae hora V non digna mori si possint fata moveri\n
EDCS-13600364.translation: To Abinia, a most faithful wife, to Marcius, her husband, holy, who died at the fifth hour, undeserving of death, if fate could be turned.
EDCS-13600364.summary: A poignant epitaph for Abinia, a wife mourning her deceased husband Marcius, expressing grief and the unfairness of her death, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13600378.text_edition: \n pro salute Eudemi Auggustalis sic coloniae Carnuntesium Ulpia Secia Eudemilla ex voto Lupo et Maximo consulibus\n
EDCS-13600378.translation: For the well-being of Eudemus, an Augustalis, of the colony of Carnuntum. Ulpia Secia Eudemilla, by vow, when Lupo and Maximo were consuls.
EDCS-13600378.summary: A votive inscription for the well-being of Eudemus, an Augustalis (a prominent freedman or official), made by Ulpia Secia Eudemilla, dated by the consulship of Lupo and Maximo, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13600379.text_edition: \n Aureliae Tyche Aquileia vixit annos Marcus Aurelius St miles legionis XIIII immun[is ------\n
EDCS-13600379.translation: Aurelia Tyche of Aquileia lived for... Marcus Aurelius St..., a soldier of the 14th legion, immune...
EDCS-13600379.summary: A fragmented inscription likely recording the life and death of Aurelia Tyche and Marcus Aurelius St, a soldier of the 14th legion, with details of his status incomplete, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-13600381.text_edition: \n Nymphis Lucius Maticeius Clemens libens laetus posuit\n
EDCS-13600381.translation: Lucius Maticeius Clemens willingly and joyfully placed this for the Nymphs.
EDCS-13600381.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs by Lucius Maticeius Clemens.

EDCS-13600382.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri libens dedit Septimius Florus\n
EDCS-13600382.translation: Septimius Florus willingly gave this to Silvanus Silvestris.
EDCS-13600382.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Silvestris by Septimius Florus.

EDCS-13900088.text_edition: \n Cohors VII praetoria Cai Viseli centuria Nepotis\n
EDCS-13900088.translation: The Seventh Praetorian Cohort, of Nepos' century under Caius Viselius.
EDCS-13900088.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or property related to the Seventh Praetorian Cohort and a specific century and officer.

EDCS-13900488.text_edition: \n Marcus Cocceius Caupianus princeps civitatis Boiorum vivus fecit sibi et Cocceiae Dagovassae coniugi annorum LV\n
EDCS-13900488.translation: Marcus Cocceius Caupianus, a leader of the Boii people, made this while alive for himself and his wife Cocceia Dagovassa, aged 55.
EDCS-13900488.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Marcus Cocceius Caupianus for himself and his wife, dating to the Roman period based on names and context.

EDCS-13900489.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Faustino veterano legionis X geminae ex custode armorum\n
EDCS-13900489.translation: To the departed spirits of Iulius Faustinus, a veteran of the Legio X Gemina, formerly keeper of arms.
EDCS-13900489.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Iulius Faustinus, a veteran of the Tenth Legion, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900490.translation: As a beneficarius of the consular governor, for his mother.
EDCS-13900490.summary: This is a dedication made by a beneficarius to his mother.

EDCS-13900491.text_edition: \n Fortunae Auggustorum sacrum Caius Valerius Valerianus veteranus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-13900491.translation: Caius Valerius Valerianus, a veteran, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Fortuna Augusta.
EDCS-13900491.summary: This is a votive offering to Fortuna Augusta by Caius Valerius Valerianus, a veteran, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-13900492.text_edition: \n Nymfis Augustis pro salute Auggustorum Caius Valerius Valerianus et Flavia Victorina votum solverunt libentes laeti\n
EDCS-13900492.translation: Caius Valerius Valerianus and Flavia Victorina, willingly and joyfully fulfilled their vow for the safety of the August Emperors to the August Nymphs.
EDCS-13900492.summary: This is a joint votive offering to the Nymphs by Caius Valerius Valerianus and Flavia Victorina for the safety of the emperors, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400609.text_edition: \n Marti Acclo Quinti Proculei Finiti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14400609.translation: Quinti Proculi Finiti fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly to Mars Acclois.
EDCS-14400609.summary: This is a votive offering to Mars Acclois by Quinti Proculi Finiti, likely from a Roman provincial context.

EDCS-14400614.text_edition: \n Invicto Deo Mithrae Ialius Tertinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14400614.translation: Ialius Tertinus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly to the unconquered god Mithras.
EDCS-14400614.summary: This is a votive offering to the god Mithras by Ialius Tertinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-14400616.text_edition: \n Invicto Deo Mithrae Longinius Expertus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14400616.translation: Longinius Expertus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the unconquered god Mithras.
EDCS-14400616.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a fulfillment of a vow to the god Mithras by Longinius Expertus, likely from the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-14400618.text_edition: \n norinus V votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14400618.translation: Norinus V gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14400618.summary: This is a short votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by an individual named Norinus, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-14400619.summary: This is a fragmented votive inscription indicating that two individuals, possibly named ...us and Vil, fulfilled their vows.

EDCS-14400648.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Victor Secundus Marci Valeri Epricae pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14400648.translation: Victor Secundus, for himself and his family, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Marcius Valerius Epicras.
EDCS-14400648.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Marcius Valerius Epicras by Victor Secundus.

EDCS-14400649.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Rufi Speratinus et Speratianus filius (et) Speratus et Pro\n
EDCS-14400649.translation: Rufius Speratinus and his son Speratianus, and Speratus and Pro, [fulfilled their vows] to the unconquered god Mithras.
EDCS-14400649.summary: This is a fragmented votive inscription to the god Mithras, dedicated by Rufius Speratinus, his son Speratianus, and others.

EDCS-14400650.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Saxano Augusto Aurelius Aprilis Aurelius Ursulus Aurelius Angulatus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-14400650.translation: Aurelius Aprilis, Aurelius Ursulus, and Aurelius Angulatus gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vows to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Saxanus Augustus.
EDCS-14400650.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of vows to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and Saxanus Augustus by three individuals: Aurelius Aprilis, Aurelius Ursulus, and Aurelius Angulatus.

EDCS-14400651.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or record related to an individual named Avitus Secundus.

EDCS-14400677.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Lucius Annius Senecinus pro Lucio Annio Vero nepote votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14400677.translation: Lucius Annius Senecinus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the unconquered god Mithras for his grandson Lucius Annius Verus.
EDCS-14400677.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Lucius Annius Senecinus on behalf of his grandson, Lucius Annius Verus.

EDCS-14500603.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Kalandino sic annorum L veterano cohortis I Asturum tus libertus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500603.translation: To the divine spirits. Aurelius Kalandinus, aged 50, a veteran of the First Cohort of Asturians, had this set up by his freedman.
EDCS-14500603.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Kalandinus, a 50-year-old veteran of the First Cohort of Asturians, erected by his freedman.

EDCS-14500604.text_edition: \n annorum XLVII Lupianus duplicarius legionis X geminae exceptor principis praetorii gener et Septimia Potentina stolata femina filia parentibus faciendum curavit et sibi suisque\n
EDCS-14500604.translation: Lupianus, aged 47, a duplicarius of the Tenth Legion Gemina and an exceptor principis praetorii, and Septimia Potentina, a stolata woman, had this built by her parents and for themselves and their families.
EDCS-14500604.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lupianus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina, and Septimia Potentina, likely his wife, and was erected by her parents.

EDCS-14500605.text_edition: \n annorum XVIII et Iulio Ianuae filius annorum LXX Iulianus Iulionis annorum XXXV et et Avitae nepti annorum VIIII Iuli I I\n
EDCS-14500605.translation: Of the 18-year-old and Julianus son of Ianua, 70 years old, and Avita, niece, 9 years old, Iulius, I.
EDCS-14500605.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of several individuals, likely family members, without specifying the monument type or precise date.

EDCS-14500607.text_edition: \n onius Avitianus DAsuc MaximiI vivi fecerunt parentibus F\n
EDCS-14500607.translation: Onius Avitianus and Maximinus, while alive, made [this] for their parents.
EDCS-14500607.summary: This inscription records that Onius Avitianus and Maximinus commissioned a monument for their parents, likely a tomb or grave marker, while they were still alive.

EDCS-14500610.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius! Iulius Romanus et Verina coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Romulae filiae |(obitae) annorum XXX et filiis Romulo et Suriano\n
EDCS-14500610.translation: To the Spirits of the departed: Gaius Julius Romanus and his wife Verina, while alive, made [this] for themselves, for their daughter Romula, who died at the age of 30, and for their sons Romulus and Surianus.
EDCS-14500610.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Gaius Julius Romanus and his wife Verina dedicated a monument for themselves and their deceased children, Romula, Romulus, and Surianus.

EDCS-14500919.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lupus vilicus fecit Probino actori socerioni et Proba soror fratri @(obito) annorum XL et Ursae coniugi vivae faecaerunt sic\n
EDCS-14500919.translation: To the Spirits of the departed: Lupus, the steward, made [this] for Probinius, the actor and brother-in-law, and for Proba, his sister, and for Ursa, his wife, who died at the age of 40. They made [this] while alive.
EDCS-14500919.summary: This epitaph records that Lupus, a steward, commissioned a funerary monument for his brother-in-law Probinius, his sister Proba, and his wife Ursa.
