EDCS-14500981.text_edition: \n Belatullo Biraconis filio annorum LXX et Ategnatae Malsonis filiae coniugi annorum LX et Vercaio filio annorum XXXX et Livimae filiae annorum XXX et Claudiae Banonae annorum VII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-14500981.translation: For Belatullus, son of Biraco, 70 years old, and for his wife Ategnata, daughter of Malson, 60 years old, and for their son Vercaius, 40 years old, and for their daughter Livima, 30 years old, and for Claudia Banona, 7 years old, the heir had [this] made.
EDCS-14500981.summary: This funerary inscription lists the ages of deceased family members and states that the heir commissioned the monument for them.

EDCS-14500982.text_edition: \n Candidus Quinti Morsi Potentis Titiani servus sibi et Surillae coniugi optimae annorum XX defunctae\n
EDCS-14500982.translation: Candidus, slave of Quintus Mors Potentis Titianus, [made this] for himself and for his excellent wife Surilla, who died at the age of 20.
EDCS-14500982.summary: This funerary inscription records that Candidus, a slave, commissioned a monument for himself and his deceased wife Surilla.

EDCS-14501039.text_edition: \n Marcus Cornelius Marci libertus Senno nnrum LXX hic situs est patronus de suo faciundum curavit\n
EDCS-14501039.translation: Marcus Cornelius Senno, freedman of Marcus, 70 years old, lies here; his patron had [this] made at his own expense.
EDCS-14501039.summary: This epitaph records that Marcus Cornelius Senno, a freedman, is buried here, and his patron commissioned his tomb.

EDCS-14501040.text_edition: \n Caius Samuconius Spectatus et Amuca Burrani filia vivi fecerunt sibi et Respectillae filiae defunctae annorum XX\n
EDCS-14501040.translation: Caius Samuconius Spectatus and Amuca, daughter of Burranus, while alive, made [this] for themselves and for their deceased daughter Respectilla, 20 years old.
EDCS-14501040.summary: This funerary inscription records that Caius Samuconius Spectatus and Amuca commissioned a monument for themselves and their deceased daughter Respectilla.

EDCS-14501050.translation: For Masculus, son of Nonis, 30 years old.
EDCS-14501050.summary: This is a very brief funerary inscription recording the name and age of Masculus, son of Nonis.

EDCS-14501051.text_edition: \n Calvisio Calventi annorum LXX et Florentin \n
EDCS-14501051.translation: For Calvisius, 70 years old, and Florentinus.
EDCS-14501051.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or funerary record for two individuals, Calvisius and Florentinus, with the age of one specified.

EDCS-14501052.text_edition: \n Marcus Attius C veteranus legionis annorum LX Iulia S Marcus Iulius \n
EDCS-14501052.translation: Marcus Attius, a veteran of the legion, aged 60. Julia S., Marcus Julius.
EDCS-14501052.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Attius, a legionary veteran, and likely other individuals named Julia S. and Marcus Julius.

EDCS-14501053.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Optima, daughter of Lucanus.

EDCS-14501063.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Cai filius Voltinia Philippis miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorumXI \n
EDCS-14501063.translation: Gaius Valerius Philippis, son of Gaius, of the Voltinia tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 11.
EDCS-14501063.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Valerius Philippis, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion.

EDCS-14501065.text_edition: \n pro salute Augusti Quintus Attius Conerti filius Tertinus tesserarius cohortis I Aeliae sagittariorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-14501065.translation: For the safety of Augustus, Quintus Attius Tertinus, son of Conertus, a standard-bearer of the 1st Aelian Cohort of Archers, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-14501065.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Quintus Attius Tertinus for the safety of Augustus.

EDCS-14501066.text_edition: \n Cohors I Aelia Severiana equitata cui praeest Caius Aurelius Crescens tribunus Agricola et Clementino consulibus\n
EDCS-14501066.translation: The 1st Aelian Severiana Cohort, an auxiliary cavalry unit, commanded by Gaius Aurelius Crescens, a tribune, under consuls Agricola and Clementinus.
EDCS-14501066.summary: This inscription records the command structure and consuls associated with the 1st Aelian Severiana Cohort.

EDCS-14501068.translation: The work of the century of Quintus Aelius Valentis.
EDCS-14501068.summary: This inscription records a work or construction project associated with the century of Quintus Aelius Valentis.

EDCS-14600253.text_edition: \n domo Perintho Severo nnos XVII dies XI et Karae quae \n
EDCS-14600253.translation: From Perinthus, Severus, aged 17 years and 11 days, and Kara.
EDCS-14600253.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Severus, a youth from Perinthus, and Kara.

EDCS-14800174.text_edition: \n Marco Licinio Marci liberto domo Antiochia Agathonico nnorum L et Liciniae Fortunatae coniugi annorum XXV hic sita est et Liciniae coniugi annorum XL XL \n
EDCS-14800174.translation: Marcus Licinius Agathon, freedman of Marcus, from Antioch, aged 50, and Licinia Fortunata, his wife, aged 25. And Licinia, wife [of Marcus Licinius?], aged 40.
EDCS-14800174.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Licinius Agathon, his wife Licinia Fortunata, and another Licinia, likely a wife or relative.

EDCS-15000112.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Tarvenius Rufus cum suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15000112.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Gaius Tarvenius Rufus, with his family, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15000112.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter by Gaius Tarvenius Rufus and his family.

EDCS-15000113.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Valeria Luci filia Vitalis quae et Domnina votum solvit libens laeta merito\n
EDCS-15000113.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticius. Valeria Vitalis, daughter of Lucius, who is also called Domnina, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-15000113.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Silvanus Domesticius by Valeria Vitalis (Domnina).

EDCS-15700089.text_edition: \n Salvo Augusto aurea saecula videmus Honori\n
EDCS-15700089.translation: Under Salvius Augustus, we see a golden age.
EDCS-15700089.summary: This is a dedication from an unknown person to Salvius Augustus, likely dating to the early imperial period, celebrating a golden age.

EDCS-15700090.summary: This is a short inscription celebrating the victory of Augustus, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-15800410.text_edition: \n Festio Longini Iulli militis legionis XV Apollinaris filius hic situs est annorum V nutritus sine matre salivis suis spes et corona fuit patris sui Fatus et Fortuna iniquiter iudicavit pater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-15800410.translation: Festius, son of Longinus, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, is buried here. He was five years old, raised without his mother, and was the hope and crown of his own people. Fate and Fortune judged unfairly; his father had this made.
EDCS-15800410.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Festius, a five-year-old boy who was the son of a soldier, and was commissioned by his grieving father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800411.text_edition: \n Ana Garvonis filia annorum L hic sita est natione Aravisscam sic Curmisagius coniugi Turbo Vercondarius Adiaturix fili ex commune sic pecunia fecerunt\n
EDCS-15800411.translation: Ana, daughter of Garvo, fifty years old, is buried here; of the Aravissci nation. Thus Curmisagius, her husband, and Turbo, Adiaturix, and the sons made this from their shared property.
EDCS-15800411.summary: This funerary inscription records Ana, a woman of the Aravissci tribe, who is buried here and for whom her husband and sons commissioned the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800412.text_edition: \n Tudro Ariomani liberto annorum XL hic situs est\n
EDCS-15800412.translation: Tudro, freedman of Ariomanus, forty years old, is buried here.
EDCS-15800412.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Tudro, a freedman, who died at the age of forty, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15800414.text_edition: \n OIVmarae Cocceias sic Vitalina Nonnita et Fidentia sorores carissimas vivas mimoriam sic fecerunt\n
EDCS-15800414.translation: To OIVmara, Vitalina and Nonnita, and Fidentia, sisters, made this memorial while they were alive.
EDCS-15800414.summary: This funerary monument was created by sisters Vitalina, Nonnita, and Fidentia in memory of their sister OIVmara, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900110.text_edition: \n Publio Satellio Cai filio Scaptia Sodali negotiatori Quintus Varius Titi filius Scaptia Modestus avonculus sic posuit\n
EDCS-15900110.translation: To Publius Satellius, son of Caius, of the Scaptia tribe, a merchant; Quintus Varius Titi, of the Scaptia tribe, his great-uncle, placed this.
EDCS-15900110.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument to Publius Satellius, a merchant, by his great-uncle Quintus Varius Titi, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900111.text_edition: \n Caius Fabricius Titi filius Publilia Verona annorum XXXX hic situs est Titus Quintus Publius Fabricis sic fratres posuerunt\n
EDCS-15900111.translation: Caius Fabricius, son of Titus, of the Publilia tribe, forty years old, is buried here. Titus, Quintus, and Publius Fabricius, brothers, placed this.
EDCS-15900111.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Fabricius, aged forty, and was placed by his brothers Titus, Quintus, and Publius Fabricius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900113.text_edition: \n Quintus Septimius Quinti filius Niger Collina Antiochia miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXLV stipendiorum XI hic situs est heres posuit libertus patrono\n
EDCS-15900113.translation: Quintus Septimius Niger, son of Quintus, of the Collina tribe, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, twenty-four years old with eleven years of service, is buried here. His heir, a freedman, placed this for his patron.
EDCS-15900113.summary: This funerary inscription records Quintus Septimius Niger, a 24-year-old soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion with 11 years of service, for whom his heir, a freedman, erected the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900114.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpius Prosostus eques alae III Threcum sic turma Servilii C annorum XXX stipendiorum XII hic situs est Ambruco pater filio posuit\n
EDCS-15900114.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ambrucus, father, placed this for his son, Ulpius Prosostus, an equestrian soldier of the III Thracum ala, of Servilius's troop, thirty years old with twelve years of service, buried here.
EDCS-15900114.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ulpius Prosostus, a 30-year-old equestrian soldier in the III Thracum ala, who is buried here and for whom his father Ambrucus commissioned the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-15900115.text_edition: \n Publio Vedio Aquilino quaestori municipii Aelii Karnunti annorum L et FT annorum II\n
EDCS-15900115.translation: To Publius Vedius Aquilinus, quaestor of the municipium Aelius Carnuntum, aged 50, and FT, aged 2.
EDCS-15900115.summary: This inscription records the burial of Publius Vedius Aquilinus, a local official, and another individual, FT, likely his child.

EDCS-15900118.text_edition: \n Publius Maximius Maternus mensor legionis X geminae aram dedicavit civibus suis Sabino et Venusto consulibus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-15900118.translation: Publius Maximius Maternus, surveyor of the Legio X Gemina, dedicated this altar to his fellow citizens Sabinus and Venustus, happily fulfilling a vow as deserved.
EDCS-15900118.summary: This dedication of an altar was made by Publius Maximius Maternus, a surveyor of Legio X Gemina, to his fellow citizens during the consulship of Sabinus and Venustus.

EDCS-15900119.text_edition: \n Aug[g](ustorum) nostrorum tabularii vectigalis Illyrici et vilici stationis Poetovionensis Tertius servus ex voto\n
EDCS-15900119.translation: Tertius, a slave, from the treasury office of the Illyrian tax collectors and the overseer of the station at Poetovio, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-15900119.summary: This vow fulfillment was made by Tertius, a slave working for the imperial tax administration at Poetovio.

EDCS-16000053.summary: This is a short dedication to a perennial spring, likely a place of worship or a source of water.

EDCS-16000056.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Iulius Severus votum solvit\n
EDCS-16000056.translation: Iulius Severus fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domestus.
EDCS-16000056.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Iulius Severus to the god Silvanus Domestus.

EDCS-16000333.text_edition: \n Aquilae et Genio legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis Antonius Lucius primus pilus legionis supra scriptae posuit\n
EDCS-16000333.translation: Antonius Lucius, primus pilus of the above-mentioned Legio I Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, set this up for the Eagles and the Genius of the legion.
EDCS-16000333.summary: This is a dedication by Antonius Lucius, a senior centurion, to the standards and the protecting spirit of Legio I Adiutrix.

EDCS-16000569.translation: The lands of the Aelenus tribe/district.
EDCS-16000569.summary: This inscription likely marks the territory belonging to the Aelenus pagus (district or village).

EDCS-16000570.text_edition: \n Gnata Susi filia annorum XXXXV hic sita est Mulvius Victor signifer matri pro pietate\n
EDCS-16000570.translation: Gnata, daughter of Susus, aged 45, is buried here; Mulvius Victor placed her here for his mother out of filial devotion.
EDCS-16000570.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gnata, set up by her son Mulvius Victor, commemorating her burial and his filial piety.

EDCS-16000571.text_edition: \n Caius Maximius Bilenius milis sic legionis XV Apollinaris posierunt sic mater et <f=P>rater <P=R>(ublius) Valerius Proclus stipendiorum XIIII \n
EDCS-16000571.translation: Caius Maximius Bilenius, soldier of the Legio XV Apollinaris, and his mother and brother, Publius Valerius Proclus, with 14 years of service, placed this.
EDCS-16000571.summary: This tombstone was erected by Publius Valerius Proclus for his soldier brother Caius Maximius Bilenius, his mother, and himself, indicating 14 years of service.

EDCS-16000572.text_edition: \n Sextus Trebonius Quinti Fabia Proculus Beryto cornicularius legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum XIIII annorum XXX h \n
EDCS-16000572.translation: Sextus Trebonius Proculus, son of Quintus, of the Fabia tribe, from Berytus, a cornicularius (standard bearer) of Legio XV Apollinaris, with 14 years of service, aged 30, is buried here.
EDCS-16000572.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Sextus Trebonius Proculus, a soldier of Legio XV Apollinaris who served for 14 years and died at the age of 30.

EDCS-16000574.text_edition: \n Manius Geminius Mani Claudia veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris annorum L hic situs est Manius Geminius Aria patrono posuit\n
EDCS-16000574.translation: Manius Geminius, son of Mani, of the Claudia tribe, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 50, is buried here. Manius Geminius dedicated this to his patron.
EDCS-16000574.summary: A funerary inscription for a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, placed by his patron.

EDCS-16000575.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Aprilius Cai filius Surus beneficiarius legati Mediolano legionis XIIII geminae \n
EDCS-16000575.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Gaius Aprilius Surus, son of Caius, a *beneficiarius* (attendant) to the legate of the 14th Gemina legion in Mediolanum.
EDCS-16000575.summary: An epitaph for Gaius Aprilius Surus, a soldier with specific military rank, likely from Mediolanum.

EDCS-16000577.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Aurelio Firmino decurioni Iuliae Emonae quiXXIII IIMIO CIMIAN II \n
EDCS-16000577.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Aurelius Firmus, decurion of Julia Emona, who served 23 years...
EDCS-16000577.summary: A partial epitaph for Gaius Aurelius Firmus, a decurion from Julia Emona, recording his service years.

EDCS-16100341.text_edition: \n Diadumenus Titi Alfi Macrini |(centurionis) legionis X geminae servus annorum IIX hic situs est\n
EDCS-16100341.translation: Diadumenus, slave of Titus Alphius Macrinus, a centurion of the 10th Gemina legion, aged 8, is buried here.
EDCS-16100341.summary: A funerary inscription for Diadumenus, a young slave of a centurion, dated by the legion mentioned.

EDCS-16200080.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Lamponius Severus et Lucius Lamponius Candidianus decuriones coloniae Septimiae Karnunti quaestores faciendum curaverunt Pompeiano et Avito consulibus\n
EDCS-16200080.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Lucius Lamponius Severus and Lucius Lamponius Candidianus, decurions and quaestors of the colony of Septimia Carnuntum, saw to the construction, under the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.
EDCS-16200080.summary: A dedication of a public work to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by two officials in Carnuntum, dated by the consuls.

EDCS-16200081.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae sacrum Ulpius Secundus et Ulpia Secundina filia votum solverunt laeti libentes merito\n
EDCS-16200081.translation: Sacred to Nemesis Augusta. Ulpius Secundus and his daughter Ulpia Secundina fulfilled their vow gladly, willingly, and deservedly.
EDCS-16200081.summary: A votive inscription to the goddess Nemesis Augusta, dedicated by Ulpius Secundus and his daughter.

EDCS-16200082.text_edition: \n NT Fortunae Karnuntinae Caius Iulius Florentinus antistes deae votum solvit laetus libens\n
EDCS-16200082.translation: To Fortuna of Carnuntum. Gaius Julius Florentinus, priest of the goddess, fulfilled his vow gladly and willingly.
EDCS-16200082.summary: A votive inscription to Fortuna of Carnuntum by her priest, Gaius Julius Florentinus.

EDCS-16200084.text_edition: \n Lucius Armentiacus Luci filius Claudia veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris annorum LV domo Verona hic situs est testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Cai Ploti Primi et Aptae libertae et coniugis suae\n
EDCS-16200084.translation: Lucius Armentiacus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 55, from Verona, is buried here. He ordered this to be made in his will, under the supervision of Gaius Plotus Primus and Aptia, his freedwoman and wife.
EDCS-16200084.summary: A funerary inscription for a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion from Verona, detailing his burial arrangements.

EDCS-16200085.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Terti filius Pupinia miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXXIIII stipendiorum XIIII sibi et Nigro Valerio Terti filio Pupinia militi legionis eiusdem qui fuit nnorum XXXVI stipendiorum XIIII testamento fieri iussit Caius Munatius Cai filius Aniensis heres et Nigellae matri eorum posuit\n
EDCS-16200085.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Tertius, of the Pupinia tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 34 with 14 years of service, ordered this to be made in his will for himself and for Niger Valerius, son of Tertius, of the Pupinia tribe, a soldier of the same legion who was aged 36 with 12 years of service. Gaius Munatius, son of Caius, of the Aniensis tribe, his heir, and Nigella, their mother, placed this.
EDCS-16200085.summary: A double funerary inscription for two soldiers of the 15th Apollinaris legion, commissioned by will and set up by the heir and mother.

EDCS-16200086.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Titi Galeria Lugiduno Aiianius miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum XX annorum L heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200086.translation: Titus Flavius, son of Titus, of the Galeria tribe, from Lugidunum, Aianus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion with 20 years of service and aged 50, saw to its construction.
EDCS-16200086.summary: An epitaph for Titus Flavius Aianus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, recording his age and service length.

EDCS-16200087.text_edition: \n Caius Lucretius Cai filius Papiria Iustus Opitergio miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum X annorum XXX heres de propio\n
EDCS-16200087.translation: Gaius Lucretius Iustus Opitergio, son of Caius, of the Papiria tribe, a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, with 10 years of service and 30 years of age, his heir, [had this made] from his own property.
EDCS-16200087.summary: This epitaph records Gaius Lucretius Iustus Opitergio, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, who provided for his own burial.

EDCS-16200088.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Luci Claudia Cladens domo Faventia veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris annorum L Allia Luci filia Hesperis annorum XXXX hic siti sunt fili posuerunt\n
EDCS-16200088.translation: Lucius Valerius, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, from Faventia, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, aged 50, and Allia Hesperis, daughter of Lucius, aged 40, are buried here; their sons erected this.
EDCS-16200088.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lucius Valerius, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, and Allia Hesperis, with their sons as dedicants.

EDCS-16200089.text_edition: \n Marcus Antonius Marci filius Collina Lentinus Antiochia miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum III nnorum XXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-16200089.translation: Marcus Antonius Lentinus, son of Marcus, of the Collina tribe, from Antioch, a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, with 3 years of service and 25 years of age, is buried here.
EDCS-16200089.summary: This epitaph records Marcus Antonius Lentinus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-16200090.text_edition: \n Marcus Antonius Marci filius Collina Rufus Cyrrho miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum XIX annorum XXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-16200090.translation: Marcus Antonius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Collina tribe, from Cyrrhus, a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, with 19 years of service and 40 years of age, is buried here.
EDCS-16200090.summary: This epitaph records Marcus Antonius Rufus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, who died at the age of 40 after 19 years of service.

EDCS-16200091.text_edition: \n Marcus Iulius Marci Collina Clemens Chalcide miles legionis XV Apollinaris armorum custos centuria Cassiani annorum stipendiorum X hic situs est Antonius Firmus signifer heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200091.translation: Marcus Iulius Clemens, son of Marcus, of the Collina tribe, from Chalcis, a soldier and armor-bearer in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, in the century of Cassianus, aged 25, with 10 years of service, is buried here; Antonius Firmus, the standard-bearer, arranged for its construction as heir.
EDCS-16200091.summary: This epitaph records Marcus Iulius Clemens, an armor-bearer in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, whose burial was arranged by Antonius Firmus, the standard-bearer.

EDCS-16200092.text_edition: \n Lucius Salludius Luci Fabia Salvius Beryto miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum XXV annorum L heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200092.translation: Lucius Salludius Salvius, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, from Berytus, a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, with 25 years of service and 50 years of age, arranged for its construction as heir.
EDCS-16200092.summary: This epitaph records Lucius Salludius Salvius, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, who arranged for his own burial as heir.

EDCS-16200093.text_edition: \n Publius Sestius Publi Collina Valens Cyrrho veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris annorum L hic situs est Cominius Avitus miles legionis eiusdem heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200093.translation: Publius Sestius Valens, son of Publius, of the Collina tribe, from Cyrrhus, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, aged 50, is buried here; Cominius Avitus, a soldier of the same legion, arranged for its construction as heir.
EDCS-16200093.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Publius Sestius Valens, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, with Cominius Avitus as his heir.

EDCS-16200094.text_edition: \n Lucius Calpurnius Pudens Claudia Ara U<b=E>iorum miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Feni Balbi vixit annos XXV stipendiorum V hic positus Caius Marius Secundus heres posuit centuria eadem \n
EDCS-16200094.translation: Lucius Calpurnius Pudens, of the Claudia tribe, from Arae Io[rum?], a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, in the century of Feni Balbi, lived for 25 years, with 5 years of service, is placed here; Gaius Marius Secundus of the same century erected this as heir.
EDCS-16200094.summary: This epitaph records Lucius Calpurnius Pudens, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, who died at the age of 25, with Gaius Marius Secundus as the heir who erected the monument.

EDCS-16200095.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai filius Claudia Doles domo Apris miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXXIII stipendiorum XVII hic situs est Lucius Antonius Pudens heres et Fortunata liberta ei posuerunt\n
EDCS-16200095.translation: Gaius Iulius, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, from Apris, a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, aged 33, with 17 years of service, is buried here; Lucius Antonius Pudens as heir and Fortunata, his freedwoman, erected this for him.
EDCS-16200095.summary: This epitaph records Gaius Iulius, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, with Lucius Antonius Pudens and his freedwoman Fortunata as dedicants.

EDCS-16200096.text_edition: \n Quintus Iulius Quinti Claudia Proculus Savaria miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum XIII hic situs est Caius Licinius Arabus miles legionis eiusdem heres posuit\n
EDCS-16200096.translation: Quintus Iulius Proculus, son of Quintus, of the Claudia tribe, from Savaria, a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, with 13 years of service, is buried here; Gaius Licinius Arabus, a soldier of the same legion, erected this as heir.
EDCS-16200096.summary: This epitaph records Quintus Iulius Proculus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, with Gaius Licinius Arabus as the heir who erected the monument.

EDCS-16200097.text_edition: \n Quintus Valerius Seius miles legionis XV Apollinaris architectus Vienna annorum XL stipendiorum IX hic situs est heres posuit\n
EDCS-16200097.translation: Quintus Valerius Seius, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, architect from Vienna, aged 40, with 9 years of service, is buried here. His heir erected this.
EDCS-16200097.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Valerius Seius, a soldier and architect, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200098.text_edition: \n Cornelia Attici liberta Candida domo Aqui annorum XL hic sita est Iustus Publicus \n
EDCS-16200098.translation: Cornelia Candida, freedwoman of Atticus, from Aquinum, aged 40, is buried here. Iustus Publicus [erected this].
EDCS-16200098.summary: This is a tombstone for Cornelia Candida, a freedwoman, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200099.text_edition: \n Eucratus medicus Luci Iuli Euthemi medici servus annorum XXV hic situs est Lucius Iulius Euthemus dominus ob meritis eius posuit\n
EDCS-16200099.translation: Eucratus, physician and slave of Lucius Iulius Euthemus, a physician, aged 25, is buried here. His master, Lucius Iulius Euthemus, erected this in recognition of his services.
EDCS-16200099.summary: This is a tombstone for Eucratus, a physician's slave, erected by his master, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200100.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Octaviae Celerinae domo Claudia Savaria Aulus Com Cives heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200100.translation: To the departed spirits. Aulus Com Cives, heir of Octavia Celerina from Claudia Savaria, saw to its construction.
EDCS-16200100.summary: This is a dedication to the departed spirits of Octavia Celerina, erected by her heir, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200101.text_edition: \n Peregrinus Quinti Asini servus sutor caligarius natione Dacus nnorum XX hic situs est\n
EDCS-16200101.translation: Peregrinus, slave of Quintus Asinius, a shoemaker, of Dacian origin, aged 20, is buried here.
EDCS-16200101.summary: This is a tombstone for Peregrinus, a Dacian slave and shoemaker, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200102.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Titus, son of Niger, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200103.text_edition: \n Marcus Antonius Capito veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris Valeria Butto uxor Caius Antonius Longus filius et heres faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-16200103.translation: Marcus Antonius Capito, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, his wife Valeria Butto, and his son and heir Caius Antonius Longus, saw to its construction.
EDCS-16200103.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Antonius Capito, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected by his family, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200104.text_edition: \n Caius Baebius Cai filius Bassus domo Hieropoli annorum XXXXV miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Reburriana positum cura heredis Luci Cassi Crassi\n
EDCS-16200104.translation: Gaius Baebius Bassus, son of Gaius, from Hieropolis, aged 45, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, Reburrus's century. Erected by the heir of Lucius Cassius Crassus.
EDCS-16200104.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Baebius Bassus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected by the heir of Lucius Cassius Crassus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200105.text_edition: \n Petilliena Ingenua annorum VII hic sita est Caius Petillienus Luci filius Lemonia veteranus eques et Cassia Modesta filiae suae posuerunt\n
EDCS-16200105.translation: Petilliena Ingenua, aged 7, is buried here. Gaius Petillienus Lemonius, son of Lucius, a veteran cavalryman, and Cassia Modesta, erected this for their daughter.
EDCS-16200105.summary: This is a tombstone for the young Petilliena Ingenua, erected by her parents, one of whom was a veteran cavalryman, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200106.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Cai filius Secundus domo Claudia Viruno veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris hic situs est Suadulla coniux Iulius Tertius Secundina filia et Lucius Viri Verecundus heredes facti ex testamento faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-16200106.translation: Gaius Valerius Secundus, son of Gaius, from Virunum, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, is buried here. His wife Suadulla, his daughter Secundina, and Lucius Virius Verecundus, his heirs, saw to its construction in accordance with the will.
EDCS-16200106.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Valerius Secundus, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected by his heirs as stipulated in his will, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16200107.text_edition: \n Titus Cai filius us Aquis cornicularius praefecti classis praetoriae Ravennat[is ------\n
EDCS-16200107.translation: Titus, son of Caius, from Aquae, a cornicularius (a type of non-commissioned officer) to the prefect of the Praetorian fleet at Ravenna [served for]...
EDCS-16200107.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus, a cornicularius serving in the Praetorian fleet at Ravenna, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200109.text_edition: \n Decimus Marius Decimi Voltinia Vegetus Alba miles legionis X geminae centuria Taciti annorum XXV aerum IV hic situs est\n
EDCS-16200109.translation: Decimus Marius Vegetus, son of Decimus, of the Voltinia tribe, from Alba, a soldier in the 10th Gemina legion, in the century of Tacitus, aged 25, served for 4 years, is buried here.
EDCS-16200109.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Decimus Marius Vegetus, a 25-year-old soldier of the 10th Gemina legion, who died after 4 years of service, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200110.text_edition: \n Lucius Pompeius Luci Voltinia Pacatus Aquis miles legionis X geminae centuria nne annorum XXII aerum IV hic situs est heres ex testamento posuit\n
EDCS-16200110.translation: Lucius Pompeius Pacatus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, from Aquae, a soldier in the 10th Gemina legion, in the century of Nne (?), aged 22, served for 4 years, is buried here; his heir placed this by will.
EDCS-16200110.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Pompeius Pacatus, a 22-year-old soldier of the 10th Gemina legion, who died after 4 years of service, with his heir arranging the burial as per his will, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200111.text_edition: \n Quintus Pompeiius sic Quinti filius Voltinia Restitutus Aquis miles legionis X geminae centuria Suedi annorum XXV aerum III hic situs est heres ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200111.translation: Quintus Pompeius Restitutus, son of Quitus (?), of the Voltinia tribe, from Aquae, a soldier in the 10th Gemina legion, in the century of Suedius, aged 25, served for 3 years, is buried here; his heir took care to have this made.
EDCS-16200111.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Quintus Pompeius Restitutus, a 25-year-old soldier of the 10th Gemina legion, who died after 3 years of service, with his heir arranging the burial as per his will, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200112.text_edition: \n Marcus Mogetius Marci filius Pudens domo Dinace nnorum XXXV miles legionis XIII geminae stipendiorum XI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Caius Iulius Primus heres posuit\n
EDCS-16200112.translation: Marcus Mogetius Pudens, son of Marcus, from Dinace, aged 35, a soldier in the 13th Gemina legion, with 11 years of service, is buried here; Gaius Julius Primus, his heir, placed this as he ordered by will.
EDCS-16200112.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Mogetius Pudens, a 35-year-old soldier of the 13th Gemina legion who served for 11 years, with his heir arranging the burial as per his will, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200113.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Marci Voltinia Secundus nna miles legionis XIII geminae centuria Belici <s=C>tipendiorum sic XVI annorum XXX hic situs est heres posuit \n
EDCS-16200113.translation: Marcus Valerius Secundus, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe, a soldier in the 13th Gemina legion, in the century of Belicus, with 16 years of service, aged 30, is buried here; his heir placed this.
EDCS-16200113.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Valerius Secundus, a 30-year-old soldier of the 13th Gemina legion who served for 16 years, with his heir arranging the burial, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200114.text_edition: \n Titus Attius Titi filius Voltinia Vegetus Luco miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis annorum L stipendiorum XXI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200114.translation: Titus Attius Vegetus, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, from Luco, a soldier in the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion, aged 50, with 21 years of service, is buried here; the heir took care to have this made as he ordered by will.
EDCS-16200114.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Titus Attius Vegetus, a 50-year-old soldier of the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion who served for 21 years, with his heir arranging the burial as per his will, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200115.text_edition: \n Lucius Lucceius Luci filius Sabatina Blaesus Firmo miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis annorum XXXV stipendiorum XVIII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200115.translation: Lucius Lucceius Blaesus, son of Lucius, of the Sabatina tribe, from Firmo, a soldier in the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion, aged 35, with 18 years of service, his heir took care to have this made.
EDCS-16200115.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucius Lucceius Blaesus, a 35-year-old soldier of the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion who served for 18 years, with his heir arranging the burial, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200116.text_edition: \n Caius Lucretius Cai filius Voltinia Suadullus Alba miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis annorum XL stipendiorum XIX hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200116.translation: Caius Lucretius Suadullus, son of Caius, of the Voltinia tribe, from Alba, a soldier in the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion, aged 40, with 19 years of service, is buried here; the heir took care to have this made as he ordered by will.
EDCS-16200116.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Lucretius Suadullus, a 40-year-old soldier of the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion who served for 19 years, with his heir arranging the burial as per his will, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200117.text_edition: \n Caius Arestius Cai filius Claudia Firmus veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XL hic situs est Vettia Sabina coniux et fili pro pietate\n
EDCS-16200117.translation: Caius Arestius Firmus, son of Caius, of the Claudia tribe, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 40, is buried here; Vettia Sabina, his wife, and his sons, out of piety.
EDCS-16200117.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Arestius Firmus, a 40-year-old veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, set up by his wife and sons, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-16200118.text_edition: \n Titus Deunatius Titi Voltinia Frequens Apta miles legionis X geminae centuria Fufici annorum XXV aerum IV hic situs est Gaius Valerius Rufus centuria eadem heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200118.translation: Titus Deunatius Frequens, son of Titus, of the Voltinian tribe, a soldier in the Tenth Gemina Legion, in the century of Fuficius, aged 25, serving for 4 years, lies here. Gaius Valerius Rufus, of the same century, saw to the construction as heir.
EDCS-16200118.summary: A tombstone for Titus Deunatius Frequens, a soldier in the Tenth Legion, erected by Gaius Valerius Rufus.

EDCS-16200119.text_edition: \n Titus Iulius Titi Papiria Vegetus Augusta miles \n
EDCS-16200119.translation: Titus Julius Vegetus, son of Titus, of the Papirian tribe, soldier of Augusta.
EDCS-16200119.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording Titus Julius Vegetus, a soldier.

EDCS-16200121.text_edition: \n Lucius Marcius Luci Sergia Marcianus Tuccis miles legionis X geminae centuria Iusti nnorum XXXV aerum XI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis frater ex voluntate sua faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-16200121.translation: Lucius Marcius Marcianus, son of Lucius, of the Sergian tribe, nicknamed Tuccis, a soldier in the Tenth Gemina Legion, in the century of Justus, aged 35, serving for 11 years, lies here. May the earth rest lightly on you. His brother had this made according to his wishes.
EDCS-16200121.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Marcius Marcianus, a soldier in the Tenth Legion, erected by his brother.

EDCS-16200122.text_edition: \n Marcus Careius Marci Voltinia Carus Reiis miles legionis X geminae centuria Spedi annorum XX aerum IV hic situs est\n
EDCS-16200122.translation: Marcus Careius Carus, son of Marcus, of the Voltinian tribe, nicknamed Reiis, a soldier in the Tenth Gemina Legion, in the century of Spedius, aged 20, serving for 4 years, lies here.
EDCS-16200122.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Careius Carus, a soldier in the Tenth Legion.

EDCS-16201583.text_edition: \n Severa Magnianci annorum XXX hic sita est Veneria filia annorum IIII Ammodia Malius Vecco filiis et coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-16201583.translation: Severa, daughter of Magniancus, aged 30, lies here. Veneria, daughter of Ammodia and Malius Veccus, aged 4, was placed here for her children and husband.
EDCS-16201583.summary: A tombstone for Severa and her daughter Veneria, erected by Ammodia and Malius Veccus.

EDCS-16201584.text_edition: \n Umma Tabiconis filia annorum XLV hic sita est Illo Itedonis filius coniugi de pecunia sua posuit\n
EDCS-16201584.translation: Umma, daughter of Tabiconis, aged 45, lies here. Illo, son of Itedonis, erected this for his wife with his own money.
EDCS-16201584.summary: A tombstone for Umma, erected by her husband Illo.

EDCS-16201585.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Titi Flavi Cobromari libertus Uxavilus annorum XL hic situs est Primio filius patri posuit\n
EDCS-16201585.translation: Titus Flavius Uxavilus, freedman of Titus Flavius Cobromarus, aged 40, lies here. His son Primio erected this for his father.
EDCS-16201585.summary: A tombstone for Titus Flavius Uxavilus, a freedman, erected by his son Primio.

EDCS-16201586.text_edition: \n Tita Flavia Titi Flavi Vegeti liberta Prima annorum LV hic sita est Sasulus Titi Flavi Renati servus vivus sibi et suis\n
EDCS-16201586.translation: Tita Flavia Prima, freedwoman of Titus Flavius Vegetus, aged 55, lies here. Sasulus, slave of Titus Flavius Renatus, for himself and his family.
EDCS-16201586.summary: A tombstone for Tita Flavia Prima, a freedwoman, and an inscription by Sasulus, a slave, for himself and his family.

EDCS-16400357.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum pro se et suis Cornelius libens <f=E>(ecit) \n
EDCS-16400357.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Cornelius gladly made this for himself and his family.
EDCS-16400357.summary: A dedication to Silvanus made by Cornelius.

EDCS-16400405.text_edition: \n Caius Petronius Cai filius domo Mopsisto annorum LXXIII stipendiorum XXVI missus alae gemellianae hic situs est Urbana liberta et coniux posuit\n
EDCS-16400405.translation: Gaius Petronius, son of Caius, from Mopsistus, aged 73, with 26 years of service, discharged from the Gemellian cavalry ala, lies here. Urbana, his freedwoman and wife, erected this.
EDCS-16400405.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Petronius, a cavalryman, erected by his freedwoman and wife, Urbana.

EDCS-16400406.text_edition: \n Tiberius Iulius Rufus militavit ala Scubulorum stipendiorum L vixit annos XXCV hic situs est Iulia Tiberi filia Rufilla viva fecit sibi et patri\n
EDCS-16400406.translation: Tiberius Iulius Rufus served in the ala Scubulorum for 50 years; he lived 95 years. Iulia Rufilla, daughter of Tiberius, made this for herself and her father.
EDCS-16400406.summary: A tombstone for Tiberius Iulius Rufus, a soldier who served for 50 years and lived to 95, erected by his daughter Iulia Rufilla.

EDCS-16500035.text_edition: \n Paulina aversa sit a viris omnibus et deficsa sic sit ne quid possit mali facere Firminam clodas sic ab omnibus humanis\n
EDCS-16500035.translation: May Paulina turn away from all men and be cast out, so that she can do no harm; may Firmina be cast out from all human [company].
EDCS-16500035.summary: This is a magical curse inscription aimed at two women, Paulina and Firmina.

EDCS-16500063.summary: A dedication to the goddess Salus by Publius Geminius.

EDCS-16500089.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius It<t=I>o tubicen votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-16500089.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aurelius Ittito, a trumpeter, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-16500089.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Ittito, a trumpeter.

EDCS-16500090.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Deo Quintus Livanius Senecio veteranus legionis XIIII geminae votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-16500090.translation: To Sol Invictus Deus, Quintus Livanius Senecio, a veteran of the 14th Legion Gemina, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-16500090.summary: A votive inscription to the Unconquered Sun God by Quintus Livanius Senecio, a veteran of the 14th Legion.

EDCS-16500091.text_edition: \n Plotius Menenia Vicetia miles legionis XV Apollinaris \n
EDCS-16500091.translation: Plotius Menenia Vicetia, a soldier of the 15th Legion Apollinaris.
EDCS-16500091.summary: This inscription records the name and military affiliation of Plotius Menenia Vicetia, a soldier.

EDCS-16500240.text_edition: \n Daeipora Calaeti liberta annorum XXC Hispana Dextri serva annorum XXX hic sitae sunt Caius Petronius Domesticus et Ambatus fratres matri et Ambati coniugi posuerunt\n
EDCS-16500240.translation: Daeipora, freedwoman of Calaetus, aged 20, and Hispana Dextri, slavewoman, aged 30, are buried here. Caius Petronius Domesticus and Ambatus, brothers, set this up for their mother and for Ambatus' wife.
EDCS-16500240.summary: A funerary inscription set up by two brothers for their mother and one brother's wife, recording the ages and status of the deceased.

EDCS-16600461.text_edition: \n Bonae Valetudini Auli Iuli Severi Sall Optatus et Ulpius Senecio ob honorem aedilitatis donum dederunt Orfito et Rufo consulibus\n
EDCS-16600461.translation: To Good Health, Aulus Julius Severus, Optatus and Ulpius Senecio, gave this gift in honor of the aedileship, when Orfitus and Rufus were consuls.
EDCS-16600461.summary: A dedication to Bona Valetudo (Good Health) made by Optatus and Ulpius Senecio in honor of an aedileship, dated by the consuls Orfitus and Rufus.

EDCS-16600462.text_edition: \n Genio |(centuriae) Cusini Rufi Titus Voccius Victorinus custos armorum renovavit Fusciano II et Silano consulibus\n
EDCS-16600462.translation: Titus Voccius Victorinus, keeper of the arms, renewed this for the Genius of the century of Cusinus Rufus, when Fuscianus II and Silanus were consuls.
EDCS-16600462.summary: An inscription recording the renewal of something by Titus Voccius Victorinus for the Genius of a centuria, dated by the consuls Fuscianus II and Silanus.

EDCS-16600463.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo D--- Taviano Caius Iccius Cassius |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae aram posuit\n
EDCS-16600463.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, D[---] Taviano, Caius Iccius Cassius, a centurion of the 14th Legion Gemina, set up this altar.
EDCS-16600463.summary: A dedication of an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Iccius Cassius, a centurion of the 14th Legion.

EDCS-16600465.text_edition: \n Crescens Licinianus tribunus cohortis XVIII voluntariorum domo Mauritania sic vixit annos XLV Abudia Murinilla uxor v(iva) fecit\n
EDCS-16600465.translation: Crescens Licinianus, tribune of the 18th cohort of volunteers, from Mauretania, lived 45 years. His wife Abudia Murinilla, while alive, erected this.
EDCS-16600465.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Crescens Licinianus, a tribune in the Roman army, erected by his wife.

EDCS-16600467.text_edition: \n Genio |(centuriae) legionis XIIII geminae Philippianae Claudius Flavianus optio votum solvit in honorem commanipularium Imperatore Philippo et Titiano consulibus\n
EDCS-16600467.translation: To the Genius of the century of the 14th Gemina Philippiana legion. Claudius Flavianus, optio, fulfilled his vow in honor of his fellow soldiers, during the consulship of Emperors Philip and Titianus.
EDCS-16600467.summary: This inscription records a vow made to the Genius of a legionary century by a soldier named Claudius Flavianus.

EDCS-16800444.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus F T et I C titulum posueru<nt=M> filio suo Valerio Saturnino militi legionis M D CONARIVS annos vi<x=S>(it) XXX \n
EDCS-16800444.translation: To the spirits of the departed. His parents placed this monument for their son Valerius Saturninus, a soldier in the M D legion, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-16800444.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valerius Saturninus, a soldier, erected by his parents.

EDCS-16800465.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Valerio Severo nobilissimo Caesare milia passuum XXXIIII\n
EDCS-16800465.translation: For our lord Flavius Valerius Severus, most noble Caesar, 34 miles.
EDCS-16800465.summary: This inscription marks a distance of 34 miles dedicated to the Caesar Flavius Valerius Severus.

EDCS-16800466.text_edition: \n Impperatores CCaesares Vibii Gallus et Volusianus Auggusti PPii milia passuum XXX\n
EDCS-16800466.translation: For the Emperors and Caesars Vibius Gallus and Volusianus Augusti, Fathers of the Fatherland, 30 miles.
EDCS-16800466.summary: This inscription marks a distance of 30 miles dedicated to the emperors Vibius Gallus and Volusianus.

EDCS-16900030.text_edition: \n Octo Macurigis filius annorum L hic situs est fratres posierunt sic\n
EDCS-16900030.translation: Octo, son of Macurigis, 50 years old, lies here. His brothers erected this.
EDCS-16900030.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octo, son of Macurigis, erected by his brothers.

EDCS-16900108.text_edition: \n Vibius Cnaei libertus Logus annorum XIX natione Ermundurus \n
EDCS-16900108.translation: Vibius Logus, freedman of Cnaeus, 19 years old, of Ermundurus nationality.
EDCS-16900108.summary: This inscription identifies Vibius Logus, a freedman of Cnaeus, and notes his age and origin.

EDCS-16900140.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae Festus Primi publici portorii vilici vicarius votum solvit\n
EDCS-16900140.translation: To the unconquered Mithras. Festus, deputy customs official of Primi publicus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16900140.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mithras by Festus, a customs official.

EDCS-16900141.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Optimus Vitalis Sabini Verani publici portorii vilici vicarius votum solvit\n
EDCS-16900141.translation: To the unconquered god Mithras. Optimus Vitalis, deputy customs official of Sabinus Veranius, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-16900141.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mithras by Optimus Vitalis, a customs official.

EDCS-17600070.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Flavius Homuncio cum suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-17600070.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Marcus Flavius Homuncio, with his family, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-17600070.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter, dedicated by Marcus Flavius Homuncio and his family.

EDCS-17600073.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Vero vixit annos II menses II dies IIII Publius Aelius Primus et Aelia Vera parentes fecerunt\n
EDCS-17600073.translation: Publius Aelius Verus lived for 2 years, 2 months, and 4 days. Publius Aelius Primus and Aelia Vera, his parents, made this.
EDCS-17600073.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of a child and set up by his parents.

EDCS-17600074.text_edition: \n Aurelius Dialo veteranus cohor(tis) VII Breucorum annorum L hic situs est Troucissae vivo Aurelia Matera <f>(ilia) viva ponendum curavit patri\n
EDCS-17600074.translation: Aurelius Dialus, a veteran of the VIIth Cohort of Breuci, aged 50 years, is buried here. Aurelia Matera, his daughter, arranged for his burial while she was alive.
EDCS-17600074.summary: A funerary epitaph for a veteran named Aurelius Dialus, erected by his daughter, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-17600075.text_edition: \n Lucius Barbius domo Prhygia! Fortunatae coniugi annorum vivus fecit et liberto SCR \n
EDCS-17600075.translation: Lucius Barbius from Phrygia made this for his wife Fortunata, aged [ ] years, while he was alive, and also for his freedman SCR.
EDCS-17600075.summary: An epitaph set up by Lucius Barbius for his wife Fortunata and a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-17800009.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto pro salute domini nostri Marci Aureli Severi Antonini Pii Felicis Augusti \n
EDCS-17800009.translation: To Hercules Augustus, for the safety of our lord Marcus Aurelius Severus Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-17800009.summary: A dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, likely erected for his well-being.

EDCS-17800010.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Septimius Veranus veteranus legionis II adiutricis piae fidelis pro voto suscepto in expeditione Parthica Imperatore Antonino et [Oclatinio] [consulibus]\n
EDCS-17800010.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Septimius Veranus, a veteran of the II Adjutrix Pia Fidelis legion, fulfilled his vow made during the Parthian campaign when Antoninus and [Oclatinus] were consuls.
EDCS-17800010.summary: A votive dedication by a veteran of the II Adjutrix legion to Jupiter, commemorating a vow made during a Parthian campaign, likely under Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-17800012.text_edition: \n Sedato Augusto sacrum Publius Aelius Crescens magister collegii centonariorum votum solvit <l=I>(ibens) merito Faustino e<t=I> Rufino consulibus \n
EDCS-17800012.translation: Sacred to Sedatus Augustus. Publius Aelius Crescens, master of the guild of fullers (centonarii), willingly fulfilled his vow, deservedly, under consuls Faustinus and Rufinus.
EDCS-17800012.summary: A religious dedication by the master of the fullers' guild, Publius Aelius Crescens, to Sedatus Augustus, likely during the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus.

EDCS-17800013.text_edition: \n Genio templensium Domitius Niger miles cohortis III Batavorum et Aurelius Victor sacerdotes templi divi Marci Kalendis Mais Gentiano et Basso consulibus dederunt\n
EDCS-17800013.translation: To the Genius of the Templensians, Domitius Niger, a soldier of the III Cohort of Batavians, and Aurelius Victor, priests of the temple of the divine Marcus, gave this on the Kalends of May, when Gentianus and Bassus were consuls.
EDCS-17800013.summary: A dedication to the Genius of the Templensians by a soldier and priests, likely commemorating a festival in the temple of the deified Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-17800014.text_edition: \n Caio Claudio (3) filio Maximo IIII viro viarum curandarum tribuno legionis IIII Scythicae donis militaribus a divo Traiano donanto quaestori urbano ab actis senatus tribuno plebis praetori curatori viae Aureliae legato legionis I adiutricis iuridico pro praetore utriusque Pannoniae legato pro praetore Pannoniae Inferioris consuli sodali Augustali curatori aedium sacrarum canabenses publice \n
EDCS-17800014.translation: To Gaius Claudius Maximus, son of [ ], aged 4, a commissioner of roads, tribune of the IV Scythica legion, decorated with military honours by the deified Trajan, urban quaestor, secretary of the senate, tribune of the plebs, praetor, curator of the Via Aurelia, legate of the I Adjutrix legion, legal official with pro-praetorian authority over both Pannonias, legate with pro-praetorian authority over Lower Pannonia, consul, sodalis Augustalis, curator of sacred buildings, the canabenses (inhabitants of the canabae) publicly [dedicated this].
EDCS-17800014.summary: An extensive cursus honorum for Gaius Claudius Maximus, detailing his military and administrative career, likely dating to the early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-17800015.text_edition: \n Pro salute Impperatorum Luci Septimi Severi et Marci Aureli Antonini Auggustorum et Publi Septimi Getae Caesaris\n
EDCS-17800015.translation: For the safety of the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augusti and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar.
EDCS-17800015.summary: A dedication for the well-being of the Severan imperial family, including Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, and Caesar Geta.

EDCS-17800016.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Flaviano quondam militi legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum VIIII vixit nnos XXX Aelio Iustiano militi legionis eiiusdem sic stipendiorum VI vixsit sic nnos XXVI qui deciderunt in expeditione Parthica Cornel \n
EDCS-17800016.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Aelius Flavius, formerly a soldier of the II Adjutrix legion, with 9 years of service, who lived 30 years. To Aelius Iustianus, a soldier of the same legion, with 6 years of service, who lived 26 years, who perished in the Parthian campaign. Cornel [ias made this].
EDCS-17800016.summary: A joint funerary epitaph for two soldiers of the II Adjutrix legion who died during the Parthian campaign, detailing their service and ages.

EDCS-17800017.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Atilius Marcianus domu Savaria vixit nnis XXXI mensibus III diebus X<I=L> militavit nnis VII mensibus \n
EDCS-17800017.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Atilius Marcianus, from Savaria, lived 31 years, 3 months, and 10 days. He served as a soldier for 7 years and 3 months.
EDCS-17800017.summary: A funerary epitaph for Atilius Marcianus, a soldier, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800018.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Dazanus legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum XXVI vixit annis sama mater et coniux et Aurelius <her=FTZ>e<s=H> VI \n
EDCS-17800018.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelius Dazanus of the II Adjutrix Legion, with 26 years of service. Sama, his mother, wife, and Aurelius Here(s?) [or unnamed person] erected this.
EDCS-17800018.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Dazanus, a soldier of the II Adjutrix Legion, likely dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800019.text_edition: \n Aurelio Secundino quondam decurioni cohortis I Ulpiae nnoniorum ex singularibus stipendiorum XX vixit nnos XLIIII Aurelia Secundina filia et Aurelius <F>lo<r>i<an>us et Aurelius Exsuperatus f(ecerunt)\n
EDCS-17800019.translation: For Aurelius Secundinus, formerly a decurion of the I Ulpia Cohort of Non-Juries, among the distinguished soldiers with 20 years of service. He lived 44 years. Aurelia Secundina (his daughter), Aurelius Florianus, and Aurelius Exsuperatus (his sons) made this.
EDCS-17800019.summary: A funerary monument for Aurelius Secundinus, a former decurion and distinguished soldier, erected by his children, likely dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800020.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Aurelius Tertullus sexvirum coloniae Aquincensium sibi et Iuliae Ursulae coniugi karissimae aeternitatis memoriam posuit\n
EDCS-17800020.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Aurelius Tertullus, one of the six prominent men (sexvir) of the colony of Aquincensium, erected this to himself and to his dearest wife, Julia Urusla, in memory of eternal life.
EDCS-17800020.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius Tertullus and his wife Julia Ursula, a prominent citizen of Aquincensium, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800022.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Dignius Secundianus natione Raetus vivus fecit sibi et Aureliae Decciae coniugi et municipi piissimae et feminae rarissimae ac pudicissimae cuius mortem dolens per absentiam mei contigisse per culpam curantium conqueror vixit annis XXVIII mensibus X diebus XXIIII et Caio Dignio Decorato filio vixit annis II mensibus VIIII diebus XVII item viventibus Digniae Decoratae et Aureliae Secundinae filiabus Augustalis municipii Brigetionis\n
EDCS-17800022.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Caius Dignius Secundianus, by nation Raetian, made this while alive for himself and for Aurelia Deccia, his most dutiful wife and rarest and most modest woman, whose death, though happening in my absence, I lament as occurring through the fault of those caring for her. She lived 28 years, 10 months, and 24 days. And for Caius Dignius Decoratus, his son, who lived 2 years, 9 months, and 17 days. Also for his living daughters, Dignia Decorata and Aurelia Secundina, wives of Augustalis of the municipium of Brigetio.
EDCS-17800022.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Dignius Secundianus, his wife Aurelia Deccia, and son Caius Dignius Decoratus, recording their ages and lamenting the circumstances of the wife's death, likely dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800023.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Papiria Honorato \n
EDCS-17800023.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Caius Iulius Papirius Honoratus.
EDCS-17800023.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Caius Iulius Papirius Honoratus, dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800024.text_edition: \n Cai filius domo Iulia Secundinaniti Vitalis et heredes ex testamento curaverunt\n
EDCS-17800024.translation: His sons, Vitalis from Iulia Secundina, and his heirs by will, took care of this.
EDCS-17800024.summary: A testamentary funerary inscription indicating that Vitalis and his heirs arranged for a monument, likely dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800025.text_edition: \n ------] miles legionis IIII Flaviae felicis stipendiorum VIIII Marcus Pacilius Rufus |(centurio) eius\n
EDCS-17800025.translation: A soldier of the Legio IV Flavia Felix, with 9 years of service. Marcus Pacilius Rufus, his centurion, (arranged for this).
EDCS-17800025.summary: A funerary epitaph for a soldier of the Legio IV Flavia Felix, erected by his centurion, likely dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800026.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpi Magni beneficiarii Claudius Alexander heres fecit\n
EDCS-17800026.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Claudius Alexander, his heir, made this for Ulpius Magnus, a beneficarius.
EDCS-17800026.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpius Magnus, a beneficarius (an auxiliary soldier with a special duty), erected by his heir, likely dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800028.text_edition: \n Gaio Valerio Maximo |(centurioni) legionis II Parthicae |(centurioni) legionis VI ferratae |(centurioni) legionis XIIII geminae Martiae Gaius Valerius Praxit<e=F>l<e=F>s libertus et heres patrono pientissimo faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-17800028.translation: For Gaius Valerius Maximus, centurion of the Legio II Parthica, centurion of the Legio VI Ferrata, centurion of the Legio XIIII Gemina Martia. Gaius Valerius Praxitel(es), his freedman and heir, arranged for this most devoted patron to be memorialized.
EDCS-17800028.summary: A funerary dedication for Gaius Valerius Maximus, a distinguished centurion who served in multiple legions, commissioned by his freedman and heir, likely dating from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-17800029.summary: This is a dedication to a deceased daughter, likely from a parent.

EDCS-18300312.text_edition: \n ------ miles] cohortis V Breucorum civium Romanorum equitatae duplicarius annorum XXX stipendiorum XIII hic situs est Titus optio heres posuit\n
EDCS-18300312.translation: Here lies Titus, optio, centurion's assistant, age 30, 13 years of service, a duplicarius of the 5th cohort of Breuci, Roman citizens, cavalry; his heir set this up.
EDCS-18300312.summary: This is a military tombstone recording the death and service details of Titus, an optio from the 5th Cohort of Breuci, with his heir as the dedicator.

EDCS-20401534.translation: Turoius, son of Pletor Feucontis, made this while alive; the monument's space is 20 feet in every direction.
EDCS-20401534.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Turoius, son of Pletor Feucontis, marking a plot of 20x20 Roman feet.

EDCS-20601188.text_edition: \n Diis Itinerariis utriusque viae Lucius Pompeius Longus suo Marciaeque coniugis et Rustici fili nomine votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-20601188.translation: Lucius Pompeius Longus gladly and deservedly paid a vow to the gods of the road for both roads, in his own name and in the name of his wife Marcia and his son Rusticus.
EDCS-20601188.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Diis Itinerariis (gods of travel) by Lucius Pompeius Longus, honoring his family.

EDCS-20601189.text_edition: \n Ituno et Itunae Quartus et Flavia Iulia votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-20601189.translation: Quartus and Flavia Iulia gladly and deservedly paid a vow to Itunus and Ituna.
EDCS-20601189.summary: This is a votive inscription to the deities Itunus and Ituna, dedicated by Quartus and Flavia Iulia.

EDCS-20601190.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum Publius Titius Optatus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-20601190.translation: Sacred to Mercury. Publius Titius Optatus gladly and deservedly paid a vow.
EDCS-20601190.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mercury, dedicated by Publius Titius Optatus.

EDCS-20601191.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caetronius Festus votum solvit\n
EDCS-20601191.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Caetronius Festus paid a vow.
EDCS-20601191.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Caetronius Festus.

EDCS-20601193.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus aeternae quieti perpetuae securitat<i=L> ob memoriam Aureliae Carae quae vixit nnos XXXIIII Aur<e=I>lius Sabinus tesserarius coniugi bene merenti faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-20601193.translation: For the eternal quiet and perpetual security of the spirits of Aurelia Cara, who lived 34 years, Aurelius Sabinus, a tesserarius, took care to have this made for his deserving wife.
EDCS-20601193.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Cara, set up by her husband Aurelius Sabinus, a tesserarius, emphasizing eternal peace and her age of 34.

EDCS-20900004.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Anicius Quintus beneficiarius legati legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-20900004.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Anicius Quintus, a beneficiarius of the legate of the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion, gladly, joyfully, and deservedly paid a vow.
EDCS-20900004.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Anicius Quintus, a beneficiarius of a legate in the 14th Legion, expressing gratitude.

EDCS-21000722.summary: This is likely a name inscribed, possibly as part of a larger inscription or a simple marker.

EDCS-22500342.text_edition: \n K et LR A I decurio coloniae Karnunti et Aurelius A decurio coloniae Karnunti et Max AMM \n
EDCS-22500342.translation: K. and LR. A. I., decurion of the colony of Carnuntum, and Aurelius A., decurion of the colony of Carnuntum, and Max A. M. M. [erected this].
EDCS-22500342.summary: This is a dedication by two decurions of the colony of Carnuntum, likely for themselves or a superior official, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-22500358.text_edition: \n Publienus filius Proculus sacerdos flamen tribunus militum legionis II Adiutricis impensa sua\n
EDCS-22500358.translation: Publienus Proculus, son of Publienus, priest, flamen, and military tribune of the Second Legion Adiutrix, [erected this] at his own expense.
EDCS-22500358.summary: This inscription records the self-funded dedication by Publienus Proculus, a high-ranking soldier and priest, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-22500364.text_edition: \n Soll Lucius Ma Lucius Antonius tius Seus centuria Iuli C\n
EDCS-22500364.translation: Soll Lucius Ma, Lucius Antonius tius Seus, of the century of Iulius C., [dedicated this monument].
EDCS-22500364.summary: This fragment names individuals and a military century, likely part of a funerary or dedication inscription from the Roman army, possibly dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-22500367.text_edition: \n rinus et Gaius Aurelius VC rus et Lucius A \n
EDCS-22500367.translation: rinus and Gaius Aurelius VC rus and Lucius A. [dedicated this].
EDCS-22500367.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions at least three individuals, likely commemorating a dedication or funerary monument from the Roman period.

EDCS-22500377.text_edition: \n M Victorinus FOVIENTI ianus veteranus leg(ionis?) XV Apollinaris TEDT Imperator-- CLETI MDEG ET VCI CLEM LXII STIP \n
EDCS-22500377.translation: M. Victorinus, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, [honored?] the Emperor ... with his own funds, aged 62 years of service.
EDCS-22500377.summary: This inscription honors an emperor and mentions a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-23702079.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Gai Voltinia Verecundus Tolosa annorum XXV aerum V miles legionis X geminae centuria Campani hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Iulius rinus et Lucius Aemilius \n
EDCS-23702079.translation: Gaius Iulius Verecundus of the Voltinia tribe, from Tolosa, aged 25, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina legion, of the century of Campanus, is buried here; may the earth rest lightly upon you. Iulius rinus and Lucius Aemilius [set this up].
EDCS-23702079.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Verecundus, a 25-year-old soldier of the Tenth Gemina legion, erected by his relatives, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702080.text_edition: \n inio Catul corniculario legionis XV Apollinaris annorum stipendiorum XX fius aquilifer municeps\n
EDCS-23702080.translation: inio Catul, a cornicularius of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 20 years of service, a standard-bearer and a citizen, [is buried here].
EDCS-23702080.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an individual named Catul, a cornicularius and standard-bearer of the XV Apollinaris legion with 20 years of service, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23702081.text_edition: \n Voltinia Reiis miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXXV stipendiorum XVI hic situs est heres posuit\n
EDCS-23702081.translation: Voltinia Reiis, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 35 years, 16 years of service, is buried here; his heir set this up.
EDCS-23702081.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Voltinia Reiis, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion with 16 years of service, erected by his heir, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23702086.text_edition: \n Quintus Fortesius Quinti filius Pomptina Saturninus miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXX stipendiorum X hic situs est testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-23702086.translation: Quintus Fortesius Saturninus, son of Quintus, of the Pomptina tribe, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 30 years, 10 years of service, is buried here; he ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-23702086.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Fortesius Saturninus, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion with 10 years of service, who specified its erection in his will, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23702087.text_edition: \n Sextus Peticius Spuri filius Arnensi eques legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XLVI stipi(endiorum) XXVI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Marci Corneli Marci fili Aniensi et Cai Anaeni Luci fili Lemonia veterani\n
EDCS-23702087.translation: Sextus Peticius, son of Spurius, from Arnesis, a cavalryman of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 46 years, 26 years of service, is buried here; he ordered this to be made in his will, under the supervision of Marcus Cornelius, son of Marcus, from Aniensis, and Caius Anaenius, son of Lucius, from Lemonia, veterans.
EDCS-23702087.summary: This funerary epitaph records Sextus Peticius, a 46-year-old cavalryman of the XV Apollinaris legion with 26 years of service, who willed his monument, erected under the supervision of two veterans, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-23702088.text_edition: \n Caius Attius Cai filius Voturia Exoratus miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnrum XXXXIV stipendiorum XXIIII hic situs est Marcus Minicius et Successus libertus posierunt sic\n
EDCS-23702088.translation: Gaius Attius Exoratus, son of Gaius, of the Voturia tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 44, with 24 years of service, is buried here. Marcus Minicius and Successus, freedmen, erected this.
EDCS-23702088.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Attius Exoratus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected by his freedmen, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702090.text_edition: \n Marcus Cassius Spuri filius Voturia Placentia miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXV-- hic situs est \n
EDCS-23702090.translation: Marcus Cassius Placentia, son of Spurius, of the Voturia tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 45, with 25 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-23702090.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Cassius Placentia, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702091.text_edition: \n Caius Sentius Auli filius Scaptia Rufus Faesulis eques legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXXVI stipendiorum XIIX hic situs est Lucius Sentius fratri posuit\n
EDCS-23702091.translation: Gaius Sentius Rufus, son of Aulus, of the Scaptia tribe, an *eques* (cavalryman) of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 36, with 18 years of service, is buried here. Lucius Sentius erected this for his brother.
EDCS-23702091.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Sentius Rufus, an *eques* of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected by his brother, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702092.text_edition: \n Quintus Varius Auli filius Pupinia miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XLV stipendiorum XXIIII hic situs est Quintus Varius Sabinus filius pro pietate\n
EDCS-23702092.translation: Quintus Varius, son of Aulus, of the Pupinia tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 45, with 24 years of service, is buried here. Quintus Varius Sabinus erected this for his father out of filial piety.
EDCS-23702092.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Varius, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected by his son, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702094.text_edition: \n Caius Caesonius Cai filius Tromentina veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris vivus testamento fieri iussit Batulla Mettia viva fecit coniugi suo sibi filio \n
EDCS-23702094.translation: Gaius Caesonius, son of Gaius, of the Tromentina tribe, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, ordered this to be made during his lifetime by his will. Batulla Mettia made this during her lifetime for her husband, herself, and her son.
EDCS-23702094.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Caesonius and his family, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, commissioned by him and built by his wife, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702095.text_edition: \n Marcellina annorum V hic sita est Caius Clodius Marcellus miles legionis XV Apollinaris filiae posuit\n
EDCS-23702095.translation: Marcellina, aged 5, is buried here. Gaius Clodius Marcellus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected this for his daughter.
EDCS-23702095.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcellina, a child, erected by her father, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702098.text_edition: \n Marcus Laelius Luci filius Papiria Opitergio Marcellus miles legionis XV Appollinaris sic nnrum XXXXV stipendiorum XXVI \n
EDCS-23702098.translation: Marcus Laelius Marcellus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, from Opitergium, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 45, with 26 years of service.
EDCS-23702098.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Laelius Marcellus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702101.text_edition: \n Caius Cornelius Marci filius Velina miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnrum XXXVII stipendiorum XVI testamento fieri iussit hic situs est\n
EDCS-23702101.translation: Gaius Cornelius, son of Marcus, of the Velina tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 37, with 16 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will. He is buried here.
EDCS-23702101.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Cornelius, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, who left instructions for its erection in his will, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702106.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Marci filius Claudia Albanus Aequiculis veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris annorum LX hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-23702106.translation: Marcus Valerius Albanus, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, from Aequicula, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 60, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by his will; his heir took care to have it done.
EDCS-23702106.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Albanus, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, who commissioned it in his will, with his heir ensuring its completion, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702107.text_edition: \n Caius Aquilonius Cai Fabia Statutus P<a=E>tavio miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum XVIIII annorum XXXVI hic situs est Marcus Antonius ius Longus heres faciundum curavit \n
EDCS-23702107.translation: Gaius Aquilonius Statutus, son of Gaius, of the Fabia tribe, from Patavium, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, with 19 years of service, aged 36, is buried here. Marcus Antonius Longus, heir, took care to have this made.
EDCS-23702107.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Aquilonius Statutus, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected by his heir, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-23702108.text_edition: \n Caius Curius Placidus annorum XX hic situs est Caius Curius Valerius veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris et Cavia Nice posuerunt filio suo et sibi\n
EDCS-23702108.translation: Gaius Curius Placidus, aged 20, lies here. Gaius Curius Valerius, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris Legion, and Cavia Nice erected this for their son and themselves.
EDCS-23702108.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Curius Placidus, dedicated by his parents, one of whom was a veteran of the XV Apollinaris Legion.

EDCS-23702110.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Rufus missicius legionis XV Apollinaris hic situs Lucius Atilius Valens optio posuit\n
EDCS-23702110.translation: Gaius Julius Rufus, a discharged soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, lies here. Lucius Atilius Valens, a optio, erected this.
EDCS-23702110.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Julius Rufus, erected by Lucius Atilius Valens, an optio of the XV Apollinaris Legion.

EDCS-23702112.text_edition: \n Caius Licinnius Cai filius Claudia Rufus Savaria miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum XIX hic situs est Spatale liberta patrono posuit\n
EDCS-23702112.translation: Gaius Licinnius Rufus, son of Caius, of Claudia, from Savaria, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, with 19 years of service, lies here. Spatale, his freedwoman, erected this for her patron.
EDCS-23702112.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Licinnius Rufus, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, erected by his freedwoman.

EDCS-23702114.text_edition: \n annorum XI Lucius Cluvius Secundus miles legionis XV Apo<l=E>(linaris) posuit pro merito \n
EDCS-23702114.translation: Lucius Cluvius Secundus, aged 11, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, erected this for his merit.
EDCS-23702114.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Cluvius Secundus, erected by himself (or a benefactor) for his merit.

EDCS-23702116.text_edition: \n Firmus Appius Marci filius Publilia Verona miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum L stipendiorum XXVI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Publi Gabini heredis\n
EDCS-23702116.translation: Firmus Appius, son of Marcus, of Publilia, from Verona, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, aged 50, with 26 years of service, lies here. He ordered this to be made by his will, under the supervision of his heir, Publius Gabinius.
EDCS-23702116.summary: A funerary monument for Firmus Appius, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, erected according to his will.

EDCS-23702118.text_edition: \n Lucius Caridemus Salvi filius Aniensis Fronto miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XL stipendiorum XXI hic situs est Crescens libertus patrono testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-23702118.translation: Lucius Caridemus Fronto, son of Salvius, of the Aniensis tribe, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, aged 40, with 21 years of service, lies here. Crescens, his freedman, ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-23702118.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Caridemus Fronto, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, erected by his freedman according to his will.

EDCS-23702124.text_edition: \n Aemilius Diogenis sic Aniensis |(centurio) legionis XV Apollinaris fratri pro pietate\n
EDCS-23702124.translation: For Aemilius Diogenes, of the Aniensis tribe, centurion of the XV Apollinaris Legion, from his brother, for filial devotion.
EDCS-23702124.summary: A dedication for Aemilius Diogenes, a centurion of the XV Apollinaris Legion, made by his brother out of piety.
