EDCS-23702126.text_edition: \n Quintus Varius Quinti Claudia Sabinus Savaria miles legionis I adiutricis beneficiarius legati consularis stipendiorum XVII annorum XXXV Publius Valerius eques legionis XV secundus heres ex testamento Surus\n
EDCS-23702126.translation: Quintus Varius Sabinus, son of Quintus, of Claudia, from Savaria, a soldier of the I adiutrix Legion, a beneficiarius of the consular legate, with 17 years of service, aged 35. Publius Valerius, an equestrian soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, his second heir, Surus, according to the will.
EDCS-23702126.summary: A funerary monument for Quintus Varius Sabinus, a soldier of the I adiutrix Legion, erected by his heir, an equestrian soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion.

EDCS-23702137.text_edition: \n Posidonia annorum LX Cassius filius Severus coni(ugi) pientissimae veteranus? legionis XV Apollinaris et Cassius nus \n
EDCS-23702137.translation: Posidonia, aged 60. For her most devoted husband, Cassius Severus, son of Cassius, a veteran? of the XV Apollinaris Legion, and Cassius nus.
EDCS-23702137.summary: A funerary monument likely for Posidonia, erected by her husband, possibly a veteran of the XV Apollinaris Legion.

EDCS-23800845.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly related to a public monument or temple, mentioning Caesar and a pontifex maximus, date uncertain.

EDCS-24200374.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Lucilio Iustino Quirina Augusta Treverorum annorum XXX Publius Lucilius RI\n
EDCS-24200374.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Lucilius Iustinus of the Quirina tribe from Augusta Treverorum, aged 30. Publius Lucilius [erected this].
EDCS-24200374.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Lucilius Iustinus, who died at the age of 30, likely erected by a relative of the same name.

EDCS-24400830.summary: A brief dedication to the god Serapis, possibly by a person named Iustus.

EDCS-24400831.text_edition: \n Isidi Augustae sacrum Martialis Firmini Quinti Sabini Verani tertiae partis conductoris portorii Illyrici arcarii vicarius voto suscepto donum dedit sacerdotibus Tito Flavio Martiale et Flavio Marullino filio\n
EDCS-24400831.translation: Sacred to Isis Augusta. Martialis Firmini Quinti Sabini Verani, an agent of the collector of customs duties for the third part of Illyricum, the treasurer, fulfilled his vow and gave this gift to the priests Titus Flavius Martialis and his son Flavius Marullinus.
EDCS-24400831.summary: This is a votive offering to Isis Augusta, dedicated by an official responsible for customs duties in Illyricum, presented to two priests, likely in the late 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-24400832.text_edition: \n Isidi Augustae signum cum basi Victorin-- ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-24400832.translation: Victorinus set up this statue with its base to Isis Augusta in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-24400832.summary: A votive offering of a statue and base to Isis Augusta, dedicated by Victorinus.

EDCS-24400836.text_edition: \n Serapi Conservatori et Isidi ceterisque dis deabusque immortalibus [[]] [[]] [] votum solvit p a s cum Fabia Titiana uxore et Titiano filio\n
EDCS-24400836.translation: He fulfilled his vow to Serapis the Preserver and Isis and all the other immortal gods and goddesses, along with his wife Fabia Titiana and his son Titianus.
EDCS-24400836.summary: A man fulfilled a vow to several deities, including Serapis and Isis, accompanied by his family.

EDCS-24400837.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Sarapidi pro salute Im<p=R>(eratoris) Luci Septimii Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi et Imperatoris Marci Aurelii Antonini Augusti Flavius Quirinalis Maximus tribunus militum legionis X germinae piae fidelis votum solvit libens laetus merito \n
EDCS-24400837.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Sarapis. For the safety of the Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus and the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus. Flavius Quirinalis Maximus, military tribune of the Tenth Legion Gemina, pious and faithful, willingly, gladly, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24400837.summary: A military tribune named Flavius Quirinalis Maximus dedicated a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Sarapis for the safety of Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, likely during their co-reign (198-211 CE).

EDCS-24400838.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Sarapidi idem Maximus\n
EDCS-24400838.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Sarapis. The same Maximus [fulfilled his vow].
EDCS-24400838.summary: A brief dedication by Maximus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Sarapis, likely referring to the same individual from the preceding inscription.

EDCS-24500511.text_edition: \n Tito Aelio Verano veterano alae primae Aravacorum annorum LX et Aeliae Vinillae dulcissimae coniugi annorumIus hic siti sunt Titus Aelius Avitus parentibus piissimis\n
EDCS-24500511.translation: Titus Aelius Veranus, a veteran of the First Wing of Aravacorum, aged 60, and Aelia Vinilla, his most beloved wife, aged [...]. Titus Aelius Avitus laid here his most dutiful parents.
EDCS-24500511.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Aelius Veranus, a veteran, and his wife Aelia Vinilla, placed there by their son Titus Aelius Avitus.

EDCS-24500512.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Nonius Valentinus speculator legionis X geminae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-24500512.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Caius Nonius Valentinus, a scout of the Tenth Gemina Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-24500512.summary: A soldier named Caius Nonius Valentinus, a scout in the Tenth Gemina Legion, dedicated a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-24500514.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly dedicating something to the god Liber.

EDCS-24500516.text_edition: \n Caius Helvius Luci filius Pollia Fuscus |(centurio) legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum LV hic situs est Ianuarius et Modestus liberti posuerunt\n
EDCS-24500516.translation: Gaius Helvius Fuscus, son of Lucius, of the Pollia tribe, a centurion of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 55, lies here. Ianuarius and Modestus, his freedmen, erected this monument.
EDCS-24500516.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Helvius Fuscus, a centurion, erected by his freedmen.

EDCS-24500519.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus o veterano legionis I adiutricis obito annorum Faustinae aelia coniux et heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-24500519.translation: To the departed spirits. Faustina, wife and heir of a veteran of the 1st Adjutrix legion, who died at the age of [unknown], had this made.
EDCS-24500519.summary: This funerary inscription records that Faustina commissioned a monument for her deceased veteran husband.

EDCS-24500522.text_edition: \n In honorem Genii centuriae pro salute Augusti Flavius Victorinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-24500522.translation: In honor of the Genius of the century, for the safety of the Emperor. Flavius Victorinus willingly fulfilled his vow deservedly.
EDCS-24500522.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius Victorinus, honoring a military divinity for the emperor's well-being.

EDCS-24500523.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cassio Sexto militi cohortis D Maurorum Cassii Exsoratus sic Surus Quintus fratres et heredes titulum posuerunt\n
EDCS-24500523.translation: To the departed spirits. For Cassius Sextus, a soldier of the Cohort D of Moors. Cassii Exsoratus and Surus Quintus, brothers and heirs, erected this monument.
EDCS-24500523.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument for Cassius Sextus, a soldier, by his brothers and heirs.

EDCS-24900095.translation: Publius Petronius, son of Publius, and Gaius Fabius, son of Gaius, registrars of the district, saw to the building of a shrine to Ae<c=QU>orna from the district.
EDCS-24900095.summary: This inscription records the construction of a shrine to the goddess Ae<c=QU>orna by two district registrars.

EDCS-25300517.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio Primo equiti singulari I<m=N>peratoris nostri natione militavit annos XVI vixi<t=S> annos XXXIII heres faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-25300517.translation: To the departed spirits. For Titus Aurelius Primus, a select cavalryman of our Emperor, who served for 16 years and lived for 33 years. His heir had this made.
EDCS-25300517.summary: This funerary inscription is for Titus Aurelius Primus, a cavalryman, recording his service and lifespan.

EDCS-25402311.text_edition: \n Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aeli Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae consulis III tribunicia potestate VI a Karnunto mille passuum I\n
EDCS-25402311.translation: One mile from Carnuntum, under the authority of the tribunes for the 6th time, of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, consul for the 3rd time.
EDCS-25402311.summary: This is a milestone inscription dating to the reign of Antoninus Pius, marking one mile from Karnuntum.

EDCS-25402315.text_edition: \n Imperatori Vespasiano Caesari Augusto pontifici maximo \n
EDCS-25402315.translation: To Emperor Vespasian Caesar Augustus, Pontifex Maximus.
EDCS-25402315.summary: This is an inscription dedicating something to Emperor Vespasian in his capacity as Pontifex Maximus.

EDCS-25402317.text_edition: \n Pro salute Augusti Titus Valerius Ingenuus cultor domini parietem longum pedes a solo pedes inpensa sic sua fecit\n
EDCS-25402317.translation: For the safety of the Emperor. Titus Valerius Ingenuus, a worshipper of the master, built the long wall from the ground up at his own expense.
EDCS-25402317.summary: This inscription records Titus Valerius Ingenuus building a wall at his own expense for the emperor's safety.

EDCS-25402318.text_edition: \n Legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Luci Atili Silvani cohortis X hastati prioris\n
EDCS-25402318.translation: Of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, the century of Lucius Atilius Silvanus, of the 10th Cohort, the first line of spearmen.
EDCS-25402318.summary: This inscription identifies a specific military unit and century within the Roman army.

EDCS-25402319.text_edition: \n Cohortis IIII centuria Cassi Aeliani principis prioris\n
EDCS-25402319.translation: Belonging to the fourth cohort, the century of Cassius Aelianus, principalis prior.
EDCS-25402319.summary: This inscription likely records a military unit designation or an item belonging to it, perhaps from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25402325.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana by Mascellio, likely an offering from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500188.translation: To the Genius of the century. Claudius Tiberianus, keeper of the arms.
EDCS-25500188.summary: This inscription records Claudius Tiberianus, identified as the keeper of arms for a century, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500194.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum pro salute Augusti Publius Atalonius Proculus veteranus legionis XIV geminae Martiae victricis Mamertino et Rufo consulibus\n
EDCS-25500194.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, sacred. For the health of the Emperor, Publius Atalonius Proculus, a veteran of the legion XIV Gemina Martia Victrix, in the consulship of Mamertinus and Rufus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25500194.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a veteran of the XIV Gemina legion, dedicated for the emperor's health and dated by the consulship of Mamertinus and Rufus, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-25500197.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Valerianus beneficiarius praefecti legionis XIIII geminae Philippianae votum solvit\n
EDCS-25500197.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Titus Flavius Valerianus, a beneficiarius of the prefect of the legion XIIII Gemina Philippiana, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25500197.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus was made by Titus Flavius Valerianus, an officer's assistant in the XIV Gemina legion, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500198.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo centuria Valeri Flavi Cascellus Germanus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-25500198.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. The century of Valerius Flavius Cascellus Germanus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-25500198.summary: This votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus is by the century of Valerius Flavius Cascellus Germanus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500200.text_edition: \n Matri Deum Magnae sacrum Iulia Mansueta posuit\n
EDCS-25500200.translation: Sacred to the Great Mother of the Gods. Iulia Mansueta erected [this].
EDCS-25500200.summary: This is a dedication to the Magna Mater by Iulia Mansueta, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500201.text_edition: \n M sacrum Gaius Ap votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-25500201.translation: Sacred to M[...]. Gaius Ap[...] fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-25500201.summary: This is a partial votive inscription to an unnamed deity (initial M) by Gaius Ap[...], likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500202.text_edition: \n Mercurio Restitutus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-25500202.translation: To Mercury. Restitutus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-25500202.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mercury by Restitutus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500206.text_edition: \n Mithrae CRIO OM CI aram merito fecit\n
EDCS-25500206.translation: To Mithras. Crio Om Ci made this altar deservedly.
EDCS-25500206.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Mithras by Crio Om Ci, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25500209.translation: Marcia Musa fulfilled her vow at the crossroads.
EDCS-25500209.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcia Musa, likely from the Imperial period, recording the fulfillment of a vow at a crossroads.

EDCS-25500212.translation: Fuscus gladly fulfilled his vow to the Silvani.
EDCS-25500212.summary: This is a votive inscription by Fuscus, likely from the Imperial period, dedicating a gift to the Silvani (spirits of the woods).

EDCS-25500218.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Loco felici Victor camararius ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-25500218.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Victor, the chamberlain, set this up in a fortunate place in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-25500218.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus by Victor, a chamberlain, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25500219.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Finitus set this up.
EDCS-25500219.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus by Finitus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25500220.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Caius Valerius Maximus veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-25500220.translation: Caius Valerius Maximus, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-25500220.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus by Caius Valerius Maximus, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25500221.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a dedication to Silvanus by someone named Valerius, possibly from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25500226.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Euticia pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-25500226.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Euticia gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for herself and her family.
EDCS-25500226.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus Domesticus by Euticia for herself and her family, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600001.text_edition: \n miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXV stipendiorum IIX hic situs est Titus Farsuleius fratri fecit\n
EDCS-25600001.translation: A soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 25, with 8 years of service, lies here. Titus Farsuleius made this for his brother.
EDCS-25600001.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a 25-year-old soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion with 8 years of service, erected by his brother Titus Farsuleius, likely from the late Republic or early Imperial period.

EDCS-25600005.text_edition: \n us domoos miles legionis nnorum L stipendiorum XXX sit tibi terra levis Flavi-- Arc Severus et scius Secundinus heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-25600005.translation: A soldier of the [---] legion, aged 50, with 30 years of service, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Flavi-- Severus and his kinsman Secundinus, the heirs, erected this.
EDCS-25600005.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a soldier of a named legion, aged 50 with 30 years of service, set up by his heirs, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600007.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus vila Nlannorum LX si Garrius Hilarus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-25600007.translation: To the Divine Shades. Vila, aged 60 years. Garrius Hilarus set this up for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-25600007.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vila, wife of Garrius Hilarus, who died at the age of 60, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-25600009.translation: Ingenuus, son of Lucius, dedicated this.
EDCS-25600009.summary: A funerary dedication by Ingenuus, son of Lucius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600010.text_edition: \n F Iuventius Marci filius annorum XXXX hic situs est Iuma libert-- et F Cosius F Vitalis heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-25600010.translation: Iuventius, son of Marcius, aged 40, lies here; his freedwoman Iuma and his heir Cosius Vitalis, son of Furius, set this up.
EDCS-25600010.summary: A tombstone for Iuventius, son of Marcius, erected by his freedwoman and heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600012.text_edition: \n situs est Sextus Iulius Sentius fratri posuit\n
EDCS-25600012.translation: Sextus Iulius Sentius lies here; he placed this for his brother.
EDCS-25600012.summary: A tombstone recording Sextus Iulius Sentius who erected this for his brother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600013.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Valens miles legionis X geminae optio stipendiorum X annorumV hic situs est Musonius tus heres posuit\n
EDCS-25600013.translation: Caius Iulius Valens, a soldier of the X Gemina legion, optatio of the pay office, aged 25, lies here; Musonius, his heir, set this up.
EDCS-25600013.summary: A tombstone for Caius Iulius Valens, a soldier and pay officer in the X Gemina legion, erected by his heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600014.text_edition: \n Lucius Livius Luci filius Galeria Rusticus Ulia miles legionis X geminae centuria Luccreti sic annorum VL aerum XXVI hic situs est heres sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-25600014.translation: Lucius Livius Rusticus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, a soldier of the X Gemina legion, centuria of Luccretus, aged 45, with 26 years of service, lies here; his heir set this up. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-25600014.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Livius Rusticus, a soldier in the X Gemina legion, erected by his heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600834.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus perpetuae securitatis Aureliae Verinae memoriam posuerunt vixit annos LX Marcianus Marcellinus Revocatus cari coniugi dulcissimae matri fecerunt\n
EDCS-25600834.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For the perpetual security of Aurelia Verina. Marcianus Marcellinus and Revocatus made this for their dearest wife and sweetest mother. She lived 60 years.
EDCS-25600834.summary: A funerary monument for Aurelia Verina, erected by her husband and son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600836.text_edition: \n Buka sic Dociai filia annorum XXX hic sita est Atecorius coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-25600836.translation: Buka, daughter of Docianus, aged 30, lies here; Atecorius set this up for his wife.
EDCS-25600836.summary: A tombstone for Buka, daughter of Docianus, erected by her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600838.text_edition: \n lia Quinti liberta Secunda annorum XV hic sita est Marcus Valerius Serenus vir et heres posuit\n
EDCS-25600838.translation: Secunda, freedwoman of Quintus, aged 15, lies here; Marcus Valerius Serenus, her husband and heir, set this up.
EDCS-25600838.summary: A tombstone for Secunda, a freedwoman, erected by her husband and heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600839.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caesernius Victor et Pontinia Iusta Caesernio Iustiano filio nnorum IIII facere curaverunt\n
EDCS-25600839.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caesernius Victor and Pontinia Iusta had this made for their son Caesernius Iustianus, aged 4.
EDCS-25600839.summary: A funerary monument for the young child Caesernius Iustianus, commissioned by his parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600846.text_edition: \n Eicia Duisfilia viva sibi filio fecit\n
EDCS-25600846.translation: Eicia, daughter of Duilius, made this for herself while alive and for her son.
EDCS-25600846.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Eicia, daughter of Duilius, for herself and her son during her lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-25600848.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Exsuperata viva sibi et Aureliano coniugi karissimo\n
EDCS-25600848.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Exsuperata, while alive, for herself and for Aurelianus, her dearest husband.
EDCS-25600848.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Exsuperata for herself and her husband Aurelianus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25601341.text_edition: \n Pro salute Imperatoris Silvano Silvestri sacrum Publius Attilius fecit\n
EDCS-25601341.translation: For the safety of the Emperor, Publius Attilius dedicated this sacred offering to Silvanus.
EDCS-25601341.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Publius Attilius for the health of the Emperor, likely dedicated to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-25601346.text_edition: \n L I M Aurelius Dignus decurio coloniae Karnunti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-25601346.translation: Aurelius Dignus, decurion of the colony of Carnuntum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25601346.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Aurelius Dignus, a decurion in the Roman colony of Carnuntum, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25601347.text_edition: \n Flavius Aelianus et Flavius Aemilianus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-25601347.translation: Flavius Aelianus and Flavius Aemilianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-25601347.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Flavius Aelianus and Flavius Aemilianus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25601350.text_edition: \n sacrum Marcus Tuccius A veteranus legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-25601350.translation: Sacred. Marcus Tuccius, a veteran of the Legio XIV Gemina, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-25601350.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Tuccius, a veteran of the Legio XIV Gemina, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25601351.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a name, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-25601363.text_edition: \n Aia Primi filia annorum X hic sita est pater et Casata Magiona posuit m\n
EDCS-25601363.translation: Aia, daughter of Primus, aged 10, is buried here. Her father and Casata Magiona placed [this].
EDCS-25601363.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aia, daughter of Primus, who died at the age of 10, placed by her father and Casata Magiona.

EDCS-25601364.text_edition: \n Marcus Alfienus Marci filius Collina Na miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Venusti stipendiorum annorum L hic situs est Marcus Alfienus Marci filius frater et heres posuit\n
EDCS-25601364.translation: Marcus Alfienus, son of Marcus, of the Collina tribe, a soldier of Legio XV Apollinaris, in the century of Venustus, with 50 years of service, aged 50, is buried here. Marcus Alfienus, son of Marcus, his brother and heir, erected this.
EDCS-25601364.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Alfienus, a soldier of Legio XV Apollinaris, erected by his brother and heir.

EDCS-25601366.text_edition: \n beneficiarius consularis VF AMMI G stipendiorum VII I \n
EDCS-25601366.translation: Assistant to the governor, of good standing, AMMI G., with 7 years of service.
EDCS-25601366.summary: This inscription refers to a subordinate official, likely a soldier or clerk, with 7 years of service, holding the rank of 'beneficiarius consularis'.

EDCS-25601368.text_edition: \n Sextus Aponius Sexti filius Quirina Valens Camunnus miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis annorum XXXV stipendiorum XI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-25601368.translation: Sextus Aponius Valens, son of Sextus, of the Quirina tribe, a Camunnian, soldier of Legio XIIII Geminae Martiae Victricis, aged 35, with 11 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made in his will; his heir saw to its construction.
EDCS-25601368.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Aponius Valens, a soldier of Legio XIIII Geminae, who arranged for his burial through his will.

EDCS-25601369.text_edition: \n stipendiorum X hic situs est Quintus Atilius amico de suo fecit\n
EDCS-25601369.translation: Quintus Atilius erected this for 10 years of service, at his own expense.
EDCS-25601369.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Atilius, recording 10 years of service, with the monument erected at his own expense.

EDCS-25601371.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription bearing the name of Publius Aufidius Liberalis.

EDCS-25601372.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio Claudia Marino Elusa miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis stipendiorum XIIannorum XXXV Titus Avidius Albinus armorum custos heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-25601372.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Titus Avidius Albinus, armour-keeper and heir, took care to erect this for Titus Aurelius Marinus Elusa, a soldier of the 14th Gemina Martis Victrix legion, with 12 years of service and aged 35.
EDCS-25601372.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Aurelius Marinus Elusa, a soldier of the 14th Gemina Martis Victrix legion, erected by his heir Titus Avidius Albinus, dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25601373.text_edition: \n Dis inferis Manibus Aureliae Aureliani annos natae XXXV et Bono filio annos nato X quem mihi crudelis Genesis abstulit de schola immerentem et Iustae filiae annos natae V quae erat ingressa atminestrare sic parentibus dulcissimam aetatem Bonosus Firmani immunis speculariarius legionis XIIII geminae qui ipse coniugi et filiis suis fecit\n
EDCS-25601373.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Bonosus, an immunis (exempt soldier) and specularius (mirror-maker) of the 14th Gemina legion, erected this for his wife Aurelia Aureliana, aged 35, and for his son Bono, aged 10, whom cruel Fate took from me undeservedly, and for his daughter Iusta, aged 5, who was beginning to minister to her parents, most sweet in youth; he himself erected it for his wife and sons.
EDCS-25601373.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelia Aureliana, her son Bono, and her daughter Iusta, erected by her husband Bonosus, a specularius of the 14th Gemina legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25601374.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Carinae annorum XXIII Aurelius Augustus pequarius sic legionis XIIII geminae coniugi carissimae et Aurelia Anna mater faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-25601374.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, Aurelius Augustus, a butcher of the 14th Gemina legion, and Aurelia Anna, the mother, took care to erect this for Aurelia Carina, aged 23, his most beloved wife.
EDCS-25601374.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelia Carina, a woman of 23, erected by her husband Aurelius Augustus, a butcher of the 14th Gemina legion, and her mother Aurelia Anna, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25601375.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus m<e=I>moriae aeternae Aur<e=I>lia Cir<e=I>scentina <q=O>u<ae=I> vixc<i=E>t sic ann<o=V>s XXV Aur<e=I>lius Secu<n=M>dinus s<e=I>pu<lcr=RCL>um posuuit car<ae=I> coniugi s<uae=OI> h<o=I>c t<o=Y>t<i=E>que domus <ae=I>terna erit \n
EDCS-25601375.translation: For the eternal memory of Aurelia Circescentina, who lived 25 years. Aurelius Secundinus placed this tomb for his wife, so that this home may be eternal.
EDCS-25601375.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelia Circescentina, who died at 25, erected by her husband Aurelius Secundinus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25700020.text_edition: \n Maesia Titi filia Maxima annorum XXVI hic sita est Alexandria mater filiae posuit\n
EDCS-25700020.translation: Maesia Maxima, daughter of Titus, aged 26, lies here. Her mother Alexandria placed this for her daughter.
EDCS-25700020.summary: This is a tombstone for Maesia Maxima, aged 26, erected by her mother Alexandria.

EDCS-25700022.text_edition: \n Magurus Ri filius annorum XVI hic situs est pater posuit\n
EDCS-25700022.translation: Magurus, son of Ri, aged 16, lies here. His father placed this.
EDCS-25700022.summary: This is a tombstone for Magurus, aged 16, erected by his father.

EDCS-25700025.text_edition: \n Marcus Matius Marci filius Fabia Maximus Brixia miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis annorum XXX stipendiorum VI hic situs est heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-25700025.translation: Marcus Matius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe, from Brixia, aged 30 and 6 years of service, lies here. His heir took care to erect this; he was a soldier of the 14th Gemina Martis Victrix legion.
EDCS-25700025.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Matius Maximus, a soldier of the 14th Gemina Martis Victrix legion, aged 30 with 6 years of service, erected by his heir, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-25700027.text_edition: \n Mssaino Vallionis filius nnorum LXX hic situs est O coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-25700027.translation: Mssaino, son of Vallio, aged 70, lies here. O. placed this for his wife.
EDCS-25700027.summary: This is a tombstone for Mssaino, aged 70, erected by 'O.' for his wife.

EDCS-25700028.text_edition: \n Messia Flora annorum XLV hic sita est\n
EDCS-25700028.translation: Messia Flora, aged 45, is buried here.
EDCS-25700028.summary: This is a tombstone for Messia Flora, who died at age 45.

EDCS-25700029.text_edition: \n Narcissus Cai Pandulei Sedati servus annorum XIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-25700029.translation: Narcissus, slave of Caius Panduleius Sedatus, aged 13, is buried here.
EDCS-25700029.summary: This is a tombstone for Narcissus, a slave who died at age 13.

EDCS-25700030.text_edition: \n Tertius Ninnius Luci filius Pupinia Tergeste miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXVIII stipendiorum VI hic situs est Caius Ninnius frater posuit\n
EDCS-25700030.translation: Tertius Ninnius, son of Lucius, of the Pupinia tribe, from Tergeste, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 28, with 6 years of service, is buried here; his brother Caius Ninnius erected this.
EDCS-25700030.summary: This tombstone for Tertius Ninnius, a soldier aged 28, was erected by his brother.

EDCS-25700031.text_edition: \n Caius Ofanius Cai filius Collina Valens Cyrrho miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum VIII nnrum XXX hic situs est Caius Iulius Capretanus tribunus militum legionis eiusdem heres posuit\n
EDCS-25700031.translation: Caius Ofanius Valens, son of Caius, of the Collina tribe, from Cyrrhus, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, with 8 years of service, aged 30, is buried here; Caius Iulius Capretanus, military tribune of the same legion, erected this as his heir.
EDCS-25700031.summary: This tombstone for Caius Ofanius Valens, a soldier aged 30, was erected by his heir, a military tribune.

EDCS-25700032.text_edition: \n ia Plavi filia annorum XXV hic sita est Samaconius Helveionis vivus sibi et coniugi pientissimae posuit\n
EDCS-25700032.translation: Ilia, daughter of Plavius, aged 25, is buried here. Samaconius dedicated this, while alive, for himself and his most devoted wife.
EDCS-25700032.summary: This tombstone, dedicated by Samaconius for himself and his wife, records the burial of Ilia, daughter of Plavius, who died at age 25.

EDCS-25700033.text_edition: \n Lucius Plotidius Luci filius Lemonia Vitalis domo Bononia miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum L stipendiorum XXIII hic situs est Annia Maxima viro suo carissimo posuit structoria asciam normam\n
EDCS-25700033.translation: Lucius Plotidius Vitalis, son of Lucius, of the Lemonia tribe, from Bononia, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 50, with 23 years of service, is buried here. Annia Maxima erected this for her dearest husband, a builder.
EDCS-25700033.summary: This tombstone for Lucius Plotidius Vitalis, a soldier aged 50, was erected by his wife Annia Maxima, who was a builder.

EDCS-25700034.text_edition: \n Lucius Ponpeius sic Luci filius Voltinia Taurinus Tolosa miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis annorum XL stipendiorum XIX hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-25700034.translation: Lucius Pompeius Taurinus, son of Lucius, of the Voltinia tribe, from Tolosa, a soldier of the XIIII Gemina Martia Victrix legion, aged 40, with 19 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made in his will, and his heir saw to its construction.
EDCS-25700034.summary: This tombstone for Lucius Pompeius Taurinus, a soldier aged 40, was erected posthumously as per his will.

EDCS-25700035.text_edition: \n IIAItetio primipilaris et Lucio Praesenti |(centuria eiusdem\n
EDCS-25700035.translation: for Aelius, a primipilaris, and Lucius Praesens, of his same century.
EDCS-25700035.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records the burial or commemoration of Aelius, a primipilaris, and Lucius Praesens.

EDCS-25700036.text_edition: \n Primigenia Cai Petroni ancilla hic sita est quisquis ades nomenque meum tibi perlegis hospes aspice quam mortis sim miseranda meae bis denos aetas mihi iam compleverat annos quom rapuit miseram mors et iniqua dea felices illae quibus est data longior hora nam tulerunt vitae damna minora suae non ego luxuriis avolsa fui maledictis aetatis carpsi munditiasque meas per longum vivas et me felicius opto et levis humatae sit mihi terra roges\n
EDCS-25700036.translation: Primigenia, slave of Caius Petronius, is buried here. Whoever you are, stranger, who reads my name, look upon my pitiable death. My age had already completed twenty years when death, the unjust goddess, snatched me away. Happy are those to whom a longer hour is given, for they have suffered lesser losses in their lives. I was not torn away by excesses; I enjoyed the purity of my youth. I wish that you live long and more happily than I, and that the earth rest lightly upon me.
EDCS-25700036.summary: This epitaph for Primigenia, a slave girl who died at age 20, is a poignant reflection on an early death and a wish for the living.

EDCS-25700037.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Refio Marcello filio infelicissimo nnorum V et mensium V Valeria Serena mater et Refius Celerinus |(centurio) pater et Valeria Optima avia domo Sergia Savaria filio pientissimo faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-25700037.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Marcello, son of Refius, most unfortunate, aged 5 years and 5 months, and for his most dutiful son; Valeria Serena his mother and Refius Celerinus, centurion, his father, and Valeria Optima, grandmother, from Savaria, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-25700037.summary: This tombstone for the very young Marcello, son of Refius, was erected by his parents and grandmother.

EDCS-25700038.text_edition: \n Lucius Roscius Luci filius Publilia miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum III annorum XXII Titus Lucretius frater de suo posuit hic situs est iuvenis S \n
EDCS-25700038.translation: Lucius Roscius, son of Lucius, of the tribe Publilia, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, with 3 years of service and 22 years old, Titus Lucretius, his brother, erected this at his own expense. Here lies a young man.
EDCS-25700038.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Roscius, a soldier who died young, erected by his brother, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-25700039.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus RPAILLI Adiectae Masuetae Agili Sollemni\n
EDCS-25700039.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Pailli Adiecta, Masueta, Agilis, and Sollemnis.
EDCS-25700039.summary: This is a dedication to the spirits of the departed for several individuals, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-25700041.translation: Sammus, son of Atto, PACA VIO.
EDCS-25700041.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription possibly recording a name and an epithet or location.

EDCS-25800250.text_edition: \n Deae Veneri Victrici sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Commodi Augusti veterani |(centuriae) Crispiniani missione honesta accepta Iulius Atta Iulius Respec<t=I>us Ursus \n
EDCS-25800250.translation: Sacred to the Goddess Venus Victrix, for the health of the Emperor Commodus Augustus. Julius Atta, Julius Respectus, and Ursus, veterans of the Crispinian century, having received an honorable discharge, dedicated this.
EDCS-25800250.summary: This is a religious dedication to Venus Victrix by veterans for the emperor's health, dating to the reign of Commodus.

EDCS-25800252.text_edition: \n annorum XX Val(erius) Sceolinus et Valeri Maximianus Midas Bonosus filii sororum sibi vivi faciendum curaverunt resta viator et relege misera dum maxime florebam abscessi defuncta sculpt VR IMO\n
EDCS-25800252.translation: At the age of 20, Valerius Sceolinus and Valerius Maximianus, Midas, and Bonosus, sons of his sisters, had this made for themselves while still alive. I was restored, and read, alas, I passed away though I was flourishing. Sculpted.
EDCS-25800252.summary: This is a tombstone for Valerius Sceolinus, who died at 20, with the monument erected by his nephews, likely from the later Roman period.

EDCS-25800253.text_edition: \n Caius Sentius Cai filius Sergia Sabinus Iader miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Semproni Nigri annorum XXXVI stipendiorum XVI hic situs est fecit Lucius Valerius Genialis eques fratri\n
EDCS-25800253.translation: Caius Sentius Sabinus, son of Caius, of the tribe Sergia, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, in the century of Sempronius Niger, 36 years old, with 16 years of service, lies here. Lucius Valerius Genialis, an equestrian, made this for his brother.
EDCS-25800253.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Sentius Sabinus, a soldier, erected by his brother, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-25800254.text_edition: \n Septimius Alcides veteranus legionis XIIII geminae Iuliae Victorinae coniugi pienstissimae annorum XXX viridiarium suo inpendio faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-25800254.translation: Septimius Alcides, a veteran of the 14th Gemina Iulia legion, had this constructed at his own expense for his most devoted wife, Victorina, 30 years old, his gardener.
EDCS-25800254.summary: This is a funerary monument for Victorina, erected by her husband Septimius Alcides, a veteran, likely from the later Roman period.

EDCS-25800255.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimio Maximiano militi legionis XIIII geminae nnorum L stipendiorum XXI Claudia Severa coniux faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-25800255.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Septimius Maximianus, soldier of the 14th Gemina Iulia legion, 50 years old, with 21 years of service. Claudia Severa, his wife, had this made.
EDCS-25800255.summary: This is a tombstone for Septimius Maximianus, a soldier, erected by his wife, likely from the later Roman period.

EDCS-25800257.text_edition: \n Lucius Sertorius Papiria Severus Ticino miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Grani Catulli miletavit sic stipendia VI vixit annos XXX heres ex testamento curavit faciendum\n
EDCS-25800257.translation: Lucius Sertorius Severus, of the tribe Papiria, from Ticino, a soldier of the 11th Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, in the century of Granius Catullus, served 6 years and lived 30 years. The heir by will took care to have this made.
EDCS-25800257.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Sertorius Severus, a soldier who died at 30, with his burial arranged by his heir, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-25800259.text_edition: \n isionis Curmisagius ula viva Curmisagius INAII ]\n
EDCS-25800259.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription likely mentioning a person named Curmisagius, possibly in relation to a living individual, from an unknown period.

EDCS-25800260.text_edition: \n Summa Calitigis filia annorum LXXX hic sita est Titus Flavius Cobromarus filius eius et Mamua et Tincomara matri posuerunt\n
EDCS-25800260.translation: Summa Calitigis, aged eighty, lies here. Titus Flavius Cobromarus, her son, and Mamua and Tincomara erected this for their mother.
EDCS-25800260.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Summa Calitigis, erected by her son and others.

EDCS-25800261.summary: This inscription identifies Taurinus as the prefect of the 14th Legion, likely commemorating his service or an associated monument.

EDCS-25800262.text_edition: \n Marcus Terentius Marci filius Menenia Vicetia miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXXII stipendiorum XII hic situs est ex testamento positum arbitratu Luci et Cai Aureliorum heredum meorum\n
EDCS-25800262.translation: Marcus Terentius Vicetia, son of Marcus, of the Menenia tribe, a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, aged thirty-two, with twelve years of service, lies here. Placed according to his will by the arbitration of Lucius and Caius Aurelius, my heirs.
EDCS-25800262.summary: This is a soldier's funerary epitaph, detailing his name, legion, age, and years of service, with instructions for its erection by his heirs.

EDCS-25800268.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Cai filius Voltinia Philippis miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum stipendiorum XI \n
EDCS-25800268.translation: Caius Valerius Philippis, son of Caius, of the Voltinia tribe, a soldier in the 15th Apollinaris Legion, aged [---], with eleven years of service.
EDCS-25800268.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph for a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, identifying his name, tribe, and length of service.

EDCS-25800270.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Papiria Verinus Traiana veteranus legionis X geminae piae fidelis annorumSP \n
EDCS-25800270.translation: Lucius Valerius Verinus, of the Papiria tribe, Trajan's veteran of the 10th Gemina Pia Fidelis Legion, aged [---].
EDCS-25800270.summary: This fragmentary inscription honors Lucius Valerius Verinus, a veteran of the 10th Gemina legion.

EDCS-25800271.text_edition: \n IS hic situs est Vallauna Magni et filii patri posuerunt\n
EDCS-25800271.translation: Vallauna lies here. Magni and his son erected this for their father.
EDCS-25800271.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Vallauna, erected by his son and Magni.

EDCS-25800272.text_edition: \n Vasso Vaicae libertus annorum LXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-25800272.translation: Vasso, freedman of Vaica, aged seventy, lies here.
EDCS-25800272.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Vasso, a freedman, and his age.

EDCS-25800273.text_edition: \n Vasso erta filia hic sita est illis posit sic\n
EDCS-25800273.translation: Vasso, son of erta, lies here. Placed for them thus.
EDCS-25800273.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Vasso, son of erta, with a note about its placement.

EDCS-25800275.text_edition: \n Veneto Liccei filius miles cohortis V Breucorum civium Romanorum equitatae annorum XXV stipendiorum VI hic situs est Ventus frater posuit\n
EDCS-25800275.translation: Veneto Liccei, son of Licceius, a soldier in the 5th Breucorum cohort, Roman citizens, cavalry, aged twenty-five, with six years of service, lies here. Ventus, his brother, erected this.
EDCS-25800275.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Veneto Liccei, a soldier in the 5th Breucorum cohort, erected by his brother.

EDCS-25800276.text_edition: \n Vextila liberta Maricaes sic annorum LX hic est sita filia matri posuit\n
EDCS-25800276.translation: Vextila, freedwoman of Marica, aged sixty, lies here. His daughter erected this for her mother.
EDCS-25800276.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Vextila, a freedwoman, erected by her daughter.

EDCS-25800277.text_edition: \n Lucius Vibius Luci patro DE O et SIC bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-25800277.translation: Lucius Vibius, son of Lucius, made this for the god and for himself, who deserved well.
EDCS-25800277.summary: A dedication to a god by Lucius Vibius, son of Lucius, for himself as a reward for his merits.

EDCS-25800278.text_edition: \n Victor Aureli filius annorum IIII pater posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-25800278.translation: Victor, son of Aurelius, aged 4, is laid here; his father placed this.
EDCS-25800278.summary: A tombstone erected by a father for his 4-year-old son, Victor, son of Aurelius.

EDCS-25800279.text_edition: \n Vinucia Publi liberta Quarta annorum XXXV hic sita est Publius Vinucius Publi filius Claudia Rufus veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris vivus fecit sibi et coniugi suae \n
EDCS-25800279.translation: Vinucia Quarta, freedwoman of Publius, aged 35, is laid here. Publius Vinucius Rufus, son of Publius, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, made this for himself and his wife while he was alive.
EDCS-25800279.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vinucia Quarta, freedwoman of Publius, and her husband Publius Vinucius Rufus, a veteran.

EDCS-25800280.text_edition: \n mati Viricati filius annorum XV est situs pater filio posuit\n
EDCS-25800280.translation: Mati, son of Viricatus, aged 15, is laid here; his father placed this for his son.
EDCS-25800280.summary: A tombstone erected by a father for his 15-year-old son, Mati, son of Viricatus.

EDCS-25900548.text_edition: \n Marcus Visellius Germanus miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Cai Petroni annorum XXX stipendiorum XII hic situs est heres posuit\n
EDCS-25900548.translation: Marcus Visellius Germanus, a soldier in the centuria of Caius Petronius in the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 30, with 12 years of service, is laid here; his heir placed this.
EDCS-25900548.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Visellius Germanus, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, whose heir erected the monument.

EDCS-25900549.text_edition: \n Caius Visius Cai filius Publilia Proculus Verona miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis annorum XL stipendiorum XV hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-25900549.translation: Gaius Visius, son of Gaius, from Verona, a soldier in the XIV Gemina Martia Victrix legion, aged 40, with 15 years of service, is laid here; he ordered this to be made by his will and his heir saw to its construction.
EDCS-25900549.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Visius, a soldier of the XIV Gemina Martia Victrix legion, who arranged for the monument in his will.

EDCS-25900551.text_edition: \n Vodercila Tatae filia hic est sita annorum XX Cortilus et Ato fratri et Sava coniugi suae posuerunt\n
EDCS-25900551.translation: Vodercila, daughter of Tata, aged 20, is laid here. Cortilus and Ato placed this for their brother and Sava, his wife.
EDCS-25900551.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vodercila, daughter of Tata, erected by Cortilus and Ato for their brother and his wife Sava.

EDCS-25900552.text_edition: \n Ursus Valeri filius a<nn=M>orum XI Coelina mater posuit \n
EDCS-25900552.translation: Ursa, daughter of Valerius, aged 11, is laid here; her mother Coelina placed this.
EDCS-25900552.summary: A tombstone erected by a mother, Coelina, for her 11-year-old daughter, Ursa.

EDCS-25900553.text_edition: \n Publius Urvinius Publi filius Fortunatus annorum XVII hic situs est Sucesa mater filio posuit\n
EDCS-25900553.translation: Publius Urvinius Fortunatus, son of Publius, aged 17, is laid here; his mother Sucesa placed this for her son.
EDCS-25900553.summary: A tombstone erected by a mother, Sucesa, for her 17-year-old son, Publius Urvinius Fortunatus.

EDCS-25900555.text_edition: \n tra stipendiorum XVII a mater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-25900555.translation: [...] with 17 years of service, his mother saw to its construction.
EDCS-25900555.summary: A partial funerary inscription recording military service and mentioning a mother overseeing the construction of the monument.

EDCS-25900560.text_edition: \n ona ae filius optio cohortis urix \n
EDCS-25900560.translation: Lucius [son of] Ona, optative of the cohort, [died].
EDCS-25900560.summary: This is a fragmentary tomb inscription recording the death of a military officer, Lucius, son of Ona.

EDCS-25900561.summary: This is a tomb inscription stating that the deceased's heirs erected the monument.

EDCS-25900564.text_edition: \n miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum XX annorum XL heres posuit\n
EDCS-25900564.translation: A soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, with twenty years of service and forty years of age, his heir set this up.
EDCS-25900564.summary: This tombstone commemorates a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion who served for 20 years and lived to be 40.

EDCS-25900569.text_edition: \n pi filia hic sita annorum ia Vir \n
EDCS-25900569.translation: Pi, daughter of [---], lies here, aged [---] years, [---] man.
EDCS-25900569.summary: This is a fragmentary tomb inscription recording the age and status of a woman named Pi.

EDCS-25900572.text_edition: \n legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXV stipendiorum IIX \n
EDCS-25900572.translation: Of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 25 years, with 2 years of service.
EDCS-25900572.summary: This funerary inscription records the age (25) and service length (2 years) of a soldier from the XV Apollinaris legion.

EDCS-25900576.summary: This is a very fragmentary tomb inscription indicating the age and parentage of the deceased.

EDCS-25900577.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. D(is) M(anibus) [---].
EDCS-25900577.summary: This is a dedication to the underworld gods, likely for a tomb, with missing details.

EDCS-25900579.summary: This inscription, likely from a tomb, records the age of the deceased as 60.

EDCS-25900580.translation: Sons set this up for their parents.
EDCS-25900580.summary: This inscription records that sons erected a monument for their parents.

EDCS-25900581.summary: This fragmentary inscription identifies the deceased as a veteran.

EDCS-25900582.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording military service or tenure, with the number of years of service possibly indicated.

EDCS-25900593.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely a funerary or dedicatory marker for Titus Flavius, son of Titus.

EDCS-25900596.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely a funerary or dedicatory marker for Caius Iulius Sone.

EDCS-25900598.text_edition: \n Claudius Tac Publius Voconius Valens Marcus Barbius Culcia Octavia Claudia Umma Flavius Mercator Long Marcellin Caius Iulius Augustus Caius Safidius Valentinus Marcus Solius Euthymus Caius Po Marcus Co Marcus Bae Lucius App Marcus Valerius Satonius Cassia Iul Cel Anto\n
EDCS-25900598.translation: Claudius Tac, Publius Voconius Valens, Marcus Barbius, Culcia Octavia, Claudia Umma, Flavius Mercator, Longinus, Marcellinus, Caius Iulius Augustus, Caius Safidius Valentinus, Marcus Solius Euthymus, Caius Poppaeus, Marcus Cornelius, Marcus Baebius, Lucius Appius, Marcus Valerius Satonius, Cassia Iulia, Celer, Antonius.
EDCS-25900598.summary: This extensive list of names likely represents a group of individuals involved in a legal or administrative matter, possibly a legal document or a dedication by a group.

EDCS-26001194.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely military, referring to an equestrian soldier of a cohort.

EDCS-26001201.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a personal name with a gentilicium ending in -icius and a cognomen or another gentilicium.

EDCS-26001208.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely funerary, recording Sergia, daughter of Rufus, of the Aelia family.

EDCS-26001219.text_edition: \n Caius Coranius Florus magister figlinarum IN \n
EDCS-26001219.translation: Caius Coranius Florus, master of the potteries, in...
EDCS-26001219.summary: This inscription records Caius Coranius Florus, identified as a master of the potteries, possibly in relation to a trade guild or a business establishment.

EDCS-26001247.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcellina, daughter of Senecio, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-26001248.text_edition: \n Titus Marcius Titi filius Velina TI MV \n
EDCS-26001248.translation: Titus Marcius, son of Titus, of the Velina tribe. Ti. Mu...
EDCS-26001248.summary: This is a personal name inscription, likely funerary or legal, for Titus Marcius, son of Titus, registered in the Velina tribe, with further fragmented information.

EDCS-26001249.translation: To Nito, daughter of Tamacus, wife of Nerus.
EDCS-26001249.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Nito, wife of Nerus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26001252.text_edition: \n Ioeto et mmuni Saturnini filiae coniugi annorum et Senno patri annorum V filii parentibus pientissimis fecerunt\n
EDCS-26001252.translation: Ioeto, daughter of Saturninus, and Senno, his father, aged 5 years, erected this for their most devoted parents.
EDCS-26001252.summary: This funerary inscription records that Ioeto and Senno erected a monument for their parents, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26001254.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Titi filius Germanus annorum V hic situs est Titus Flavius Agricola et Iulia Dubitata vivi sibi et filio posuerunt\n
EDCS-26001254.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Titus Flavius Germanus, son of Titus Flavius, aged 5 years, lies here. Titus Flavius Agricola and Iulia Dubitata, living, erected this for themselves and their son.
EDCS-26001254.summary: This is a family tombstone recording the death of a young boy, Titus Flavius Germanus, and erected by his parents, Titus Flavius Agricola and Iulia Dubitata, during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26001255.text_edition: \n Petronius Rufus annorum XX hic situs est Iulia Petroni liberta Urbana viva fecit sibi\n
EDCS-26001255.translation: Petronius Rufus, aged 20 years, lies here. Iulia Urbana, freedwoman of Petronius, erected this for herself while alive.
EDCS-26001255.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Petronius Rufus, aged 20, and was erected by his freedwoman, Iulia Urbana, during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26001256.text_edition: \n Sattulus Tai filius annorum LV hic situs est et Pusinna filia annorum XV\n
EDCS-26001256.translation: Sattulus, son of Tai, aged 55 years, lies here, and his daughter Pusinna, aged 15 years.
EDCS-26001256.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Sattulus and his daughter Pusinna, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26001258.text_edition: \n Vala Saturionis filia annorum XX hic sita est pater filiae posuit\n
EDCS-26001258.translation: Vala, daughter of Saturio, aged 20 years, lies here. Her father erected this for his daughter.
EDCS-26001258.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Vala, daughter of Saturio, aged 20, and was erected by her father, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26100488.text_edition: \n Legio XIIII gemina Martia victrix cohors I princeps prior centuria Terentii Vernae\n
EDCS-26100488.translation: The Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix, the First Cohort, the first regiment, the century of Terentius Verna.
EDCS-26100488.summary: This inscription identifies a specific military unit and sub-unit, the first century of Terentius Verna within the First Cohort of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26100490.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Lollius Clarus pro se et suis votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-26100490.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Lollius Clarus fulfilled his vow for himself and his family willingly.
EDCS-26100490.summary: This votive inscription records Lucius Lollius Clarus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for himself and his family, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26100492.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Apollini et Sironae Aesculapio Publius Aelius Lucius |(centurio) legionis X votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-26100492.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Apollo and Sirona, to Aesculapius. Publius Aelius Lucius, centurion of the Tenth Legion, fulfilled his vow willingly, gladly, deservedly.
EDCS-26100492.summary: This votive inscription records Publius Aelius Lucius, a centurion of the Tenth Legion, fulfilling vows to Jupiter, Apollo, Sirona, and Aesculapius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26100497.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Ulpius Genialis miles legionis X votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-26100497.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Ulpius Genialis, soldier of the Tenth Legion, fulfilled his vow willingly, gladly, deservedly.
EDCS-26100497.summary: This votive inscription records Ulpius Genialis, a soldier of the Tenth Legion, dedicating something to Silvanus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26100498.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Sor Nionis servus dispensator annonae Norici Augusti\n
EDCS-26100498.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, from Sorus, freedman and administrator of the grain supply of Noricum, of the Emperor.
EDCS-26100498.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus, made by a freedman serving as an administrator of the grain supply in Noricum, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-26100499.text_edition: \n R R nio |(centurio) legionis X geminae piae fidelis Severianae Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26100499.translation: Nio, centurion of the tenth legion Gemina, Pious, Faithful, Severian, Antoninian, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-26100499.summary: This is a votive inscription by a centurion of the Legio X Gemina, likely from the Severan or Antonine period, recording the fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-26100500.text_edition: \n mil--- legionis X geminae Severianae O annos XXXV et Pro vixit nnos EXVOT VI vixit annos Aurelius Ursus \n
EDCS-26100500.translation: Milo, XXXV years old, and Pro... lived XV years. Aurelius Ursus lived VI years.
EDCS-26100500.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a funerary monument recording the ages of two individuals, Milo and Pro, and likely a third person, Aurelius Ursus, possibly associated with the Legio X Gemina.

EDCS-26100502.text_edition: \n Gemellae et Primo filiis et Tutoni Mogetius posuit\n
EDCS-26100502.translation: Mogetius dedicated this to Gemella and Primus, his sons, and Tutus.
EDCS-26100502.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Mogetius for his sons Gemella and Primus, and Tutus.

EDCS-26100504.text_edition: \n D M Caius Mus Cattus TI legio X\n
EDCS-26100504.translation: To the departed spirits, Gaius Mus Cattus, serving in the 10th legion.
EDCS-26100504.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the 'Di Manes' by Gaius Mus Cattus, a soldier in the Legio X.

EDCS-26100505.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimus et Quieta vivi sibi Paterno parentes fecerunt\n
EDCS-26100505.translation: To the departed spirits, Septimus and Quieta, while living, made this for their parent Pater, [or] for their child Pater.
EDCS-26100505.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to the 'Di Manes' by Septimus and Quieta for their parent or child named Pater, made during their own lifetime.

EDCS-26100507.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sitto Ettoni convirginiae vivus obitae posuit annorum XXIII\n
EDCS-26100507.translation: To the departed spirits, Sitto dedicated this, while living, for Ettonia, his wife, who died at the age of twenty-three.
EDCS-26100507.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Sitto during his lifetime for his deceased wife Ettonia, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-26100508.translation: The chief magistrate for five years undertook its construction.
EDCS-26100508.summary: This inscription records that the duovir quinquennalis (a type of local magistrate) was responsible for the construction of this monument.

EDCS-26100510.translation: Of the century of Romulus, of the century of Attus.
EDCS-26100510.summary: This inscription likely marks the boundary or ownership of land belonging to the centuries of Romulus and Attus, possibly related to military land grants.

EDCS-26600108.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus tribunicia potestate <X=A>V <c=G>onsul III et Imperator Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus tribunicia potestate consul iterum divi Antonini <f=E>ilii divi Hadriani nepotes divi Traiani Parthici pronepotes divi divi Nervae abnepotes \n
EDCS-26600108.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, with tribunician power 15, consul III; and Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, with tribunician power, consul II; sons of the divine Antoninus, grandsons of the divine Hadrian, great-grandsons of the divine Trajanus Parthicus, great-great-grandsons of the divine Nerva.
EDCS-26600108.summary: This inscription is a formal titulature of Emperors Marcus Aurelius and Lucius Verus, listing their honours and extensive imperial ancestry, dating to around 175-176 AD based on the tribunician power.

EDCS-26600109.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Felix Pertinax \n
EDCS-26600109.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Felix Pertinax
EDCS-26600109.summary: This is a formal titulary of Emperor Septimius Severus, likely part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-26600110.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis VIIII imperator XI consul II proconsul pater patriae et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix tribuniciae potestatis IIII proconsul et Publius Septimius Geta Caesar vias et pontes vetustate conruptas restituerunt curante Fabio Cilone legato Augusti pro praetore\n
EDCS-26600110.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus, chief priest, with the ninth tribunician power, emperor for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, proconsul, father of the country, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix, with the fourth tribunician power, proconsul, and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar restored roads and bridges corrupted by age, under the supervision of Fabius Cilo, imperial legate with praetorian power.
EDCS-26600110.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, overseen by the legate Fabius Cilo, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600112.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XI consul II proconsul pater patriae et imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix tribunicia potestate proconsul et Publius Septimius Geta Caesar vias et pontes vetustate conruptas restituerunt\n
EDCS-26600112.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, chief priest, with the ninth tribunician power, emperor for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, proconsul, father of the country, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix, proconsul, and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar restored roads and bridges corrupted by age.
EDCS-26600112.summary: This inscription records the restoration of roads and bridges by Emperors Septimius Severus, Caracalla, and Geta, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600114.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IX imperator XI consul II proconsul pater patriae et Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix tribunicia potestate IV et Publius Sep[timius Geta ------\n
EDCS-26600114.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus maximus, chief priest, with the ninth tribunician power, emperor for the eleventh time, consul for the second time, proconsul, father of the country, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus Felix, with the fourth tribunician power, and Publius Sep[timius Geta ------
EDCS-26600114.summary: This fragmentary inscription names Emperors Septimius Severus and Caracalla, and begins to name Publius Septimius Geta, likely from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600116.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavius Attius cives sic Trever eques alae I nnanefatium stipendiorum VII hic situs est heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600116.translation: To the departed spirits. Flavius Attius, a citizen of Trier, a cavalryman of the I Ala nnanefatium, with seven years of service, is buried here. His heir had this made.
EDCS-26600116.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Flavius Attius, a cavalryman serving in the Ala I nnanefatium, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-26600117.text_edition: \n Lucius Marcius Sexti filius Scaptia miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXXIIX stipendiorum XVII hic situs est frater posuit\n
EDCS-26600117.translation: Lucius Marcius, son of Sextus, of the Scaptia tribe, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 38, with 17 years of service, is buried here. His brother erected this.
EDCS-26600117.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Marcius, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, erected by his brother, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-26600118.text_edition: \n Quinti filius Publilia Mercator miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XX stipendiorum VIII hic situs est \n
EDCS-26600118.translation: Publilia, wife of Quintus, daughter of Quintus, Mercator, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 20, with 8 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-26600118.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion named Mercator, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-26600119.text_edition: \n Publius Quintius Publi filius Publilia veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris nnrum LX \n
EDCS-26600119.translation: Publius Quintius, son of Publius, of the Publilia tribe, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, aged 60.
EDCS-26600119.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Publius Quintius, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris legion, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-26600120.text_edition: \n Claudia Rufus Ara Agrippinensium miles legionis XV Apollinaris \n
EDCS-26600120.translation: Claudia Rufus, from the tribe of the Agrippinensium, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion.
EDCS-26600120.summary: This is an incomplete inscription identifying Claudia Rufus as a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-26600122.text_edition: \n miles legionis XV Apollinaris tubicen annorum stipendiorum XVII Marcus Tenatius Luci filius Publilia heres de suo fecit\n
EDCS-26600122.translation: A soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, a trumpeter, aged [--], with 17 years of service. Marcus Tenatius, son of Lucius, of the Publilia tribe, his heir, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-26600122.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a trumpeter soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, erected by his heir Marcus Tenatius, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-26600123.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Cai filius Sergia Hier(apolis tubicen miles legionis XV Apollinaris stipendiorum XVI nnorum XXXVI hic situs est vivite felices quibus est data longior hora vixi ego dum licuit dulciter ad Superos dicite si merui sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-26600123.translation: Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, from Hierapolis, a trumpeter, soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, with sixteen years of service and thirty-six years of age, is buried here. Live happily, you for whom a longer span of life has been granted! I lived sweetly as long as I could. Tell the gods above: if I deserved it, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26600123.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Valerius, a trumpeter in the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, who died at 36 years old with 16 years of service.

EDCS-26600124.text_edition: \n Titus Valerius Titi Claudia Quartus Viruno miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Eceti Primi annorum XXXX stipendiorum XVII heres posuit\n
EDCS-26600124.translation: Titus Valerius Quartus, son of Titus, of the Claudia tribe, from Virunum, a soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, in the century of Ecetus Primus, forty years of age and with seventeen years of service, his heir erected this.
EDCS-26600124.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Valerius Quartus, a soldier in the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, erected by his heir.

EDCS-26600125.text_edition: \n veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum LXXXV hic situs est et Valerio Rufo \n
EDCS-26600125.translation: A veteran of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, eighty-five years of age, is buried here, and Valerius Rufus.
EDCS-26600125.summary: This tombstone records the burial of a veteran of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, aged 85, and Valerius Rufus.

EDCS-26600126.text_edition: \n Caius Vibius Cai filius Stellatina Secundus miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XLV stipendiorum XXI hic situs est ex testamento\n
EDCS-26600126.translation: Gaius Vibius Secundus, son of Gaius, of the Stellatina tribe, a soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, forty-five years of age and with twenty-one years of service, is buried here, in accordance with his will.
EDCS-26600126.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Vibius Secundus, a soldier in the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, who died at 45 years old with 21 years of service, his burial arranged by his will.

EDCS-26600127.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai Claudia Secundus Savaria miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Iuli Celerini vixit nnos XXVI menses VII\n
EDCS-26600127.translation: Gaius Iulius Secundus, son of Gaius, of the Claudia tribe, from Savaria, a soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, in the century of Iulius Celerinus, lived for 26 years and 7 months.
EDCS-26600127.summary: This tombstone records the life of Gaius Iulius Secundus, a soldier in the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, who died at 26 years and 7 months old.

EDCS-26600128.text_edition: \n Lucius Longinius Valens Claudia Ara miles legionis XV Apollinaris veteranus annorum XXXX \n
EDCS-26600128.translation: Lucius Longinius Valens, of the Claudia tribe, from Ara, a soldier and veteran of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, forty years of age.
EDCS-26600128.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Longinius Valens, a soldier and veteran of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, who died at 40 years old.

EDCS-26600129.text_edition: \n Quintus Magius Quinti filius Pu miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XL stipendiorum XXII posuerunt frater et Sextus Sabinus eques\n
EDCS-26600129.translation: Quintus Magius, son of Quintus, of the Pu tribe, a soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, forty years of age and with twenty-two years of service, was buried here by his brother and Sextus Sabinus, an equestrian.
EDCS-26600129.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Quintus Magius, a soldier in the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, aged 40 with 22 years of service, by his brother and Sextus Sabinus.

EDCS-26600130.text_edition: \n Lucius Cautius Luci filius Veturia miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXXV stipendiorum XIIX hic situs est Publius Atilius et Titus Herennius heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-26600130.translation: Lucius Cautius, son of Lucius, of the Veturia tribe, a soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, thirty-five years of age and with twelve years of service, is buried here. Publius Atilius and Titus Herennius, his heirs, erected this.
EDCS-26600130.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Lucius Cautius, a soldier in the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, who died at 35 years old with 12 years of service, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-26600131.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Luci filius Firmus Teretina Arelate miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXX stipendiorum X hic situs est Caius Vibius Cai filius miles legionis XV Apollinaris tubicen heres posuit\n
EDCS-26600131.translation: Lucius Cornelius Firmus, son of Lucius, of the Teretina tribe, from Arelate, a soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, thirty years of age and with ten years of service, is buried here. Gaius Vibius, son of Gaius, a trumpeter and soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, his heir, erected this.
EDCS-26600131.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Lucius Cornelius Firmus, a soldier and trumpeter in the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, aged 30 with 10 years of service, erected by his heir.

EDCS-26600132.text_edition: \n Titus Exomnius Craxa miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Iuliani nnorum XXX \n
EDCS-26600132.translation: Titus Exomnius, from Craxa, a soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, in the century of Iulianus, thirty years of age.
EDCS-26600132.summary: This tombstone records the age of Titus Exomnius, a soldier in the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, who died at 30 years old.

EDCS-26600133.text_edition: \n Cai filius Silla miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXI stipendiorum II hic situs est Baebia C \n
EDCS-26600133.translation: Silla, son of Cai, a soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, aged 21, with 2 years of service, is buried here. Baebia C.
EDCS-26600133.summary: A funerary epitaph for Silla, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600134.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo posuit Rubrius Martialis |(centurio) legionis XV Apollinaris\n
EDCS-26600134.translation: Rubrius Martialis, a centurion of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, dedicated this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-26600134.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Rubrius Martialis, a centurion of the XV Apollinaris legion, probably from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600135.text_edition: \n Quintus Caecilius Luci filius Arnensi Felix T Nineianus \n
EDCS-26600135.translation: Quintus Caecilius Felix, son of Lucius, of the Arnensis tribe, from Nineveh.
EDCS-26600135.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording the name and tribal affiliation of Quintus Caecilius Felix, possibly a citizen from Nineveh, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600136.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae fautori imperii sui Iovii et Herculii religiosissimi Augusti et Caesares sacrarium restituerunt\n
EDCS-26600136.translation: The most religious Augusti and Caesars restored this sanctuary to the Unconquered God Mithras, patron of their empire, and to Jupiter and Hercules.
EDCS-26600136.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a sanctuary to Mithras, Jupiter, and Hercules by the emperors, highlighting Mithras's role as a patron of the empire, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600137.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrhae Caius Sacidius Barbarus |(centurio) legionis XV Apollinaris ex voto [] [[]] [[]]\n
EDCS-26600137.translation: Gaius Sacidius Barbarus, a centurion of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, fulfilled his vow to the Unconquered Mithras.
EDCS-26600137.summary: A votive inscription to Mithras by Gaius Sacidius Barbarus, a centurion of the XV Apollinaris legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600138.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Valerius Victorinus optio legionis X geminae votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-26600138.translation: Valerius Victorinus, a deputy centurion (optio) of the tenth Gemina legion, willingly paid his vow to the Unconquered God.
EDCS-26600138.summary: A votive inscription to the Unconquered God by Valerius Victorinus, an optio of the X Gemina legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600139.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Taurinus optio legionis XIIII geminae Antoninianae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-26600139.translation: Taurinus, deputy centurion (optio) of the fourteenth Gemina Antoniniana legion, willingly and joyfully fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domesticanus, as deserved.
EDCS-26600139.summary: A votive inscription to Silvanus Domesticanus by Taurinus, an optio of the XIV Gemina Antoniniana legion, possibly from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600142.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Marci filius Quirina Dasius Sirmio miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria optio annorum XL stipendiorum XX hic situs est Statius Firmus heres fecit\n
EDCS-26600142.translation: Marcus Ulpius Dasius, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, from Sirmio, a soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, deputy centurion (optio), aged 40, with 20 years of service, is buried here. Statius Firmus, the heir, made this.
EDCS-26600142.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Dasius, a soldier and optio of the XV Apollinaris legion, who served for 20 years, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600143.text_edition: \n Aelius Verissimus praefectus collegii quinquennale scholae Variano in honorem collegi posuit Arriano et Papo consulibus VIII Kalendas Ianuarias\n
EDCS-26600143.translation: Aelius Verissimus, prefect of the quinquennial college of the Varian school, dedicated this in honor of his college, during the consulship of Arrianus and Papus, on the eighth day before the Kalends of January.
EDCS-26600143.summary: An inscription recording the dedication of a monument by Aelius Verissimus, prefect of a college, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE, given the consuls and the date.

EDCS-26600144.text_edition: \n les et s Adauctus magistri collegiorum veteranorum centonariorum impensis suis posuerunt\n
EDCS-26600144.translation: Adauctus and [---]ius, masters of the colleges of veteran centonarii, erected this at their own expense.
EDCS-26600144.summary: An inscription recording the erection of a monument by the masters of veteran colleges of centonarii, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600145.text_edition: \n centuria Marci Alpini Agrippae hastati posterioris\n
EDCS-26600145.translation: Of the century of Marcus Alpinus Agrippa, posterior hastatus.
EDCS-26600145.summary: This inscription marks a military unit, likely a tombstone or marker for the century of Marcus Alpinus Agrippa, dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600146.text_edition: \n Caius Arruntius Cai filius Pollia Assta Primus veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum LXXXV hic situs est filius pro pietate posuit\n
EDCS-26600146.translation: Gaius Arruntius Primus, son of Gaius, of the Pollia tribe, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, aged 85, is buried here; his son placed this monument out of filial piety.
EDCS-26600146.summary: This tombstone records the death of Gaius Arruntius Primus, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, and was erected by his son, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600147.text_edition: \n Lucius Aurelius Claudia Celer Ara miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Cassi Flaviani stipendiorum XXIII nnorum X liberti eius posuerunt hic situs est\n
EDCS-26600147.translation: Lucius Aurelius Celer, son of Claudia, of the Ara tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, with 23 years of service, aged 40, is buried here; his freedmen placed this monument.
