EDCS-26600147.summary: This tombstone commemorates Lucius Aurelius Celer, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected by his freedmen, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600148.text_edition: \n Abilus Turanci filius domo Lucocadiacus eques alae Pannoniorum nnorum XLIII stipendiorum XXIII hic situs est ex testamento heredes posuerunt Bovegius Vemini filius Lanciensis Pentius Dovideri filius Aligantiensis\n
EDCS-26600148.translation: Abilus, son of Turancius, from Lucocadia, a cavalryman of the Pannonian ala, aged 43, with 23 years of service, is buried here; his heirs placed this monument according to his will: Bovegius, son of Veminius, from Lancium, and Pentius, son of Doviderus, from Aligantia.
EDCS-26600148.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Abilus, a cavalryman from the Pannonian ala, with his heirs and witnesses named, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600149.text_edition: \n ? Priscus Elmalli filius Cittus eques alae nnniorum annorum XLIIII stipendiorum XXIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-26600149.translation: Priscus, son of Elmalius, from Citta, a cavalryman of the ala, aged 44, with 23 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-26600149.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Priscus, a cavalryman of an unspecified ala, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600150.text_edition: \n Quintus Lurius Quinti filius Pupinia Maxumus sic veteranus legionis XV nnorum LXV hic situs est titulum fieri iussit\n
EDCS-26600150.translation: Quintus Lurius Maximus, son of Quintus, of the Pupinia tribe, a veteran of the 15th legion, aged 65, is buried here; he ordered this monument to be made.
EDCS-26600150.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Quintus Lurius Maximus, a veteran of the 15th legion, who himself commissioned the monument, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600151.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a place of origin, possibly a tombstone, from the Roman period.

EDCS-26600153.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum Publius Aelius Romanus veteranus ex actario legionis X geminae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-26600153.translation: Sacred to Diana. Publius Aelius Romanus, a veteran from the records office of the 10th Gemina legion, willingly and gladly fulfilled his vow according to his deserts.
EDCS-26600153.summary: This dedication records a vow made to Diana by Publius Aelius Romanus, a veteran from the 10th Gemina legion, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600154.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Marcus Sattellius Eros votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600154.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Marcus Sattellius Eros fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-26600154.summary: This dedication records a vow made to Hercules Augustus by Marcus Sattellius Eros, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600155.text_edition: \n Isidi Augustae et Bubasti Gaius Pomponius Philinus Pomponi Severi libertus votum solvit\n
EDCS-26600155.translation: To Isis Augusta and Bubastis. Gaius Pomponius Philinus, freedman of Pomponius Severus, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600155.summary: This dedication records a vow made to Isis Augusta and Bubastis by Gaius Pomponius Philinus, a freedman, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-26600156.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Sextus Proculeius Luci filius Lemonia Rufus veteranus legionis XV posuuit\n
EDCS-26600156.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Sextus Proculeius Rufus, son of Lucius, of the Lemonia tribe, a veteran of the Fifteenth Legion, erected this.
EDCS-26600156.summary: This dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus was set up by Sextus Proculeius Rufus, a veteran of the Fifteenth Legion.

EDCS-26600157.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae Lucius Avitius Maturus decurio coloniae Karnunti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600157.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras. Lucius Avitius Maturus, a decurio of the colony of Carnuntum, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600157.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras by Lucius Avitius Maturus, a decurio of Carnuntum.

EDCS-26600158.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae sacrum Iulius Saturninus ex voto posuit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600158.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered God Mithras. Iulius Saturninus erected this, willingly and deservedly, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600158.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to the Unconquered God Mithras by Iulius Saturninus.

EDCS-26600159.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Septimius Iustianus armorum custos legionis XIIII geminae Antoninianae votum solvit\n
EDCS-26600159.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, Septimius Iustianus, keeper of the arms of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600159.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras by Septimius Iustianus, an official responsible for military equipment.

EDCS-26600160.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Septimius Iustianus armorum custos legionis XIIII geminae Antonianae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600160.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, Septimius Iustianus, keeper of the arms of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Antoniniana, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600160.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras by Septimius Iustianus, an official responsible for military equipment.

EDCS-26600162.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae sacrum Caius Valerius Veieas nomine suo et coniugis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600162.translation: Sacred to Nemesis Augusta. Caius Valerius Veieas, in his own name and that of his wife, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600162.summary: This votive inscription is dedicated to Nemesis Augusta by Caius Valerius Veieas for himself and his wife.

EDCS-26600163.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto Marcus Appianius Ursulus Agrippinensis |(centurio) legionis V piae fidelis |(centurio) legionis C |(centurio) legionis \n
EDCS-26600163.translation: To Silvanus Augustus. Marcus Appianius Ursulus of Agrippinensis, a centurion of the Fifth Legion Pia Fidelis, a centurion of the [Thirteenth?] Legion, a centurion of the [Third?] Legion.
EDCS-26600163.summary: This inscription records the military career of Marcus Appianius Ursulus, a centurion who served in multiple legions, likely as a dedication to Silvanus Augustus.

EDCS-26600164.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Tiberius Iulius Quintilianus decurio municipii Flavi Scarbantiae quaestor pecuniae publicae aedilis IIvir iure dicundo auguratus at sic pristinam speciem restituit\n
EDCS-26600164.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Tiberius Iulius Quintilianus, a decurio of the Flavian municipality of Scarbantia, quaestor of public funds, aedile, duovir with judicial authority, restored its former appearance after his augurship.
EDCS-26600164.summary: This dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Tiberius Iulius Quintilianus, a prominent local official in Scarbantia, records his restoration of a public building.

EDCS-26600165.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Vanamius eques alae I Hispanorum Tiberius Claudius Aplo eques alae heres posuit\n
EDCS-26600165.translation: Tiberius Claudius Vanamius, an **eques** of the First Wing of Spaniards, and Tiberius Claudius Aplo, an **eques** of the Wing, his heir, erected this.
EDCS-26600165.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Tiberius Claudius Vanamius, a cavalryman of the First Wing of Spaniards, and Tiberius Claudius Aplo, his heir.

EDCS-26600166.text_edition: \n Publius Pompeius Publi filius Voltinia Colonus Viana veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae felicis annorum LXX hic situs est titulum fieri iussit arbitratu Pompeiae Fuscae libertae\n
EDCS-26600166.translation: Publius Pompeius Colonus, son of Publius, of the Voltinia tribe, from Viana, a veteran of the IV Flavia Felix legion, aged 70, lies here; he ordered this tombstone to be made under the supervision of Pompeia Fusca, his freedwoman.
EDCS-26600166.summary: A funerary epitaph for a 70-year-old veteran of the Legio IV Flavia Felix, commissioned by his freedwoman. The inscription is likely from the Roman Empire period.

EDCS-26600167.text_edition: \n Marcus Vibius Cai filius Aniensis veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit arbiter Luci Larti et Titi Mursi heredum\n
EDCS-26600167.translation: Marcus Vibius, son of Caius, of the Aniensis tribe, a veteran of the fifteenth legion Apollinaris, aged 21, is buried here; he ordered this to be made by his will, with Lucius Lartius and Titus Mursius as arbiters of his heirs.
EDCS-26600167.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Vibius, a veteran of the fifteenth legion Apollinaris, who died at age 21 and arranged for his tomb by will.

EDCS-26600168.summary: This very fragmentary inscription records a man who served as a military tribune, with no further details provided.

EDCS-26600169.text_edition: \n Lucius Flavius Fortunatus sevir Augustalis municipii Scarbantiae NAIIXV et SAN et MEL MON nno--- XXXV\n
EDCS-26600169.translation: Lucius Flavius Fortunatus, Augustalis sevir of the municipality of Scarbantia, aged 45, in his [---] year of service.
EDCS-26600169.summary: This inscription records Lucius Flavius Fortunatus, an Augustalis sevir in Scarbantia, noting his age and years of service.

EDCS-26600170.text_edition: \n Titus Canius Titi libertus Cinnamus negotiator annorum LXX hic situs est Titus Canius Titi filius Aeternalis Lucius Canius Titi filius Cinna filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-26600170.translation: Titus Canius Cinnamus, freedman of Titus, a merchant, aged 70, is buried here; Titus Canius Aeternalis, son of Titus, and Lucius Canius Cinna, sons of Titus, erected this.
EDCS-26600170.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Canius Cinnamus, a freedman and merchant, with his tomb erected by his sons.

EDCS-26600171.text_edition: \n Publius Domatius Publi filius Teretina Citio negotiator hic situs est et Domatiae Publi libertae Vimpiae coniugi eius Publius Domatius Peregrinus parentibus posuit\n
EDCS-26600171.translation: Publius Domatius Peregrinus placed this for his parents: Publius Domatius Citio, son of Publius, of the Teretina tribe, a merchant, is buried here, and for his wife Domatia Vimpia, freedwoman of Publius.
EDCS-26600171.summary: This funerary inscription records Publius Domatius Peregrinus dedicating a tomb for his merchant father, Publius Domatius Citio, and his freedwoman wife, Domatia Vimpia.

EDCS-26600172.text_edition: \n Aulus Terentius Auli filius Dento annorum XX Aulus Terentius Auli filius Martialiis annorum XXV hR(ic) siti sunt nati Salonis Iulia Titia Titi filia mater viva filiis suis et sibi posuit\n
EDCS-26600172.translation: Aulus Terentius Dento, son of Aulus, aged 20, and Aulus Terentius Martialis, son of Aulus, aged 25, are buried here, born in Salona; Iulia Titia, daughter of Titus, their mother, while alive, placed this for her sons and herself.
EDCS-26600172.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two sons, Aulus Terentius Dento and Aulus Terentius Martialis, with the tomb erected by their mother, Iulia Titia.

EDCS-26600173.text_edition: \n Culcia Cai liberta Iucunda annorum XXC Gemella Cai Culci Secundi filia anni I hic sitae sunt Caius Culcius Ingenuos sic Caius Culcius Primus Caius Culcius Secundus Culcia Prisca matri et neptiae posuerunt\n
EDCS-26600173.translation: Culcia Iucunda, freedwoman of Caius, aged 20, and Gemella, daughter of Caius Culcius Secundus, aged 1, are buried here; Caius Culcius Ingenuos, Caius Culcius Primus, and Caius Culcius Secundus erected this for their mother and granddaughter.
EDCS-26600173.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Culcia Iucunda and Gemella, with the tomb erected by Caius Culcius Ingenuos, Caius Culcius Primus, and Caius Culcius Secundus for their mother and niece.

EDCS-26600174.text_edition: \n Sextus Erbonius Sexti filius Melantio nnorum XIII hic situs est Sextus Erbonius filius Asclepiodotus pater posuit\n
EDCS-26600174.translation: Sextus Erbonius, son of Sextus, aged 13, is buried here; his father Sextus Erbonius Asclepiodotus placed this.
EDCS-26600174.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Sextus Erbonius, aged 13, with the tomb erected by his father, Sextus Erbonius Asclepiodotus.

EDCS-26600175.text_edition: \n Caio Farragi Cai liberto Ascanio annorum L hic situs Caius Farrax Iucundus patronus posuit\n
EDCS-26600175.translation: To Caius Farragi Ascanio, freedman of Caius, aged 50, buried here; his patron Caius Farrax Iucundus placed this.
EDCS-26600175.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Caius Farragi Ascanio, a freedman aged 50, with the tomb erected by his patron.

EDCS-26600176.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Successus annorum LXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-26600176.translation: Titus Flavius Successus, aged 70, is buried here.
EDCS-26600176.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Flavius Successus, aged 70.

EDCS-26600177.text_edition: \n Gaius Flavi Vitalis annorum XL- hic situs est Verna conservus sua pecunia\n
EDCS-26600177.translation: Gaius Flavius Vitalis, aged 40, is buried here. Verna, fellow slave, with her own money.
EDCS-26600177.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Gaius Flavius Vitalis, aged 40, erected by his fellow slave Verna.

EDCS-26600178.text_edition: \n Iulia Tiberi liberta Donata nnorum XXV hic sita est Tiberius Iulius Princeps libertae et nutrici posuit\n
EDCS-26600178.translation: Iulia Donata, freedwoman of Tiberius, aged 25, is buried here. Tiberius Iulius Princeps erected this for his freedwoman and nurse.
EDCS-26600178.summary: A tombstone for Iulia Donata, aged 25, erected by Tiberius Iulius Princeps, likely her former master, who also identifies her as his nurse.

EDCS-26600179.text_edition: \n lia Quarta TXII Septimi-- VediCPEMAMVS coniunx Quintus Mamios filius SILIOHTVLO\n
EDCS-26600179.translation: lia Quarta, aged 12, wife of Septimius... [ ]... and Quintus Mamilius Siliohtulus.
EDCS-26600179.summary: A fragmented tombstone for Lia Quarta, aged 12, and Quintus Mamilius Siliohtulus, wife and possibly husband.

EDCS-26600180.text_edition: \n Lucius Sempronius Catumarus annorum L Sempronius Iustus annorum XX hic siti sunt Sempronia Catta viva sibi et coniugi filio posuit his opto terram levem\n
EDCS-26600180.translation: Lucius Sempronius Catumarus, aged 50, and Sempronius Iustus, aged 20, are buried here. Sempronia Catta erected this for herself, her husband, and son while alive. May the earth lie lightly upon them.
EDCS-26600180.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Sempronius Catumarus (50) and Sempronius Iustus (20), erected by Sempronia Catta for herself, her husband, and son.

EDCS-26600181.text_edition: \n Teuriscus Tutiae filius nnorum XX hic situs est Ammoda mater sibi et filio viva fecit Coccus et Cassio fratres posuerint sic\n
EDCS-26600181.translation: Teuriscus, son of Tutia, aged 20, is buried here. Ammoda, mother, made this for herself and her son while alive. His brothers Coccus and Cassius erected this.
EDCS-26600181.summary: A tombstone for Teuriscus, aged 20, erected by his mother Ammoda for herself and her son, with further erection by his brothers Coccus and Cassius.

EDCS-26600182.text_edition: \n Liccai fili-- nnorum XXX hic sit-- est frater posuit\n
EDCS-26600182.translation: [ ] son of Liccaius, aged 30, is buried here. His brother erected this.
EDCS-26600182.summary: A fragmented tombstone for an individual aged 30, son of Liccaius, erected by his brother.

EDCS-26600184.text_edition: \n Claudio Galonio annorum XXV scribae Mursellae hic situs est et Claudio Nigrino nnorum LXXXV et Ulpiae Finitae annorum LX Claudia Gallonia ob memoriam fratris et parentibus vivis faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600184.translation: For Claudius Galo, aged 25, scribe of Mursella, is buried here, and for Claudius Nigrinus, aged 85, and for Ulpia Finita, aged 60. Claudia Gallonia arranged for this to be made in memory of her brother and for her parents while they were alive.
EDCS-26600184.summary: A tombstone erected by Claudia Gallonia for her brother Claudius Galo (25), scribe of Mursella, and her parents Claudius Nigrinus (85) and Ulpia Finita (60).

EDCS-26600185.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae Gaius Iulius Maximianus veteranus ex signifero Victorino et Severo consulibus\n
EDCS-26600185.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to Juno Regina. Gaius Iulius Maximianus, veteran, during the consulship of Victorinus and Severus.
EDCS-26600185.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and Juno by Gaius Iulius Maximianus, a veteran, during the consulship of Victorinus and Severus.

EDCS-26600186.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Nymphidius Sabinus prae<f=L>(ectus) \n
EDCS-26600186.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Caius Nymphidius Sabinus, prefect.
EDCS-26600186.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest by Caius Nymphidius Sabinus, identified as a prefect.

EDCS-26600187.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Treboniano Gallo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli patri patriae proconsuli eqquites alae III Augustae Thracum Gallianae Volusianae devoti numini maiestatique eorum sic\n
EDCS-26600187.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, Holder of Tribune Power for the 2nd time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul. The equites of the 3rd Ala Augusta Thracum Galliana Volusiana, devoted to their divinity and majesty.
EDCS-26600187.summary: A dedication from the equites of the 3rd Ala Augusta Thracum Galliana Volusiana to Emperor Trebonianus Gallus, acknowledging his titles and their devotion.

EDCS-26600188.text_edition: \n Herculi Invicto pro salute Augusti Aelius Martinus magister collegii iuventutis ob honorem collegii suprascripti donum dedit\n
EDCS-26600188.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered, for the safety of Augustus, Aelius Martinus, master of the college of youth, gave this gift in honor of the aforesaid college.
EDCS-26600188.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules, likely made in the 2nd or 3rd century CE, by a college official for the emperor's well-being.

EDCS-26600189.text_edition: \n Deae Balti Flavia Victorina pro se et pro suis omnibus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600189.translation: Flavia Victorina has willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the goddess Baltis, for herself and all her family.
EDCS-26600189.summary: This is a votive inscription to the goddess Baltis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, by a woman named Flavia Victorina.

EDCS-26600190.text_edition: \n Imperator- Caesar- Marc-- Aureli-- Antonin-- S RI SC P\n
EDCS-26600190.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, Augustus...
EDCS-26600190.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to name Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely from the mid-3rd century CE, but its purpose is unclear.

EDCS-26600191.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus aeternae quieti eti perpetuae securitati Aureliae Aeternae coniugi rarissimae quae vixit nnos XXX et Tiberio Claudio Macedoni qui vixit nnos X menses III et Claudiae Iustinae quae vixit nnos III menses VI et Claudiae Ligurinae quae vixit nnum I menses VII filiis eius et Ulpiae Faventinae Tiberius Claudius Valentinus veteranus ex custode armorum legionis I adiutricis et coniugi et filiis et socrui karissimis faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600191.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld and for eternal rest and perpetual peace, Tiberius Claudius Valentinus, a veteran and formerly a guard of arms of the I Adiutrix legion, had this made for his dearest Aurelia Aeterna, his wife, who lived 30 years; and for his sons Tiberius Claudius Macedo, who lived 10 years 3 months, and Claudius Iustinus, who lived 3 years 6 months, and Claudia Ligurina, who lived 1 year 7 months; and for Ulpia Faventina, his mother-in-law.
EDCS-26600191.summary: This is a funerary monument from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, set up by a veteran for his deceased wife, three children, and mother-in-law.

EDCS-26600192.text_edition: \n Bato Dasentis filius annorum XXV explorator Dases pater posuit <h=FI>(ic) situs est \n
EDCS-26600192.translation: Bato, son of Dasentis, aged 25, scout. His father Dases placed [this here]. He lies here.
EDCS-26600192.summary: This is a tombstone from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, commemorating a scout named Bato, set up by his father.

EDCS-26600193.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et perpetuae securitati im sic memoriam Metili Valentini quondam immaginiferi sic legionis I adiutricis Aurelia Maximina sibi et filio viva faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600193.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld and for perpetual peace, in memory of Metilius Valentinus, formerly a standard-bearer of the I Adiutrix legion, Aurelia Maximina had this made for herself and her son while she was alive.
EDCS-26600193.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, dedicated by a woman to her deceased husband and for herself and her son.

EDCS-26600194.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerio Saturnino veterano ex duplicario alae I Ulpiae contariorum qui vixit annos L et Valerio Sabino militi legionis I adiutricis stipendiorum III vixit annos XXV filio Caesia Digna marito et filio faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600194.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Valerius Saturninus, veteran and former duplicarius of the I Ulpia Contariorum alae, who lived 50 years, and Valerius Sabinus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion with 3 years of service, who lived 25 years, his son. Caesia Digna had this made for her husband and son.
EDCS-26600194.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, commemorating a veteran and his son, erected by the wife and mother.

EDCS-26600195.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Victoriae Verinae coniugi pientissimae domu Foro Hadrianensi provincia Germania inferiori vixit nnos XXX Aemilius Deciminus medicus ordinarius legionis I adiutricis maritus bene meritae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600195.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Aemilius Deciminus, a regular medical officer of the I Adiutrix legion, had this made for his most devoted wife Victoria Verina, from Forum Hadriani in the province of Germania Inferior, who lived 30 years, his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-26600195.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, set up by a Roman army doctor for his deceased wife.

EDCS-26600196.text_edition: \n missi honesta missione Decembr-- Largo et Messalino consulibus votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-26600196.translation: Having been honorably discharged, they fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly, in the consulship of Decembrius Largo and Messalinus.
EDCS-26600196.summary: This inscription records soldiers fulfilling a vow after honorable discharge, dated by the consuls to an unspecified year, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600197.text_edition: \n Modiasio Lucio et Aureliae Valentinae socris pientissimis et Modiasiae Luciae coniugi karissimae quae vixit nnos XXV Aurelius Anthiochianus sic <d=F>ecurio municipii Brigetionis faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-26600197.translation: Aurelius Anthiochianus had this made for his most devoted parents-in-law, Modiasus Lucius and Aurelia Valentina, and for his dearest wife Modiasia Lucia, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-26600197.summary: This is a funerary monument likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE, set up by Aurelius Anthiochianus for his wife and parents-in-law.

EDCS-26600198.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Dasi Tasti vixit annos LXXX Anna Sammonis coniux et Aurelius Capito heredes titulum <f=E>aciendum curaverunt ex voluptate testamenti \n
EDCS-26600198.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Dasius Tastus lived 80 years. Anna Sammonis, his wife, and Aurelius Capito, his heirs, took care to have this monument made out of the pleasure expressed in his will.
EDCS-26600198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Dasius Tastus, set up by his wife and heirs according to his will, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600199.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fabio Marco qui vixit nnos XLVIII Iulia Eutichiane sic coniugi dulcissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600199.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Eutichiane erected this for her most beloved husband, Fabio Marco, who lived 48 years.
EDCS-26600199.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabio Marco, erected by his wife Iulia Eutichiane, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600200.text_edition: \n Memoriae Aureli Iuliani quondam tribuni cohortis I Alpinorum qui vixit annis Aelia Eniana marito suo\n
EDCS-26600200.translation: To the memory of Aurelius Iulianus, formerly a tribune of the I Alpine Cohort, who lived [years]. Aelia Eniana [erected this] for her husband.
EDCS-26600200.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Iulianus, a military officer, erected by his wife Aelia Eniana, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600201.text_edition: \n Apollini Daphnaeo sacrum Cnaeus Curius Lycao |(centurio) legionis I adiutricis\n
EDCS-26600201.translation: Sacred to Apollo Daphnaeus. Gnaeus Curius Lycao, centurion of the I Adjutant Legion.
EDCS-26600201.summary: This is a dedication to Apollo Daphnaeus by Gnaeus Curius Lycao, a centurion in the I Adjutant Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-26600202.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Curius Lycao |(centurio) legionis I adiutricis\n
EDCS-26600202.translation: Gnaeus Curius Lycao, centurion of the I Adjutant Legion.
EDCS-26600202.summary: This inscription identifies Gnaeus Curius Lycao as a centurion in the I Adjutant Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-26600203.text_edition: \n Genio centuriae Lucius Septimius Constans signifer votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600203.translation: To the Genius of the century. Lucius Septimius Constans, standard-bearer, gladly fulfilled his vow according to his deserts.
EDCS-26600203.summary: This is a votive offering to the Genius of the century by Lucius Septimius Constans, a standard-bearer, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600204.text_edition: \n Genio commerci et negotiantium Primitivus Iuli Procli conductoris VIII servus vilicus XX\n
EDCS-26600204.translation: To the Genius of the market and the merchants. Primitivus, the overseer of Iulus Proclus, [aged] 20; the steward, [aged] 20.
EDCS-26600204.summary: This dedication to the Genius of the market and merchants records two individuals, likely freedmen, with their roles and ages, possibly from the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-26600205.text_edition: \n Genio loci et Fortunae Conservatrici pro salute domini nostri Claudi Augusti Aurelius Superinus praefectus legionis I adiutricis agens vices legati ex voto posuit domino nostro Invicto Claudio Augusto et Paterno consulibus\n
EDCS-26600205.translation: For the safety of our lord Claudius Augustus, Aurelius Superinus, prefect of the I Adjutant Legion, acting as legate, dedicated this, according to his vow, to the Unconquered Lord Claudius Augustus and to Paternus, during their consulship.
EDCS-26600205.summary: This is a military dedication by Aurelius Superinus, prefect of the I Adjutant Legion, to Emperor Claudius Augustus and Paternus, mentioning their consulship, likely from the mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-26600206.text_edition: \n Iovem sic Iunonem sic Minervam sic Septimius Su \n
EDCS-26600206.translation: To Jupiter, so be it; to Juno, so be it; to Minerva, so be it. Septimius Su[...]
EDCS-26600206.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva by Septimius Su..., likely a personal vow or dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600208.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Aurelius Vegabius et Valentis principis\n
EDCS-26600208.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aurelius Vegabius and Valens, the princeps...
EDCS-26600208.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Vegabius and Valens, possibly a princeps (chief centurion or standard-bearer), likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-26600209.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute mea et meorum ego Domitius Terentianus optio primi pili ex voto posui libens merito\n
EDCS-26600209.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, for the safety of myself and my family, I, Domitius Terentianus, optio of the first cohort, have gladly and deservedly set this up according to my vow.
EDCS-26600209.summary: This inscription records a private votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a military officer named Domitius Terentianus for his well-being.

EDCS-26600211.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Marcius Verus decurio municipii Brigetionis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600211.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Caius Marcius Verus, decurion of the municipality of Brigetio, has gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600211.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Caius Marcius Verus, a decurion from Brigetio, for fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-26600213.text_edition: \n Invicto Deo votum solvit Caius A Rodius pro salute sua libens laetus\n
EDCS-26600213.translation: To the Unconquered God, Caius A. Rodius has gladly and joyfully fulfilled his vow for his own safety.
EDCS-26600213.summary: This inscription records a private votive offering to an Unconquered God by Caius A. Rodius for his safety.

EDCS-26600214.text_edition: \n Libero et Liberae Auggustis pro salute Marci Aureli Terentiani veterani legionis I adiutricis Aurelius Demophilus libertus votum solvit libens merito Impperatoribus ddominis nostris Severis consulibus\n
EDCS-26600214.translation: To Liber and Libera, August, for the safety of Marcus Aurelius Terentianus, a veteran of the First Adjutant Legion, Aurelius Demophilus, his freedman, has gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, during the consulship of our Lords the Emperors Severus and [consul].
EDCS-26600214.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Liber and Libera made by a freedman, Aurelius Demophilus, for the safety of his patron, Marcus Aurelius Terentianus, a veteran, during the consulship of the Severan emperors.

EDCS-26600215.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum ex viso Marcus Valerius Marinus veteranus legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis ex signifero decurio Brigetionis qui magistrat votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600215.translation: Sacred to Mercury, as seen in a vision. Marcus Valerius Marinus, veteran of the First Adjutant Legion, pious and faithful, from standard-bearer to decurion of Brigetio, who held the magistracy, has gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600215.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Mercury, prompted by a vision, by Marcus Valerius Marinus, a veteran and former officer from Brigetio.

EDCS-26600216.text_edition: \n Minervae Sanctae Flavius Silvanus |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-26600216.translation: To holy Minerva, Flavius Silvanus, centurion of the Fourteenth Gemina Legion, has gladly and willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600216.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Minerva by Flavius Silvanus, a centurion in the Fourteenth Gemina Legion.

EDCS-26600217.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Alagabalo sic Ammudati milites legionis I adiutricis bis piae fidelis Constantis \n
EDCS-26600217.translation: To the God Sol Alagabalus, thus the soldiers of the First Adjutant Legion, twice pious and faithful, of Constans.
EDCS-26600217.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Sol Alagabalus by soldiers of the First Adjutant Legion serving under Constans.

EDCS-26600218.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Aurelius Iunianus beneficiarius praefecti posuit votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600218.translation: To the Unconquered Sun, Aurelius Iunianus, beneficiary of the prefect, has set this up and gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600218.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Sol Invictus made by Aurelius Iunianus, an officer under a prefect.

EDCS-26600219.translation: Nonius Bassinus has deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600219.summary: This very brief inscription records Nonius Bassinus fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-26600220.text_edition: \n Silvano Valerius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600220.translation: To Silvanus, Valerius has gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-26600220.summary: This inscription records Valerius fulfilling a votive offering to Silvanus.

EDCS-26600221.text_edition: \n Ulpia Candida Silvanis votum solvit merito\n
EDCS-26600221.translation: Ulpia Candida fulfilled her vow to the Silvanes willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-26600221.summary: This inscription records Ulpia Candida fulfilling a vow to the Silvanes, likely a group of deities or spirits associated with forests, probably in the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600222.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico A I Saturninus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600222.translation: Saturninus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-26600222.summary: This inscription records Saturninus fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Domesticus, a household deity associated with forests, likely in the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600223.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri M3 Maximus signifer legionis I adutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600223.translation: Maximus, signifer of the First Legion Adiutrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Silvestris.
EDCS-26600223.summary: This inscription records Maximus, a standard-bearer (signifer) of the Legio I Adiutrix, fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Silvestris, a woodland deity, likely in the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600224.text_edition: \n C ns et - Aelius Baebius veteranus legionis votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-26600224.translation: C. Ns and Aelius Baebius, veterans of the legion, willingly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-26600224.summary: This inscription records two veterans, C. Ns and Aelius Baebius, fulfilling a vow, likely in the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600225.text_edition: \n Claudius Pusiinnio miles legionis I adutricis et Aurelius Eutyches salariarius legionis supra scriptae cum suis votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-26600225.translation: Claudius Pusiinnio, a soldier of the First Legion Adiutrix, and Aurelius Eutyches, a salarian (paymaster) of the aforementioned legion, with their families, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-26600225.summary: This inscription records Claudius Pusiinnio and Aurelius Eutyches of the Legio I Adiutrix fulfilling a vow, likely in the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600226.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pio Pertinaci Augusto Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo Brigetionenses publice decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-26600226.translation: The Brigetiones, by public decree of their decurions, [dedicated this] to the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus.
EDCS-26600226.summary: This is a public dedication by the Brigetiones to Emperor Septimius Severus, likely erected between 193-211 AD.

EDCS-26600227.text_edition: \n Aelio Septimo optioni legionis I adiutricis qui desideratus est in expedit(ione) Naristarum qui vixit \n
EDCS-26600227.translation: To Aelius Septimus, opto of the First Legion Adiutrix, who was missed in the campaign against the Naristae, who lived...
EDCS-26600227.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a memorial to Aelius Septimus, an opto of the Legio I Adiutrix, who died during a campaign, likely in the 2nd-3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600228.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et memoriae Luci Antisti Belliciani veterani legionis I adiutricis et Iuliae Proculae eius Lucii Antistii Bellicus miles legionis II adiutricis beneficiarius consularis et Bellicianus miles legionis supra scriptae exactus officii praesidis parentibus karissimis faciendum ccuraverunt\n
EDCS-26600228.translation: To the spirits of the underworld and to the memory of Lucius Antistius Bellicianus, veteran of the First Legion Adiutrix, and his wife Julia Procula; Lucius Antistius Bellicus, beneficiarius consularis of the Second Legion Adiutrix, and Bellicianus, a soldier of the aforementioned legion and an official of the governor's office, arranged for this to be made for their dearest parents.
EDCS-26600228.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Antistius Bellicianus and Julia Procula, erected by their sons who were soldiers in Legio II Adiutrix and Legio I Adiutrix respectively, likely in the late 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600229.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Adiutor veteranus legionis I adiutricis Severianae missus honesta missione qui vixit nnos L Marcia Rufina coniunx et heres et Aurelius Vibius filius faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-26600229.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aurelius Adiutor, veteran of the First Legion Adiutrix Severiana, honorably discharged, who lived 50 years, and his wife and heir Marcia Rufina, and his son Aurelius Vibius, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-26600229.summary: This is a funerary monument for Aurelius Adiutor, a veteran of the Legio I Adiutrix Severiana, erected by his wife and son, likely in the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600230.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Candido quondam veterano ex imaginifero legionis I adiutricis misso honesta missione Aelia Mercatilla uxor et Aurelius Emeritus filius et heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-26600230.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Aurelius Candidus, formerly a veteran and standard-bearer (imaginifer) of the First Legion Adiutrix, honorably discharged. Aelia Mercatilla, his wife, and Aurelius Emeritus, his son and heir, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-26600230.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Aurelius Candidus, a former standard-bearer and veteran of the Legio I Adiutrix, erected by his wife and son, likely in the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-26600231.text_edition: \n Aurelius Ianuarius beneficiarius tribuni Valeriae Lucillae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit annos XX in se coniugio fuerunt annis III quae reliquit filium Aurelium Maximinum annorum duum ob memoriae defunctae L I SMAES\n
EDCS-26600231.translation: To Aurelius Ianuarius, staff officer to the tribune, for his most devoted wife Valeria Lucilla, who lived 20 years in marriage with him for 3 years, and who left behind a son, Aurelius Maximinus, two years old. In memory of the deceased.
EDCS-26600231.summary: This is a tombstone set up by a military staff officer for his deceased wife and young son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600232.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus aeternae quieti et perpetuae securitati Marco Aurelio quondam Valenti |(centurioni) legionis I adiutricis qui vixit nnos LIII stipendiorum XXXII Marcus Aurelius Alexander primipilaris fratri et Iulia Theodora coniugi et Aurelias sic Sosicratia et Valentina patri pientissimo faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-26600232.translation: To the eternal rest and perpetual peace of the Divine Shades, for Marcus Aurelius Valens, formerly a centurion of the I Adjutrix Legion, who lived 53 years and served 32 years. Marcus Aurelius Alexander, a primipilaris, and his wife Iulia Theodora, and Aurelias Sosicratia and Valentina cared for the making of this for their most devoted father.
EDCS-26600232.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Marcus Aurelius Valens, a centurion of the I Adjutrix Legion, and was erected by his family, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600233.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Vindex Andautonia eques cohortis I Thracum annorum XXXXV Aurelia Amma PFSARI\n
EDCS-26600233.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Aurelius Vindex of Andautonia, an equestrian soldier of the I Thracum Cohort, aged 45.
EDCS-26600233.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Vindex, an equestrian soldier of the I Thracum Cohort, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600235.text_edition: \n Aureliae Victori<a=D>e libertae et Hermetioni alumno Aurelius Heutichi<a>nus sic veteranus ex beneficiario legati \n
EDCS-26600235.translation: For Aurelia Victoria, freedwoman, and Hermetion, his adopted son. Aurelius Heutichianus, veteran, formerly a staff officer to a legate.
EDCS-26600235.summary: This dedication records Aurelius Heutichianus, a veteran staff officer, honoring his freedwoman and adopted son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600236.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio V trierarcho classis Flaviae nnonicae ex beneficiario consularis\n
EDCS-26600236.translation: To Titus Flavius, trierarch of the Italian fleet, formerly a staff officer to a governor.
EDCS-26600236.summary: This inscription honors Titus Flavius, a trierarch in the Roman navy and former staff officer, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600237.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Cai filio Fabia Attiano annorum stipendiorum XL Caius Iulius Crescens libertus faciendum curavit HS L nummum et eo amplius patroni piissimi adiecit P FAM et SPE vivus CVN \n
EDCS-26600237.translation: For Gaius Julius Attianus, son of Gaius, of the Fabia tribe, aged 40, served 40 years. Gaius Julius Crescens, his freedman, arranged for this to be made at a cost of 50,000 sesterces and more; the most devoted patron added more. Alive.
EDCS-26600237.summary: This funerary monument was commissioned by a freedman for his deceased patron, Gaius Julius Attianus, specifying the cost and including a sum for future expenses, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600238.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Cai filio Luperco domo Sala veterano ex decurione alae III Thracum vixit annos L Caius Iulius Candidianus beneficiarius legati legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis nepos qui et heres avonculo sic pientissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600238.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Gaius Julius Lupercus of Sala, a veteran of the III Thracum Ala, who lived 50 years. Gaius Julius Candidianus, staff officer to a legate of the I Adjutrix Legion, loyal and devoted, his grandson and heir, arranged for this to be made for his most devoted grand-uncle.
EDCS-26600238.summary: This tombstone commemorates Gaius Julius Lupercus, a veteran cavalry officer, and was erected by his grandson, a military staff officer, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600239.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Iulio Proculo veterano legionis I adiutricis militavit annos XXVI corniculario tribuni annos XVI vixit annos LXXX Iulius Primio Augustalis municipii Brigetionis et Iulius Eutyches liberti et heredes et Gellia Quintilia coniunx bene merenti faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-26600239.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Marcus Julius Proculus, a veteran of the I Adjutrix Legion, who served 26 years, a cornicularius to a tribune for 16 years, and lived 80 years. Julius Primus Augustalis of the municipality of Brigetio, and Julius Eutyches, his freedmen, and Gellia Quintilia, his wife, arranged for this to be made for the well-deserving deceased.
EDCS-26600239.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Julius Proculus, a veteran of the I Adjutrix Legion and a decorated officer, who lived to 80 and was commemorated by his freedmen and wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600240.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et memoriae Cai Petroni Nundini Augustalis municipii Brigetionis Lucius Septimius Petronius custos armorum avonculo sic faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600240.translation: To the Divine Shades and memory of Gaius Petronius Nundinus, Augustalis of the municipality of Brigetio. Lucius Septimius Petronius, custos armorum, arranged for this to be made for his grand-uncle.
EDCS-26600240.summary: This inscription records the memorial set up by Lucius Septimius Petronius for his grand-uncle, Gaius Petronius Nundinus, an Augustalis of Brigetio, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600241.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimio Caeserno duplario legionis I adiutricis stipendiorum XX vixit annos XL Caesernius Sabinianus signifer legionis I adiutricis p(iae)? f(idelis) faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600241.translation: To the Divine Shades, for Septimius Caessernus, a duplarius of the I Adjutrix Legion, served 20 years, lived 40 years. Caesernius Sabinianus, a signifer of the I Adjutrix Legion, loyal and devoted, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-26600241.summary: This tombstone commemorates Septimius Caessernus, a senior soldier in the I Adjutrix Legion, and was erected by Caesernius Sabinianus, a standard-bearer in the same legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600242.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio O pri legionis vixit annos M STE\n
EDCS-26600242.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius O... of the legion lived 30 years.
EDCS-26600242.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius O..., a soldier of a legion, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-26600243.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Valerio Candido militi legionis I adiutricis Titius Candidus signifer posuit\n
EDCS-26600243.translation: To the Divine Shades. Titius Candidus, standard-bearer, set this up for Quintus Valerius Candidus, a soldier of the First Adjutant Legion.
EDCS-26600243.summary: This is a tombstone inscription set up by Titius Candidus for Quintus Valerius Candidus, a soldier and standard-bearer of the First Adjutant Legion.

EDCS-26600245.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marci Ulpi Prisci beneficiarii legati legionis I adiutricis stipendiorum XXII Marcus Iulius Vitalis optio hastati legionis eiusdem heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600245.translation: To the Divine Shades. Marcus Iulius Vitalis, optio of the first rank of the same legion, saw to it that this was made, the heir, with 22 years of service, Marcus Ulpius Priscus, a beneficiary of the legate of the First Adjutant Legion.
EDCS-26600245.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Ulpius Priscus, a beneficiary of a legionary legate, paid for by his heir Marcus Iulius Vitalis, an optio.

EDCS-26600246.text_edition: \n IQ CHDM natione Ant ANVVSI XXI et nati annorum IX |(centurio) decurio O PECO\n
EDCS-26600246.translation: IQ CHDM of the Ant... tribe, 21 years old and of 9 years, centurion, decurion O PECO.
EDCS-26600246.summary: This fragmented inscription records a man of unknown origin and tribal affiliation, identified as a centurion and decurion, who lived to 21 and had a 9-year-old child.

EDCS-26600247.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cusi Callistionis quondam Augustalis municipii Brigetionis mariti et Aureli Claudiani @(obiti) filii infelicissimorum qui vixit nnos -XXIII Aurelia Claudilla coniugi carissimo et filio faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600247.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelia Claudilla set this up for her dearest husband, Cusus Callistion, formerly Augustalis of the municipium of Brigetio, and for her son Aurelius Claudianus, who died at the age of 23, and for her son, who lived 23 years.
EDCS-26600247.summary: This tombstone commemorates Aurelia Claudilla's husband, Cusus Callistion (a former Augustalis of Brigetio), and their son Aurelius Claudianus, who died at 23.

EDCS-26600248.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Antoniano domo ex civitate Zeugma qui vixit nnos XXV Heliodorus filius patri dulcissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600248.translation: Heliodorus, his son, set this up for Marcus Aurelius Antonianus of Zeugma, who lived 25 years, his dearest father.
EDCS-26600248.summary: This tombstone was erected by Heliodorus for his father, Marcus Aurelius Antonianus from Zeugma, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-26600249.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus memoriae Cassiae quondam Fabiae quae vixit annos XXX Aurelius Valentinus coniugi incomparabili\n
EDCS-26600249.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Valentinus, his incomparable spouse, for Cassia Fabia, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-26600249.summary: This tombstone was erected by Aurelius Valentinus for his wife, Cassia Fabia, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-26600252.text_edition: \n Miniciae Romulae dulcissimae adque pientissimae coniug[i] coniugi quae vixit annos XXVIII SED et Annio Princ<i=E>pio filio carissimo qui vixit annis duobus diebus XXX Annius Sperantius decurio coloniae Bri<g=C>(etionis) memoriam fecit \n
EDCS-26600252.translation: Annius Sperantius, decurion of the colony of Brigetio, made this memorial for Minicia Romula, his sweetest and most dutiful wife, who lived 28 years, and for his dearest son, Annius Princ<i=E>pius, who lived two years and thirty days.
EDCS-26600252.summary: This tombstone, erected by Annius Sperantius, a decurion of Brigetio, commemorates his wife Minicia Romula (who died at 28) and his son Annius Princ<i=E>pius (who died at 2 years and 30 days).

EDCS-26600253.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpiae Martinae annorum LXXX Pubius Aelius Traianus decurio municipii Bri<g=C>(etionis) aviae piissimae faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-26600253.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Aelius Traianus, decurion of the municipium of Brigetio, had this made for his most dutiful grandmother, Ulpia Martina, 80 years old.
EDCS-26600253.summary: This tombstone was erected by Publius Aelius Traianus, a decurion of Brigetio, for his grandmother, Ulpia Martina, who died at the age of 80.

EDCS-26600255.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Publi filio decurioni municipii Mogetianae Rufiniano IIviro quinquennali annorum XXIIX et Aeliae quondam Can quae vixit nnos XXIII dies XI coniugi pientissimae et Publius Aelius Publi filius sepul et Publius Aelius Publi filius Rufinus fratres et heredes parentibus karissimis faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-26600255.translation: Publius Aelius Publi son Rufinus and Publius Aelius Publi son, brothers and heirs, had this made for their dearest parents: Publius Aelius Publi son, a decurion of the municipium of Mogetiana, Rufinianus, a quinquennial duovir, 28 years old, and Aelia Can, formerly his most dutiful wife, who lived 23 years and 11 days.
EDCS-26600255.summary: This tombstone was erected by brothers and heirs for their parents: Publius Aelius Rufinianus, a decurion and quinquennial duovir of Mogetiana (died at 28), and his wife Aelia Can (died at 23 years and 11 days).

EDCS-26600256.text_edition: \n Annio Novo Felicis C Venn amico fecit in memoriam Anniae Mariae coniugi\n
EDCS-26600256.translation: Annio Novo, son of Felix, made this for his friend C. Venn, in memory of his wife Annia Maria.
EDCS-26600256.summary: This epitaph records a dedication made by Annio Novo to his friend C. Venn, and also commemorates Annia Maria, likely his wife.

EDCS-26600257.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caeserniae quondam Severae quae vixsit sic annis XLIII Aurelius Silvanus coniugi carissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600257.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Silvanus had this made for his dearest wife Caesernia Severa, who lived for 43 years.
EDCS-26600257.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Caesernia Severa, who lived to be 43, and the memorial erected by her husband Aurelius Silvanus.

EDCS-26600258.text_edition: \n Cai- IuliI fili--- Claudia German I coniugi k[arissim- ------\n
EDCS-26600258.translation: To Caius Iulius, son of [---], and his dearest wife Claudia Germa [---].
EDCS-26600258.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription likely dedicated to Caius Iulius and his wife Claudia Germa.

EDCS-26600259.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus memoriae Iuliae Emeritae quae vixit annos XXX Valeria Masclenia filiae piissimae\n
EDCS-26600259.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for the memory of Iulia Emerita, who lived for 30 years, [this was dedicated by] Valeria Masclenia, her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-26600259.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Iulia Emerita, who died at age 30, and was erected by her devoted daughter Valeria Masclenia.

EDCS-26600260.translation: May you live forever! Licinius Maximus [dedicated this].
EDCS-26600260.summary: This is a very brief inscription recording a wish for eternal life, attributed to Licinius Maximus.

EDCS-26600262.text_edition: \n Perpetuae securitati Mataconiis Tasgillae et Clarae Primanius Quintinus parentibus Mataconius Augendus patronis\n
EDCS-26600262.translation: For the eternal security of Mataconis Tasgilla and Clara, clients of Primanius Quintinus, and of Mataconius Augendus, patrons.
EDCS-26600262.summary: This inscription records dedications for the eternal well-being of clients and patrons, likely from a collegium or a family.

EDCS-26600270.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Arruntio Vindicio OCT civitatis TSVNVSINN DNO \n
EDCS-26600270.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Gaius Arruntius Vindicius, a citizen of T... [---].
EDCS-26600270.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Arruntius Vindicius, a citizen of an unknown place.

EDCS-26600272.text_edition: \n Bonae Fortunae Marcus Septimius Valentinus decurio Brigetionensium ex voto\n
EDCS-26600272.translation: To Good Fortune. Marcus Septimius Valentinus, decurion of the Brigetiones, [made this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600272.summary: This votive inscription records a dedication to Bona Fortuna (Good Fortune) by Marcus Septimius Valentinus, a local official from Brigetio.

EDCS-26600273.text_edition: \n Nymphis sacrum Lucius Attius Macro legatus Augusti\n
EDCS-26600273.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs. Lucius Attius Macro, legate of the Emperor, [dedicated this].
EDCS-26600273.summary: This is a religious dedication to the Nymphs made by Lucius Attius Macro, an imperial legate.

EDCS-26600274.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Capriae Primigeniae uxori pientissimae Apulonius Firmus |(centurio) legionis I adiutricis\n
EDCS-26600274.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Apulonius Firmus, a centurion of the First Adjutant Legion, [had this made] for his most dutiful wife Capria Primigenia.
EDCS-26600274.summary: This funerary inscription records the memorial erected by Apulonius Firmus, a centurion, for his wife Capria Primigenia.

EDCS-26600276.text_edition: \n Dis deabus ala I Ulpia contariorum |(miliaria) civium Romanorum cui praeest Quintus Terentius Ingenuus PA \n
EDCS-26600276.translation: To the gods and goddesses: the 1st Wing of Ulpian Auxiliary Cavalrymen of Roman citizens, commanded by Quintus Terentius Ingenuus, standard-bearer.
EDCS-26600276.summary: This inscription records a dedication to gods and goddesses by the 1st Wing of Ulpian Auxiliary Cavalrymen.

EDCS-26600277.text_edition: \n Dianae sacrum Caius Aelius Brocchus praefectus alae I Ulpiae contariorum\n
EDCS-26600277.translation: Sacred to Diana: Caius Aelius Brocchus, prefect of the 1st Wing of Ulpian Auxiliary Cavalrymen.
EDCS-26600277.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana by Caius Aelius Brocchus, prefect of the 1st Wing of Ulpian Auxiliary Cavalrymen.

EDCS-26600278.text_edition: \n Dianae Titus Flavius Flaccus praefectus alae I Ulpiae contariorum\n
EDCS-26600278.translation: To Diana: Titus Flavius Flaccus, prefect of the 1st Wing of Ulpian Auxiliary Cavalrymen.
EDCS-26600278.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana by Titus Flavius Flaccus, prefect of the 1st Wing of Ulpian Auxiliary Cavalrymen.

EDCS-26600279.text_edition: \n Dianae sacravit Titus Flavius Italicus praefectus alae I Ulpiae contariorum |(miliariae) civium Romanorum\n
EDCS-26600279.translation: Titus Flavius Italicus, prefect of the 1st Wing of Ulpian Auxiliary Cavalrymen of Roman citizens, dedicated this.
EDCS-26600279.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Diana by Titus Flavius Italicus, prefect of the 1st Wing of Ulpian Auxiliary Cavalrymen.

EDCS-26600280.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Minervae Neptuno Libero Patri Dianae ceterisque dibus Lucius Antonius Sabinianus cornicularius legati legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis Severianae templum vetustate conlapsum faciundum curavit cum Aurelia Aeliana coniuge\n
EDCS-26600280.translation: Lucius Antonius Sabinianus, a clerk (cornicularius) of the 1st Adjutant Legion, pious and faithful Severian, had this temple, collapsed through age, rebuilt for Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Juno Regina, Minerva, Neptune, Liber Pater, Diana, and the other gods, together with his wife Aurelia Aeliana.
EDCS-26600280.summary: This inscription records the rebuilding of a temple to various gods by Lucius Antonius Sabinianus and his wife, associated with the 1st Adjutant Legion.

EDCS-26600281.text_edition: \n Victoriae Auggustorum nnostrorum et legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis Antoninianae Publius Marcius Publi filius Sextianus Epheso pecunia publica decreto decurionum dedicante Egnatio Victore legato Auggustorum pro praetore et Claudio Pisone legato legionis V Idus Iunias Apro et Maximo consulibus\n
EDCS-26600281.translation: For the Victory of our August Emperors and the 1st Adjutant Legion, pious and faithful Antoninian: Publius Marcius Sextianus, son of Publius, from Ephesus, dedicated this with public money by decree of the town councilors, with Egnatius Victor, governor, and Claudius Piso, legate of the 5th Legion, presiding, on the Ides of June, in the consulship of Apro and Maximus.
EDCS-26600281.summary: This inscription records a dedication for the Victory of the Emperors and the 1st Adjutant Legion, funded publicly and overseen by officials, during the consulship of Apro and Maximus.

EDCS-26600282.text_edition: \n August-- sacrum Aurelius Latinianus strator consularis votum solvit libens laetus\n
EDCS-26600282.translation: Sacred to the Augusti: Aurelius Latinianus, an attendant (strator) of a consular governor, fulfilled his vow willingly and joyfully.
EDCS-26600282.summary: This inscription records a personal vow fulfilled by Aurelius Latinianus, an attendant of a consular governor.

EDCS-26600283.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Traiano Hadriano Augusto patri patriae tribunicia potestate XXI consuli III imperatori II Lucius Aelius Caesar filius tribunicia potestate consul II proconsul XVvir sacris faciundis\n
EDCS-26600283.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, father of the country, in his 21st year of tribunician power, 3rd consulship, acclaimed emperor twice: Lucius Aelius Caesar, his son, in his tribunician power, 2nd consulship, proconsul, and member of the board of fifteen for sacred rites.
EDCS-26600283.summary: This inscription details the titles and offices held by Lucius Aelius Caesar, son of Emperor Hadrian.

EDCS-26600284.text_edition: \n Acrabanis Ababunis filius ala Augusta Itura<e=I>orum annorum XLV stipendiorum XII hic situs est Hanicus frater heres pos<ui=IV>t \n
EDCS-26600284.translation: Acrabanis, son of Ababunis, of the Augusta Ituraeorum Wing, aged 45, with 12 years of service, lies here. Hanicus, his brother and heir, placed this monument.
EDCS-26600284.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Acrabanis of the Augusta Ituraeorum Wing, placed by his brother.

EDCS-26600285.text_edition: \n Albanus Balvi filius decurio alae Augustae Ituraeorum domo Betavos sic annorum XLII stipendiorum XX hic situs est titulum memoriae posuerunt Tiberius Iulius Reitugenus et Lucanus decuriones alae Augustae Ituraeorum\n
EDCS-26600285.translation: Albanus, son of Balvus, a decurion of the Augusta Ituraeorum Wing, from Betavos, aged 42, with 20 years of service, lies here. Tiberius Iulius Reitugenus and Lucanus, decurions of the Augusta Ituraeorum Wing, placed this memorial inscription.
EDCS-26600285.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Albanus of the Augusta Ituraeorum Wing, placed by fellow decurions.

EDCS-26600286.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Dorisoni veterano ex statore praefecti alae contariorum annorum XLIIX et Aurelio Mussati filio annorum VII Aurelia Noeren liberta et coniunx et Aurelii Lupus et Aulupor et Basilla filii titulum patri et fratri faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-26600286.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Dorisonus, a veteran, formerly a foot soldier (statore) of a prefect of the ala contariorum, aged 48, and his son Aurelius Mussatus, aged 7. Aurelia Noeren, freedwoman and wife, and her children Aurelius Lupus, Aurelius Aulupor, and Aurelia Basilla, took care to have this monument made for their husband and father and brother.
EDCS-26600286.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aurelia Noeren and her children for her husband Aurelius Dorisonus, a veteran, and her son Aurelius Mussatus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600287.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Marco veterano alae I Ulpiae contariorum ni et Aureliae Veneriae libertae Aurelius Teres veteranus alae supra scriptae heres iuxta testamentum fecit\n
EDCS-26600287.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Marcus, a veteran of the I Ulpia ala contariorum, and Aurelia Veneria, freedwoman. Aurelius Teres, a veteran of the aforementioned ala, as heir, made this monument in accordance with the will.
EDCS-26600287.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aurelius Teres, following a will, for Aurelius Marcus, a veteran, and Aurelia Veneria, a freedwoman, of the I Ulpia ala contariorum, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600288.text_edition: \n Bargathes Regebali filius eques alae Augustae Ityraeorum domo Ityraeus annorum XXV stipendiorum V hic situs est Zanis decurio et Baramna Beliabi filius et Bricbelus frater heredes pos<u=I>erunt \n
EDCS-26600288.translation: Bargathes, son of Regebalus, a cavalryman of the ala Augusta Ityraeorum, from Ityraea, aged 25, with 5 years of service, lies here. Zanis, a decurion, and Baramna, son of Beliabus, and Bricbelus, his brother, his heirs, placed (this).
EDCS-26600288.summary: A tombstone for Bargathes, a cavalryman of the ala Augusta Ityraeorum from Ityraea, erected by his heirs including a decurion, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600289.text_edition: \n Bato Buli filius Colap eques alae Pannoniorum turma Sceni Scenobarvi fili nnorum XXX stipendiorum IX hic situs est Scenus Scenobarvi sic filius heres posuit\n
EDCS-26600289.translation: Bato, son of Buli, a Breucian cavalryman of the ala Pannoniorum, in the troop of Scenus, son of Scenobarvus, aged 30, with 9 years of service, lies here. Scenus, son of Scenobarvus, his brother, his heir, placed (this).
EDCS-26600289.summary: A tombstone for Bato, a cavalryman of the ala Pannoniorum, erected by his brother and heir Scenus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600290.text_edition: \n Crispus Mac Siscianus eques alae I Aravacorum annorum XXXV stipendiorum XV hic situs est Ianuarius decurio heres posuit\n
EDCS-26600290.translation: Crispus, son of Mac, a Dacian cavalryman of the I ala Aravacorum, aged 35, with 15 years of service, lies here. Ianuarius, a decurion, his heir, placed (this).
EDCS-26600290.summary: A tombstone for Crispus, a cavalryman of the I ala Aravacorum, erected by his heir Ianuarius, a decurion, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600291.text_edition: \n cohortis Alpinorum equitatae domo asis heres curavit\n
EDCS-26600291.translation: [This monument was] taken care of by the heir for the cohort of the Alpinus, cavalry (equitatae), from Asis.
EDCS-26600291.summary: An incomplete funerary monument mentioning an heir and the cohort of the Alpinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600292.text_edition: \n Iulio Paterno quondam signifer legionis I adiutricis stipendiorum VI qui vixit annos XXVIIII qui est bello desiderato et Iulio Fusco quondam militi legionis supra scriptae stipendiorum II qui vixit annos XXVII qui est bello desiderato Iulia Fuscinilla mater filiis pientissimis faciendam curavit\n
EDCS-26600292.translation: For Iulius Paternus, formerly a standard-bearer (signifer) of the I Adiutrix legion, with 6 years of service, who lived 29 years, who was lost in a desired battle; and for Iulius Fuscus, formerly a soldier of the aforementioned legion, with 2 years of service, who lived 27 years, who was lost in a desired battle. Iulia Fuscinilla, their mother, took care to have this made for her most devoted sons.
EDCS-26600292.summary: A funerary monument erected by Iulia Fuscinilla for her two sons, Iulius Paternus (a standard-bearer) and Iulius Fuscus (a soldier), both killed in action, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600293.text_edition: \n Crali filius colonia aeques sic alae nnniorum signifer turmae Arti nnorum XXXIIII stipendiorum XVI hic situs est Lirus Plassari filius heres posuit\n
EDCS-26600293.translation: Cralus, son of Cralus, a cavalryman of the ala [...], standard-bearer of the troop of Artus, aged 32, with 16 years of service, lies here. Lirus, son of Plassarius, his heir, placed (this).
EDCS-26600293.summary: A tombstone for Cralus, a standard-bearer and cavalryman of an ala, erected by his heir Lirus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600294.text_edition: \n Scilus Batonis filius Breucus eques alae Pannoniorum annorum XXX stipendiorum X hic situs est Deculus decurio et Iulius Leucaspis heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-26600294.translation: Scilus, son of Bato, a Breucian cavalryman of the ala Pannoniorum, aged 30, with 10 years of service, lies here. Deculus, a decurion, and Iulius Leucaspis, his heirs, placed (this).
EDCS-26600294.summary: A tombstone for Scilus, a Breucian cavalryman of the ala Pannoniorum, erected by his heirs Deculus (a decurion) and Iulius Leucaspis, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600295.text_edition: \n Ulpius Eptatralis eques alae I contariorum stipendiorum XV annorum XXXX domo Bessus hic situs est Ulpius Au<l=I>uc<e=C>ntus heres faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-26600295.translation: Ulpius Eptatralis, a cavalryman of the I ala contariorum, with 15 years of service, aged 40, from Bessia, lies here. Ulpius Aulucentus, his heir, took care to have this made.
EDCS-26600295.summary: A tombstone for Ulpius Eptatralis, a cavalryman of the I ala contariorum from Bessia, erected by his heir Ulpius Aulucentus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600296.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpio Variano RE veterano ex statore alae I Ulpiae contariorum domo Africa vixsit sic nnos XLV et Aeliae Sabinae filiae vixit annos X Aelia Domitia coniunx et Ulpius Sabinianus et Ulpius Celerinus et Sabinilla filii vivi cum matre patri karissimo posuerunt\n
EDCS-26600296.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ulpius Varianus, a veteran former cavalryman of the I Ulpia Contariorum ala, originally from Africa, lived 45 years, and Aelia Sabina, his daughter, lived 10 years. Aelia Domitia his wife, and his sons Ulpius Sabinianus, Ulpius Celerinus, and Sabinilla, with their mother, erected this for their dearest father.
EDCS-26600296.summary: A funerary monument set up by a wife and sons for their deceased husband and father, Ulpius Varianus, a veteran, and also commemorating their deceased daughter and sister, Aelia Sabina.

EDCS-26600298.text_edition: \n stator EO CTTCRMIMANO SSV hic situs OCC Victor RES faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600298.translation: Victor, a former cavalryman, saw to the making of this monument for his father, a former cavalryman.
EDCS-26600298.summary: A funerary monument erected by Victor, a former cavalryman, for his father, also a former cavalryman.

EDCS-26600299.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Cocceius.
EDCS-26600299.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for an individual named Cocceius.

EDCS-26600300.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marciae Candidae matri pientissimae Aurelius Laurentius filius vivus vivae fecit\n
EDCS-26600300.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Laurentius erected this for his most devoted mother, Marciae Candidae, while he is alive for her who is alive.
EDCS-26600300.summary: A funerary monument erected by a son, Aurelius Laurentius, for his devoted mother, Marcia Candidae.

EDCS-26600301.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aurelius Orapus.
EDCS-26600301.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Marcus Aurelius Orapus.

EDCS-26600302.text_edition: \n II annorum LXX hic sita est Caius Salvius Iulianus coniugi carissimae pientissimae et desiderantissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-26600302.translation: She is buried here, having lived 72 years. Caius Salvius Iulianus saw to the making of this for his dearest and most devoted and most missed wife.
EDCS-26600302.summary: A funerary monument erected by Caius Salvius Iulianus for his wife, who lived to be 72 years old.

EDCS-26600303.text_edition: \n Statoria Marci filia Acanthis annorum XIV hic sita est tus \n
EDCS-26600303.translation: Statoria Acanthis, daughter of Marcus, 14 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-26600303.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Statoria Acanthis, daughter of Marcus, who died at the age of 14.

EDCS-26600304.text_edition: \n annorum XI TVTAVI Urilia filia II patri F PP\n
EDCS-26600304.translation: 11 years old. Urilia, daughter of [---], [erected this] for her father.
EDCS-26600304.summary: A funerary monument erected by Urilia for her father, recording her own age as 11.

EDCS-26600305.text_edition: \n Herculi Aauugustorum Genio collegii iuventutis Caesonius Tertius decurio municipii Brigetionis IIvir patrono Geminio Epigono\n
EDCS-26600305.translation: To Hercules Augustus, to the Genius of the youth association, Caesonius Tertius, decurion of the municipality of Brigetio, as aedile, for his patron Geminius Epigonus.
EDCS-26600305.summary: A dedication to Hercules Augustus and the Genius of a youth association, set up by Caesonius Tertius, a municipal official, for his patron Geminius Epigonus.

EDCS-26600306.text_edition: \n Vivae Ulpiae Paratianae laudabili feminae Valeria mater filiae contra votum quae vixit annos XVI\n
EDCS-26600306.translation: Valeria set this up for her admirable daughter, Ulpia Paratiana, against her wishes, who lived 16 years.
EDCS-26600306.summary: A funerary monument set up by a mother, Valeria, for her daughter, Ulpia Paratiana, who died at the age of 16.

EDCS-26600308.text_edition: \n Titus Canius Titi filius vinno IIXX II SI Cania Titi filia Sura CI IIPPIC\n
EDCS-26600308.translation: Titus Canius, son of Titus, called Vinno, 28 years old; Cania, daughter of Titus, called Sura, 25 years old.
EDCS-26600308.summary: This inscription records the ages of Titus Canius Vinno and Cania Sura, likely on a tombstone, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600309.text_edition: \n Caius Atilius Brincaius <s>ibi e<t> Iustae matri pientissimae annorum \n
EDCS-26600309.translation: Gaius Atilius Brincaius, at his own place, to his most dutiful mother Iusta, [aged] years.
EDCS-26600309.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Gaius Atilius Brincaius to his mother Iusta, probably on a tomb, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600311.text_edition: \n Nymphis Iulia Martia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600311.translation: Iulia Martia willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to the Nymphs.
EDCS-26600311.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Iulia Martia to the Nymphs, likely on a shrine or altar, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600312.text_edition: \n Silvano sac VICAIIVS votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600312.translation: Vicaivs willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-26600312.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Vicaivs to the god Silvanus, likely on a shrine or altar, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600313.text_edition: \n Vivus fecit Publius Antonius Ursio annorum et Albaniae Maximae coniugi et Antoniae Maximillae filiae defunctae annorum XXV hic sita est\n
EDCS-26600313.translation: While alive, Publius Antonius Ursio, aged [years], made this for his wife Albaniae Maxima, aged [years], and for his daughter Antonia Maximilla, deceased, aged 25 years; she is buried here.
EDCS-26600313.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Publius Antonius Ursio for his wife and deceased daughter, indicating he made arrangements while alive, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600315.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus P Dubius hic situs est annorum LXX P Tertius scriba municipii Mogetianensium annorum LXXX fratri carissimo posuit\n
EDCS-26600315.translation: To the Divine Shades. P. Dubius is buried here, aged 70. P. Tertius, scribe of the municipality of Mogetia, aged 80, erected this for his dearest brother.
EDCS-26600315.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by P. Tertius for his brother P. Dubius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600316.text_edition: \n ------] Aureli--? VPAI F Valeria? tina hic s(iti)? s(unt) Tiberius Claudiustus sibi? et? suis vivus fecit \n
EDCS-26600316.translation: [...] Aurelius? VPAI F Valeria? tina? are buried here? Tiberius Claudius? made this for himself? and his own people while alive.
EDCS-26600316.summary: This is a partially restored funerary inscription where Tiberius Claudius made provision for himself and his family while alive, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600319.text_edition: \n VIinus posuit vivus obitae coniugi Aureliae Severae annorum XX\n
EDCS-26600319.translation: Viinus placed this while alive for his deceased wife Aurelia Severa, aged 20.
EDCS-26600319.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Viinus for his wife Aurelia Severa, indicating he made arrangements while alive, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600320.translation: For the eternal inhabitants of the underworld.
EDCS-26600320.summary: This is a brief inscription possibly related to the underworld deities or the deceased, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600322.text_edition: \n ILIII Domitinus nnorum XXV hic situs est Claudii Potens et Nigrinus fratri pientissimo faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-26600322.translation: Domitinus, aged 25, is buried here. Claudius Potens and Nigrinus arranged for this to be made for their most dutiful brother.
EDCS-26600322.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Domitinus and was commissioned by his brothers Claudius Potens and Nigrinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-26600324.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Caius Atilius Brincaius et Gaius Venustius Valerianus benefiaciarii consularis votum solverunt laeti libentes merito Imperatore domino nostro Alexandro consule\n
EDCS-26600324.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Atilius Brincaius and Gaius Venustius Valerianus, imperial attendants of the governor, happily, willingly, and deservedly fulfilled their vow under Emperor our lord Alexander as consul.
EDCS-26600324.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by two imperial attendants of the governor to Jupiter, dating to the consulship of Alexander (likely Emperor Alexander Severus).

EDCS-26600326.text_edition: \n Fatis Tenatia Rhodia Spuri filia et Tenatia Dido Tiberi filia fecerunt ex voto\n
EDCS-26600326.translation: Tenatia Rhodia, daughter of Spurius, and Tenatia Dido, daughter of Tiberius, made this in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-26600326.summary: This inscription records two women, Tenatia Rhodia and Tenatia Dido, fulfilling a vow by dedicating something.

EDCS-26600327.text_edition: \n Genio candidatorum Veneris Victricis Daphnus coloniae Savariae vilicus kalendarii Septimiani sacerdote Publio Aelio Sabiniano donum dedit\n
EDCS-26600327.translation: Daphnus, steward of the calendar of Septimius, priest of the divine spirit of the candidates of Victorious Venus in the colony of Savaria, gave this offering.
EDCS-26600327.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Daphnus, a steward and priest in the colony of Savaria, to the divine spirit of the candidates of Venus Victrix.

EDCS-26600328.text_edition: \n Herculi Augustocanae kius Auggustorum nnostrorum servus |contrascriptor votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-26600328.translation: To Hercules of Augusta, the slave of our divine Emperors, a scribe, fulfilled his vow willingly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-26600328.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by a slave and scribe to Hercules of Augusta.

EDCS-26600329.text_edition: \n Isidi Augustae sacrum Tiberius Barbius Tiberi filius Velina Valens decurio coloniae Claudiae Savariae quaestor IIvir iure dicundo pontifex \n
EDCS-26600329.translation: Sacred to Isis Augusta. Tiberius Barbius Valens, son of Tiberius, of the Velina tribe, a decurion of the Claudian colony of Savaria, quaestor, iuir, and pontifex.
EDCS-26600329.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Isis Augusta by Tiberius Barbius Valens, a prominent civic official in the colony of Savaria.

EDCS-26600330.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Claudius Victor et Claudia Avita votum solverunt libentes merito duobus Aspris consulibus Idibus Iuniis\n
EDCS-26600330.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and Juno Regina. Claudius Victor and Claudia Avita fulfilled their vow happily, willingly, and deservedly under the two Aspri as consuls on the Ides of June.
EDCS-26600330.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Claudius Victor and Claudia Avita to Jupiter and Juno Regina, dated to the consulship of the two Aspri in June.

EDCS-26600331.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Peticius Serenus veteranus donum dedit\n
EDCS-26600331.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Quintus Peticius Serenus, a veteran, gave this offering.
EDCS-26600331.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Quintus Peticius Serenus, a veteran.

EDCS-26600332.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Successus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600332.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Quintus Successus fulfilled his vow willingly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-26600332.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Quintus Successus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-26600334.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae sacrum Heliodorus Augusti nostri vilicus stationis Savariensis pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600334.translation: Sacred to Nemesis Augusta. Heliodorus, steward of our Emperor in the Savaria station, fulfilled his vow for the welfare of himself and his family, happily, willingly, and deservedly.
EDCS-26600334.summary: This inscription records a vow for health made to Nemesis Augusta by Heliodorus, an imperial steward in Savaria.

EDCS-26600337.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Claudius Quietus et Valentina votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-26600337.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Claudius Quietus and Valentina fulfilled their vow happily, willingly, and deservedly.
EDCS-26600337.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Claudius Quietus and Valentina to Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-26600338.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Gaius Iulius Maximus pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-26600338.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Gaius Julius Maximus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-26600338.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Domesticus by Gaius Julius Maximus for his family's well-being, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-26600339.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum pro salute Quinti R H Felicissimus pro se et suis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-26600339.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Felicissimus, steward of Quintus R. H., gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for himself and his family.
