EDCS-28800113.translation: Ulpius Valentinus, a veteran of the 10th Gemina Legion, gladly, willingly, and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest, along with his family.
EDCS-28800113.summary: This is a votive inscription by a veteran, dedicating a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800114.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Marci filius Verus decurio municipii Aelii Karnunti IIIIvir iure dicundo et Aelia Publi filia Lucilla vivi sibi et Marco Ulpio Ulpiano filio annorum V hic sito fecerunt\n
EDCS-28800114.translation: Marcus Ulpius Verus, son of Marcus, decurion of the municipium of Aelius Carnuntum, and IIvir with judicial power, and Aelia Lucilla, daughter of Publius, while alive made this for themselves and for their son, Marcus Ulpius Ulpianus, aged 5.
EDCS-28800114.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a family, erected by the parents for themselves and their young son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800115.text_edition: \n Tatucae Macioni filiae annorum C Fovea Secae filia annorum LXXX hic sitae vivae fecerunt MII\n
EDCS-28800115.translation: Fovea Secae, daughter of Seca, aged 80, and Tatuca, daughter of Macio, aged 100, erected this while alive in the year MII.
EDCS-28800115.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for two women, erected by one of them during her lifetime, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800117.text_edition: \n Deorum Prosperitati Gaius Marcius Marcianus decurio municipii Vindobonensium quaestor aedilis IIvir iure dicundo praefectus collegii fabrum votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28800117.translation: For the Prosperity of the Gods. Gaius Marcius Marcianus, decurion of the municipium of Vindobonenses, quaestor, aedile, IIvir with judicial power, and prefect of the guild of fabric workers, gladly, willingly, and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-28800117.summary: This is a votive inscription by a prominent local official, dedicating a vow to the gods for prosperity, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800118.text_edition: \n Fortunae Conservatrici Genioque huius loci in honorem Titi EIIIVN Tiberianici tribuni militum legionis X geminae piae <f=P>(idelis) <D=F>ecianae Titus Claudius Valentinus cornicullarius ex voto posuit V <K=I>alendas Iunias Aemiliano et Aquilino consulibus Nomina bbenef<f=E>(iciariorum) LAVACIO Aurelius Avallaus GEMA Flavius Auinus SIBICO Potens Iustinus OSIMS COC \n
EDCS-28800118.translation: For Fortuna Conservatrix and the Genius of this place, in honor of Titus Tiberianicus, military tribune of the 10th Gemina Legion, pious and faithful Deciana. Titus Claudius Valentinus, a cornicularius, set this up in fulfillment of a vow on the Kalends of June, during the consulship of Aemilianus and Aquilinus. Names of the beneficiaries: Aurelius Avallaus (Lavacius), Flavius Avinus (Gema), Potens Iustinus (Sibico), OSIMSCOC.
EDCS-28800118.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Fortuna and the Genius Loci by a military officer, mentioning beneficiaries, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800119.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Censor beneficiarius procuratoris votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800119.translation: Tiberius Claudius Censor, a beneficiarius of the procurator, gladly, willingly, and deservedly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-28800119.summary: This is a votive inscription by an imperial administrative official, dedicating a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800120.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum Marcus Cassius Severus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28800120.translation: Sacred to Mercury, Marcus Cassius Severus willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28800120.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Mercury by Marcus Cassius Severus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800121.text_edition: \n Nymphis sacrum Titus Vettius Rufus |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae merito\n
EDCS-28800121.translation: Sacred to the Nymphs, Titus Vettius Rufus, centurion of the 14th Legion Gemina, deservedly offered this.
EDCS-28800121.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the Nymphs by Titus Vettius Rufus, a centurion of the 14th Legion Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800122.text_edition: \n Victoriae et Fortunae Augustae Marcus Aurelius Maximus libens laetus animo votum posuit\n
EDCS-28800122.translation: Marcus Aurelius Maximus willingly, joyfully, and with his whole heart set up this vow to Victoria and Fortune Augusta.
EDCS-28800122.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Victoria and Fortune Augusta by Marcus Aurelius Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800123.text_edition: \n sacerd(ote) Gellio Ianuario collegio donum dedit\n
EDCS-28800123.translation: In the priesthood of Gellius Ianuarius, [he] gave a gift to the college.
EDCS-28800123.summary: A dedication of a gift by Gellius Ianuarius, who was a priest, to a collegium, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800125.text_edition: \n Publio Claudio Pallanti Honorato Repentino adlecto inter tribunicios legato pro praetore provinciae Africae praetori legato pro praetore provinciae Asiae legato Augusti legionis X geminae Caius Iulius Magnus decurio coloniae Karnunti equo publico ex V decuriis dignissimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28800125.translation: To Publius Claudius Pallantius Honoratus Repentinus, admitted among the tribunes, legate pro praetore of the province of Africa, praetorian legate pro praetore of the province of Asia, legate of Augustus of the 10th Legion Gemina, Gaius Julius Magnus, decurion of the colony of Carnuntum, with public equestrian rank from five decuries, a worthy place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28800125.summary: A grant of a place of honor by the decurions of Carnuntum to Gaius Julius Magnus, recognizing his distinguished career as a military officer and imperial legate, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800127.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antonius Ingenuus tesserarius Antoniae Nubili coniugi et filiis pro pietate faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28800127.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Antonius Ingenuus, a signaller, arranged for this to be made for his wife Antonia Nubila and his sons out of piety.
EDCS-28800127.summary: A tomb inscription set up by Antonius Ingenuus for his family, honoring their memory and his own piety, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800128.text_edition: \n Caius Atius Quinti filius Aniensis miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXIIX stipendiorum X hic situs est\n
EDCS-28800128.translation: Gaius Atius, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe, a soldier of the 15th Apollinaris Legion, 28 years old with 10 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-28800128.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Atius, a soldier of the 15th Legion Apollinaris, recording his age and service length, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800129.text_edition: \n Attio Maximo militi legionis X annorum XXV Maxima An cara soror f LLPIAMMC annorum XXXVI C cum filiis AV APIN\n
EDCS-28800129.translation: For Attius Maximus, soldier of the 10th Legion, 25 years old, Maxima Ancara, sister, [and] 36-year-old C. [daughter?] with sons...
EDCS-28800129.summary: A funerary inscription for Attius Maximus, a soldier of the 10th Legion, and likely his sister Maxima Ancara, with incomplete information about her age and children, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800130.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Attio Romulo Augusta militi legionis X geminae ex armorum custode XIIII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28800130.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Gaius Attius Romulus Augusta, a soldier of the 10th Legion Gemina, from the 14th rank of armor guards, his heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-28800130.summary: A tomb inscription for Gaius Attius Romulus Augusta, a soldier and armor guard of the 10th Legion Gemina, commissioned by his heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800131.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Secundinae Caius Iulius Primitivus veteranus missus honesta missione A H\n
EDCS-28800131.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Aurelia Secundina, Gaius Julius Primitivus, a veteran discharged with honorable dismissal, arranged [this].
EDCS-28800131.summary: A tomb inscription for Aurelia Secundina, commissioned by Gaius Julius Primitivus, a veteran with an honorable discharge, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800132.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Ursulae coniugi carissimae nnorum XLVI Lucius Septimius Celsinus veteranus legionis X geminae vivus sibi et suis fecit\n
EDCS-28800132.translation: To the Divine Manes. Lucius Septimius Celsinus, veteran of the Legio X Gemina, made this monument while alive for himself and his family, for his dearest wife Aurelia Ursula, aged 46.
EDCS-28800132.summary: A funerary monument set up by a veteran for his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800133.text_edition: \n Titi Flavi Barbi veterani alae I Flaviae Augustae Britannicae |(milliariae) civium Romanorum Licinius Memor fratri suo posuit\n
EDCS-28800133.translation: Licinius Memor dedicated this to his brother, Titus Flavius Barbus, a veteran of the I Flavia Augusta Britannica milliaria civium Romanorum ala.
EDCS-28800133.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a brother to his deceased sibling, a veteran of an auxiliary cavalry unit, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800134.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Verecundus Mag eques alae I Flaviae Augustae Britannicae |(milliariae) civium Romanorum turma Italici annorum XXXX stipendiorum XIX hic intus situs est Proculus et Priscinus vexillarii et Ingenuus haeredes sic\n
EDCS-28800134.translation: Titus Flavius Verecundus, decurion of the turma Italici of the I Flavia Augusta Britannica milliaria civium Romanorum ala, aged 40 with 19 years of service, lies buried within; Proculus and Priscinus, standard-bearers, and Ingenuus, his heirs, arranged for this.
EDCS-28800134.summary: A funerary inscription for a decurion of an auxiliary cavalry unit, indicating his age and service length, with his heirs arranging the burial, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800135.text_edition: \n militi legionis X geminae centuria Senecae annorum XXV aerorum II hic situs est heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28800135.translation: To a soldier of the Legio X Gemina, century of Senecae, aged 25 with 2 years of service, buried here; his heir had this made.
EDCS-28800135.summary: A funerary inscription for a soldier of the Legio X Gemina, noting his age and service, with his heir arranging the burial, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800136.text_edition: \n Legio XIIII gemina Martia centuria Volumni Pudentis\n
EDCS-28800136.translation: Legio XIIII Gemina Martia, century of Volumnius Pudentis.
EDCS-28800136.summary: An inscription identifying a legion and a century, likely marking a military structure or grave, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800137.summary: A funerary inscription for Amandus, noting his age and the dedicant, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800138.text_edition: \n Atpomarus Ilonis filius nnorum XXV hic intus est situs Brogimarus fratri posuit\n
EDCS-28800138.translation: Atpomarus, son of Ilon, aged 25, lies buried within; Brogimarus dedicated this to his brother.
EDCS-28800138.summary: A funerary inscription for Atpomarus, noting his age and that it was dedicated by his brother, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800139.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Valeriano T militi legionis X anno--- XXIII defuncto in et Aureli-- Maiori NAN Florentinae RVSTRI et EFC f\n
EDCS-28800139.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Aurelius Valerianus, soldier of the Legio X, aged 23, who died in... and Aurelia Maiori, NAN Florentina Rustri, and EFC.
EDCS-28800139.summary: A damaged funerary inscription for a soldier of the Legio X and at least one other individual, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800140.text_edition: \n Cateius Scali filius et Verecundus Catei filius hic siti sunt Apia Cuma patri suo et fratri posuit\n
EDCS-28800140.translation: Cateius Scali and Verecundus, son of Cateius, lie buried here; Apia Cuma dedicated this to her father and brother.
EDCS-28800140.summary: A funerary inscription for a father and son, dedicated by the wife/mother, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800141.text_edition: \n Publius Titius Finitus vivus fecit sibi et Iucundae Civis filiae coniugi annorum XL\n
EDCS-28800141.translation: Publius Titius Finitus made this while alive for himself and for his wife Iucunda Civi, daughter of [---], aged 40.
EDCS-28800141.summary: A funerary monument set up by a man during his lifetime for himself and his wife, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800144.translation: From the Dalmatian cavalry wing, stationed in Pannonia.
EDCS-28800144.summary: This inscription records a dedication from a Dalmatian cavalry unit, likely originating from Pannonia.

EDCS-28800145.text_edition: \n Fortunae Caius Lucretius Apollinaris praefectus equitum\n
EDCS-28800145.translation: To Fortuna, Caius Lucretius Apollinaris, prefect of cavalry.
EDCS-28800145.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Fortuna by Caius Lucretius Apollinaris, a prefect of cavalry.

EDCS-28800148.text_edition: \n Pro salute Augusti iusso deae posita sorores quae contulerunt ad aram Iulia Censorina Maxumilia sic Vestina Semiri Lucia ndina Gallia s Laelia Nigrina asia Vera Suria Adiuncta Valeria Ursula Provincia Remula udauria Kalendas Ianuarias Imperatore Antonino II et Sacerdote consulibus\n
EDCS-28800148.translation: For the safety of Augustus, placed by order of the goddess, the sisters who contributed to the altar: Iulia Censorina Maxumilia, Vestina Semiri, Lucia Ndina, Gallia S., Laelia Nigrina, Asia Vera, Suria Adiuncta, Valeria Ursula, Provincia Remula, Udauria, on the Kalends of January, when Emperor Antoninus and Sacerdos were consuls.
EDCS-28800148.summary: This inscription records a dedication for the safety of the emperor, made by a group of women to an altar, likely during the consulship of Antoninus and Sacerdos (early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-28800150.text_edition: \n Avi veterano anno--- LXXX PERIACONA con sic quo vixit concorditer bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-28800150.translation: To the veteran Avi [died] in his 80th year... placed for him who lived harmoniously and well deserved.
EDCS-28800150.summary: This is a tombstone for Avi, a veteran who died in his 80s, erected by his family.

EDCS-28800151.text_edition: \n Merasius Luci filius domo Ticino Vitalis legionis IIII Flaviae felicis defunctus annorum LXXV et Novellae P\n
EDCS-28800151.translation: Vitalis, son of Lucius Merasius, from Ticinus, a soldier of the IV Flavia Felix legion, died at 75 years old, and Novella P...
EDCS-28800151.summary: This inscription records the death of Vitalis, a soldier of the IV Flavia Felix legion, and his wife Novella, with Vitalis being 75 years old.

EDCS-28800152.text_edition: \n Ariomanus Iliati filius Boius annorum XV hic situs est pater posuit\n
EDCS-28800152.translation: Ariomanus, son of Iliatus, a Boian, aged 15, is buried here; his father placed this.
EDCS-28800152.summary: This tombstone records the death of Ariomanus, a 15-year-old Boian, and indicates it was placed by his father.

EDCS-28800153.text_edition: \n Acaino Sumoti filiae annorum XL hic sita est Dibugius filius posuit\n
EDCS-28800153.translation: Acaino, daughter of Sumotius, aged 40, is buried here; her son Dibugius placed this.
EDCS-28800153.summary: This tombstone records the death of Acaino, aged 40, and indicates it was placed by her son Dibugius.

EDCS-28800154.text_edition: \n Caius Statius Cai libertus Gratus nnorum XXXV hic situs est Caius Statius Severus |(centurio) legionis XIII patronus posuit\n
EDCS-28800154.translation: Caius Statius Gratus, freedman of Caius Statius, aged 35, is buried here; Caius Statius Severus, a centurion of the XIII legion, his patron, placed this.
EDCS-28800154.summary: This tombstone records the death of Caius Statius Gratus, a freedman, and was erected by his patron, Caius Statius Severus, a centurion.

EDCS-28800155.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vegeto Silvani annorum L domo Savariae\n
EDCS-28800155.translation: To the departed spirits, Vegetus, son of Silvanus, aged 50, from Savaria.
EDCS-28800155.summary: This inscription is a tombstone for Vegetus of Savaria, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-28800156.text_edition: \n Marco Atilio Amoeno Atiliae Marci libertae Man Atiliae M Atiliae Atili sibi et coniugi \n
EDCS-28800156.translation: To Marcus Atilius Amoenus, Atilia, freedwoman of Marcus, and to Atilia M., Atilia, [and] Atilius, for themselves and their spouse.
EDCS-28800156.summary: This inscription records a dedication or tombstone for Marcus Atilius Amoenus, Atilia (freedwoman), and possibly other family members, intended for themselves and a spouse.

EDCS-28800157.text_edition: \n IConis fili-- hic sit-- est nus posuit\n
EDCS-28800157.translation: IConis, son of [--], was placed here; Nus erected it.
EDCS-28800157.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of IConis and its erection by Nus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800158.text_edition: \n Matta Catonis filia annorum LXXV hic YI sita est filii matri posuerunt\n
EDCS-28800158.translation: Matta, daughter of Caton, aged 75, is placed here; her sons erected this for their mother.
EDCS-28800158.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Matta, daughter of Caton, erected by her sons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800159.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae Titus Aurelius Cassianus praefectus legionum IIII Scythicae et XIIII geminae Antoninianarum et Aurelia Lucilla coniunx eius et Aurelia Terentia et Aurelius Cassianus Iunior filii votum solverunt libentes laeti merito\n
EDCS-28800159.translation: To the August Goddess Diana, Titus Aurelius Cassianus, prefect of the 4th Scythian and 14th Gemina Antoniniana legions, and his wife Aurelia Lucilla, and their children Aurelia Terentia and Aurelius Cassianus the Younger, fulfilled their vow willingly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-28800159.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Diana Augusta by a legionary prefect, his wife, and children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800160.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae sacrum Marcus Ulpius Marianus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800160.translation: Sacred to the August Goddess Diana. Marcus Ulpius Marianus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28800160.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Diana Augusta by Marcus Ulpius Marianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800161.text_edition: \n Dis Patris et Haerec(urae) sic Caesius Iustus et Flavius Fronto votum solverunt libentes merito sacerdote Flavio Salviano\n
EDCS-28800161.translation: To the Gods Father and Mother (Pluto and Proserpina), Caesius Iustus and Flavius Fronto fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly, with Flavius Salvianus as priest.
EDCS-28800161.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Pluto and Proserpina by Caesius Iustus and Flavius Fronto during the priesthood of Flavius Salvianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800164.text_edition: \n Fortunae Domesticae sacrum Sextus Afranius Firmus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800164.translation: Sacred to the Household Goddess Fortuna. Sextus Afranius Firmus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28800164.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Fortuna Domestica by Sextus Afranius Firmus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800165.text_edition: \n Fortunae Reginae Aurelius Marcus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28800165.translation: To Fortuna the Queen, Aurelius Marcus fulfilled his vow willingly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-28800165.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Fortuna Regina by Aurelius Marcus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800166.text_edition: \n Genio |(centuriae) Albani Longini Lucius Barbius Vitalis donum dedit\n
EDCS-28800166.translation: To the Genius of the century of Albanius Longinus, Lucius Barbius Vitalis gave this offering.
EDCS-28800166.summary: This inscription records an offering to the Genius of a specific centuria by Lucius Barbius Vitalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800167.text_edition: \n Genium Iovis Optimi Maximi Dolicheni sub sacerdotibus Antiocho et Marino\n
EDCS-28800167.translation: To the Genius of Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, under the priests Antiochos and Marinus.
EDCS-28800167.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Genius of Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus under the priesthood of Antiochos and Marinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800168.translation: To Hercules Augustus, the scouts with their own money.
EDCS-28800168.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Hercules Augustus by scouts using their own funds, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800169.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Sextus Aienius [dedicated this].
EDCS-28800169.summary: This is an altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sextus Aienius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800170.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Claudius Avitus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800170.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Claudius Avitus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28800170.summary: This inscription records Claudius Avitus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800171.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Gallius Celsinus cornicularius legati votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800171.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Gallius Celsinus, cornicularius of a legate, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28800171.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Gallius Celsinus, a cornicularius, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800172.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Servilius Emeritus miles legionis XIIII geminae vico Gallorum donum dedit aram Scapula Tertullo Tineio Clemente consulibus\n
EDCS-28800172.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of the Emperor, Servilius Emeritus, a soldier of the Legio XIIII Gemina from the Vicus Gallorum, dedicated this altar, [during the consulship] of Scapula Tertullus and Tineius Clemens.
EDCS-28800172.summary: This inscription records a soldier, Servilius Emeritus, dedicating an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the Emperor's health, with a clear consular date, suggesting the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-28800173.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Valerius Passerianus signifer legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800173.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Valerius Passerianus, signifer of the Legio XIIII Gemina, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28800173.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Valerius Passerianus, a signifer, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800174.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Vibo Marcellinus \n
EDCS-28800174.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius Vibo Marcellinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-28800174.summary: This is an altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Vibo Marcellinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800175.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Iunoni Reginae sacrum Iulia Censorina et Aurelius Mestrinus votum solverunt libentes laeti merito\n
EDCS-28800175.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to Juno Regina, Iulia Censorina and Aurelius Mestrinus fulfilled their vows willingly, joyfully, and deservedly.
EDCS-28800175.summary: This inscription records Iulia Censorina and Aurelius Mestrinus fulfilling vows to both Jupiter and Juno Regina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800177.text_edition: \n Marti Caletonius Secundus veteranus ex duplikario sic votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28800177.translation: To Mars, Caletonius Secundus, a veteran and former duplicarius, thus fulfilled his vow willingly, joyfully, and deservedly.
EDCS-28800177.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caletonius Secundus, a veteran, to Mars, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800178.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Adiectus servus Tiberi C V genitori luminis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28800178.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Adiectus, slave of Tiberius C.V. and father of Luminis, fulfilled his vow willingly, joyfully, and deservedly.
EDCS-28800178.summary: This inscription records a slave named Adiectus fulfilling a vow to Mithras, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800179.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Aurelius Victor ex voto posuit p<r>o suuis\n
EDCS-28800179.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Aurelius Victor placed this from his vow for his family.
EDCS-28800179.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Victor dedicating something to Mithras from his vow for his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800180.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Cauti Titus Flavius Verecundus Claudia Savaria |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28800180.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, Titus Flavius Verecundus, of Claudia Savaria, a centurion of the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion, gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28800180.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the god Mithras by Titus Flavius Verecundus, a centurion of the 14th legion, likely from the late 1st to early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800181.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Iulius Pacatus ex voto Marco Valerio sacerdote dedit\n
EDCS-28800181.translation: To the Unconquered god, Julius Pacatus gave this from his vow, with Marcus Valerius as priest.
EDCS-28800181.summary: This inscription records a gift to the Unconquered god, dedicated by Julius Pacatus and performed during the priesthood of Marcus Valerius, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800182.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Caius Atius Secundus vir perfectissimus templum vetustate conlabsum sic restituit\n
EDCS-28800182.translation: To the Unconquered god Mithras, Caius Atius Secundus, a man of the highest rank, restored this temple, which had collapsed from age.
EDCS-28800182.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple to the god Mithras by Caius Atius Secundus, a high-ranking official, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800184.text_edition: \n Nymphis Ulpius Titianus pro se et suis\n
EDCS-28800184.translation: To the Nymphs, Ulpius Titianus for himself and his family.
EDCS-28800184.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Nymphs by Ulpius Titianus for himself and his household, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800185.text_edition: \n Nymphis Augustis sacrum pro salute Cai Iuli Superi procuratoris Auggustorum provinciae Siciliae Iulia Aristiana liberta\n
EDCS-28800185.translation: Sacred to the August Nymphs for the health of Caius Julius Superus, procurator of the August emperors for the province of Sicily, by Iulia Aristiana, his freedwoman.
EDCS-28800185.summary: This inscription is a sacred offering to the Nymphs, made by the freedwoman Iulia Aristiana for the health of her patron, Caius Julius Superus, a procurator in Sicily, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800186.text_edition: \n Petrae Genetrici Publius Aelius Nigrinus sacerdos votum solvit\n
EDCS-28800186.translation: To the Mother Stone, Publius Aelius Nigrinus, priest, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28800186.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the Mother Stone by the priest Publius Aelius Nigrinus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800188.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto Genio loci pro salute Cai Vettii Sabiniani legati Augusti pro praetore Nymphicus libertus eius aediculam ex voto restituit\n
EDCS-28800188.translation: To Silvanus Augustus, guardian spirit of this place, Nymphicus, his freedman, restored a shrine from his vow for the health of Caius Vettius Sabinianus, imperial legate with proconsular power.
EDCS-28800188.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a shrine to Silvanus Augustus and the Genius Loci by Nymphicus, a freedman, for the health of his patron Caius Vettius Sabinianus, an imperial legate, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800191.summary: This is a sacred dedication to the household god Silvanus Domestus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800192.summary: This is a sacred dedication to the household god Silvanus Domestus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800193.summary: This is a sacred dedication to the household god Silvanus Domestus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800194.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus, likely from a private individual.

EDCS-28800195.text_edition: \n Deo Sancto Silvano Domestico Publius Acceptius Victorinus\n
EDCS-28800195.translation: To the Holy God Silvanus Domesticus, Publius Acceptius Victorinus.
EDCS-28800195.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus by Publius Acceptius Victorinus.

EDCS-28800196.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Gaius Domitius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800196.translation: Gaius Domitius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-28800196.summary: This inscription records Gaius Domitius fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Domesticus, likely on a small shrine or altar.

EDCS-28800197.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Gaius Domitius Tertius votum solvit libens laetus\n
EDCS-28800197.translation: Gaius Domitius Tertius gladly and joyfully fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-28800197.summary: This inscription records Gaius Domitius Tertius fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Domesticus with joy.

EDCS-28800198.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Lucius Geminius Victor votum solvit\n
EDCS-28800198.translation: Lucius Geminius Victor fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-28800198.summary: This inscription records Lucius Geminius Victor fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-28800199.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Publius S Firminus\n
EDCS-28800199.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus, Publius S. Firminus.
EDCS-28800199.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Publius S. Firminus.

EDCS-28800200.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Seius Venator votum solvit merito\n
EDCS-28800200.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus, Seius Venator deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28800200.summary: This inscription records Seius Venator fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-28800201.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Ulpius Candidus strator consularis\n
EDCS-28800201.translation: Ulpius Candidus, consular attendant, to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-28800201.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Domesticus by Ulpius Candidus, identified as a consular attendant.

EDCS-28800202.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico et Magulae sacrum Septimius enus miles legionis XIIII geminae Antoninianae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28800202.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus and the Magulae; Septimius Enus, a soldier of the XIIII Geminae Antoninianae legion, gladly and joyfully and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28800202.summary: This inscription records Septimius Enus, a soldier of the XIIII Geminae Antoninianae legion, fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Domesticus and the Magulae.

EDCS-28800203.text_edition: \n Silvanabus sic et Quadribiis sic Augustis sacrum Caius Antonius Valentinus veteranus legionis XIIII geminae murum a fundamentis cum suo introito sic et porticum cum accubito vetustate conlabsum sic impendio suo restituit Gentiano et Basso consulibus\n
EDCS-28800203.translation: Sacred to the Silvani and the Quadribia, revered deities; Caius Antonius Valentinus, a veteran of the XIIII Geminae legion, restored at his own expense the wall from its foundations with its entrance, and the portico with its resting place, which had collapsed from age, during the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus.
EDCS-28800203.summary: This inscription records Caius Antonius Valentinus, a veteran of the XIIII Geminae legion, restoring a wall and portico during the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus, likely dedicated to local deities.

EDCS-28800204.text_edition: \n Silvanis Silvestribus sacrum Marcus Aurelius Cupitianus votum libens solvit merito\n
EDCS-28800204.translation: Sacred to Silvanus and the Forest Deities, Marcus Aurelius Cupitianus gladly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-28800204.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the forest deities Silvanus and Silvestres by Marcus Aurelius Cupitianus.

EDCS-28800205.text_edition: \n Summae Parcae Aelius Vetu PA Imbrius FO \n
EDCS-28800205.translation: Aelius Vetu PA Imbrius FO (dedicated this) to the Supreme Fates.
EDCS-28800205.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication to the Fates by Aelius Vetu PA Imbrius FO.

EDCS-28800206.text_edition: \n Tranosito Gaius Cassius Apronianus custos armorum in honorem collegii votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28800206.translation: Gaius Cassius Apronianus, guardian of the arms, gladly and joyfully fulfilled his vow for the honor of the guild.
EDCS-28800206.summary: This inscription records Gaius Cassius Apronianus fulfilling a vow for the honor of the guild of armory guardians.

EDCS-28800207.text_edition: \n Tutelae et Genio loci Agathodorus actor\n
EDCS-28800207.translation: Agathodorus, the agent, (dedicated this) to the Tutela and the Genius of the place.
EDCS-28800207.summary: This inscription records Agathodorus, an agent, dedicating something to the protective deities (Tutela) and the Genius of the location.

EDCS-28800208.text_edition: \n Veneri sacrum pro salute Stata Marc et votum solverunt libentes laeti merito\n
EDCS-28800208.translation: For the health of Stata Marc, (they) gladly and joyfully fulfilled their vow to Venus, as was deserved.
EDCS-28800208.summary: This inscription records a vow made to Venus for the health of Stata Marc by unnamed dedicants.

EDCS-28800209.text_edition: \n Volcano Augusto Marcus Mucius Frontinuas et Lucius Valerius Cyrillus Augustales curatores thermarum\n
EDCS-28800209.translation: Marcus Mucius Frontinuas and Lucius Valerius Cyrillus, Augustales and curators of the baths, (dedicated this) to Vulcan Augustus.
EDCS-28800209.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Vulcan Augustus by Marcus Mucius Frontinuas and Lucius Valerius Cyrillus, who held public office as Augustales and bath curators.

EDCS-28800210.translation: For the health of Publius Milesius Ponticus.
EDCS-28800210.summary: This short inscription records a dedication or prayer for the health of Publius Milesius Ponticus.

EDCS-28800211.text_edition: \n Pro? sal(ute) Augusti Lucius Vibius Marcellinus veteranus legionis XIIII geminae Martiae votum solvit\n
EDCS-28800211.translation: For the health of the Emperor, Lucius Vibius Marcellinus, a veteran of the Fourteenth Gemina Martia legion, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28800211.summary: This inscription records Lucius Vibius Marcellinus, a veteran of a specific legion, fulfilling a vow for the Emperor's health.

EDCS-28800212.text_edition: \n Ulpius Terentius veteranus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800212.translation: Ulpius Terentius, a veteran, gladly fulfilled his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-28800212.summary: This inscription records Ulpius Terentius, a veteran, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28800214.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Pio Felici Augusto Parthico maximo Brittannico sic maximo pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVI imperatori II consuli III designato IIII patri patriae proconsuli cornicularii commentarienses speculatores legionum III Antoninianarum Pannoniae superioris devotissimi numini eius Cornicularii Antonius Probus Clarenius Marcellinus Mogetius Paulinus commentarienses Iulius Augurinus Septimius Restutus Ulpius Sabinus speculatores Flavius Marcus Severius Severus Aurelius Candidianus Septimius Licinius Valerius Victorinus Flavius Martialis Aelius Epianus Iulius Severus Septimius Lucius Licinius Festianus Claudius Claudianus Iulius Severus S NVS Aquilius Romulus Cassius Cassianus Turranius Titus Aurelius Florus Cetronius Cupitianus nnius Licinianus Aelius Gratus Coclius Ursulus Livius Valerianus Iulius Dignus Ulpius Violanus Terentius Terentianus Numenius Ingenuus Iulius Romanus nnius Antoninus Aurelius Virianus\n
EDCS-28800214.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus, conqueror of Parthia, conqueror of Britannia, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 3rd time, designated Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, Proconsul, the Cornicularii, Commentarienses, and Speculatores of the three Antoninian Legions of Upper Pannonia, most devoted to his divine majesty: Cornicularii Antonius Probus Clarenius Marcellinus, Mogetius Paulinus; Commentarienses Iulius Augurinus, Septimius Restutus, Ulpius Sabinus; Speculatores Flavius Marcus Severius Severus, Aurelius Candidianus, Septimius Licinius Valerius Victorinus, Flavius Martialis, Aelius Epianus, Iulius Severus, S NVS Aquilius Romulus, Cassius Cassianus, Turranius Titus Aurelius Florus, Cetronius Cupitianus, nnius Licinianus, Aelius Gratus Coclius Ursulus, Livius Valerianus, Iulius Dignus, Ulpius Violanus, Terentius Terentianus, Numenius Ingenuus, Iulius Romanus, nnius Antoninus, Aurelius Virianus.
EDCS-28800214.summary: This extensive inscription records a dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix by various military personnel including cornicularii, commentarienses, and speculatores from legions stationed in Upper Pannonia, likely dated during his 16th tribunician power and 2nd acclamation as Imperator.

EDCS-28800216.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aurelius Flavianus annorum XXXXII stipendiorum XX hic situs est arbitratu Marci Atili municipis sui Caius Quintinus heres posuit\n
EDCS-28800216.translation: To the shades of the departed. Publius Aurelius Flavianus, aged 42 and with 20 years of service, lies here; by the decision of Marcus Atilius, his fellow townsman, Caius Quintinus, his heir, placed this.
EDCS-28800216.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Publius Aurelius Flavianus, a soldier, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800217.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Laficus Rediti filius veteranus legionis XIIII geminae domo Nicapolis vivos sic sibi et Septimiae Crispinae coniugi meae et Aureliae Augustae filiae qui meae quietavit annorum quinque et Aureliae Septuminae libertae meae annorum LXXIII vivos sic sibi fecit\n
EDCS-28800217.translation: Marcus Aurelius Laficus, son of Reditus, a veteran of the 14th Gemina legion, from Nicopolis, made this for himself while alive, and for Septimia Crispinia, his wife, and for Aurelia Augusta, his daughter, who passed away at the age of five, and for Aurelia Septumina, his freedwoman, aged 73.
EDCS-28800217.summary: This tombstone records Marcus Aurelius Laficus, a veteran of the 14th Gemina legion, who commissioned his own tomb and those of his wife, daughter, and freedwoman, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800218.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Sextinae domo Germania superiore nurui et Aurelio Valentino militi legionis XIIII geminae marito eius et Aurelio Magno militi legionis XIIII geminae filio eius et Aurelis Micuni et Valentiae alumnis\n
EDCS-28800218.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Aurelia Sextina, from Upper Germania, his daughter-in-law, and for Aurelius Valentinus, a soldier of the 14th Gemina legion, her husband, and for Aurelius Magnus, a soldier of the 14th Gemina legion, her son, and for Aurelis Micunus and Valentia, his adopted children.
EDCS-28800218.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to multiple family members, including soldiers of the 14th Gemina legion, and is likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800219.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Campaniano frumentario legionis I adiutricis vixit annorum XXXIII stipendiorum XV Aelia Capitolina coniugi pientissimo fecit\n
EDCS-28800219.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Caius Campanianus, a grain official of the 1st Adjutrix legion, who lived 33 years and served 15 years. Aelia Capitolina, his most devoted wife, made this.
EDCS-28800219.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Campanianus, a frumentarius of the 1st Adjutrix legion, and was erected by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800220.text_edition: \n Quintus Cerfonius Quinti filius Scaptia tribu Celer Florentia miles legionis X geminae centuria - Aili sic Saturnini annorum XLVII aerum Prim\n
EDCS-28800220.translation: Quintus Cerfonius Celer, son of Quintus, of the Scaptia tribe, a soldier of the 10th Gemina legion, in the century of Saturninus, son of Ailus, aged 47, [paid for] the bronze [tomb]...
EDCS-28800220.summary: This inscription fragment records Quintus Cerfonius Celer, a soldier of the 10th Gemina legion, who died at age 47, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800221.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Crenscens eques alae Tampianae vexillationis Britannicae annorum XXX stipendiorum XV domo Durocortoro Remorum hic situs est Flavius Silvanus decurio alae eiusdem heres <f=E>(ecit) \n
EDCS-28800221.translation: Titus Flavius Crenscens, an equestrian of the Tampiana ala, of the British detachment, aged 30 and with 15 years of service, from Durocortorum of the Remi, lies here. Flavius Silvanus, a decurion of the same ala, his heir, made [this].
EDCS-28800221.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Flavius Crenscens, an equestrian officer from Durocortorum, and was erected by his heir, Flavius Silvanus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800222.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavio Crescentino Flavii filio annorum II et Claudio Crescentino militi legionis XIIII geminae annorum XXV stipendiorum X Flavius Secundinus miles legionis eiusdem filio et socero\n
EDCS-28800222.translation: To the shades of the departed. For Flavius Crescentinus, son of Flavius, aged 2 years, and for Claudius Crescentinus, a soldier of the 14th Gemina legion, aged 25 and with 10 years of service. Flavius Secundinus, a soldier of the same legion, [erected this for] his son and father-in-law.
EDCS-28800222.summary: This funerary inscription honors two individuals, a young child and a soldier of the 14th Gemina legion, erected by Flavius Secundinus, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800223.text_edition: \n turma Flavi nnorum XXXVIII stipendiorum XVIII hic situs est faciendum curavit Flavius Fronto decurio posuit\n
EDCS-28800223.translation: [Soldier of the] troop of Flavius, aged 38 and with 18 years of service, lies here. Flavius Fronto, a decurion, saw to its construction and placed [this].
EDCS-28800223.summary: This funerary inscription records a soldier belonging to the troop of Flavius, who died at age 38 after 18 years of service, with a decurion named Flavius Fronto overseeing its erection, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800224.text_edition: \n centuria Flavi Macri annorum stipendiorum X Pon Sa H\n
EDCS-28800224.translation: Century of Flavius Macr[us], aged [X] and with 10 years of service...
EDCS-28800224.summary: This inscription fragment names the century of Flavius Macr[us] and mentions 10 years of service, likely part of a military dedication or funerary record from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800225.text_edition: \n ntis stipendiorum XV annorum XXXV hic situs est Titus Flavius Verus \n
EDCS-28800225.translation: ... of service, aged 35, lies here. Titus Flavius Verus.
EDCS-28800225.summary: This funerary inscription records Titus Flavius Verus, who died at age 35 after 15 years of service, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800226.text_edition: \n Lucio Genucio Honoratiano nnorum VIII mensium X et Lucio Genucio Luciano annorum II dierum XXX et Lucio Genucio Kapitoni mensium IIII et Lucio Genucio Lucio annorum IIII mensium IIII dierum XV Lucius Genucius Exsoratus sic summus curator eqquitum singularium consularis et Cantabriaca Exsorata sic parentes domu Flavia Siscia fecerunt\n
EDCS-28800226.translation: To Lucius Genucius Honoratianus, 8 years, 10 months old, and Lucius Genucius Lucianus, 2 years, 30 days old, and Lucius Genucius Capito, 4 months old, and Lucius Genucius Lucius, 4 years, 4 months, 15 days old. Lucius Genucius Exsoratus, chief curator of the singulares equites, and Cantabrica Exsorata, his wife, from the Flavian house of Siscia, erected this. They were parents.
EDCS-28800226.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of four young children, identified by name and age, and was erected by their parents, Lucius Genucius Exsoratus and Cantabrica Exsorata, likely in Siscia.

EDCS-28800227.text_edition: \n Marcus Iulius Datus miles legionis II adiutricis stipendiorum VII annorum XXX Caio Iulio Att fratri pientissimo legionis eiusdem heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28800227.translation: To Marcus Iulius Datus, a soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, with 7 years of service, aged 30, his most dutiful brother Caius Iulius Att of the same legion, his heir, had this made.
EDCS-28800227.summary: This funerary epitaph records Marcus Iulius Datus, a soldier of the II Adiutrix legion who served for 7 years and died at 30, and was commissioned by his brother and heir.

EDCS-28800228.text_edition: \n Aulus Laebus Cai filius Fabatus annorum XL Ennia Veta coniux de suo posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-28800228.translation: Aulus Laebus, son of Caius, Fabatus, aged 40. Ennia Veta, his wife, placed this from her own means; he is buried here.
EDCS-28800228.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aulus Laebus Fabatus, aged 40, stating it was erected by his wife, Ennia Veta.

EDCS-28800229.text_edition: \n mater miserissima Septimio Ingenuo equiti legionis XIIII Geminae qui Parthia decidit in bello Septimio Iuliano equiti legionis XI et AVTENN\n
EDCS-28800229.translation: A most sorrowful mother to Septimius Ingenuus, a cavalryman of the XIV Gemina legion, who fell in battle in Parthia; to Septimius Iulianus, a cavalryman of the XI legion; and to AVTENN.
EDCS-28800229.summary: This epitaph, set up by a grieving mother, commemorates two cavalrymen, Septimius Ingenuus of the XIV Gemina legion who died in battle in Parthia, and Septimius Iulianus of the XI legion.

EDCS-28800230.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Cai filius Fabia Silvinus Valentia miles legionis centuria Rufili annorum LX aerum XXXIIII amicis ex testamento curantibus hic situs est carus suis tibi terra\n
EDCS-28800230.translation: Caius Valerius Silvinus, son of Caius, of the Fabia tribe, from Valentia, a soldier of the legion, in the centuria of Rufilius, aged 60, with 34 years of service. His friends, carrying out his will, had this placed. He is buried here, dear to his own. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-28800230.summary: This funerary epitaph records Caius Valerius Silvinus, a soldier aged 60 with 34 years of service, from Valentia, whose friends erected this monument according to his will.

EDCS-28800231.text_edition: \n Veiania Hospita nnorum XLV hic sita est et Lucius Fabricius Clemens miles cohortis I praetoriae Luci Fabrici evocati filius qui decessit in praetorio nnorum XXIIX stipendiorum VIII Fabricia Luci filia Marcella matri suae et fratri posuit felix terra precor leviter super ossa residas matris et et fratris comprecor ecce soror pars iacet ipsa mei maior geminatque dolorem filia matri simul fratre iacent filio comprecor ut vobis sit pia terra levis\n
EDCS-28800231.translation: Veiania Hospita, aged 45, is buried here, and Lucius Fabricius Clemens, a soldier of the I Praetorian cohort, son of Lucius Fabricius, an evocatus, who died in the Praetorium at the age of 28, with 8 years of service. Fabricia Marcella, daughter of Lucius, placed this for her mother and brother. May the earth rest lightly on you. I implore you, may the earth be kind to you. See, his sister lies here too, doubling my grief. Daughter lies with mother and brother. I implore that the earth may be light upon you.
EDCS-28800231.summary: This complex epitaph commemorates Veiania Hospita, aged 45, and her son Lucius Fabricius Clemens, a soldier aged 28 with 8 years of service, erected by his sister Fabricia Marcella.

EDCS-28800232.text_edition: \n Gaio Victorio Urso veterano legionis I Adiutricis Iuliae Hispanillae coniugi eius ipsi sibi faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-28800232.translation: To Gaius Victorius Urso, a veteran of the I Adiutrix legion, and to Iulia Hispanilla, his wife. They arranged for this to be made for themselves.
EDCS-28800232.summary: This funerary epitaph records Gaius Victorius Urso, a veteran of the I Adiutrix legion, and his wife Iulia Hispanilla, for whom they commissioned their tomb.

EDCS-28800233.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Iulio Candidiano equiti Romano annorum XIII et Iuliae Macrinae nnorum XIII Gaius Iulius <P=R>roculus decurio municipii Mursellae ne<p=R>oti et filiae faciendum c(uravit) \n
EDCS-28800233.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Iulius Proculus, decurion of the municipality of Mursella, and his wife, erected this for Marcus Iulius Candidianus, a Roman cavalryman, aged 13, and Iulia Macrina, aged 13.
EDCS-28800233.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Marcus Iulius Candidianus and Iulia Macrina, both aged 13, and was erected by Gaius Iulius Proculus, a decurion of Mursella, and his wife.

EDCS-28800235.text_edition: \n legionis XIV annorum XL stipendiorum XVIII NS CCEMEICV \n
EDCS-28800235.translation: Of the 14th legion, aged 40, with 18 years of service.
EDCS-28800235.summary: This fragmentary epitaph records a soldier of the 14th legion who was 40 years old and had served for 18 years.

EDCS-28800237.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Titi filius Sergia Probus decurio municipii Carnunti ex V decuriis ob honorem auguratus Titi Flavi Probi filii sui decurionis municipii eiusdem equo publico donum dat locus datus decreto decurionum Orfito et Rufo consulibus Kalendis Septembribus\n
EDCS-28800237.translation: Titus Flavius Probus, son of Titus, of the Sergia tribe, decurion of the municipality of Carnuntum, from the group of ten decurions, by public decree of the decurions, gives this as a gift in honor of his son, Titus Flavius Probus, a decurion of the same municipality, honored with the public horse, on the Kalends of September, in the consulship of Orfitus and Rufus.
EDCS-28800237.summary: This inscription records a public donation made by Titus Flavius Probus, a decurion of Carnuntum, to honor his son, also named Titus Flavius Probus, a decurion who had received the public horse, with the act formalized by decree.

EDCS-28800238.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Celsus Imperatoris lecti<c=R>arius decuria Eutacti hic situs est \n
EDCS-28800238.translation: Titus Flavius Celsus, bodyguard of the Emperor from the decuria of Eutactus, is buried here.
EDCS-28800238.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Flavius Celsus, an imperial bodyguard, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-28800239.text_edition: \n ------] I Amabilis cives sic Aeduus defunctus in \n
EDCS-28800239.translation: I Amabilis, a citizen of the Aedui, having died here...
EDCS-28800239.summary: This fragmentary tombstone records the death of Amabilis, a citizen of the Aedui, whose full epitaph is lost.

EDCS-28800240.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Betulonius Amandus domi sic Treverorum annorum LX hic situs est Betulonius Creticus libertus et heres pro pietate faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28800240.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Betulonius Amandus, of the Treveri by birth, aged sixty years, is buried here. Betulonius Creticus, freedman and heir, had this made as a duty of piety.
EDCS-28800240.summary: This tombstone for Lucius Betulonius Amandus, a man from the Treveri tribe, was erected by his freedman and heir, dating likely to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28800241.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Naevio Primigenio domo Naristo nnorum LXXV filia Creusa parenti pientissimo et Naevia coniunx posuerunt et ceteri sui\n
EDCS-28800241.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Marcus Naevius Primigenius, of Naristus by birth, aged seventy-five years. His daughter Creusa and his wife Naevia placed this for their most dutiful parent, and also his other relatives.
EDCS-28800241.summary: This tombstone honors Marcus Naevius Primigenius, who was from Naristus and died at seventy-five, and was erected by his daughter and wife, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28800742.text_edition: \n Quintus Idimius LIFEG VL IAR AVO annorum XXIII F IPLM SYRANIT Quinto Idimio QIII PATERO anT Quinto Idimi fil viator vale libertis liber<t=F>abusque posterisque suis hoc monumentum <h=L>(eredem) non sequetur \n
EDCS-28800742.translation: Quintus Idimius, aged twenty-three years, son of Q. Idimius Pater, son of Quintus Idimius. Traveler, farewell. This monument will not follow its heir, freedmen, freedwomen, and descendants.
EDCS-28800742.summary: This tombstone for Quintus Idimius, aged 23, states that the monument is not to be inherited, possibly indicating it was a family burial plot and likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28800744.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus viva fecit Aelia Publi filia Matrona sibi et suis\n
EDCS-28800744.translation: Aelia Matrona, daughter of Publius, made this for herself and her family while she was alive.
EDCS-28800744.summary: This tombstone for Aelia Matrona, daughter of Publius, records her setting up the monument for herself and her family, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28800745.text_edition: \n Memoriae aeternae Aurelius Reginus Alexsi<a=VS> sic cara coniux posuit et amantia \n
EDCS-28800745.translation: Aurelius Reginus placed this for the eternal memory of his beloved wife Alexsia and her affections.
EDCS-28800745.summary: This tombstone records Aurelius Reginus setting up a memorial for his wife Alexsia, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28800746.text_edition: \n In memoriam Aureliae Rufinae coniugi obsequentissimae adque pudicissimae et innocenttissimae Aurelius Nicander VNLCV dies XV ob memoriam posuit\n
EDCS-28800746.translation: In memory of Aurelia Rufina, his most obedient, chaste, and innocent wife. Aurelius Nicander placed this for fifteen days in memory.
EDCS-28800746.summary: This tombstone is dedicated by Aurelius Nicander to his wife Aurelia Rufina, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28800747.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that begins with a dedication to Crispina, with very little further information.

EDCS-28800748.text_edition: \n Lucernio qui vixet sic annos VII et Gaudentianus frater ipsius Germanus qui vixit annus sic IIII <G=C>raecianae uxori \n
EDCS-28800748.translation: For Lucernius, who lived for seven years, and Gaudentianus, his brother, who lived for four years. For Graeciana, his wife...
EDCS-28800748.summary: This fragmentary tombstone records the deaths of two young brothers, Lucernius and Gaudentianus, and mentions their mother Graeciana, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28800749.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Marcio tri carissimo P\n
EDCS-28800749.translation: To the divine shades of Lucius Marcius, most dear father.
EDCS-28800749.summary: A funerary dedication to Lucius Marcius, identified as the father of the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800750.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et Maximianae Aemiliae et securitati eius perpetuae quae vixit nnos XXXVII Flavio Ursino dulcissimo marito meo in XXX et VIII ascendens animam deposui meam vos itaque Inferi ad quos me praecipitem Di Superi coegerunt minimo cum tormento admittite ossaque mea in hac arca bene composita condidit Flavius Ursinus maritus dulcissimus et Flavius Maximianus filius meus et post haec omnia valete Flavius Ursinus Maximianae Aemiliae coniugi karissimae et castissimae posui vivusque feci cum qua vixi nnis XVIIII mensibus VIIII ponique me post mortem meam ab herede meo in hac arca iussi AI qui et Iulia Maximiana homo fuit mortua est hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-28800750.translation: To the divine shades and to Maximiana Aemilia, and to her perpetual security, who lived for 37 years. To Flavius Ursus, my sweetest husband, I laid down my soul in my 38th year. And so, Inferi, to whom the Gods Above have hurried me headlong, admit me with the least torment, and my bones were well-arranged in this chest and buried by Flavius Ursus, dearest husband, and Flavius Maximianus, my son. And after all these things, farewell. Flavius Ursus erected this for his dearest and most chaste wife, Maximiana Aemilia, with whom he lived for 19 years and 9 months, and ordered me to be placed in this chest after my death by my heir. AI, who was also Iulia Maximiana, was a person. She died. This monument will not follow an heir.
EDCS-28800750.summary: A lengthy funerary inscription commemorating Maximiana Aemilia, who died at 37, and her husband Flavius Ursus, who erected the monument and planned for his own burial there, likely from the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-28800751.text_edition: \n Domui aeternae Messillae factum quae vixit cum conpatre suo Romano annis XXXVIII hoc est sepulcrum commune ma<r=T>ito cum \n
EDCS-28800751.translation: For the eternal house of Messilla, who lived with her partner Romanus for 38 years. This is a shared tomb for the husband.
EDCS-28800751.summary: A funerary inscription for Messilla, who lived with her partner Romanus for 38 years, indicating a shared tomb, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800752.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septumiae sic Marcellae quae vixit nnos XX Cassia Candida filiae karissimae\n
EDCS-28800752.translation: To the divine shades of Septumia Marcella, who lived 20 years. To Cassia Candida, dearest daughter.
EDCS-28800752.summary: A funerary dedication from Cassia Candida to her dearest daughter, Septumia Marcella, who died at age 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800757.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Graecus DE FA MAX TITV CVOCS MVMAPIO filio \n
EDCS-28800757.translation: To the divine shades of Graecus. [text illegible] son.
EDCS-28800757.summary: A partial funerary inscription dedicated to the divine shades of a person named Graecus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800758.translation: To the deified Commodus, from the res publica (state) of the Iasi.
EDCS-28800758.summary: An inscription dedicating an offering or monument to the deified emperor Commodus by the civic community of the Iasi, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28800763.text_edition: \n Marcus Nunnidius Successus veteranus cohortis XXXII civium Romanorum annorum XC Petroniae Cai filiae Proclae coniugi annorum LX Nunnidia Vitalis annorum XIX Marco Nunnidio Saturnino annorum VII sibi et suis vivus posuit\n
EDCS-28800763.translation: Marcus Nunnidius Successus, a veteran of the 32nd cohort of Roman citizens, aged 90; to Petronia Cai, his daughter, Procla, aged 60; to Nunnidia Vitalis, aged 19; to Marcus Nunnidius Saturninus, aged 7; for himself and his family, erected while alive.
EDCS-28800763.summary: A funerary monument erected by a veteran, Marcus Nunnidius Successus, for himself and his family, including his wife, daughter, and two children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800764.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Publius Aelius Verus pro salute sua et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800764.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Publius Aelius Verus paid his vow freely and deservedly for his own health and that of his family.
EDCS-28800764.summary: A dedication to Hercules Augustus by Publius Aelius Verus, fulfilling a vow for his and his family's well-being, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800768.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Antonius Arga A \n
EDCS-28800768.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Antonius Arga A.
EDCS-28800768.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Antonius Arga, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800769.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Clodius Avitus decurio coloniae Ulpiae Traianae Poetovionensis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800769.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Clodius Avitus, decurion of the colony of Ulpia Traiana Poetovionensis, paid his vow freely and deservedly.
EDCS-28800769.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Clodius Avitus, a decurion from Poetovio, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800770.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Fortunatus Augusti libertus adiutor tabulariorum provinciae Pannoniae superioris\n
EDCS-28800770.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Fortunatus, freedman of the emperor and assistant of the record keepers of the province of Upper Pannonia.
EDCS-28800770.summary: This dedication to Jupiter was made by Fortunatus, an imperial freedman and an assistant in the provincial record office of Upper Pannonia.

EDCS-28800771.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Gongi Nestoriani procuratoris Augggustorum nnnostrorum Ianuarius eorundem servus contrascriptor votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800771.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. For the safety of Gongius Nestor, procurator of our emperors, Ianuarius, a clerk of the same, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28800771.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Ianuarius, a clerk, for the health of Gongius Nestor, a procurator of the emperors.

EDCS-28800772.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Iulius Maximus trierarcha classis Flaviae Pannonicae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800772.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Lucius Iulius Maximus, trierarch of the Flavian Pannonian fleet, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28800772.summary: This dedication to Jupiter was made by Lucius Iulius Maximus, a trierarch of the Flavian Pannonian fleet.

EDCS-28800773.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Livia Genetiva ex voto\n
EDCS-28800773.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Livia Genetiva, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28800773.summary: This brief dedication to Jupiter indicates that Livia Genetiva fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-28800774.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Marcus Octavius Verinus pro salute sua et ae MarTOC \n
EDCS-28800774.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Marcus Octavius Verinus, for his own safety and that of Mar... .
EDCS-28800774.summary: This dedication to Jupiter by Marcus Octavius Verinus was made for his own safety and that of another person, whose name is partially lost.

EDCS-28800775.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus Valerius Nymphodotus pro salute sua et Sexti Valeri Severini filii votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800775.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Sextus Valerius Nymphodotus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly for his own safety and that of his son Sextus Valerius Severinus.
EDCS-28800775.summary: This inscription records Sextus Valerius Nymphodotus fulfilling a vow for the health of himself and his son, Sextus Valerius Severinus.

EDCS-28800776.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Culminali Iunoni Reginae et Genio loci Philadespotus Auggustorum nnostrorum verna custos tabularii pro salute sua suorumque omnium ex voto \n
EDCS-28800776.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest Culminalis, to Juno Regina, and to the Genius of the place. Philadespotus, imperial freedman, a steward of the imperial household and keeper of the record office, for his own safety and that of all his family, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28800776.summary: This dedication to Jupiter, Juno Regina, and the local Genius was made by Philadespotus, an imperial freedman serving as a steward and record office keeper, for the well-being of himself and his family.

EDCS-28800777.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Marcus Ulpius Ceionius decurio coloniae Poetovionensium sacerdotalis vir egregius pro salute sua et Speciatiae Caesiae uxoris suorumque omnium votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800777.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest Depulsor. Marcus Ulpius Ceionius, decurion of the colony of Poetovio, an outstanding priest, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly for his own safety and that of his wife Speciatia Caesia and all his family.
EDCS-28800777.summary: This inscription records Marcus Ulpius Ceionius, a prominent decurion and priest of Poetovio, fulfilling a vow for the health of himself, his wife Speciatia Caesia, and their family.

EDCS-28800778.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori pro salute Secundina filia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800778.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest Depulsor. For the safety of his daughter Secundina, he fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28800778.summary: This dedication to Jupiter Depulsor was made by an unnamed individual for the well-being of his daughter Secundina.

EDCS-28800779.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Didymus Augggustorum libertus ex nummulario provinciae Pannoniae superioris pro salute sua et Aureliae Alexandriae coniugis votum solvit libens merito Apro et Maximo consulibus\n
EDCS-28800779.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest Dolichenus. Didymus, freedman of the emperors and a nummularius of the province of Upper Pannonia, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly for his own safety and that of his wife Aurelia Alexandria, in the consulship of Apro and Maximus.
EDCS-28800779.summary: This dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus was made by Didymus, an imperial freedman and financial official in Upper Pannonia, for the health of himself and his wife Aurelia Alexandria during the consulship of Apro and Maximus.

EDCS-28800780.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Titus Flavius Carinus pro salute sua et Flaviae Victorinae coniugis et suorum votum solvit libens merito <I=T>I <Si=FL>E?lanis consulibus \n
EDCS-28800780.translation: Titus Flavius Carinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus for the safety of himself, his wife Flavia Victorina, and their family, under the consuls Ti. Flavius Silanus.
EDCS-28800780.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus by Titus Flavius Carinus for his family's safety, mentioning the consuls for dating.

EDCS-28800781.text_edition: \n Templum Deii sic Solis Invicti Mithrae Aurelius Iustinianus vir perfectissimus dux labefactatum restituit\n
EDCS-28800781.translation: Aurelius Iustinianus, a most excellent vir perfectissimus and duke, restored the temple of the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, which had fallen into disrepair.
EDCS-28800781.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple dedicated to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras by Aurelius Iustinianus, a high-ranking official.

EDCS-28800782.text_edition: \n Soli sacrum Caius Domitius Hermes votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28800782.translation: Caius Domitius Hermes willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Sun God.
EDCS-28800782.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the Sun God by Caius Domitius Hermes.

EDCS-28800783.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae sacrum Statius Marci filius Saturninus pro Caio Statio Cassiano fratre ex voto\n
EDCS-28800783.translation: Dedicated to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, Statius Marci filius Saturninus fulfilled his vow for his brother Caius Statins Cassianus.
EDCS-28800783.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the Unconquered Sun God Mithras by Statius Marci filius Saturninus on behalf of his brother, Caius Statins Cassianus.

EDCS-28800785.text_edition: \n Nymphis Augustis sacrum Eucarpus Augusti libertus tabularius provinciae Pannoniae superioris\n
EDCS-28800785.translation: Dedicated to the August Nymphs, Eucarpus, a freedman of Augustus and a clerk of the province of Upper Pannonia, [erected this].
EDCS-28800785.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the August Nymphs by Eucarpus, an imperial freedman and provincial clerk in Upper Pannonia.

EDCS-28800786.text_edition: \n IVLITT Tiberius Claudius Rufus procurator Augusti ex voto\n
EDCS-28800786.translation: Tiberius Claudius Rufus, procurator of Augustus, [erected this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-28800786.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Tiberius Claudius Rufus, an imperial procurator.

EDCS-28800788.text_edition: \n OMO Augusti nostri dispensator rationis patrimonii privatae ar<c=K>ae cum suis omnibus ex voto \n
EDCS-28800788.translation: The dispenser of the account of the imperial private estates, Arcae, with all his household, fulfilled his vow to the Emperor.
EDCS-28800788.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by the imperial administrator Arcae and his family to the Emperor.

EDCS-28800790.translation: The people of Poetovio to Julia Augusta, mother of the camps.
EDCS-28800790.summary: This inscription is a dedication from the people of Poetovio to Julia Augusta, likely the mother of Emperor Caracalla.

EDCS-28800791.text_edition: \n ex decurione alae Caius Cassius Silvester veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae ex signifero \n
EDCS-28800791.translation: Caius Cassius Silvester, a veteran of the IV Flavia legion and a former standard-bearer, from the rank of decurion.
EDCS-28800791.summary: This inscription records the military career of Caius Cassius Silvester, a veteran and former standard-bearer.

EDCS-28800792.text_edition: \n mensibus III Fortunatus Augusti libertus adiutor tabularariorum testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-28800792.translation: Fortunatus, a freedman of Augustus and assistant to the clerks, ordered this to be made in his will for three months.
EDCS-28800792.summary: This inscription records a posthumous dedication ordered in the will of Fortunatus, an imperial freedman and assistant clerk.

EDCS-28800793.text_edition: \n Restituta Feliciano Augusti nostri liberto ex tabulario vectigalis Illyrici cum Felicissimo filio Augusti nostri liberto\n
EDCS-28800793.translation: Restituta, freedwoman of our emperor, from the tax office of Illyricum, together with Felicissimus, a freedman of our emperor, made this with her son.
EDCS-28800793.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or a building repair made by Restituta, a freedwoman of the emperor and an official from the Illyrian tax office, and her son Felicissimus.

EDCS-28800795.text_edition: \n Venuleio Proculo vixit anno I diebus X Proculus Auggustorum nnostrorum verna vilicus XX hereditatium utrarumque nnoniarum cum Valentina filio fecerunt\n
EDCS-28800795.translation: Proculus, a household slave of our emperors, steward of the XX Inheritance office for both regions, with his son Valentinus, made this when he was 1 year and 10 days old.
EDCS-28800795.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Proculus, a slave and steward, who died at the age of one year and ten days, with the monument erected by his father and brother.

EDCS-28800797.text_edition: \n Patrini decurionis coloniae Ulpiae Traianae Poetovionensis Titus Allius Celer decurio coloniae eiusdem frater et Quintus Germanius Valens\n
EDCS-28800797.translation: Titus Allius Celer, decurion of the same colony, brother, and Quintus Germanicus Valens [erected this for] Patrinus, decurion of the colony of Ulpia Traiana Poetovio.
EDCS-28800797.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Titus Allius Celer and Quintus Germanicus Valens in honor of their brother, Patrinus, a decurion in the colony of Poetovio.

EDCS-28800799.text_edition: \n Caius Caesius Cai filius Papiria Ingenuus Poetovione vivus fecit sibi et Ulpiae Adiutae coniugi et Caesiae Ingenuae filiae et libertis libertabusque\n
EDCS-28800799.translation: Gaius Cassius Ingenuus, son of Papiria, made this while alive for himself, for his wife Ulpia Adiuta, for his daughter Cassia Ingenua, and for his freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-28800799.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a tomb built during the lifetime of Gaius Cassius Ingenuus for himself and his family, located in Poetovio.

EDCS-28800801.text_edition: \n Calligeniae filiae dulcissimae annorum III mensium VI dierum XV Aurelius Callimorphus et Aelia Sabina parentes infelicissimi\n
EDCS-28800801.translation: For Calligenia, dearest daughter, aged 3 years, 6 months, and 15 days. Aurelius Callimorphus and Aelia Sabina, most unhappy parents.
EDCS-28800801.summary: This is a deeply personal funerary inscription commemorating the death of a young child named Calligenia, erected by her grieving parents.

EDCS-28800804.translation: To the divine spirits of Iulia Maxima, [deceased] daughter of DIVI...
EDCS-28800804.summary: This is the beginning of a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Maxima, a daughter of a deified individual.

EDCS-28800805.text_edition: \n rus V Memmius Nepos vivi ndus F H \n
EDCS-28800805.translation: Memmius Nepos, while alive, [made this for]... H
EDCS-28800805.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, indicating that Memmius Nepos erected something during his lifetime.

EDCS-28800806.text_edition: \n III Salvia Quintiana coniugi karissimo viva fecit et sibi et Tit\n
EDCS-28800806.translation: Salvia Quintiana, while alive, made this for her dearest husband, and for herself, and for Tit...
EDCS-28800806.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Salvia Quintiana, while alive, prepared a tomb for her husband and herself, with part of the dedication lost.

EDCS-28800807.text_edition: \n Gaius Septumius sic Gai filius Severus annorum XXIIII hic situs est Septumia sic Gai liberta Festa mater sibi et filio posuit\n
EDCS-28800807.translation: Gaius Septimius Severus, son of Gaius, aged 24 years, lies here; his mother Festa, freedwoman of Gaius, placed this for herself and her son.
EDCS-28800807.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Septimius Severus at age 24, with the monument erected by his mother Festa.

EDCS-28800808.text_edition: \n Victori Quadrati et Iuliaes! Veranillaes! Iulius Victorius Quadratus filius eorum hic positus\n
EDCS-28800808.translation: For Victor Quadratus and Iulia Veranilla. Their son Iulius Victor Quadratus is placed here.
EDCS-28800808.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Iulius Victor Quadratus, with the monument likely erected by his parents, Victor Quadratus and Iulia Veranilla.

EDCS-28800809.text_edition: \n Marcelliano menses V dies XIII IO F S X C collegium lanionum AT\n
EDCS-28800809.translation: Marcellianus: 5 months, 13 days. IO F S X C. Guild of butchers.
EDCS-28800809.summary: This is likely a tombstone inscription, recording the duration of life or a period of service, possibly related to a butcher's guild, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800819.translation: Spectatilla fulfilled her vow willingly, as deserved.
EDCS-28800819.summary: This is a votive inscription, recording a vow fulfilled by Spectatilla, likely dedicated to a deity, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800827.text_edition: \n Egnati--- o Valentini filio o Ceius Maximus Augustalis coloniae conlibertus et Perpetua et sibi et Aemilio Lucidae coniugi et Valentinae et Maximae et Vitali et Verino et Maximiano et Lucidae et Luc conlibertis in fronte pedes XXXXV FX o in agro pedes \n
EDCS-28800827.translation: To Egnatius Valentinus, son of [---]. Ceius Maximus Augustalis, freedman of the colony, and Perpetua, and for themselves, and for Aemilius Lucidas, husband, and for Valentina, and Maxima, and Vitalis, and Verinus, and Maximianus, and Lucida, and Luc, freedmen. 45 feet in front, [---] feet in the field.
EDCS-28800827.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Egnatius Valentinus and others, set up by Ceius Maximus Augustalis and Perpetua, and specifies the dimensions of the plot, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800828.text_edition: \n Imperator Vespasianus Marco Annio Afrino leg \n
EDCS-28800828.translation: Emperor Vespasian to Marcus Annius Afrinus, legate.
EDCS-28800828.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Emperor Vespasian and Marcus Annius Afrinus as a legate, indicating a military or administrative context, likely from the Flavian period (AD 69-96).

EDCS-28800829.summary: This is a brief inscription, possibly a dedication or a tombstone, mentioning Ulpia Vinda and Viator, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800830.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Depulsori Caius Tiberinius Faventinus decurio coloniae Poetovionis praefectus fabrum quaestor praefectus pro IIviris pro salute sua suorumque omnium\n
EDCS-28800830.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Repeller. Gaius Tiberinius Faventinus, decurion of the colony of Poetovio, prefect of the engineers, quaestor, prefect for praetor, for his own health and that of all his family.
EDCS-28800830.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Gaius Tiberinius Faventinus, listing his various official positions in the colony of Poetovio, and praying for his health and that of his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800831.text_edition: \n nnorum XXXV et Helbonius Maximinus annorum IV hic siti sunt Helbonia Faventina viva fecit sibi et coniugi et filio rogata testamento Maximi Voltionis con Helbonius Speratus annorum IV\n
EDCS-28800831.translation: [---] years, 35, and Helbonius Maximinus, 4 years old. Helbonia Faventina made this while alive for herself, and for her husband, and for her son, at the request in the will of Maximus Voltionis, son of Helbonius Speratus, 4 years old.
EDCS-28800831.summary: This is a tombstone inscription commemorating Helbonius Maximinus (35 years old) and another Helbonius Speratus (4 years old), erected by Helbonia Faventina for herself, her husband, and her son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800834.text_edition: \n Genium C cum suo templo Quintus Claudius Lucanus Augustalis coloniae Augustae Treverorum C Quintus Claudius Secundus Augustalis coloniae Claudiae <S=C>(avariae) \n
EDCS-28800834.translation: To the Genius of C[---] with his temple. Quintus Claudius Lucanus Augustalis of the colony of Augusta Treverorum. Quintus Claudius Secundus Augustalis of the colony of Savaria.
EDCS-28800834.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Genius of C[---] and mentions two individuals, Quintus Claudius Lucanus and Quintus Claudius Secundus, both holding the title Augustalis in different colonies, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28800836.text_edition: \n Claudia Quarta annorum LV hic sita est Claudius Cives filius faciendum curavit annorum LXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-28800836.translation: Claudia Quarta, 55 years old, is buried here. Claudius Cives, her son, arranged for this to be made. [He is] 80 years old and is buried here.
