EDCS-29000576.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Aurelius Celerinus, veteran of the II Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana legion, a beneficiary of a tribune, along with his family, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly, during the consulship of Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander and Dio.
EDCS-29000576.summary: This inscription is a votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Aurelius Celerinus, a veteran, made during the consulship of Severus Alexander and Dio, indicating a date of 224 AD.

EDCS-29000577.text_edition: \n pro salute Auggustorum Quintus Maenius Domitius \n
EDCS-29000577.translation: For the safety of the Emperors, Quintus Maenius Domitius.
EDCS-29000577.summary: This inscription records a vow or prayer for the well-being of the emperors, made by Quintus Maenius Domitius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-29000578.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Marciano in flore aetatis constituto hominibus erepto Aurelius Marcus decurio coloniae pater et sibi\n
EDCS-29000578.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Marcianus, established in the prime of life, taken from men, Aurelius Marcus, decurion of the colony, and his father, and for himself.
EDCS-29000578.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Marcus Aurelius Marcianus, erected by Aurelius Marcus, a decurion of the colony, and his father, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-29000582.text_edition: \n Buttoni Romani filio nnorum IXX pater posuit\n
EDCS-29000582.translation: To Buttonius Romanus, son of [father's name], aged 19 years; his father erected [this].
EDCS-29000582.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument for Buttonius Romanus, aged 19, erected by his father, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-29000586.text_edition: \n Nymphis Medicis sacrum Caius Iulius Commodus Orfitianus legatus Augusti pro praetore votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29000586.translation: Sacred to the Healing Nymphs, Gaius Julius Commodus Orfitianus, legate of the emperor with proconsular power, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-29000586.summary: This inscription records a votive dedication to the Nymphs Mediceae by Gaius Julius Commodus Orfitianus, a high-ranking imperial legate, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-29000587.translation: To Neratus, son of Ursa, aged 45 years.
EDCS-29000587.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication for Neratus, son of Ursa, aged 45, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-29000588.text_edition: \n Roma Surus qui officium dedicatum habet vivat per multa saecula semper\n
EDCS-29000588.translation: Roma Surus, who has a dedicated office, may he live for many ages forever.
EDCS-29000588.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Roma Surus, likely a soldier, expressing a wish for his eternal life.

EDCS-29100230.text_edition: \n Marti Victori filius Oufentina ianus veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris IMPLICETI\n
EDCS-29100230.translation: To Mars the Victorious, Janus, a veteran of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, from Impliceti.
EDCS-29100230.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Mars the Victorious by Janus, a veteran of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, likely from the region of Impliceti.

EDCS-29100231.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Cai Iuli Veri Maximini Pii Felicis Invicti Augusti Ulpius Amandianus miles legionis XIIII geminae librarius numeri suprascripti custos armorum signifer optio octavi principis prioris candidatus numini cum Ulpio Amando veterano legionis supra scriptae posuit\n
EDCS-29100231.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, for the health of the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Ulpius Amandianus, soldier of the fourteenth Gemina legion, scribe of the aforementioned unit, custos armorum, standard-bearer, optio of the eighth prior princeps, and candidate, dedicated this with Ulpius Amandus, a veteran of the aforementioned legion.
EDCS-29100231.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus for the health of Emperor Maximinus Thrax, made by Ulpius Amandianus, a soldier with various roles in the 14th legion, and a veteran named Ulpius Amandus.

EDCS-29100232.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Heliopolitano Lucius Pompeius Caeneus princeps legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis ex viso votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29100232.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus of Heliopolis, Lucius Pompeius Caeneus, princeps of the fourteenth Gemina Martia Victrix legion, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly, as revealed in a vision.
EDCS-29100232.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter of Heliopolis by Lucius Pompeius Caeneus, a princeps in the 14th Gemina legion, fulfilling a vow made after a vision.

EDCS-29100236.text_edition: \n censor Augusti filius consul V designatus \n
EDCS-29100236.translation: Censor, son of Augustus, designated consul.
EDCS-29100236.summary: This is a brief inscription likely referring to a high-ranking official, possibly commemorative of his titles and lineage.

EDCS-29100238.text_edition: \n Caius Aufidius Luci filius Quirina Sura domo Heuporia veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris \n
EDCS-29100238.translation: Gaius Aufidius Sura, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, from Heuporia, a veteran of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion.
EDCS-29100238.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Aufidius Sura, identifying him by his name, tribe, origin, and military service as a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion.

EDCS-29100240.text_edition: \n Ti(berio) Claudio Leoni Savaria veterano legionis XV Apollinaris Titus Claudius Victor fecit\n
EDCS-29100240.translation: Titus Claudius Victor made this for Tiberius Claudius Leo of Savaria, a veteran of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion.
EDCS-29100240.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titus Claudius Victor for Tiberius Claudius Leo, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion.

EDCS-29100241.translation: Of the Seventh Cohort, under the centurion Cornelius Sulla.
EDCS-29100241.summary: This inscription identifies a military unit, the Seventh Cohort, and its commanding centurion, Cornelius Sulla.

EDCS-29100242.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Titi Pollia Secundus Castris miles legionis XV Apollinaris custos armorum centuria Anni Cassi stipendiorum XXII Titus Flavius Ingenuus signifer heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29100242.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Flavius Secundus Castris, soldier of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, custos armorum, of the centuria of Annius Cassius, with twenty-two years of service, and Titus Flavius Ingenuus, signifer, his heir, saw to the construction of this monument.
EDCS-29100242.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Secundus Castris, a soldier and custos armorum of the 15th Apollinaris legion, erected by his heir Titus Flavius Ingenuus.

EDCS-29100244.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cai Fruti Serae Cai fili Patavio veterani legionis XV Apollinaris annorum vivo patre matri Iuniae Albuci filiae annorum XLI et Lucius Cai Fruti Serae filius patri et matri posuit\n
EDCS-29100244.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Gaius Frutius Seras, son of Gaius, from Patavium, a veteran of the fifteenth Apollinaris legion, aged [---], during his father's lifetime, and for Iunia Albuci, daughter of [---], aged forty-one, and for Lucius Gaius Frutius Seras, son of the veteran, for his father and mother, he erected this.
EDCS-29100244.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Gaius Frutius Seras for his parents, Gaius Frutius Seras, a veteran of the 15th Apollinaris legion, and Iunia Albuci.

EDCS-29100245.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sexto Gellio Urbico militi legionis I adiutricis cohortis VI natione Cilix vixit annos XXXIIII militavit annos XVI Gellia Nica fecit patrono \n
EDCS-29100245.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gellia Nica erected this for Sextus Gellius Urbicus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix Legion, VI Cohort, a Cilician by birth, who lived 34 years and served 16 years, her patron.
EDCS-29100245.summary: This is a tombstone for a soldier named Sextus Gellius Urbicus, erected by Gellia Nica, likely his patron or benefactress, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100246.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Cai <f=E>(ilius) Voltinia Luco Vocontiorum Veranus veteranus legionis XIIII geminae decurio coloniae Claudiae Savariae annorum LX hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Valerius Vitalis municeps et heres faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-29100246.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Lucius Veranus, a veteran of the XIIII Gemina Legion, a decurion in the colony of Savaria, aged 60, is buried here; he ordered by his will that this be made, and Valerius Vitalis, a fellow citizen and heir, saw to its construction.
EDCS-29100246.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Lucius Veranus, a veteran and decurion from Savaria, erected by his heir Valerius Vitalis, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100247.text_edition: \n legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Iuniani annorum XXI heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29100247.translation: Of the XV Apollinaris Legion, Iunius's century. Aged 21. The heir saw to its construction.
EDCS-29100247.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone recording the death of a soldier from the XV Apollinaris Legion, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100248.text_edition: \n Quintus Iuventius Cai filius Lemonia miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXII hic situs est Caius Octavius heres posuit\n
EDCS-29100248.translation: Quintus Iuventius, son of Gaius, of the Lemonia tribe, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, aged 45 and having served 22 years, is buried here. Gaius Octavius, the heir, erected it.
EDCS-29100248.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Iuventius, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, erected by his heir Gaius Octavius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100249.text_edition: \n Caius Pedusius Marci filius Aniensis Cremona veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum LX stipendiorum XXIIII hic situs est Amanda liberta posuit invida mors rapuit fato crudelis iniquo nec licuit pretium sumere militiae non tantum petii pro me quam ut reddere possem libertae et dignae pluruma sic quam foveat huic precor in longum extendant sua fila sorores ac nostros adeat saepe memor cineres\n
EDCS-29100249.translation: Gaius Pedusius, son of Marcus, of the Aniensis tribe, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris Legion, aged 60 and served 24 years, is buried here. Amanda, his freedwoman, erected it. Cruel, unjust fate snatched me away; nor was it permitted to receive the reward of my service. I did not ask for so much for myself as that I might be able to repay my worthy and deserving freedwoman. Therefore, I pray the sisters (of Fate) to extend their threads for a long time for her, and that she may often visit my ashes in memory.
EDCS-29100249.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Pedusius, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris Legion, erected by his freedwoman Amanda, who expresses her gratitude and wishes for her own long life, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100250.text_edition: \n Proculo Augusta militi legionis XV Apollinaris \n
EDCS-29100250.translation: For Proculus, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion.
EDCS-29100250.summary: This is a brief inscription honouring Proculus, a soldier of the XV Apollinaris Legion, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100251.text_edition: \n Titus Statius Titi Claudia Vitalis Camuloduni stipendiorum III annorum XXIII (centuria) Arrunti Expectati\n
EDCS-29100251.translation: Titus Statius, son of Titus, of the Claudia tribe, from Camulodunum, served 3 years, aged 23, in the century of Arruntius Expectatus.
EDCS-29100251.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Statius, a soldier serving in the century of Arruntius Expectatus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100254.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavi Critici fratris karissimi annorum XXV et Flavi Saturniniani filii Flavia Germana soror fecit\n
EDCS-29100254.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavia Germana erected this for her most beloved brother Flavinus Criticus, aged 25, and for Flavinus Saturninianus, her son.
EDCS-29100254.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Flavia Germana for her brother Flavinus Criticus and her son Flavinus Saturninianus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100256.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Valerius veteranus legionis X geminae vivus fecit sibi et Septimiae Florae coniugi pientissimae karissimae anorum L et Iulio Floro filio karissimo annorum V\n
EDCS-29100256.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gaius Julius Valerius, a veteran of the X Gemina Legion, erected this in his lifetime for himself and for his most beloved and pious wife Septimia Flora, aged 50, and for his most beloved son Iulius Florus, aged 5.
EDCS-29100256.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Julius Valerius for himself and his family, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100971.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Latiniani et Moderatillae Theopompus servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29100971.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the health of Latinianus and Moderatilla. Theopompus, a slave, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-29100971.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by a slave named Theopompus for the health of Latinianus and Moderatilla, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100972.text_edition: \n Medus Cai Trotedi negotiatoris Neptuno Oviano \n
EDCS-29100972.translation: Medus, slave of the merchant Cai Trotedius, [dedicated this] to Neptune Ovianus.
EDCS-29100972.summary: This is a dedication to Neptune Ovianus by Medus, a slave of a merchant, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100973.text_edition: \n Sextus Capenius Viator vivus sibi fecit et Tulliae Marcellae matri --- II \n
EDCS-29100973.translation: Sextus Capenius Viator made this for himself while alive, and for his mother Tullia Marcella [and] II others.
EDCS-29100973.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Sextus Capenius Viator for himself and his mother, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100974.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mithras, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100976.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus nutia Materna viva fecit sibi et Ges Ge coniugi carissimo occiso a barbaris Ges Sabinus et Valentin faciendum <c=O>uraverunt \n
EDCS-29100976.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Nutia Materna, alive, made this for herself and for Ges, her dearest husband, killed by barbarians; Ges Sabinus and Valentin saw to its construction.
EDCS-29100976.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Nutia Materna for herself and her husband Ges, who was killed by barbarians, with Ges Sabinus and Valentin overseeing the construction, likely dating to the late Imperial period.

EDCS-29100978.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus C Iuvenali et A IF \n
EDCS-29100978.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For C. Iuvenalis and A. F.
EDCS-29100978.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to C. Iuvenalis and A. F., dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100979.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Rufinus vivus fecit sibi et Claudiae Coromarae coniugi pientessimae sic annorum XXXVII hic sita est Flaviae Rufillae filiae annorum XIII\n
EDCS-29100979.translation: Titus Flavius Rufinus, while alive, made this for himself and for his most dutiful wife Claudia Coromara, aged 37, who lies here, and for Flavia Rufilla, daughter, aged 13.
EDCS-29100979.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Titus Flavius Rufinus for himself, his wife, and his daughter, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100980.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Marcius Maroninus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29100980.translation: To Hercules Augustus. Marcius Maroninus gladly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-29100980.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules Augustus by Marcius Maroninus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100981.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Vibius P \n
EDCS-29100981.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Vibius P.
EDCS-29100981.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Vibius P., likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100982.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Gaius Iulius Dignus beneficiarius consularis legionis X geminae votum solvit libens merito Kalendis Octobribus Imperatore domino nostro Decio Augusto et Grato consulibus\n
EDCS-29100982.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place. Gaius Iulius Dignus, a beneficiarius of a consular, of the tenth Gemina legion, gladly paid his vow, as was deserved. On the Kalends of October, during the consulship of our Emperor and Lord Decius Augustus and Gratus.
EDCS-29100982.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place by Gaius Iulius Dignus, a soldier of Legio X Gemina, dating to 248 AD.

EDCS-29100984.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Litugena Canonis viva fecit sibi et Mugiae filiae fili \n
EDCS-29100984.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Litugena Canonis, alive, made this for herself and for her daughter Mugia.
EDCS-29100984.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Litugena Canonis for herself and her daughter, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29100985.text_edition: \n Vepo Caionis annorum XXX hic situs est Caio Boudionis vivus fecit sibi et Suadullae coniugi\n
EDCS-29100985.translation: Vepo son of Caio, aged 30, is buried here. Caio son of Boudio made this for himself and his wife Suadulla while he was alive.
EDCS-29100985.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vepo, set up by his father Caio, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100988.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Antonius Iulianus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29100988.translation: Marcus Antonius Iulianus, a *beneficiarius consularis*, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-29100988.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus Antonius Iulianus, a military official, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100990.text_edition: \n Lucius Caes Boniatus pro se <et=TI> suis \n
EDCS-29100990.translation: Lucius Caes Boniatus for himself and his family.
EDCS-29100990.summary: A dedication or funerary inscription by Lucius Caes Boniatus for himself and his family, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100993.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus s(acrum) Mogetius Manus vivus fecit sibi etr Suadrae coniugi carissimae \n
EDCS-29100993.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Mogetius Manus made this for himself and his dearest wife Suadra while he was alive.
EDCS-29100993.summary: A funerary epitaph for Mogetius Manus and his wife Suadra, set up by Mogetius Manus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100995.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Severinus Maronis et Maximina coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Auscae filiae annorum XII\n
EDCS-29100995.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Severinus son of Maro and his wife Maximina made this for themselves and their daughter Ausca, aged 12, while they were alive.
EDCS-29100995.summary: A funerary epitaph for Severinus, his wife Maximina, and their daughter Ausca, set up by Severinus and Maximina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200145.text_edition: \n Felicissimae et SACMENEVNO coniugi dominus Victorinus maritus sepulcrum eorum collocavit Felicissima quae vixit cum eo annis XIII anno urbis DXVII SARVNAM VIISICDXPHY Deo\n
EDCS-29200145.translation: Victorinus the husband placed this tomb for his most happy wife Felicissima, who lived with him for 13 years, in the year of the city 517.
EDCS-29200145.summary: A funerary monument for Felicissima, set up by her husband Victorinus, dating to 517 AUC (465 AD), indicating a late Roman or early post-Roman period.

EDCS-29200170.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Lucanius Attillus iussu dei inpendio suo fecit\n
EDCS-29200170.translation: Lucanius Attillus made this at the command of the god and at his own expense for Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.
EDCS-29200170.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Lucanius Attillus, made in response to divine command, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200175.text_edition: \n Pro salute Augusti sacrum Cassius Firmianus aram numini et virtutibus Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno votum solvit laetus libens merito\n
EDCS-29200175.translation: Sacred to the well-being of the Emperor. Cassius Firmianus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow with an altar to the divinity and virtues of Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.
EDCS-29200175.summary: A votive dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Cassius Firmianus, also honouring the Emperor's well-being, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200177.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Luci Aeli Ver Pannoni consularis I legionis\n
EDCS-29200177.translation: For the well-being of Lucius Aelius Verus of Pannonia, *consularis* of the I Legion, for Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus.
EDCS-29200177.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus, likely for the health or success of Lucius Aelius Verus, a high-ranking official, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200178.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Caius Sollius Optatus et Sollius Siro filius iussu dei donum dederunt sub sacerdote Ant\n
EDCS-29200178.translation: Caius Sollius Optatus and his son Sollius Siro gave this gift to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus at the command of the god, under the priest Ant[...].
EDCS-29200178.summary: A votive offering to Jupiter Dolichenus by Caius Sollius Optatus and his son Sollius Siro, made at divine command, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200179.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Caius Secundius Sacer votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-29200179.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, Caius Secundius Sacer gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29200179.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus by Caius Secundius Sacer, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29200180.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno et religioni pro salute Augusti Caius Iulius Dionicus et Marcus Folnius Primus curatores et Quintus Gavius Zosimus scriba donum dederunt ex voto Imperatore Commodo Victorino consulibus sub sacerdotibus Basso et Crispo\n
EDCS-29200180.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, and to the religious observance for the safety of the Emperor, Caius Julius Dionicus and Marcus Folnius Primus, curators, and Quintus Gavius Zosimus, scribe, gave this offering in fulfillment of a vow, when Victorinus was consul, under the high priests Bassus and Crispus.
EDCS-29200180.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus and the imperial cult for the Emperor's safety, made by officials during the consulship of Victorinus, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29200181.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno Atilius Primus |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae ex evocato legionis X geminae piae fidelis ex viso pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens laetus merito domo Sergia Marsis\n
EDCS-29200181.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, Atilius Primus, centurion of the XIV Gemina legion, formerly an evocatus of the X Gemina Pia Fidelis legion, saw this vision and gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow for the safety of himself and his family; from his home in the Sergia tribe, Marsis.
EDCS-29200181.summary: This is a vow fulfilled by Atilius Primus, a military officer, to Jupiter Dolichenus for his and his family's safety, probably dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29200182.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Commodi Augusti Gaius Spurius Silvanus |(centurio) legionis X geminae et Valeria Digna votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-29200182.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Dolichenus, for the safety of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Commodus Augustus, Gaius Spurius Silvanus, centurion of the X Gemina legion, and Valeria Digna gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-29200182.summary: This inscription records a vow made by a centurion and Valeria Digna to Jupiter Dolichenus for the safety of Emperor Commodus, likely dating to his reign (177-192 CE).

EDCS-29200328.summary: This is a very short inscription identifying Julius Sevs as a 'strator consularis', an official attendant to a magistrate, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-29200329.translation: [son] of ... aged 12; Buia Rustica, daughter of ..., died aged 50, and Voltarus, died aged 50.
EDCS-29200329.summary: This fragmented inscription records the deaths of at least three individuals, including a child and two adults, possibly from a family burial, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-29601134.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto patri patriae res publica colonia Bassiana devota Numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-29601134.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus, father of the fatherland, the state of the colony of Bassiana, devoted to his divinity and majesty.
EDCS-29601134.summary: This is a declaration of devotion by the colony of Bassiana to Emperor Gordian III, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-29601582.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucilius Florentinus curator votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29601582.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Lucilius Florentinus, curator, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29601582.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter, by Lucilius Florentinus, who served as a curator, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-29601583.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Satzousa votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29601583.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Satzousa fulfilled his vow gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-29601583.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by Satzousa, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-29601584.text_edition: \n Teutio Vercombogionis filius magister structorum Aravisco sic hic situs est\n
EDCS-29601584.translation: Teutius, son of Vercombogio, master builder, lies here in Aravisco.
EDCS-29601584.summary: This inscription records the burial of Teutius, a master builder, and identifies his father and location, likely from the Roman imperial period in a region with local influences.

EDCS-29601586.text_edition: \n Herculi Marcus C Paternus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29601586.translation: Marcus C. Paternus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Hercules.
EDCS-29601586.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a vow fulfilled to the god Hercules by Marcus C. Paternus.

EDCS-29601588.text_edition: \n Manibus Dis atai sic annorum XXII coniugi carissimae Inginuus sic victigalis sic I<ll=II>yrici servus contrascriptor s<t=I>ationis Aquincensis \n
EDCS-29601588.translation: To the Manes and the gods. Atata, wife of Inginuus, victigalist (tax collector) and contrascriptor (accountant) of the Aquincense station, who lived 22 years, to his dearest wife. Inginuus, also a victigalis and contrascriptor of the Aquincense station.
EDCS-29601588.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Atata, a 22-year-old wife, and her husband Inginuus, a tax collector and accountant at the Aquincense station.

EDCS-29601589.translation: Sacred to the gods of the departed. Tertia and Cattonius Maximus erected this monument while alive for themselves and for VIC[---]. The monument's location: 12 feet in front, 12 feet in the field.
EDCS-29601589.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Tertia and Cattonius Maximus for themselves and others, with specifications for its dimensions and placement.

EDCS-29601590.translation: Sacred to Jupiter. Gaius Fullius Viator willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, in a place granted by public authority.
EDCS-29601590.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a vow fulfilled to Jupiter by Gaius Fullius Viator, in a publically allocated space.

EDCS-29601592.translation: To the gods of the departed. For Marcus Voltilus Firmus, who died at 65 years of age, and for Sabina, daughter of Urbanus, 60 years of age, and for Marcus Voltilus Maximianus, 25 years of age, and for Voltilius Urbanus, who died at 20 years of age. The heirs Voltili Voltognas and Firmina erected this.
EDCS-29601592.summary: This funerary inscription records the deaths of four individuals, likely family members, and was erected by the heirs Voltili Voltognas and Firmina.

EDCS-29601594.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Licinii votum solverunt\n
EDCS-29601594.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. The Licinii fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-29601594.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a vow fulfilled to the god Silvanus Domesticus by the Licinii family.

EDCS-29601595.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Augusto Aeli Silvini servus vilicus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29601595.translation: To Liber Pater Augustus. Aelius Silvinus, his freedman and bailiff, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29601595.summary: This is a votive inscription recording a vow fulfilled to Liber Pater Augustus by Aelius Silvinus, a freedman and bailiff.

EDCS-29601597.text_edition: \n Marcus A CR PEC et M\n
EDCS-29601597.translation: Marcus A. CR PEC and M.
EDCS-29601597.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name Marcus A. and two other elements, CR PEC and M, with no clear context.

EDCS-29601598.text_edition: \n Cartoria Calandina viva fecit sibi et Aurelio Verino coniugi qui vixi<t=I> annos IXX <e=C>t Aureliae Verae filiae <e=C>t Aureliae Verecundae \n
EDCS-29601598.translation: Cartoria Calandina erected this while alive for herself and for her husband Aurelius Verinus, who lived 70 years, and for her daughter Aurelia Vera, and for Aurelia Verecunda.
EDCS-29601598.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Cartoria Calandina for herself, her husband Aurelius Verinus (70 years old), and her daughters Aurelia Vera and Aurelia Verecunda.

EDCS-29601599.text_edition: \n Vicarius Publi Musculei libertus Musculeia Publi liberta Sura annorum XVIIII hic sita est patronus posuit\n
EDCS-29601599.translation: Vicarius, freedman of Publius Musculeius, and Musculeia Publi, freedwoman, 17 years old, is buried here. Her patron erected this.
EDCS-29601599.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Vicarius, a freedman, and Musculeia Publi, a freedwoman aged 17, with her patron as the erector.

EDCS-29601600.text_edition: \n Iulia Ingenua coniugi suo et filiae de suo posuit\n
EDCS-29601600.translation: Iulia Ingenua dedicated this from her own funds to her husband and daughter.
EDCS-29601600.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Iulia Ingenua for her husband and daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601601.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Celerinus votum merito libens solvit\n
EDCS-29601601.translation: Iulius Celerinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-29601601.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Iulius Celerinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601602.text_edition: \n Ei patere aleam Marcus Aurelius Avitianus beneficiarius praefecti Caius Iulius \n
EDCS-29601602.translation: May the dice fall favorably for him, Marcus Aurelius Avitianus, beneficiary of the prefect Caius Iulius.
EDCS-29601602.summary: This inscription, possibly a dedication or a personal motto, mentions Marcus Aurelius Avitianus, a beneficiary under a prefect named Caius Iulius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601604.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae sacrum Valerius Atta pro se votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-29601604.translation: Valerius Atta willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the divine Nemesis Augusta.
EDCS-29601604.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Nemesis Augusta by Valerius Atta, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601605.summary: This is a fragmented inscription mentioning the emperor Gordian, likely a dedication or acclamation, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601606.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Maria Calia <Eit=ETI>yche votum solvit libens laeta merito \n
EDCS-29601606.translation: Maria Calia Eutyche willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Silvanus Domestus.
EDCS-29601606.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Silvanus Domestus by Maria Calia Eutyche, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601607.text_edition: \n annnorum XXXII hic sit-- est Iustus filius Lisinia Iucunda mater Crescens frater Ingenua et Tertulla et Gratilla sorores posuerunt\n
EDCS-29601607.translation: Aged 32 years, Iustus, his son, Lisinia Iucunda, his mother, Crescens, his brother, and Ingenua, Tertulla, and Gratilla, his sisters, erected this.
EDCS-29601607.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the age and the family members who erected the monument for Iustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601609.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Publiilius, prefect...
EDCS-29601609.summary: This is a fragmented inscription recording a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a prefect named Publiilius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601611.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelius Tertullus immunis legionis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29601611.translation: Aurelius Tertullus, exempt soldier of the legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29601611.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Aurelius Tertullus, an immunis (exempt soldier) of a legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601612.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Aelius Tertius pequarius sic votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-29601612.translation: Aelius Tertius, a pig-dealer, thus willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus of the Woods.
EDCS-29601612.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Silvanus by Aelius Tertius, a negotiator (dealer) in pigs, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601618.text_edition: \n Legio XIIII gemina Martia victrix centuria Publi Fanni Maxsimi sic\n
EDCS-29601618.translation: From the century of Publius Fannius Maximus of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina Martia Victrix.
EDCS-29601618.summary: This inscription records a military marker from the Fourteenth Legion, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601619.text_edition: \n Legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Cai Aconi volentes\n
EDCS-29601619.translation: From the century of Gaius Aconius of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, willingly.
EDCS-29601619.summary: This inscription records a military marker from the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601620.text_edition: \n Afrania Hilara annorum XXV hic sita est Publius Afranius Maior miles legionis XV Apollinaris libertae bene meritae posuit\n
EDCS-29601620.translation: Afrania Hilara, aged 25, is buried here. Publius Afranius Maior, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, erected this for his well-deserving freedwoman.
EDCS-29601620.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Afrania Hilara, a freedwoman, erected by a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601621.text_edition: \n Marcus Anicius Marci Claudia Censor Dinia miles legionis XV Apollinaris centuria Bagienni annorum XXXVIII stipendiorum XVIIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-29601621.translation: Marcus Anicius, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, from Dinia, aged 38, with 19 years of service, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, from the century of Bagiennus, is buried here.
EDCS-29601621.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Anicius, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601622.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai filius Cornelia Thessalonica miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXXI stipendiorum XII hic situs est Caius Cluvius et Bassus libertus heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-29601622.translation: Gaius Iulius, son of Gaius, of the Cornelia tribe, from Thessalonica, aged 31, with 12 years of service, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, is buried here. Gaius Cluvius and Bassus, his freedmen, erected this as heirs.
EDCS-29601622.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, erected by his freedmen heirs, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601624.text_edition: \n Rufus Lucilius Marci filius Camilia Augusta miles legionis XV Apollinaris nnorum XXI stipendiorum IIII hic situs est Marcus Lucilius filio posuit\n
EDCS-29601624.translation: Rufus Lucilius, son of Marcus, of the Camilia tribe, from Augusta, aged 21, with 4 years of service, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, is buried here. Marcus Lucilius erected this for his son.
EDCS-29601624.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Rufus Lucilius, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, erected by his father, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601625.text_edition: \n Tertius Valerius Cai filius Aniensis Cremona miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XXV \n
EDCS-29601625.translation: Tertius Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Aniensis tribe, from Cremona, aged 25, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris.
EDCS-29601625.summary: This inscription records the name and details of Tertius Valerius, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29601626.text_edition: \n Quintus Veratius Luci filius Scaptia miles legionis XV Apollinaris annorum XL stipendiorum XIX hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Lucius Sertorius Faustus heres fecit\n
EDCS-29601626.translation: Quintus Veratius, son of Lucius, of the Scaptia tribe, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, aged 40, with 19 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made in his will. Lucius Sertorius Faustus made it as heir.
EDCS-29601626.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Veratius, a soldier of the Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, erected by his heir according to his will, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29900067.text_edition: \n Valentinus miles legionis I adiutricis Severianae beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito Fusco et Dextro consulibus\n
EDCS-29900067.translation: Valentinus, a soldier of the First Legion Adjutrix Severiana, a beneficiary of the consular legate, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly. Fuscus and Dexter were consuls.
EDCS-29900067.summary: This inscription records a soldier of the First Legion Adjutrix Severiana fulfilling a vow during the consulship of Fuscus and Dexter, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29900069.text_edition: \n Silvano Magno sacrum Appinius Serotinus pro salute sua et suorum votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-29900069.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Magnus. Appinius Serotinus fulfilled his vow willingly, gladly, and deservedly for his safety and that of his family.
EDCS-29900069.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Magnus by Appinius Serotinus for his and his family's well-being, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29900123.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Aurelius Doncius votum solvit libens laetus merito miles legionis X geminae\n
EDCS-29900123.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Aurelius Doncius, a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900123.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Silvanus made by Aurelius Doncius, a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-29900124.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Cepasius Secundus miles legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-29900124.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Cepasius Secundus, a soldier of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900124.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Silvanus made by Cepasius Secundus, a soldier of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-29900125.text_edition: \n Silvano Flavius Albinus miles legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-29900125.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Flavius Albinus, a soldier of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900125.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Silvanus made by Flavius Albinus, a soldier of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-29900126.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Gaius Iu<l=I>(ius) Fortis miles legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis votum libens solvit merito \n
EDCS-29900126.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Gaius Iulius Fortis, a soldier of the First Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900126.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Silvanus made by Gaius Iulius Fortis, a soldier of the First Legion Adiutrix Pia Fidelis.

EDCS-29900127.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Pap Terminalis et Iulius Atilius milites legionis XIIII geminae votum solverunt libentes laeti merito\n
EDCS-29900127.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Gaius Pap Terminalis and Iulius Atilius, soldiers of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled their vows.
EDCS-29900127.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Silvanus made by two soldiers, Pap Terminalis and Iulius Atilius, of the Fourteenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-29900128.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Ulpius Taurus miles legionis I adiutricis ex voto\n
EDCS-29900128.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Ulpius Taurus, a soldier of the First Legion Adiutrix, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900128.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the god Silvanus made by Ulpius Taurus, a soldier of the First Legion Adiutrix.

EDCS-29900129.text_edition: \n miles legionis X geminae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29900129.translation: A soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-29900129.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by an unnamed soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-29900132.translation: Sacred to the August nurses. Sati [dedicated this].
EDCS-29900132.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Nutrices Augustae (nurses of the imperial family) by Sati.

EDCS-29900134.text_edition: \n Nutr(icibus) Augustis Haeliodorus sic |(contra)scriptor Augusti nostri ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-29900134.translation: Haeliodorus, a counter-scribe to our Emperor, placed this as a votive offering to the August nurses.
EDCS-29900134.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to the Nutrices Augustae made by Haeliodorus, who held the position of counter-scribe to the Emperor.

EDCS-29900135.summary: This inscription identifies an object or location as 'Felix' and functioning as a boundary marker.

EDCS-29900136.text_edition: \n nnius Florentinus miles legionis XIIII geminae et Ulpia Severina coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Fanniis Florentinae nnorum XVI et Superae nnorum IIII et Floriano nnorum II et Floro nni I filiis\n
EDCS-29900136.translation: Lucius Valerius Florentinus, a soldier of the Legio XIIII Gemina, and his wife Ulpia Severina made this for themselves and for their children Fannia Florentina, aged 16, Supera, aged 4, Florianus, aged 2, and Florus, aged 1.
EDCS-29900136.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a soldier of Legio XIIII Gemina and his wife for themselves and their deceased children, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29900137.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Valerius Restutus vivus fecit sibi et Billieniae Donatae coniugi annorum et Valerio Ingenuo militi legionis XIIII geminae filio annorum et Valeriae Restutae filiae annorum XX\n
EDCS-29900137.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Valerius Restutus, while alive, made this for himself, for his wife Billienia Donata (aged unknown), for his son Valerius Ingenuus, a soldier of Legio XIIII Gemina (aged unknown), and for his daughter Valeria Restuta (aged 20).
EDCS-29900137.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quintus Valerius Restutus for his family, including a son who served in Legio XIIII Gemina, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29900138.text_edition: \n Pro salute Impperatorum Diocletiani et Maximiani Auggustorum nnostrorum Constanti et Maximiani nobilissimorum Caesarum ex iusso sic Barbius Valentinus beneficiarius consularis inscrip\n
EDCS-29900138.translation: For the safety of our Emperors Diocletian and Maximian, Augusti, and of Constantius and Maximian, the most noble Caesars, by order of Barbius Valentinus, *beneficiarius consularis*.
EDCS-29900138.summary: This is a dedication recording an order for a monument or inscription for the safety of the co-emperors and caesars, likely dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-29900139.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Philumenus Gai Spuri Onesimi ex voto libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-29900139.translation: Philumenus, formerly the slave of Gaius Spurius Onesimus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-29900139.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by a freedman to Jupiter, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-29900141.text_edition: \n Pro salute Commodi Nemesi? Caius Atius Castus |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae ex iussu posuit Marullo et Aeliano consulibus\n
EDCS-29900141.translation: For the safety of Commodus. Nemesi(?), Caius Atius Castus, centurion of Legio XIIII Gemina, set this up by order during the consulship of Marullus and Aelianus.
EDCS-29900141.summary: This is a dedication made for the health of the emperor Commodus by a centurion of Legio XIIII Gemina, dating to the consulship of Marullus and Aelianus (likely late 2nd century CE).

EDCS-29900142.text_edition: \n Marti Lucius Valerius Titovius evocatus votum solvit merito\n
EDCS-29900142.translation: To Mars. Lucius Valerius Titovius, an *evocatus*, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900142.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mars by a soldier who served as an *evocatus*, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29900143.text_edition: \n Deae Nemesi simulacrum Quintus Refius Mansuetus primus pilus legionis XIIII geminae posuit\n
EDCS-29900143.translation: Quintus Refius Mansuetus, *primus pilus* of Legio XIIII Gemina, set up this statue of the goddess Nemesis.
EDCS-29900143.summary: This is a dedication of a statue to the goddess Nemesis by a senior centurion (*primus pilus*) of Legio XIIII Gemina, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29900144.text_edition: \n Nemesi Reginae et Deanae sacrum Marcus Anc Secundinus n missione honesta veteranus legionis XIIIII geminae votum solvit libens Anullino et Frontone consulibus\n
EDCS-29900144.translation: Sacred to Nemesis Regina and the goddess. Marcus Anc[i]us Secundinus, a veteran of Legio XIIII Geminae, who received an honorable discharge, gladly fulfilled his vow during the consulship of Anullinus and Fronto.
EDCS-29900144.summary: This is a votive inscription to Nemesis Regina by a veteran of Legio XIIII Geminae, dating to the consulship of Anullinus and Fronto (likely late 3rd century CE).

EDCS-29900740.translation: [...], *triumvir*, made this while alive for himself and for his wife Quintia (aged 55) and [...] and [...] Valerius.
EDCS-29900740.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a *triumvir* and his wife, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29900743.translation: For Saturnina Untilis, who died at the age of 50. Hostila, daughter of Severus, set this up.
EDCS-29900743.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Saturnina Untilis, set up by her daughter, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-29900752.translation: Sacred to Neptune Augustus. Marcus Asinius Masinus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900752.summary: This is a dedication to Neptune Augustus by Marcus Asinius Masinus, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-29900754.translation: Lucius Marcius Philotimus, freedman of Lucius, a sexvir in Aquileia, willingly and happily fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900754.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Marcius Philotimus, a freedman and sexvir of Aquileia, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-29900780.text_edition: \n Diis Deabusque omnibus votum solvit libens merito Ulpi-- Pris[c------\n
EDCS-29900780.translation: Ulpius Priscus fulfilled his vow to all the gods and goddesses, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-29900780.summary: This inscription records Ulpius Priscus fulfilling a vow to all gods and goddesses.

EDCS-29900781.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Caius Apuleius Sabinus et Marcus I<u=N>nius Dexter bbenefficiarii consularis vvota ssolverunt llibentes mmeritis Albino et Maximo consulibus \n
EDCS-29900781.translation: Gaius Apuleius Sabinus and Marcus Junius Dexter, beneficiaries of the consular governor, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vows to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, during the consulship of Albinius and Maximus.
EDCS-29900781.summary: This is a record of vows fulfilled by Gaius Apuleius Sabinus and Marcus Junius Dexter to Jupiter and the local genius, during a specific consular term.

EDCS-29900787.text_edition: \n Aureliae Veneriae ob honorem et florem iuventutis subita morte occupatae mihi pientissimae virgini Publius Aelius Iulianus ex numero collegii dendrophorum vivus fecit\n
EDCS-29900787.translation: Publius Aelius Iulianus, from the college of dendrophori, erected this alive for Aurelia Veneria, who was taken by sudden death, most devoted virgin, in honor of her youth and beauty.
EDCS-29900787.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius Aelius Iulianus for Aurelia Veneria, a young virgin honored for her virtue and beauty, likely by his collegium.

EDCS-29900789.text_edition: \n R Noma Maximus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29900789.translation: R. Noma Maximus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900789.summary: This is a simple votive dedication by R. Noma Maximus.

EDCS-29900795.text_edition: \n L Quinti Voturia ncio Ber legionis XIII geminae pientissimo fratri et sibi viva fecit Tullia Tauri liberta Fidelis\n
EDCS-29900795.translation: Tullia Fidelis, freedwoman of Taurus, made this for her most devoted brother, L. Quinctius, soldier of the Legio XIII Gemina, and for herself, while alive.
EDCS-29900795.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by Tullia Fidelis for her brother, L. Quinctius of the Legio XIII Gemina, and for herself.

EDCS-29900796.text_edition: \n Cai-- Calpurni-- Eutich! Augustal-- coloniae et Calpurnia- \n
EDCS-29900796.translation: Caius Calpurnius Eutychus, Augustalis of the colony, and Calpurnia...
EDCS-29900796.summary: This is a fragmented inscription likely recording dedications or family relationships involving Caius Calpurnius Eutychus, an Augustalis.

EDCS-29900801.text_edition: \n Quarto Adnamati filio annorum LXXX et Catullae Coi filiae coniugi annorum LX Uppu liberta faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-29900801.translation: Uppu, a freedwoman, arranged for this to be made for her son, Quartus Adnamatus, aged 80, and for her daughter-in-law, Catulla, daughter of Coius, aged 60.
EDCS-29900801.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by a freedwoman, Uppu, for her son and daughter-in-law.

EDCS-29900802.text_edition: \n Herculi sacrum Marcus Ulpius Kalendius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29900802.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Marcus Ulpius Kalendius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-29900802.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules by Marcus Ulpius Kalendius, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-29900803.text_edition: \n Volkano sic sacrum Marcus Ulpius Kalendius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-29900803.translation: Sacred to Vulcan; Marcus Ulpius Kalendius willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-29900803.summary: A dedication to the god Vulcan, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29900805.text_edition: \n Dasius Cai Farracis Iucundi hoc posita in tumulo pueri sunt ossa viator quem super in titulo TO \n
EDCS-29900805.translation: The bones of the boy Iucundus, son of Dasius Cai Farracis, are placed in this tomb; a traveler is above in the inscription.
EDCS-29900805.summary: A tomb inscription recording the burial of a young boy named Iucundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29900806.text_edition: \n Valerius Natalis Gauni filius nnorum IIII pater posuit\n
EDCS-29900806.translation: Valerius Natalis, son of Gaunus, aged 4, his father erected this.
EDCS-29900806.summary: A funerary monument set up by a father for his young son, Valerius Natalis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29900807.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Gaio annorum L Fortuna viva fecit sibi et coniugi pientissima\n
EDCS-29900807.translation: Fortuna viva erected this for Titus Flavius Gaius, aged 50, and for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-29900807.summary: A funerary inscription by Fortuna viva for herself, her husband Titus Flavius Gaius, and his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29900808.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Folnia Glypha.
EDCS-29900808.summary: A dedication to the underworld deities, likely a funerary inscription for Folnia Glypha, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000213.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Ulpius Sabinus miles legionis primae <a>diutricis\n
EDCS-30000213.translation: To the Unconquered God; Ulpius Sabinus, a soldier of the first 'adiutrix' legion.
EDCS-30000213.summary: A military dedication to the Sol Invictus by Ulpius Sabinus, a soldier of a legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000215.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly mentioning the virtue of 'pietas' and a name starting with 'Valen', likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000220.text_edition: \n Dis deabus et Genio |(centuriae) eius Lucius Calventius Victor optio donum dedit\n
EDCS-30000220.translation: To the gods and goddesses and the genius of his century; Lucius Calventius Victor, Optio, gave this offering.
EDCS-30000220.summary: A dedication by a military officer, Lucius Calventius Victor, to deities and the genius of his century, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000221.text_edition: \n us Lucius Vitalis strator legati legionis XIIII geminae Severianae \n
EDCS-30000221.translation: Lucius Vitalis, strator of the legate of the XIV Gemina Severiana legion.
EDCS-30000221.summary: An inscription identifying Lucius Vitalis as a strator serving a legate of the XIV Gemina Severiana legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000222.text_edition: \n Dis Deabusque Genio loci Gaius C Primus votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-30000222.translation: To the gods and goddesses and the genius of the place; Gaius C. Primus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000222.summary: A dedication to local deities and the genius loci by Gaius C. Primus, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30000223.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae sacrum Aurelius Parthemius VV--\n
EDCS-30000223.translation: Sacred to Imperial Fortuna. Aurelius Parthemius, veteran soldier...
EDCS-30000223.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the goddess Fortuna Augusta by Aurelius Parthemius, likely a veteran, possibly from the late 1st to early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30000224.text_edition: \n Genio castrorum Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Augusti Ulpius Verrinus C Iulius Ianuarius S C Aurelius Cabdnus Aurelius Brincasus Aurelius Sextianus Iulius Domitus Iulius Dasius Flavius Surianus Aelius Florinus Aurelius Super Ulpius EIus Aurelius Eonus Aurelius Exoratus Iulius Messinus Ulpius Iustinus Septimius Constans Antonius Doras P C Aurelius Victor Maximius Marcellus Aurelius Quintinus Aurelius Mucianus Claudius Surianus Cnaeus Valerius Saturninus Aurelius Flavinus Papius Innio Flavius Gemellinus Flavius Flavinus Aurelius Felicianus Ulpius Atorius Aurelius Qus\n
EDCS-30000224.translation: To the Genius of the camp and Jupiter Best and Greatest for the safety of the Emperor. Ulpius Verrinus, Gaius Iulius Ianuarius, Sacrarium Constructor, Aurelius Cabdnus, Aurelius Brincasus, Aurelius Sextianus, Iulius Domitus, Iulius Dasius, Flavius Surianus, Aelius Florinus, Aurelius Super, Ulpius Eius, Aurelius Eonus, Aurelius Exoratus, Iulius Messinus, Ulpius Iustinus, Septimius Constans, Antonius Doras, Public Constructor, Aurelius Victor, Maximius Marcellus, Aurelius Quintinus, Aurelius Mucianus, Claudius Surianus, Cnaeus Valerius Saturninus, Aurelius Flavinus, Papius Innio, Flavius Gemellinus, Flavius Flavinus, Aurelius Felicianus, Ulpius Atorius, Aurelius Qus.
EDCS-30000224.summary: This lengthy inscription records a collective dedication to the Genius of the camp and Jupiter Optimus Maximus for the safety of the Emperor, made by a group of soldiers and possibly officers, likely from the 3rd century AD given the presence of multiple dedicants with full names.

EDCS-30000225.text_edition: \n Genio |(centuriae) pro salute commanipularium Marcus Aurelius Tutor immunis \n
EDCS-30000225.translation: To the Genius of the century for the safety of his comrades. Marcus Aurelius Tutor, exempt from ordinary duties...
EDCS-30000225.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Genius of his century for the well-being of his fellow soldiers by Marcus Aurelius Tutor, an immunis (exempt soldier), probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30000227.text_edition: \n Genio |(centuriae) Axi Vetuleiani Valerius Constans aram posuit\n
EDCS-30000227.translation: Valerius Constans set up this altar to the Genius of the century of the Axi Vetuleiani.
EDCS-30000227.summary: This inscription records the erection of an altar to the Genius of a specific century, the Axi Vetuleiani, by Valerius Constans, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30000228.text_edition: \n Genio loci Valerius Iustinus votum solvit\n
EDCS-30000228.translation: Valerius Iustinus fulfilled his vow to the Genius of the place.
EDCS-30000228.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow made to the Genius loci by Valerius Iustinus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30000229.translation: Gaius Ulpius Victorinus [dedicated this] to the Genius of the place.
EDCS-30000229.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Genius loci by Caius Ulpius Victorinus, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30000231.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Claudius Marcellus meles sic legionis XIIII votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000231.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Claudius Marcellus, a soldier of the 14th Legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000231.summary: This inscription records a soldier, Claudius Marcellus of the 14th Legion, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30000232.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Claudius Presidius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000232.translation: Claudius Presidius fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-30000232.summary: This inscription records Claudius Presidius fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30000233.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Iulius Quartus sacerdos votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-30000233.translation: Gaius Iulius Quartus, priest, gladly and happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-30000233.summary: This inscription records a priest, Gaius Iulius Quartus, happily fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30000234.text_edition: \n Iunoni Nemesi Eppius Martinus et Memmius Esper tubicen legionis XIIII geminae et Iulius Rodo et\n
EDCS-30000234.translation: To Juno Nemesis. Eppius Martinus and Memmius Esper, horn-blowers of the 14th Gemina Legion, and Iulius Rodo and...
EDCS-30000234.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno Nemesis by Eppius Martinus and Memmius Esper, musicians of the 14th Legion Gemina, and Iulius Rodo, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30000236.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo diis deabusque Lucius Cominius Aphrodisius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000236.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to all the gods and goddesses, Lucius Cominius Aphrodisius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000236.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and all deities, fulfilling a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30000237.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo dis deabusque omnibus Marcus Ulpius Servianus custos armorum legionis XIIII geminae Severianae renovavit\n
EDCS-30000237.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, and to all the gods and goddesses, Marcus Ulpius Servianus, keeper of the arms of the Legio XIIII Gemina Severiana, restored this.
EDCS-30000237.summary: Marcus Ulpius Servianus, an officer in Legio XIIII Gemina, restored a monument dedicated to Jupiter and all deities, likely during the Severan period.

EDCS-30000239.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Ammoni Mercurius votum solvit laetus libens merito Maximo et Urbano consulibus\n
EDCS-30000239.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Ammon, Mercurius willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Maxius and Urbanus were consuls.
EDCS-30000239.summary: A vow fulfilled by Mercurius to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Ammon, dated by the consuls Maxius and Urbanus, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-30000241.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Heliopolitano Cornelius Vitalis tribunus militum legionis XIIII geminae iussu dei fecit\n
EDCS-30000241.translation: Cornelius Vitalis, military tribune of the Legio XIIII Gemina, made this by order of the god to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus.
EDCS-30000241.summary: Cornelius Vitalis, a military tribune, erected a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus on divine command, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30000242.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Heliopolitano Veneri Victrici Marcus Titius Heliodorus augustalis coloniae Karnunti vivus sibi sacerdotibus Vibio Crescente et Herennio Nigriniano\n
EDCS-30000242.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus and to Venus Victrix, Marcus Titius Heliodorus, an augustalis of the colony of Carnuntum, set this up while alive for himself and for the priests Vibius Crescens and Herennius Nigrinianus.
EDCS-30000242.summary: Marcus Titius Heliodorus, a prominent citizen of Carnuntum, dedicated this monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Heliopolitanus and Venus Victrix for himself and the local priests, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30000243.summary: A dedication to Mercury by Aelius, likely a small votive offering from the Roman period.

EDCS-30000244.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum Flavius Secundinus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-30000244.translation: To Mercury, Flavius Secundinus willingly, happily, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000244.summary: Flavius Secundinus fulfilled a vow to Mercury, likely a personal dedication from the Roman period.

EDCS-30000245.text_edition: \n Ulpius Vitalis custos armorum Mercurio votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000245.translation: Ulpius Vitalis, keeper of the arms, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-30000245.summary: Ulpius Vitalis, an armorum custos, fulfilled a vow to Mercury, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30000246.text_edition: \n Soli votum solvit libens merito Quintus Cottius Lalus\n
EDCS-30000246.translation: To Sol, Quintus Cottius Lalus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000246.summary: Quintus Cottius Lalus fulfilled a vow to the god Sol, likely a personal dedication from the Roman period.

EDCS-30000248.text_edition: \n Soli Divino Lucius A T ex visu posuit\n
EDCS-30000248.translation: Lucius A T placed this divine Sol here, having seen it in a vision.
EDCS-30000248.summary: Lucius A T erected a monument to the divine Sol after seeing it in a vision, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-30000250.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Lucius Aelius Leo miles legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-30000250.translation: To Sol Invictus Mithras, Lucius Aelius Leo, soldier of the 14th Gemina legion, gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000250.summary: A soldier of the 14th Gemina legion dedicates an offering to Sol Invictus Mithras, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000251.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Aurelius Exuperatus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000251.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus; Aurelius Exuperatus gladly fulfilled his vow as was deserved.
EDCS-30000251.summary: Aurelius Exuperatus dedicates an offering to Silvanus Domesticus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000252.text_edition: \n Invicto Deo Baebius Martialis et Ulpius Breucio votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-30000252.translation: To the Unconquered God, Baebius Martialis and Ulpius Breucius gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-30000252.summary: Baebius Martialis and Ulpius Breucius dedicate an offering to an Unconquered God, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000253.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Iulius Iulianus votum solvit\n
EDCS-30000253.translation: To Silvanus Domesticus, Iulius Iulianus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000253.summary: Iulius Iulianus dedicates an offering to Silvanus Domesticus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000254.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Vindillius Cupitus signifer legionis XIIII geminae Antoninianae et Vindillius Iulianus voto posuerunt patre Paterno\n
EDCS-30000254.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Vindillius Cupitus, standard-bearer of the 14th Gemina Antoniniana legion, and Vindillius Iulianus set this up by vow, with their father Paternos (as witness/benefactor).
EDCS-30000254.summary: A standard-bearer and another individual dedicate an offering to Sol Invictus Mithras, likely during the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000255.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae pro salute Augusti Veruccius Marcellus immunis votum \n
EDCS-30000255.translation: To Nemesis Augusta, for the health of the Emperor, Veruccius Marcellus, a soldier exempt from regular duties, made his vow.
EDCS-30000255.summary: Veruccius Marcellus dedicates an offering to Nemesis Augusta for the emperor's health, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000258.summary: A partial inscription dedicating something to the Supreme Fate, the date is indeterminate.

EDCS-30000260.text_edition: \n Silvano Auggustorum sacrum III Calia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000260.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus; III Calia gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-30000260.summary: A woman named III Calia dedicates an offering to Silvanus Augustus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000261.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000261.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus; (someone) gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000261.summary: An unnamed individual dedicates an offering to Silvanus Augustus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000262.summary: An inscription recording a vow to Silvanus Domesticus, the date is indeterminate.

EDCS-30000263.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-30000264.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-30000265.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-30000269.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Aurelius Hilaris votum solvit libens laetus merito Pompeiano et Paeligniano consulibus Kalendis Aprilibus\n
EDCS-30000269.translation: Aurelius Hilaris gladly and joyfully fulfilled his vow, as was deserved, to Silvanus Domesticus, under the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus, on the Kalends of April.
EDCS-30000269.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Aurelius Hilaris to Silvanus Domesticus, dated to the consulship of Pompeianus and Paelignianus (likely 168 AD).

EDCS-30000270.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus, possibly with an abbreviation of unknown meaning.

EDCS-30000271.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Claudius Firmus votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-30000271.translation: Claudius Firmus gladly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-30000271.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Claudius Firmus to Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-30000272.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Florus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000272.translation: Florus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-30000272.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Florus to Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-30000273.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Iulius Faustinus cornicularius legionis XIIII Geminae Severianae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-30000273.translation: Julius Faustinus, a junior officer of the Fourteenth Gemina Severiana legion, gladly and joyfully fulfilled his vow, as was deserved, to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-30000273.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Julius Faustinus, a soldier of the Legio XIIII Gemina Severiana, to Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-30000274.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Livia votum solvit IL ]\n
EDCS-30000274.translation: Livia gladly fulfilled her vow to Silvanus Domesticus.
EDCS-30000274.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Livia to Silvanus Domesticus, with a partially illegible addition.

EDCS-30000275.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Saturnina to the god Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-30000276.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Valeria Valentina votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000276.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus; Valeria Valentina gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-30000276.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Valeria Valentina to the god Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-30000277.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri sacrum Valentinus votum solvit\n
EDCS-30000277.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvestris; Valentinus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000277.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Valentinus to the god Silvanus Silvestris.

EDCS-30000278.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus Silvestris.

EDCS-30000280.text_edition: \n Silvanae Mammulae sacrum libens laetus merito solvit\n
EDCS-30000280.translation: Sacred to Silvana Mammula; [she] gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled [her] vow.
EDCS-30000280.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by an unnamed female (likely Silvana Mammula) to the goddess Silvana Mammula.

EDCS-30000281.text_edition: \n Iulius Crescens votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-30000281.translation: Iulius Crescens fulfilled his vow gladly, joyfully, and deservedly.
EDCS-30000281.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Iulius Crescens.

EDCS-30000282.text_edition: \n Duplicarius Iulius Victorinus duplicarius Valerius Mansuetus duplicarius Claudius Romulus duplicarius Iulius Maximinus duplicarius Iulius Ursus duplicarius Aquilius Ursinus duplicarius Ulpius Senecio duplicarius Pol Senecianus eques duplicarius Cestius Silvanus duplicarius Iulius V kandidatus Septimius kandidatus bucinator Va kandidatus Iulius S kandidatus Aurelius kandidatus Aemilius kandidatus Valerius K kandidatus Cornelius S duplicarius L N Ver duplicarius Balv \n
EDCS-30000282.translation: Duplicarius Iulius Victorinus, duplicarius Valerius Mansuetus, duplicarius Claudius Romulus, duplicarius Iulius Maximinus, duplicarius Iulius Ursus, duplicarius Aquilius Ursinus, duplicarius Ulpius Senecio, duplicarius Pol Senecianus, horseman duplicarius Cestius Silvanus, duplicarius Iulius V (candidate), Septimius (candidate), bucinator Va (candidate), Iulius S (candidate), Aurelius (candidate), Aemilius (candidate), Valerius K (candidate), Cornelius S, duplicarius L N Ver, duplicarius Balv.
EDCS-30000282.summary: This inscription lists the names and ranks of multiple members of a military unit, likely a duplicarius corps, possibly from the Roman army.

EDCS-30000283.text_edition: \n Claudius Marci filius Maximus votum solvit\n
EDCS-30000283.translation: Claudius Maximus, son of Marcius, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000283.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Claudius Maximus, son of Marcius.

EDCS-30000284.text_edition: \n io sacrum pro salute Cai Servili Potentis legionis X geminae Verus servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30000284.translation: For the health of Caius Servilius Potens of the Tenth Legion Gemina, Verus the slave gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000284.summary: This inscription is a votive offering made by Verus, a slave, for the health of Caius Servilius Potens of the Tenth Legion Gemina.

EDCS-30000286.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a command or order, possibly related to a religious or official act.

EDCS-30000288.text_edition: \n Pro salute Etruscillae Augustae matris castrorum [[\n
EDCS-30000288.translation: For the health of Etruscilla Augusta, mother of the camp.
EDCS-30000288.summary: This inscription records a prayer or dedication for the health of Etruscilla Augusta, identified as the mother of the camp, likely a prominent figure in a military settlement.

EDCS-30000290.text_edition: \n pro salute ddominorum nnostrorum Imperatorum [] [] [] Auggustorum totiusque domus divinae in honorem collegii conveteranorum Lucius Cassius Florentinus veteranus legionis XIIII geminae Maximinianae ex custode armorum magister collegii supra scripti devotus numini maiesstati\n
EDCS-30000290.translation: For the safety of our lords Emperors [Names Missing] Augusti and of the entire divine household, in honor of the college of veterans, Lucius Cassius Florentinus, a veteran of the 14th Legion Gemina Maximiana, former overseer of arms, master of the aforesaid college, devoted to the divine majesty.
EDCS-30000290.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Cassius Florentinus, a veteran and former college master, to the imperial family and the college of veterans, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE when the title Maximiana was in use.

EDCS-30000291.text_edition: \n Domitianus beneficiarius legati legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-30000291.translation: Domitianus, a beneficarius of a legate of the 14th Legion Gemina, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30000291.summary: This is a votive dedication by Domitianus, a staff officer (beneficiarius) of a legate of the 14th Legion Gemina, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30000294.translation: Priest, during the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.
EDCS-30000294.summary: This inscription records a priest during the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus, which places it in 209 CE.

EDCS-30100484.text_edition: \n Maximi filia hic sita est Vitalis onis filius annorum VIII veco coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-30100484.translation: Vitalis, son of Onis, aged 8, is laid here; Veco laid this for his wife.
EDCS-30100484.summary: This is a tombstone for Vitalis, a young boy, set up by Veco for his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100485.text_edition: \n Filio annorum IIII dulcissimo Veracillius et Vindicilla parentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-30100485.translation: For their son, aged 4, dearest of all; Veracillius and Vindicilla, parents, set this up.
EDCS-30100485.summary: This is a tombstone for a beloved young child, erected by his parents Veracillius and Vindicilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100486.text_edition: \n Mesinus Mastaonis annorum XX hic situs est pater filio pro pietate\n
EDCS-30100486.translation: Mesinus, son of Mastaon, aged 20, is laid here; his father set this up for his son out of filial piety.
EDCS-30100486.summary: This is a tombstone for Mesinus, a young man, erected by his father out of filial piety, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100487.text_edition: \n strator legati legionis X geminae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-30100487.translation: Strator of the legate of the 10th Legion Gemina, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100487.summary: This is a votive dedication by a strator (mounted attendant) of a legate of the 10th Legion Gemina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100488.text_edition: \n Segillus Iliati filius et Abua Tapponis <f=E>(ilia) Segillus annorum L hic situs est filii posierunt sic \n
EDCS-30100488.translation: Segillus, son of Iliatus, and Abua, daughter of Tappon. Segillus, aged 50, is laid here; his sons set this up.
EDCS-30100488.summary: This is a tombstone for Segillus, son of Iliatus, and his wife Abua, daughter of Tappon, erected by their sons, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100489.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Celeriano annorum LX Aureliae Sabinae filiae annorum IIII Aurelia Celeriana filia patri et sorori posuit\n
EDCS-30100489.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelia Celeriana set this up for her father, Aurelius Celerianus, aged 60, and her sister, Aurelia Sabina, aged 4.
EDCS-30100489.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Aurelia Celeriana for her father and sister, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100490.text_edition: \n Reuso Druti filius nnorum L structor fuit hic situs est Utto filius posuit\n
EDCS-30100490.translation: Reuso, son of Drutus, aged 50, was a builder; he is laid here. Utto, his son, set this up.
EDCS-30100490.summary: This is a tombstone for Reuso, a builder, erected by his son Utto, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30100491.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri sacrum Haemilius sic Quintilianus votum solvit\n
EDCS-30100491.translation: Sacred to Silvanus of the Woods. Sic Quintilianus Haemilius fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30100491.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus by Haemilius Sic Quintilianus.

EDCS-30100492.text_edition: \n Silvano Marculus legionis X voto dedit\n
EDCS-30100492.translation: Marculus of the Tenth Legion dedicated this to Silvanus by vow.
EDCS-30100492.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus by Marculus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion.

EDCS-30100493.text_edition: \n filius Aniensis Arimini |(centurio) legionis X geminae piae fidelis primus pilus legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis praefectus castrorum legionis eiusdem testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-30100493.translation: His son, from the Aniensis tribe, from Ariminum, chief centurion of the Tenth Gemina Pia Fidelis Legion, primus pilus of the Fourteenth Gemina Martia Victrix Legion, prefect of the camp of the same legion, ordered by will that this be made, and his heir saw to its construction.
