EDCS-32000875.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Lucius Antonius Magnus, a 55-year-old soldier of the X Gemina legion, buried by his wife Octavia Data, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-32000876.text_edition: \n Marcus Gavius Marci filius Aniensis Foro Iuli Cupitus centuria Sorn miles legionis X geminae annorum XXX aerum IIII hic situs est heres ex testamento faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-32000876.translation: Marcus Gavius Cupitus, son of Marcus, of the Aniensis tribe, from Forum Iulii, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina legion, 30 years old, honored with 4 bronze coins, lies here; his heir had this made according to his will.
EDCS-32000876.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Gavius Cupitus, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32000879.text_edition: \n Iulius Titi Aniensis Suavis miles [legionis X geminae ------\n
EDCS-32000879.translation: Iulius Suavis, son of Titus, of the Aniensis tribe, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina legion...
EDCS-32000879.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone inscription for Iulius Suavis, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32000880.text_edition: \n Nemesi Sanctae Numini Publius Aelius Septimius Mucapor |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae Severianae Alexandrianae EV N IANV\n
EDCS-32000880.translation: To Nemesis Sancta, divine power, Publius Aelius Septimius Mucapor, a centurion of the Fourteenth Gemina Severiana Alexandriana legion, [dedicates this].
EDCS-32000880.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Publius Aelius Septimius Mucapor, a centurion of the Fourteenth Gemina legion, likely dating to the Severan period.

EDCS-32000881.text_edition: \n Iunoni Reginae Caius Valerius Clemens |(centurio) legionis XIIII\n
EDCS-32000881.translation: To Juno Regina, Gaius Valerius Clemens, a centurion of the Fourteenth legion...
EDCS-32000881.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Juno Regina by Gaius Valerius Clemens, a centurion of the Fourteenth legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32000883.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae Valerinus Mercurialis Fortunius Andrias Valerius Pertinax clavicularii votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-32000883.translation: Valerinus Mercurialis, Fortunius Andrias, and Valerius Pertinax, the 'clavicularii' (perhaps traders or dockworkers), dedicated this vow to Nemesis Augusta, gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-32000883.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Nemesis Augusta by a group of individuals identified as 'clavicularii', likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32000884.text_edition: \n Caius Comatius Flavinus immunis caerei legionis XIIIII geminae Antoninianae ex iussu deae posuit Imperatore Domino nostro Anto[nino] Augusto IIII et Balbino consulibus MEI\n
EDCS-32000884.translation: Gaius Comatius Flavinus, an immunis (exempt soldier) from the Fourteenth Gemina Antoniniana legion, set this up by order of the goddess, when our Lord Emperor Antoninus Augustus IV and Balbinus were consuls.
EDCS-32000884.summary: This is a dedication set up by Gaius Comatius Flavinus, a soldier of the Fourteenth Gemina legion, during the consulship of Antoninus Augustus IV and Balbinus (AD 212-213).

EDCS-32000885.text_edition: \n Mercurio sacrum Caius Iulius Catullinus miles legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-32000885.translation: To Mercury, Gaius Julius Catullinus, a soldier of the Fourteenth Gemina legion, dedicated this vow, gladly, happily, and deservedly.
EDCS-32000885.summary: This is a votive inscription to Mercury by Gaius Julius Catullinus, a soldier of the Fourteenth Gemina legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32000888.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Antonius Max...
EDCS-32000888.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Antonius Max, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32000919.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription mentioning a veteran, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32000920.text_edition: \n Caius Statius Cai filius Fabia S miles \n
EDCS-32000920.translation: Gaius Statius, son of Caius, of the Fabia tribe, a soldier...
EDCS-32000920.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription for Gaius Statius, a soldier, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32100356.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Mithrae Aurelius Mac votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-32100356.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Mithras, Aurelius Mac willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-32100356.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Sol Invictus Mithras by Aurelius Mac.

EDCS-32200822.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name of Marcus Acilius Viriatus.

EDCS-32200823.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo omnipotenti sacrum pro salute Publi Alfi Maximi\n
EDCS-32200823.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Almighty, for the health of Publius Alphius Maximus.
EDCS-32200823.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter for the well-being of Publius Alphius Maximus.

EDCS-32200825.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Silvanus Domestico.

EDCS-32200826.translation: To the departed spirits, Iulia Tunn, aged [---].
EDCS-32200826.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name and age of Iulia Tunn.

EDCS-32200827.summary: This is likely a funerary or commemorative inscription recording the name of Valerius Victorinus.

EDCS-32200829.text_edition: \n Titianus et Marcellus parentibus carissimis alumni inpendio sic suo fecerunt\n
EDCS-32200829.translation: Titianus and Marcellus made this at their own expense for their dearest parents.
EDCS-32200829.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Titianus and Marcellus for their parents.

EDCS-32200874.summary: This is a dedication to the deity Silvanus Domestico.

EDCS-32200879.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valentinae bene merenti Iulius Ingenuos sic patronus fecit\n
EDCS-32200879.translation: To the departed spirits, for Valentina who deserved well, Iulius Ingenuos, her patron, made this.
EDCS-32200879.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by the patron Iulius Ingenuos for his freedwoman, Valentina.

EDCS-32200881.text_edition: \n D(is) Manibus Hyacinto cubiculario Crispinae Augustae ex privatis Commodi Aug(usti) vixit annos XXXATI FIIOL\n
EDCS-32200881.translation: To the departed spirits, for Hyacinthus, chamberlain of Crispina Augusta, from the private funds of Commodus Augustus, he lived 30 years [---].
EDCS-32200881.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Hyacinthus, a chamberlain to Crispina Augusta and from the private funds of Commodus Augustus, specifying his age.

EDCS-32200882.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Septimius Valerius Valeria filia \n
EDCS-32200882.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Septimius Valerius [dedicated this], daughter of Valeria.
EDCS-32200882.summary: This dedication to Silvanus Domesticus was made by Septimius Valerius, the daughter of Valeria, likely on a tomb or a shrine.

EDCS-32200883.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimio Aistomodio regi Germanorum Septimii Philippus et Heliodorus fratri incomparabili\n
EDCS-32200883.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Septimius Philippus and Heliodorus [dedicated this] to their incomparable brother, Septimius Aistomodus, king of the Germans.
EDCS-32200883.summary: This tomb inscription honors Septimius Aistomodus, king of the Germans, and was set up by his brothers Philippus and Heliodorus.

EDCS-32201488.text_edition: \n Dis inferis Manibus Marcus Marci filius Dasius Vatens Marcus Dasius M(arci)? f(ilius) Senilis et Valeria Valeriana parentes\n
EDCS-32201488.translation: To the underworld gods, the spirits of the departed. Marcus Marcius Dasius Vatens, son of Marcus, and Marcus Dasius Senilis, son of Marcus (?), and Valeria Valeriana, his parents [dedicated this].
EDCS-32201488.summary: This tomb inscription records the dedication made by the parents to their deceased son(s) and daughter, Marcus Marcius Dasius Vatens and Marcus Dasius Senilis, and Valeria Valeriana.

EDCS-32300151.text_edition: \n Volcano et Veneri Augustis sacrum signa et aedem Caius Avillius Chrysanthus magistratu suo de pecunia sua dedit\n
EDCS-32300151.translation: Sacred to Vulcan and Venus Augusti. Caius Avillius Chrysanthus dedicated the statues and the temple from his own funds during his magistracy.
EDCS-32300151.summary: This inscription records the dedication of statues and a temple to Vulcan and Venus by Caius Avillius Chrysanthus at his own expense while serving as a magistrate.

EDCS-32300154.text_edition: \n Ariomanus Titi Flavi Samionis servus annorum III HOPTE Samio posuit\n
EDCS-32300154.translation: Ariomanus, slave of Titus Flavius Samio, aged 3, 'HOPTE' [perhaps a nickname or a term of endearment] placed this for Samio.
EDCS-32300154.summary: This inscription marks the death of Ariomanus, a 3-year-old slave of Titus Flavius Samio, who was placed there by Samio.

EDCS-32300155.text_edition: \n Herculi sacrum Quintus votum solvit libens laetus\n
EDCS-32300155.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Quintus fulfilled his vow, willingly and joyfully.
EDCS-32300155.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules, recording that Quintus willingly and joyfully fulfilled a vow made to the god.

EDCS-32300156.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus aedilis et Aurelia Valla vivi sibi et Aureliae Flotentinae annorum XXXV hic sitae parentes filiae pientissimae fecerunt\n
EDCS-32300156.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. The aedile and Aurelia Valla made [this] while alive for themselves and for their most devoted daughter, Aurelia Flotentina, aged 35, who lies here.
EDCS-32300156.summary: This tomb inscription was set up by parents (one of whom was an aedile) for their deceased daughter Aurelia Flotentina, also recording their own burial place.

EDCS-32300165.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a dedication to the goddess Tyche.

EDCS-32300181.text_edition: \n IAC ANNFI ATER II votum solvit libens EIV VI O V\n
EDCS-32300181.translation: [Inscription is too damaged to provide a meaningful translation or summary].
EDCS-32300181.summary: This inscription is too fragmentary to provide any specific information about its content or purpose.

EDCS-32300182.text_edition: \n Iunia Cocceia pue<r=LL>ae sanctissimae annorum XX hic sita est Gallus pater filiae pientissimae sibi et coniugi vivi pos<u=I>erunt \n
EDCS-32300182.translation: Iunia Cocceia, most holy maiden, aged 20, lies here. Gallus, father, and [her mother] for their most devoted daughter, for themselves and their spouse, erected [this] while alive.
EDCS-32300182.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial of Iunia Cocceia, aged 20, and was set up by her father Gallus and mother, who also reserved a burial place for themselves and their spouse.

EDCS-32300183.text_edition: \n Lucio Flavio Rogatiano socero et Flaviae Iuturnae socruvi Titus Flavius Hilario sibi vivus et coniugi carissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-32300183.translation: Titus Flavius Hilario had this erected for his father-in-law Lucius Flavius Rogatianus and his mother-in-law Flavia Iuturna, and for himself while alive, and for his dearest wife.
EDCS-32300183.summary: This is a funerary epitaph funded by Titus Flavius Hilario for his parents-in-law and himself and his wife, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32300184.text_edition: \n Belatusa Cauti liberta Boius posuit annorum XXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-32300184.translation: Belatusa, freedwoman of Cautus, a Boian, erected this. She was 30 years old and lies here.
EDCS-32300184.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Belatusa, a freedwoman, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32300185.text_edition: \n Flavia Amuliu sic Vernaclus annorumVX EC filio IT XTEI \n
EDCS-32300185.translation: Flavia Amuliu, (daughter) of Vernaclus, aged [X] V... and [X]... son...
EDCS-32300185.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for Flavia Amuliu, daughter of Vernaclus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32300186.text_edition: \n Fortunae Conservatrici Marcus Aurelius Cocceius Florianus primus pilus legionis X geminae Severianae cum suis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-32300186.translation: Marcus Aurelius Cocceius Florianus, first-rank centurion of the Severan Tenth Gemina Legion, willingly and joyfully fulfilled his vow to Fortuna Conservatrix, as is deserved.
EDCS-32300186.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a shrine to Fortuna Conservatrix by Marcus Aurelius Cocceius Florianus, a high-ranking soldier, likely dating to the Severan period (late 2nd to early 3rd century CE).

EDCS-32300187.text_edition: \n Genio |(centuriae) primi pili Iulius Proclianus custos armorum votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-32300187.translation: Iulius Proclianus, guardian of the arms, willingly and joyfully fulfilled his vow to the Genius of the first-rank centuria, as is deserved.
EDCS-32300187.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating to the Genius of a military unit by Iulius Proclianus, an armor custodian, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32300188.text_edition: \n Invicto Deo Mithrae pro salute Augusti Ulpius Secundus miles legionis X geminae votum solvit libens laetus\n
EDCS-32300188.translation: Ulpius Secundus, a soldier of the Tenth Gemina Legion, willingly and joyfully fulfilled his vow to the unconquered god Mithras for the safety of the Emperor.
EDCS-32300188.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mithras for the emperor's safety by Ulpius Secundus, a soldier, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32300189.translation: Sacred to Neptune. Gaius Vibius Celer, centurion.
EDCS-32300189.summary: This is a dedication to Neptune by Gaius Vibius Celer, a centurion, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32300190.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico Gargini(a) Procula votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-32300190.translation: Garginia Procula willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Silvanus Domestus.
EDCS-32300190.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating to Silvanus Domestus by Garginia Procula, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32300192.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Nervae filius Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate imperator consul pater patriae \n
EDCS-32300192.translation: Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, son of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, holder of tribunician power, imperator, consul, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-32300192.summary: This is a formal titulature of Emperor Trajan, recording his titles and achievements, likely from an official monument dating to his reign (98-117 CE).

EDCS-32300193.text_edition: \n miae Lucillae quae vixit annos XXII Titius Successus veteranus legionis X geminae coniugi simae et Titi Successus et Lucillianus et Pota matri dulcissimae posuerunt\n
EDCS-32300193.translation: Titius Successus, a veteran of the Tenth Gemina Legion, and Titius Successus and Lucillianus and Pota erected this for their most sweet mother Lucilla, who lived 22 years.
EDCS-32300193.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucilla, erected by her husband Titius Successus and their children, Titius Successus, Lucillianus, and Pota, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32300194.translation: To the spirits of the departed, Gaius Julius...
EDCS-32300194.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Gaius Julius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32300197.text_edition: \n Legio XIII Gemina bonos eventus tibi orat Flavius Valentinus\n
EDCS-32300197.translation: The Thirteenth Legion Gemina prays for good fortune for you, Flavius Valentinus.
EDCS-32300197.summary: This is a prayer inscription, likely from the Roman army, where the Thirteenth Legion Gemina invokes good fortune for Flavius Valentinus, possibly a soldier or official.

EDCS-32300323.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating the maker or owner, Ritogenes, possibly related to a craft or object.

EDCS-32300331.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely identifying the owner or subject, Gnaeus Sucellus Aprilis.

EDCS-32300431.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Aterigis filio Ateboduo annorum LXXV veterano emerito cohortis II Varcianorum equiti cornuclario sic praefecti Crispinae coniugi annorum LV et Flavio Augurino nepoti annorum V heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-32300431.translation: His heirs have seen to the making of this for Titus Flavius Ateboduus, son of Aterigis, aged 75, a retired veteran of the 2nd Cohort Varcianorum, a cavalryman with a horn, and for his wife Crispa, aged 55, and for his grandson Flavius Augurinus, aged 5.
EDCS-32300431.summary: This is a funerary monument for a retired cavalry veteran, Titus Flavius Ateboduus, and his family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32300438.text_edition: \n Silvano Vibena votum solvit libens laeta merito\n
EDCS-32300438.translation: Vibena gladly and justly fulfilled her vow to Silvanus, as was deserved.
EDCS-32300438.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Vibena's fulfillment of a vow to the deity Silvanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32300484.text_edition: \n Septimus vixit LX Amasia annorum V Viator annorum X Escitatus vivus et Tertia vivi posuerunt Se<p>tuminus annorum XV\n
EDCS-32300484.translation: Septimus lived 60 years. Amasia, 5 years old. Viator, 10 years old. Escitatus, alive, and Tertia, alive, placed this. Septuminus, 15 years old.
EDCS-32300484.summary: This inscription records the ages of several individuals, Septimus, Amasia, Viator, Escitatus, Tertia, and Septuminus, who are either deceased or alive at the time of its erection, likely a family funerary marker.

EDCS-32300612.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Secundus Aviti vivus fecit sibi et Litussae Esinerti filiae annorum LX coniugi pientissimae\n
EDCS-32300612.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Secundus, son of Avitus, made this for himself while alive, and for his most devoted wife Litussa Esinertis, aged 60.
EDCS-32300612.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Secundus for himself and his deceased wife, Litussa Esinertis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32300623.text_edition: \n Gadaptissa Devognatae liberta annorum LXXX hic est fili-- matri posu---\n
EDCS-32300623.translation: Gadaptissa, freedwoman of Devognatus, aged 80, is here... for her son... mother...
EDCS-32300623.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription for Gadaptissa, a freedwoman, and mentions her son and mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-32300628.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely a funerary dedication or epitaph, for Putulla, daughter of Atecurus.

EDCS-32300629.text_edition: \n Claudia Tiberi liberta Candida viva posuit sibi et Florentini servo no coniugi suo annorum \n
EDCS-32300629.translation: Claudia Candida, freedwoman of Tiberius, erected this for herself and for Florentinus, her husband, a slave, [who was] of the age of [---].
EDCS-32300629.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a freedwoman for herself and her husband, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32300630.text_edition: \n Flavia filia Valla cineres CLVSIO Fl \n
EDCS-32300630.translation: Flavia Valla, daughter of [---], [erected this] for the ashes of CLVSIO Fl [---].
EDCS-32300630.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the erection of a monument for someone's ashes.

EDCS-32300632.text_edition: \n Marcus Tullius Successus annorum L et Attia Matrona annorum filius parentibus fecit\n
EDCS-32300632.translation: Marcus Tullius Successus, aged 50, and his son Attia Matrona, erected this for [their] parents.
EDCS-32300632.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a son and his father for their deceased parents.

EDCS-32300639.text_edition: \n Dignae filiae LI et Caio Vindiciano filio vivi vivis fecerunt et Aeliae Faustinae nepti annorum IIII et Marco Metilio Domitiano nepoti hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-32300639.translation: While still alive, [they] erected this monument for their living daughter, aged [LI], and for their son Gaius Vindicianus, and for Aelia Faustina, their granddaughter, aged 4, and for Marcus Metilius Domitianus, their grandson; they will not be succeeded by an heir.
EDCS-32300639.summary: This funerary monument was erected by parents for their children and grandchildren, with a clause excluding future heirs.

EDCS-32300672.text_edition: \n Mercurio Vatria Carnuntina votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-32300672.translation: Vatria Carnuntina willingly and deservedly paid her vow to Mercury.
EDCS-32300672.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Mercury, likely from a private individual in Roman Britain or a frontier province.

EDCS-32300673.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Mithrae Publius Aelius Valerianus miles legionis XIIII geminae strator consularis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-32300673.translation: Publius Aelius Valerianus, a soldier of the Legio XIIII Gemina, strator consularis, willingly, joyfully, and deservedly paid his vow to the unconquered god Mithras.
EDCS-32300673.summary: This is a votive inscription by a soldier of the Legio XIIII Gemina to the god Mithras, possibly from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-32300687.text_edition: \n Tertius Samuelis filius annorum XX hic est situs pater posuit filio\n
EDCS-32300687.translation: Tertius, son of Samuel, aged 20, is laid here; his father erected [this] for his son.
EDCS-32300687.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his deceased son.

EDCS-32300698.text_edition: \n Ulpia Suri filia Prima annorum X hic sita est pater filiae posuit\n
EDCS-32300698.translation: Ulpia Prima, daughter of Surus, aged 10, is laid here; her father erected [this] for his daughter.
EDCS-32300698.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a father for his young daughter.

EDCS-32300701.translation: [---]us, [his] son, erected [this] for himself while still alive.
EDCS-32300701.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph indicating a monument erected by a son for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-32300703.text_edition: \n Vindaina Veiconis filia annorum XX hic est sita Belatomar-- et Cobua Verciovi fili-- vivi Cocate Sura posuerunt sorori et nepti\n
EDCS-32300703.translation: Belatomar[---] and Cobua, son of Verciov[---], while alive, erected [this] for Vindaina Veiconis, their sister, aged 20, and for Cobua, granddaughter [of ---].
EDCS-32300703.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by siblings for their sister and niece.

EDCS-32300704.text_edition: \n NONII annorum II et Ponla filia annorum LX\n
EDCS-32300704.translation: Nonius, aged 2 years, and Ponla, his daughter, aged 60 years.
EDCS-32300704.summary: This epitaph records the ages of Nonius and Ponla, likely family members, on a funerary monument.

EDCS-32300707.text_edition: \n Firmo Primi annorum LX et Restutae Atressi filiae coniugi fili-- fec---\n
EDCS-32300707.translation: For Firmo, aged 60 years, and his wife Restuta, daughter of Atressus; their son made [this]...
EDCS-32300707.summary: This fragmented epitaph commemorates Firmo, his wife Restuta, and mentions their son, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-32300708.text_edition: \n Attia A filia coniux vivi fecerunt sibi et Avitae filiae annorum XXVIII et Avetae filiae annorum XXVII\n
EDCS-32300708.translation: Attia, daughter of A, his wife, made [this] for herself while alive, and for their daughters Avita, aged 28 years, and Aveta, aged 27 years.
EDCS-32300708.summary: This funerary inscription records Attia and her husband commissioning their tomb for themselves and their two daughters.

EDCS-32300715.text_edition: \n a Flavi Ivini annorum alia annorum XI hic sitae sunt MI filiabus pientissimis posuit\n
EDCS-32300715.translation: Here lie Flavia, daughter of Ivinius, aged 11 years, and Alia [?]; [her] most dutiful daughters placed [this].
EDCS-32300715.summary: This epitaph, set up by daughters, records the burial of Flavia and Alia, both young girls.

EDCS-32300716.text_edition: \n Pater posuit Cenumarus Sacioni filius decessit annorum VX et Gnatila annorum XX decessit\n
EDCS-32300716.translation: Cenumarus, son of Sacio, placed [this]; he died aged 15 years, and Gnatila, aged 20 years, died.
EDCS-32300716.summary: This epitaph records the deaths and ages of Cenumarus and Gnatila, with Cenumarus identified as the one who placed the monument.

EDCS-32300721.text_edition: \n o annorum LXXX liae coniugi annorum L R pro pietate faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-32300721.translation: ...aged 80 years, and his wife, aged 50 years, took care to have this made out of devotion.
EDCS-32300721.summary: This funerary inscription records the ages of a man and his wife, with their devotion being the reason for the monument's construction.

EDCS-32300727.translation: Secundio for his son Op [?], Suadula.
EDCS-32300727.summary: This very brief inscription seems to record the names of Secundio, his son Op, and Suadula, likely on a funerary monument.

EDCS-32300729.text_edition: \n miles legionis XIIII geminae parentibus pientissimis fecit\n
EDCS-32300729.translation: A soldier of the Legio XIIII Gemina made [this] for his most dutiful parents.
EDCS-32300729.summary: This dedication records a soldier of the Legio XIIII Gemina honoring his parents with a monument.

EDCS-32400006.text_edition: \n ------] us OR hic situs est Spuri filia \n
EDCS-32400006.translation: ... is buried here, daughter of Spurius.
EDCS-32400006.summary: This fragmented epitaph records that a daughter of Spurius is buried at this location.

EDCS-32400009.text_edition: \n Deo Mithrae sacrum Lucius Cassius Florentinus votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-32400009.translation: Lucius Cassius Florentinus gladly fulfilled his vow to the god Mithras.
EDCS-32400009.summary: This dedication records Lucius Cassius Florentinus fulfilling a vow made to the god Mithras.

EDCS-32400282.summary: This inscription records the name of Marcus Aurelius Secundus, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-32400284.summary: This inscription contains fragmented names, possibly indicating a dedication or ownership.

EDCS-32400288.summary: This inscription records the name Severiani Secundus, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-32400294.text_edition: \n Sextus Titius Moderatus |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis Iovi Optimo Heliopolitano votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-32400294.translation: Sextus Titius Moderatus, a centurion of the Legio XIV Gemina Martia Victrix, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Heliopolitanus.
EDCS-32400294.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sextus Titius Moderatus, a centurion, dedicating a promise to Jupiter Optimus Heliopolitanus.

EDCS-32400300.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Quintilius Pudorus, patron.
EDCS-32400300.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the memory of Quintilius Pudorus, who held the status of patron.

EDCS-32400301.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum et Maglae Septimi-- Val et Septimia Val filia \n
EDCS-32400301.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus, and Magla Septimi Val and Septimia Val, daughter.
EDCS-32400301.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus, and mentions Magla Septimi Val and her daughter Septimia Val.

EDCS-32400302.text_edition: \n Silvano libens votum solvit Flavius Lucianus miles legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis Severianae Agricola et Clementino consulibus\n
EDCS-32400302.translation: Flavius Lucianus, a soldier of the Legio XIV Gemina Martia Victrix Severiana, willingly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus, in the consulship of Agricola and Clementinus.
EDCS-32400302.summary: This is a votive inscription by Flavius Lucianus, a soldier of the Legio XIV Gemina Martia Victrix Severiana, to the god Silvanus, dated by consuls.

EDCS-32500165.text_edition: \n Ianuarius Matulli filius eque<s=X> annorum XXV in cohorte \n
EDCS-32500165.translation: Ianuarius, son of Matullus, aged 25, in the cohort.
EDCS-32500165.summary: This inscription records the name, parentage, age, and service of Ianuarius within a military cohort.

EDCS-32500167.text_edition: \n Matugenta Aprilis filia annorum IIII vernacula Titi Flavi Biturigis hic sita est pater filiae posuit\n
EDCS-32500167.translation: Matugenta Aprilis, daughter, aged 4, a native. Here lies Titi Flavius Biturigis. Her father placed this for his daughter.
EDCS-32500167.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of a young girl, Matugenta Aprilis, with her father, Titi Flavius Biturigis, identified as the dedicator.

EDCS-32500170.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Silvano signifero cohortis Dquingenaria Canathenorum stipendiorum XXI nnorum XXX domo Damasco Sattonius Ursinus \n
EDCS-32500170.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, standard-bearer of the Cohors Quingenaria Canathenorum, with 21 years of service, aged 30, from Damascus. Sattonius Ursinus [erected this].
EDCS-32500170.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Silvanus, a standard-bearer of a specific cohort, detailing his service and age, with Sattonius Ursinus identified as the dedicator.

EDCS-32500172.text_edition: \n PAI fili- annorum XXVIII faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-32500172.translation: Pai took care to have this made, at the age of 28.
EDCS-32500172.summary: This funerary inscription records the commission of a monument for Pai, who died at age 28.

EDCS-32500174.text_edition: \n Sintillius gedonis filius nnorum LX hic situs est Annitio Secconis filia coniunx viva sibi et coniugi posuit et <f=E>iliae faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-32500174.translation: Sintillius, son of Gedo, aged 60, lies here. Annitia, daughter of Seccon, his wife, alive, had this set up for herself and her husband and children.
EDCS-32500174.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Sintillius and his wife Annitia, who commissioned the monument for herself, her husband, and their children.

EDCS-32500198.text_edition: \n Caius Broc decurio coloniae Claudiae Savariae vivus fecit sibi et Saxi et AI anno--- X\n
EDCS-32500198.translation: Gaius Broc, decurion of the colony of Claudius Savaria, made this himself while alive for himself and Saxa and Al... in year 10.
EDCS-32500198.summary: This funerary inscription records Gaius Broc, a decurion in Savaria, making arrangements for his own burial and that of others.

EDCS-32500288.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticius.

EDCS-32600020.summary: This is a possessive inscription, likely a boundary marker or ownership record, for Quintus Veratius Prospectus.

EDCS-32600030.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly related to a property or family.

EDCS-32602058.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Catus Verini libertus annorum LXV Neratia Quintilla procuravit at sic cuius arbitrium per me efferri iussit\n
EDCS-32602058.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Catus, freedman of Verinus, aged 65, arranged this; he ordered it to be carried out according to his own wishes, through me.
EDCS-32602058.summary: This funerary inscription records the arrangements made by Catus, a freedman, for his burial, with instructions given to an executor.

EDCS-32700009.text_edition: \n Q Titi filius R miles legionis XV Apollinaris fecit\n
EDCS-32700009.translation: Quintas, son of Titus, a soldier of the Legio XV Apollinaris, made this.
EDCS-32700009.summary: This votive inscription records a soldier, Quintas, of the Legio XV Apollinaris, dedicating something he made.

EDCS-32700089.text_edition: \n Marti <et=RI> Victoriae Lucius Dom<i=T>tius Constitutus miles legionis X geminae beneficiarius consularis votum solvit <l=I>(ibens) merito XI Kalendas Iunias Praesente <e=F>t Extricato consulibus \n
EDCS-32700089.translation: To Mars and Victory, Lucius Domitius Constitutus, a soldier of Legio X Gemina, a beneficiarius of the consular legate, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow on the 11th day before the Kalends of June, with [name] and Extricatus as consuls.
EDCS-32700089.summary: This votive inscription records a soldier, Lucius Domitius Constitutus, fulfilling a vow to Mars and Victory during a specific consular year.

EDCS-32700096.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ressita viator et lege crudelem casum Quartinus \n
EDCS-32700096.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ressita, a traveler, and by law, Quartinus [recorded?] a cruel fate.
EDCS-32700096.summary: This funerary inscription is a brief epitaph for Ressita, noting her death as a traveler and potentially attributed to unjust circumstances.

EDCS-32700099.translation: The Fifteenth Legion Apollinaris, Quintus Cassius.
EDCS-32700099.summary: This inscription records a military unit and possibly an individual, Quintus Cassius, belonging to Legio XV Apollinaris, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-32700114.summary: This fragment mentions a Germanic woman, with the context of her identity or role being lost.

EDCS-32700115.text_edition: \n l Luperci decurionalibus ornamentis Aquinci decreto decurionum Aquincensium ornati et Tiberiae Faventinae \n
EDCS-32700115.translation: the Luperci, adorned with decurional ornaments, honored by a decree of the council of Aquincum, and Tiberia Faventina...
EDCS-32700115.summary: This inscription details the honors bestowed upon the Luperci and Tiberia Faventina by the decurions of Aquincum, indicating a civic honorific monument.

EDCS-32700118.text_edition: \n tabularius et vilicus stationis Confluentes ex voto posuit et signum AELI ex \n
EDCS-32700118.translation: the registrar and the overseer of the customs post at Confluentes set this up in fulfillment of a vow, and the statue of Aelius...
EDCS-32700118.summary: This inscription records a vow made by a registrar and an overseer of the customs post at Confluentes, possibly dedicated to a deity or a person named Aelius.

EDCS-32700128.text_edition: \n Fonti Perenni Epictetus et Viator Servandi Quinti Sabini Verani tertiae partis conductoris publici portorii vilici vicarii\n
EDCS-32700128.translation: To Fontes Perennes, Epictetus and Viator, deputies of Servandus, son of Quintus Sabinus Veranius, the supervisor of the public tax farm, and his vicarius...
EDCS-32700128.summary: This is a dedication to the "Perennial Springs" by Epictetus and Viator, who were deputies for a collector of public taxes, indicating a religious or public works dedication.

EDCS-32700155.text_edition: \n iarotus Dini <f=I>(ilius) annorum XVI nn Irotri filius annorum X hic siti sunt fratres posuerunt \n
EDCS-32700155.translation: Iarotus, son of Dinius, aged 16, and Irotus, son of Irotus, aged 10. Brothers erected this monument for them. They lie here.
EDCS-32700155.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of two brothers, Iarotus and Irotus, who died at young ages, with the monument erected by their siblings.

EDCS-32700156.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavio Flaviano Tromentina Flavini filio militi legionis X Geminae stipendiorum VII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-32700156.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Flavius Flavianus of the Tromentina tribe, son of Flavinius, a soldier of the Tenth Legion Gemina, with 7 years of service, had this made by his heir.
EDCS-32700156.summary: This funerary inscription is for Flavius Flavianus, a soldier of Legio X Gemina, who died after 7 years of service, with the monument prepared by his heir.

EDCS-33100001.translation: In the consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-33100001.summary: This inscription provides a date based on the Roman consuls Modestus and Probus, likely marking the year of another event or monument.

EDCS-34100076.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate V consul IIII pater patriae procons(ul) et Imperator Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII consul III pater patriae proconsul et Publius Cornelius Licinius Valerianus nobilissimo sic Caesar milia passuum II\n
EDCS-34100076.translation: Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 5th time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country, proconsul; and Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 4th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, proconsul; and Publius Cornelius Licinius Valerianus, most noble Caesar, two miles...
EDCS-34100076.summary: This inscription records the titles and powers of Emperors Valerian and Gallienus, along with a mention of Publius Cornelius Licinius Valerianus, and indicates a distance of two miles, possibly related to road construction or marking.

EDCS-35100003.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription with a personal name and a month, likely a dedication or a note related to an individual named L. Mu[...].

EDCS-35100006.text_edition: \n defuncto Altino annorum XX et E Marcellino militi legionis supra scriptae annorum XXXII D et Marcello militi legionis XIII annorum XXX Passeri annorum III matri fratri Marcello Aureli Quinti \n
EDCS-35100006.translation: For Altinus, deceased, aged 20, and Marcellinus, a soldier of the aforementioned legion, aged 32, and Marcello, a soldier of the 13th Legion, aged 30, and Passerus, aged 3, [dedicated by] mother, brother, Marcello, Aurelius Quintus.
EDCS-35100006.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely from a tomb, commemorating several individuals and family members, with some bearing military titles.

EDCS-36300013.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-36300013.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XXIV imperator II consul IV pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae appellantur I Aravacorum et I Ulpia contariorum |(milliaria) et III Augusta Thracum et I Thracum victrix et I nnatium et cohortibus VI I Aelia Gaesatorum sagittaria et I Ulpia nnum |(milliaria) et V Callaecorum Lucensium et XVIII voluntariorum et II Alpinorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et sunt in Pannonia superiore sub Nonio Macrino legato quinque et viginti stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis ante diem VI Idus Februarias Marco Annio Libone Quinto Numisio Iuniore consulibus alae I nnatium civium Romanorum cui praeest Trau<l=E>lius Rufinus ex gregale Nigro Siusi filius Azalo descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum <d=T>ivi Augusti ad Minervam Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XXIV imperator II consul IV pater patriae equitibus et peditibus qui militaverunt in alis V quae appellantur I Aravacorum et I Ulpia contariorum |(milliaria) et III Augusta Thracum et I Thracum victrix et I nnatium et cohortibus VI I Aeliae Gaesatorum sagittaria et I Ulpia nnoniorum |(milliaria) et V Callaecorum Lucensium et XVIII voluntariorum et II Alpinorum et I Thracum civium Romanorum et sunt in nnia superiore sub Nonio Macrino legato XXV stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt civitatem Romanam qui eorum non haberent dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singulis Ante diem VI Idus Februarias Libonee et Iuniore consulibus alae I nnatium civium Romanorum cui praeest Traullius Rufinus ex gregale Nigro Siusi filius Azalo descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Marci Servili Getae Luci Pulli Chresimi Tiberi Iuli Felicis Cai Belli Urbani Publi Graecini Crescentis Luci Senti Chrysogoni Cai Pomponi Statiani \n
EDCS-36300013.translation: Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 24th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in the five alae, which are called I Aravacorum, I Ulpia Contariorum milliaria, III Augusta Thracum, I Thracum Victrix, I nnatium, and in the six cohorts: I Aelia Gaesatorum Sagittaria, I Ulpia Nnnoniorum milliaria, V Callaecorum Lucensium, XVIII Voluntariorum, II Alpinorum, and I Thracum Civium Romanorum, and who are in Pannonia Superior under Nonius Macrinus, as legate, having completed twenty-five years of service and honourably discharged, whose names are listed below, granted Roman citizenship to those who did not have it, and the right of legal marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or with those they would subsequently marry, provided it was one wife only. On the 6th day before the Ides of February, in the consulship of Marcus Annius Libo and Quintus Numisius Iunior. For the ala I nnatium Civium Romanorum, commanded by Traullius Rufinus. From a common soldier, Niger, son of Siusus, from Azalo. Described and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the Temple of the deified Augustus, near the Temple of Minerva. Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power for the 24th time, Imperator for the 2nd time, Consul for the 4th time, Father of the Fatherland, to the cavalrymen and infantrymen who served in the five alae, which are called I Aravacorum, I Ulpia Contariorum milliaria, III Augusta Thracum, I Thracum Victrix, I nnatium, and in the six cohorts: I Aeliae Gaesatorum Sagittaria, I Ulpia Nnoniorum milliaria, V Callaecorum Lucensium, XVIII Voluntariorum, II Alpinorum, and I Thracum Civium Romanorum, and who are in Pannonia Superior under Nonius Macrinus, as legate, having completed twenty-five years of service and honourably discharged, whose names are listed below, granted Roman citizenship to those who did not have it, and the right of legal marriage with the wives they then had when citizenship was granted to them, or with those they would subsequently marry, provided it was one wife only. On the 6th day before the Ides of February, in the consulship of Libo and Iunior. For the ala I nnatium Civium Romanorum, commanded by Traullius Rufinus. From a common soldier, Niger, son of Siusus, from Azalo. Described and confirmed from a bronze tablet which is fixed in Rome on the wall behind the Temple of the deified Augustus, near the Temple of Minerva. By Marcus Servilius Geta, Lucius Pullius Chresimus, Tiberius Iulius Felix, Caius Bellius Urbanus, Publius Graecinius Crescens, Lucius Sentius Chrysogonus, Caius Pomponius Statianus.
EDCS-36300013.summary: This is an imperial rescript granting Roman citizenship and legal marriage rights to auxiliary soldiers who served in specific alae and cohorts in Pannonia Superior, issued during the reign of Antoninus Pius and recorded on a bronze tablet in Rome.

EDCS-36400133.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-36400133.translation: He gladly paid his vow to Silvanus Domesticus as he deserved.
EDCS-36400133.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the god Silvanus Domesticus.

EDCS-43201211.summary: This is a very brief inscription dedicating something to a god, likely a fragment of a larger text or a simple label.

EDCS-43201215.summary: This is a dedication or mention of a person named Thalamos, possibly a deity or a benefactor.

EDCS-43201225.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or an identification of an individual named Eutyches, possibly with a patronymic or a familial connection indicated by OORY.

EDCS-43201227.summary: This inscription is a dedication or a mention of an individual named Maximos.

EDCS-43201239.text_edition: \n equiti cohortis annorum XL stipendiorum XXII Verus Praesentis filius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-43201239.translation: Verus, son of Praesens, saw to its construction for the cavalryman of the cohort, aged 40, with 22 years of service.
EDCS-43201239.summary: This is an epitaph or a dedication for a cavalryman, noting his age and length of service, with the monument arranged by Verus, son of Praesens.

EDCS-44200229.text_edition: \n Iuni Itulonis Eiconic-- X |(denarii) VIII pur\n
EDCS-44200229.translation: Of Iunius Itulo, from Eiconic, 8 denarii.
EDCS-44200229.summary: This is a record of a debt or payment, likely from an account book or ledger, indicating a sum of 8 denarii owed by or to Iunius Itulo.

EDCS-44200230.text_edition: \n Osiri Au<g=C>(usto) Isidis sacerdos Domatius Ingenuus fecit \n
EDCS-44200230.translation: The priest Domatius Ingenuus made [this] for Augustus Osiris, [son of] Isis.
EDCS-44200230.summary: This is a dedication made by Domatius Ingenuus, a priest, to the deity Osiris, who is associated with Augustus and Isis.

EDCS-44200231.text_edition: \n Mansueta Publi Kastifuci Sabini annorum XXX et Quartio annorum VI filius eius hic siti sunt Rogatus coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-44200231.translation: Here lie Mansueta, aged 30, and her son Quartio, aged 6. Rogatus placed this for his wife.
EDCS-44200231.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating a mother, Mansueta, and her son, Quartio, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-44200232.text_edition: \n haruspex decurio municipii Brigetionis curator civium Romanorum s filius et heres --- mon(umentum) magnum faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-44200232.translation: The haruspex, decurion of the municipality of Brigetio, curator of Roman citizens, and his son and heir [erected this monument].
EDCS-44200232.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument by a haruspex and decurion of Brigetio, who also held the office of curator civium Romanorum.

EDCS-44200234.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caes verna quae vixit annos XXXVIIII Claudius Arrius beneficiarius legati legionis I adiutricis coniugi obsequentissimae faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-44200234.translation: To the divine spirits. Claudius Arrius, a beneficiarius of the legate of the I Adiutrix legion, had this made for his most obedient wife, Caes [---] verna, who lived 39 years.
EDCS-44200234.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Caes verna, the wife of Claudius Arrius, a beneficiarius of the I Adiutrix legion, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44200236.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ci veterani legionis I adiutricis militis i missi h(onesta)? m(issione) --- stip(endiorum) XXIII et Noviae Fidelitati coniugi eius Titus Florius Vitalis decurio municipii Brigetionensium vice parentis dilecto posuit\n
EDCS-44200236.translation: To the divine spirits. Titus Florius Vitalis, decurion of the municipality of Brigetionensium, placed this as a parent for his beloved Novia Fidelitas, his wife, and for [himself?], a veteran of the I Adiutrix legion, discharged honorably after 23 years of service.
EDCS-44200236.summary: This funerary monument, erected by Titus Florius Vitalis, a decurion of Brigetio, honors his wife Novia Fidelitas and likely himself as a veteran of the I Adiutrix legion.

EDCS-44200238.text_edition: \n Podium cum suis spectaculis pedum LXX legioni I adiutrici piae fideli Severianae Lucius Veratius Iulianus IIvir quinquennalis municipii Brigetionensium a solo sumptibus suis extruxit\n
EDCS-44200238.translation: Lucius Veratius Iulianus, IIvir quinquennalis of the municipality of Brigetionensium, built this podium with its seating for the I Adiutrix Pia Fidelis Severiana legion from the ground up at his own expense.
EDCS-44200238.summary: This inscription records the construction of a podium with seating for the I Adiutrix legion by Lucius Veratius Iulianus, a prominent magistrate of Brigetio, likely in the Severan period.

EDCS-44200242.text_edition: \n o Pastori militi cohortis XVIII voluntariorum stipendiorum XII annorum XXXV et Papiae Helpidi annorum XXXX et Urviniae Mussae annorum XXVIII et Publio Urvinio et P(ublio) Marcellino annorum XXII Papia Matercia mater et Publius Urvinius Messius et Iulius Maior fratres\n
EDCS-44200242.translation: For Pastor, a soldier of the 18th cohort of volunteers, with 12 years of service, aged 35; and for Papia Helpidi, aged 40; and for Urvinia Musa, aged 28; and for Publius Urvinius and Publius Marcellinus, aged 22. Papia Matercia, mother, and Publius Urvinius Messius and Julius Maior, brothers.
EDCS-44200242.summary: This is a funerary inscription detailing the burials of Pastor, a soldier, and several women, with their ages and familial relationships, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-44200258.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating that the object or monument was made by a person named Cupitus.

EDCS-44200262.text_edition: \n Mercurio Augusto sacrrum Pomponius Emeritus veteranus legionis XIIII geminae Antoninianae ex beneficiario consularis \n
EDCS-44200262.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus. Pomponius Emeritus, a veteran of the XIIII Gemina Antoniniana legion, formerly a beneficiarius consularis.
EDCS-44200262.summary: This is a religious dedication to Mercury Augustus by Pomponius Emeritus, a veteran of the XIIII Gemina Antoniniana legion and former beneficiarius consularis.

EDCS-44300002.summary: This is a religious dedication to the god Silvanus Silvestris.

EDCS-44300049.text_edition: \n Claudius Tr princeps legionis XV Ap(ollinaris)\n
EDCS-44300049.translation: Claudius Tr, princeps of the XV Apollinaris legion.
EDCS-44300049.summary: This inscription identifies Claudius Tr as a princeps, a non-commissioned officer rank, in the XV Apollinaris legion.

EDCS-44300050.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus cineribus Gaiae Iuliae Calies sic annorum LX hic sita est filii matri pientissumae sic posuerunt\n
EDCS-44300050.translation: To the departed spirits, the ashes of Gaia Iulia, aged 60, lie here. Her sons erected this to their most dutiful mother.
EDCS-44300050.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaia Iulia, erected by her sons, likely dating from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-44500097.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely the beginning of a name, Gaius Octavius, possibly part of a larger dedication or funerary text.

EDCS-46400820.text_edition: \n X Imperator Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VII imperator VI consul pater patriae proconss(ul) et Imperator Caesar Valerius Licinianus Licinius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate IIII imperator III consul pater patriae proconss(ul) Exemplum sacrarum litterarum have Dalmati carissime nobis cum in omnibus pro devotione ac laboribus suis militum nostrorum commodis adque sic utilitatibus semper consultum esse cupiamus in hoc etiam dispositionum nostrarum provisione eiusdem militibus nostris consulendum esse credidimus Dalmati carissime unde intuentes labores eorundem militum nostrorum quos pro rei publicae statu et commodis adsiduis discursibus sustinent providendum ac disponendum esse credidimus ut et militiae suae tempore iucundis laborum suorum fructibus ex nostra provisione se perfrui gaudeant et post militiam quiaeto sic otio et congrua securitate potiantur itaque devotioni tuae significandum esse credidimus ut idem milites nostri militiae quidem suae tempore quinquem sic capita iuxta statutum nostrum ex censu adque sic a praestationibus sollemnibus annonariae pensitationis excusent eademque immunia habeant adque sic cum completis stipendiis legitimis honestam missionem idem fuerint consecuti sed et hii sic qu<i> licet posd sic viginti stipendia adaeque honestam missionem adepti fuerint ab annonario titulo duo kapita excusent id est tam suum quam etiam uxoris suae si quis forte ex preli<o> sic vulnere causarius fuerit effectus etiam si intra viginti stipendia ex ea causa rerum suarum vacationem fuerit consecutus ad beneficium eiusdem indulgentiae nostrae pert<i>niat sic ita ut suum et uxsoris sic suae kaput excuset adque sic ut omni modo tam quietis suae securitati quam etiam commodis consultum provisionis nostrae beneficio idem milites gratulentur licet eiusmodi antehac conconsuetudo fuerit ut plurimi homines simul honestam missionem a duce perciperent penes act<u>arium missoria permanente exempla sibi singuli quique exciperent tamen volumus ut cum vel honestam vel ca<u>sariam sicuti supra dictum est missionem milites consecuntur sic singuli quique specialem a duce in personam suam accipiant missionem quo probatione veritatis ac fidei aput sic <se> permanente securitate stabili a<c=T> firmissima perfruantur pervidet sane dicatio tua eos qui dilicti sic sui gratia dimittuntur ad beneficium legis eiusdem pertinere non posse cum utriusque rei rationem haberi oporteat ac vitae probabilis instituta adquae sic honestam missionem sed et merita militiae praemia a nobis condigna percipere conve<ni>at ut et eiusdem indulgentiae nostrae beneficio perpetuo idem milites perpetuo perfruantur ac sempiterna dispositionis nostrae provisio obtineat firmitatem volumus tenorem huius indulgentiae nostrae describtum sic per singula quaeque castra aput sic signa in tabula aerea consecrari quo tam legionarii milites quam etiam equites in vexillationibus constituti Inlyriciani sic sicuti similis sic laboris sic militiae suae sustine<n=II>t ita etiam provisionis nostrae similibus co<m=IV>modis p<e=I>rfruantur et manu divina vale Dalmati carissime nobis divo Maximiano VIII et domino nostro Maximino Augusto iterum consss(ulibus) IIII Idus Iunias Serdica S F \n
EDCS-46400820.translation: To Dalmatius, our most beloved, Emperor Caesar Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, with seventh tribunician power, sixth time Imperator, first consul, father of the country, proconsul, and Emperor Caesar Valerius Licinianus Licinius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, with fourth tribunician power, third time Imperator, first consul, father of the country, proconsul. An example of our sacred laws, for we wish to always consult for the benefit and advantage of our soldiers who, for their devotion and labors, are always attentive to our soldiers' welfare. In this also, through the provision of our arrangements, we have believed it necessary to consult for our same soldiers. Dalmatius, our most beloved, therefore, considering the labors of our same soldiers, which they endure through constant expeditions for the stability and benefit of the republic, we have believed it necessary to provide and arrange, so that during their military service they may enjoy the fruits of their labors with joy from our provision, and after military service they may attain peaceful leisure and suitable security. Thus, we have believed it necessary to make known to your devotion, that our same soldiers, during their military service, may be excused from the annual payment for five heads, according to our decree from the census and from the solemn contributions, and may have the same immunities. And when they have obtained an honorable discharge with completed legitimate service, even those who have completed twenty years of service and have obtained an honorable discharge, let two heads be excused from the annual tax, that is, both their own and that of their wife, if anyone happens to have become disabled from a wound in battle, even if he has obtained release from duty for that reason within twenty years of service, let him fall under the benefit of our same indulgence, so that their own head and that of his wife be excused. And thus, in all ways, let the soldiers rejoice in the benefit of our provision for the security of their peace and their advantage. Although it has been customary before for many men to receive an honorable discharge at once from the general, with the discharge papers remaining with the registrar, and each individual receiving them for themselves, however, we wish that when soldiers obtain either an honorable or a disability discharge, as stated above, each individual may receive a special discharge from the general in their own person, so that with the proof of truth and loyalty remaining with them, they may enjoy stable and most firm security. Your devotion certainly perceives that those who are dismissed for their own reasons cannot belong to the benefit of this law, since it is necessary to take into account both aspects: the institutions of a life worthy of praise and an honorable discharge, and also to receive fitting rewards from us for their military merits. It is fitting that the soldiers may perpetually enjoy the benefit of our same indulgence and that the provision of our eternal decree may obtain firmness. We wish the tenor of this indulgence of ours to be inscribed and consecrated on a bronze tablet in each and every camp and standard, so that both legionary soldiers and cavalrymen stationed in vexillations, who endure similar labors and military service, may also enjoy similar benefits of our provision. Farewell by divine hand, our most beloved Dalmatius. In the consulship of Divus Maximianus VIII and our lord Maximinius Augustus, the fourth day before the Ides of June, at Serdica.
EDCS-46400820.summary: This is a rescript from Emperors Constantine and Licinius to Dalmatius, detailing regulations for soldier benefits including exemptions from taxes and honorable discharges, dated June 9, 319 CE at Serdica.

EDCS-46600869.translation: Stephanus, with his family, gave this gift to Savercna.
EDCS-46600869.summary: This is a dedication by Stephanus and his family to Savercna, likely a deity or person, possibly a votive offering.

EDCS-46700064.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or commemorates something related to Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-46700065.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or commemorates something related to Emperor Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-46700068.translation: A vow to Silvanus, by a traveler named VM.
EDCS-46700068.summary: This inscription records a vow made to the god Silvanus by a traveler.

EDCS-46700070.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or commemorates something related to Emperor Nero Claudius.

EDCS-46700071.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or commemorates something related to Emperor Galba.

EDCS-47800712.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Pomponius Respectus optio votum solvit libens laetus merito Faustino et Rufino consulibus\n
EDCS-47800712.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Pomponius Respectus, a staff officer, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Faustinus and Rufinus were consuls.
EDCS-47800712.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Pomponius Respectus, a military officer, dated to the consulship of Faustinus and Rufinus.

EDCS-48001058.translation: Marcus [---], son of Quintus, of the Scaptia tribe, a veteran of the Legio VIII [---], ordered to be made by his will for himself and [---]tiana.
EDCS-48001058.summary: This funerary inscription, though heavily restored, records Marcus [---], a veteran of Legio VIII, and likely his family.

EDCS-48600011.text_edition: \n Legio XIII gemina centuria Anni Rufi\n
EDCS-48600011.translation: The Thirteenth Gemina Legion, the century of Annius Rufus.
EDCS-48600011.summary: This inscription identifies a unit of the Legio XIII Gemina, specifically the century of Annius Rufus.

EDCS-48800016.summary: This inscription records the name of Cephalon and his affiliation with the Legio II.

EDCS-48800044.summary: This inscription simply records the name Aurelius Magnius.

EDCS-48900569.text_edition: \n CI Valentina votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-48900569.translation: C(aius) Iulius Valentina willingly and deservedly paid her vow.
EDCS-48900569.summary: This dedication records C. Iulius Valentina fulfilling a vow, likely to a deity.

EDCS-48900570.text_edition: \n Legio XV Apollinaris Caius Iulius Cito milis sic legionis XV Apollin(aris)\n
EDCS-48900570.translation: Caius Iulius Cito, a soldier of the Fifteenth Apollinaris Legion.
EDCS-48900570.summary: This inscription identifies Caius Iulius Cito as a soldier serving in the Legio XV Apollinaris.

EDCS-48900571.summary: This inscription names Flavius Respectus and indicates he belonged to the century of Aper.

EDCS-48900579.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Aufidia Luci filia Gentina votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-48900579.translation: Aufidia Luci f(ilia) Gentina willingly and deservedly paid her vow to Silvanus Domestus.
EDCS-48900579.summary: This dedication records Aufidia Gentina fulfilling a vow to the deity Silvanus Domestus.

EDCS-48900581.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Varius Carus votum solvit\n
EDCS-48900581.translation: Varius Carus paid his vow to the Unconquered God.
EDCS-48900581.summary: This dedication records Varius Carus fulfilling a vow to the Unconquered God (Deo Invicto).

EDCS-48900582.text_edition: \n Dibus et Diabus Volumnius Primigenius et Caninia Adiecta votum solverunt libentes laeti merito\n
EDCS-48900582.translation: Volumnius Primigenius and Caninia Adiecta willingly, joyfully, and deservedly paid their vows to the goddesses (Dibus et Diabus).
EDCS-48900582.summary: This dedication records Volumnius Primigenius and Caninia Adiecta fulfilling vows to a pair of goddesses.

EDCS-48900584.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Antonius Restitutus votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-48900584.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Publius Antonius Restitutus gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-48900584.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Publius Antonius Restitutus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-48900589.text_edition: \n Lucius Cominius Titi filius Romilia Firmus lixa nnum XXV hic situs est Titus et Caius Cominii fratri posuerunt Cominius hic iaceo sed morte peremptus optabam in patriam dehinc cum fratribus verti quos liqui et morti debita fata dedi o utinam Italiae potius mea fata dedissent quam premeret cineres barbara? terra? meos\n
EDCS-48900589.translation: Lucius Cominius Firmus, son of Titus, of the Romilia tribe, a camp follower, aged 25, is buried here. Titus and Caius Cominii set him up, a brother. I, Cominius, lie here, but struck down by death. I wished to return to my homeland and then to my brothers, whom I left behind, and surrendered to the destined fate of death. Oh, if only my fate had led me to Italy, rather than my ashes being pressed down by a foreign land!
EDCS-48900589.summary: An epitaph for Lucius Cominius Firmus, a camp follower, who expresses regret over dying far from his homeland, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-48900590.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai filius Maecia Axalo annorum XXX stipendiorum VIIII hic situs est Caius Varius municeps posuit\n
EDCS-48900590.translation: Caius Julius Axalo, son of Caius, of the Maecia tribe, aged 30, with 9 years of service, is buried here. Caius Varius, a fellow townsman, set him up.
EDCS-48900590.summary: An epitaph for Caius Julius Axalo, a soldier who died after 9 years of service, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-48900591.text_edition: \n Marcana Luci liberta Chrysario annorum XXXV hic sita est Fausta liberta posuit\n
EDCS-48900591.translation: Marcana Chrysaris, freedwoman of Lucius, aged 35, is buried here. Fausta, a freedwoman, set her up.
EDCS-48900591.summary: An epitaph for Marcana Chrysaris, a freedwoman, erected by another freedwoman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-48900593.text_edition: \n C LV CS A pro salute Spectates sic coniugis votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-48900593.translation: For the health of Spectatus, his wife C. LV. C. S. A. gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-48900593.summary: A vow for the health of Spectatus, fulfilled by his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-48900596.text_edition: \n MA PIIS M centuria Urbici Aerti Su LTER |(denariorum) I semis\n
EDCS-48900596.translation: MA PIIS M. of the century of Urbicus, AERTUS SU... for one and a half denarii.
EDCS-48900596.summary: A fragmentary inscription that seems to record a payment or a valuation related to a centurion named Urbicus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-50000147.text_edition: \n Titus Aurelius Silvanus veteranus ex magistro navaliorum legionis XIIII geminae natione Hispanus Tarraconensis nnorum LXXXI vivus sibi et Aeliae Iustinae coniugi obsequentissimae et Aureliis Maximinae annorum VII Marco nnorum V et Floro nnorum V filiis pientissimis \n
EDCS-50000147.translation: Titus Aurelius Silvanus, a veteran and former naval officer of the 14th Gemina legion, a native of Hispania Tarraconensis, aged 81, alive, for himself and for his most obedient wife Aelia Iustina, and for his most dutiful children Maximina, aged 7, Marcus, aged 5, and Florus, aged 5.
EDCS-50000147.summary: A self-funded tomb inscription for Titus Aurelius Silvanus, a veteran of the 14th legion, and his family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-50200138.summary: A dedication by Cintusmus to Alarus, possibly a memorial or a marker, from a provincial area.

EDCS-50200140.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly containing personal names or place names, origin uncertain.

EDCS-50200145.summary: A fragmentary inscription possibly containing the name Paulinus, with additional unclear letters, origin uncertain.

EDCS-50700004.text_edition: \n Imperatori Gaio Iulio Vero Maximo nobilissimo Caesari Germanico maximo Sarmatico maximo Dacico maximo principi iuventutis filio Maximini Augusti eqquites alae III Augustae Thracum Maximinianae devoti numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-50700004.translation: To Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, the most noble Caesar, greatest of Germans, greatest of Sarmatians, greatest of Dacians, prince of the youth, son of Maximus Augustus; the equites of the III Augustae Thracum Maximianae Ala, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-50700004.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the equites of the III Augustae Thracum Maximianae Ala to Emperor Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, likely from the reign of Maximinus Thrax (235-238 AD).

EDCS-50700006.text_edition: \n Herculi Invicto et Numini Auggustorum Lucius Septimius Sabinianus ex voto\n
EDCS-50700006.translation: To the Unconquered Hercules and the Divine Majesty of the Emperors, Lucius Septimius Sabinianus, in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-50700006.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Hercules and the Emperors by Lucius Septimius Sabinianus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-50700009.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Publi filio Sergia Mogetianae Iustino veterano legionis XIIII geminae Valeria Cyrilla heres marito pientissimo vicsit sic annos L\n
EDCS-50700009.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Publius Aelius Iustinus, a veteran of the XIIII Geminae Legion, lived 50 years. Valeria Cyrilla, his heir, to her most devoted husband.
EDCS-50700009.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Publius Aelius Iustinus, a veteran of the XIIII Geminae Legion, who lived 50 years, and is dedicated by his wife Valeria Cyrilla.

EDCS-50700010.text_edition: \n Gaius Valerius Fuscus veteranus legionis II adiutricis annorum XLV et Romaniae Prososti filiae annorum XX nepotis sic Marcus P Germanus filius annorum Marius libertus hic sepultus est\n
EDCS-50700010.translation: Gaius Valerius Fuscus, a veteran of the II Adjutricis Legion, aged 45, and Romana Prososti, daughter of Protos, aged 20, and their grandson Marcus P. Germanus, son, and Marius, freedman, are buried here.
EDCS-50700010.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Valerius Fuscus, a veteran of the II Adjutricis Legion, his wife Romana Prososti, and their grandson and freedman, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-51100445.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico et Magulae sacrum Vindius Karus votum solvit\n
EDCS-51100445.translation: To Silvanus Domestica and Magula, Vindius Karus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-51100445.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Domestica and Magula by Vindius Karus, likely from a rural setting and dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51100447.summary: This very short inscription simply states the name Ulpius Ratus acted by order, possibly related to a construction or administrative task.

EDCS-51100454.translation: To Sol Invictus, Exsuperatus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-51100454.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the sun god Sol Invictus by Exsuperatus, likely from the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-51100456.text_edition: \n Invicto Deo Mithrae Aurelius Veri votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-51100456.translation: To the Unconquered God Mithras, Aurelius Verus willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-51100456.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Aurelius Verus, likely from a military context or a Mithraic sanctuary.

EDCS-51100461.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Varius Luci filius Verecundus veteranus legionis XV Apollinaris domo Lepido Regio annorum CVIII et Varia Luci liberta Margale annorum LXXX hic siti sunt \n
EDCS-51100461.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Varius Verecundus, son of Lucius, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris Legion, from Lepidus Regius, aged 108, and Varia Margale, freedwoman of Lucius, aged 80, are buried here.
EDCS-51100461.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Varius Verecundus, a veteran of the XV Apollinaris Legion, and Varia Margale, a freedwoman, detailing their ages and origins.

EDCS-51100462.summary: This inscription consists only of a name, Octavius Secundinus, possibly a tomb marker or a dedication to an individual.

EDCS-51100464.text_edition: \n Resta paulum forte viator et lege quaeso quam cito solventibus vitam tandem hanc prius apstulit fatumentia vixit annos V oppressus fato \n
EDCS-51100464.translation: Pause a moment, traveler, and read, I pray, how quickly life slips away. Fate snatched this one away too soon; she lived 5 years, overcome by fate.
EDCS-51100464.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the death of a 5-year-old child, dated by the reference to fate.

EDCS-51100465.text_edition: \n Nertomarus Viss et Toutomara Atri vivi sibi et TIRO \n
EDCS-51100465.translation: Nertomarus and Toutomara, alive, for themselves and for Tiro.
EDCS-51100465.summary: This funerary inscription, likely for a family or a specific person named Tiro, was set up by Nertomarus and Toutomara during their lifetime.

EDCS-51100467.text_edition: \n Supero annorum XXII Exsuperatae annorum XVIII hic siti est sic Aelius Vitalis et Ulpia Cavva genero et filiae parentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-51100467.translation: Superus, aged 22, and Exsuperata, aged 18. Aelius Vitalis and Ulpia Cavva, parents, placed this here for their son-in-law and daughter.
EDCS-51100467.summary: This tombstone records the burial of a son-in-law (Superus) and daughter (Exsuperata) and was erected by their parents, Aelius Vitalis and Ulpia Cavva.

EDCS-51100486.inscription_type: diplomata militaria, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-51100486.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Hadriani filius divi Traiani Parthici nepos divi Nervae pronepos Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIII imperator II consul IIII pater patriae iis qui militaverunt in classe praetoria Misenensi quae est sub Valerio Paeto sex et vi<g=C>inti stipendiis emeritis dimissis honesta missione quorum nomina subscripta sunt ipsis liberis posterisque eorum civitatem Romanam dedit et conubium cum uxoribus quas tunc habuissent cum est civitas iis data aut si qui caelibes essent cum iis quas postea duxissent dumtaxat singuli singulas ante diem VII Kalendas Novembres Lucio Petronio Sabino Caio Vicrio Rufo consulibus ex gregale Tiberio Claudio Secundi filio Masculo Boio ex nnia descriptum et recognitum ex tabula aerea quae fixa est Romae in muro post templum divi Augusti ad Minervam Publi Atti Severi Luci Pulli Daphni Marci Servili Getae Luci Pulli Chresimi Marci Sentili Iasi Tiberi Iuli Felicis Cai Iuli Silvani \n
EDCS-51100486.translation: Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, son of the deified Hadrian, grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 8th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 4th time, father of the country. To those who served in the praetorian fleet at Misenum, which is under Valerius Paetus, having completed 26 years of service and honorably discharged, whose names are subscribed, he granted Roman citizenship and the right of marriage with the wives they then had at the time citizenship was granted to them, or if they were unmarried, with those they would later marry, provided each man married only one woman. This was done on the seventh day before the Kalends of November, in the consulship of Lucius Petronius Sabinus and Gaius Vicrius Rufus. A copy and confirmation from a bronze tablet fixed at Rome on the wall behind the temple of the deified Augustus, near the temple of Minerva, under the supervision of Publius Attius Severus, Lucius Pullus Daphnus, Marcus Servilius Geta, Lucius Pullus Chresimus, Marcus Sentilius Iasus, Tiberius Julius Felix, Gaius Julius Silvanus. From a common soldier, Tiberius Claudius Masculus, son of Secundus, of the Boii tribe.
EDCS-51100486.summary: This is an imperial rescript from Emperor Antoninus Pius, dated to 148 AD, granting Roman citizenship and marriage rights to veterans of the Misenum fleet after completing 26 years of service.

EDCS-51400019.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Iulio Ingenuo equiti legionis I adiutricis piae fidelis Ailia sic Ingenua mater fecit\n
EDCS-51400019.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Ailia, mother of Marcus Julius Ingenuus, an equestrian of the First Legion Adjutrix, pious and faithful, made this.
EDCS-51400019.summary: This tombstone was erected by Ailia, mother of Marcus Julius Ingenuus, a cavalryman in the I Adjutrix legion.

EDCS-51400020.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Nigellioani Septimia Flavia Siscia quondam stipendiorum XVIII vixit annos XL beneficiario tribuni militum legionis I adiutricis Antoninianae Aelius Paternianus coregionarius eiusdem heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-51400020.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Julius Nigellianus, who served 18 years, lived 40 years, a beneficiarius of the tribune of the soldiers of the First Legion Adjutrix Antoniniana. Aelius Paternianus, his fellow countryman and heir, saw to the making of this.
EDCS-51400020.summary: This tombstone commemorates Julius Nigellianus, a soldier in the I Adjutrix legion who served 18 years and lived 40 years, and was erected by his heir, Aelius Paternianus.

EDCS-51400022.text_edition: \n Aeliae Domitiae nnorum III prae fas sapienti Aelius Domitius et Aurelia Gailla parentes infelicissimi\n
EDCS-51400022.translation: For Aelia Domitia, aged 3 years. Aelius Domitius and Aurelia Gailla, most unhappy parents.
EDCS-51400022.summary: This tombstone records the death of a 3-year-old girl, Aelia Domitia, and was erected by her grieving parents.

EDCS-51400024.translation: In the consulship of Avitus, Ulpius Viator placed this.
EDCS-51400024.summary: This inscription records that Ulpius Viator erected something (likely a monument or tomb) during the consulship of Avitus.

EDCS-52200544.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Aelia domo Roma Publi filius Mamianus tribunus cohortis I Pannoniorum equitata votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-52200544.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Publius Aelius Mamianus, son of Publius, from Rome, tribune of the First Cohort of Pannonians, cavalry, happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-52200544.summary: This dedication to Jupiter was made by Publius Aelius Mamianus, a tribune of the First Cohort of Pannonians, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-52200549.text_edition: \n pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marcus Flavius Marci filius Flavia Impetratus tribunus domo Saldas sic ex Mauretania Caesariensi votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-52200549.translation: For the safety of the Emperor Caesar. Marcus Flavius Imperatus, son of Marcus Flavius, tribune, from Saldæ, from Mauretania Caesariensis, happily and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-52200549.summary: This dedication was made by Marcus Flavius Imperatus, a tribune from Saldæ in Mauretania Caesariensis, for the safety of the Emperor.

EDCS-52200559.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum cohors I Ulpia nnoniorum |(milliaria) equitata cui praeest Marcus Flavius Marci filius Flavia Impetratus tribunus domo Saldas sic ex Mauretania Caesariensi votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-52200559.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Marcus Flavius Impetratus, son of Marcus, of the Cornelia tribe, tribunus of the First Cohort of Ulpians of Pannonia (milliaria), equipped with cavalry, from Saldae in Mauretania Caesariensis, has freely and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-52200559.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Marcus Flavius Impetratus, a tribunus of the First Ulpian Cohort of Pannonia, around the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-52200561.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum cohors I Ulpia Pannoniorum |(milliaria) equitata cui praeest Marcus Pompeius Marci filius Cornelia Pollio tribunus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-52200561.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Marcus Pompeius Pollio, son of Marcus, of the Cornelia tribe, tribunus of the First Cohort of Ulpians of Pannonia (milliaria), equipped with cavalry, has freely and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-52200561.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Marcus Pompeius Pollio, a tribunus of the First Ulpian Cohort of Pannonia, around the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-52200562.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo pro salute Imperatoris Marci Antoni Gordiani Pii Felicis Augusti cohors I Ulpia Pannoniorum |(milliaria) equitata Gordiana cui praeest rus tribunus Attico et Praetextato conssulibus\n
EDCS-52200562.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest, for the safety of the Emperor Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus. The First Cohort of Ulpians of Pannonia (milliaria), named Gordiana, commanded by [---]us, under the consulship of Atticus and Praetextatus.
EDCS-52200562.summary: An inscription dedicating the safety of Emperor Gordian III to Jupiter, erected by the First Ulpian Cohort of Pannonia, dated to their consulship in 241 CE.

EDCS-52200563.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Marcus Afranius Hannibalianus tribunus cohortis I Ulpiae Pannoniorum victtricis TER votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-52200563.translation: Sacred to Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Marcus Afranius Hannibalianus, tribunus of the First Victorious Cohort of Ulpians of Pannonia, has freely and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
